cluster_id: Plastics industry brittle failures and class-action lawsuits
display_name: Plastics industry brittle failures and class-action lawsuits

instances.name: Plastics industry class-action lawsuits
instances.summary: The plastics industry experienced multiple billion-dollar class-action lawsuits to replace brittle plastic components that failed in service.
instances.key_quote: they put in some plastics that were not so tough and they had some major failures they had billion dollar class action lawsuits to replace some of these things
instances.framework_lesson: The plastics industry did not systematically apply fracture toughness criteria to material selection until catastrophic failures and product recalls forced remediation.

instances.name: Plastics industry brittle failures and class-action lawsuits
instances.summary: The plastics industry experienced multiple major structural failures in brittle plastic products, resulting in billion-dollar class-action lawsuits for product replacement.
instances.key_quote: the plastics industry has had it several times because plastics can be very brittle or they can be very tough and they put in some plastics that were not so tough and they had some major failures they had billion dollar class action lawsuits to replace some of these things
instances.framework_lesson: Material selection standards for plastics applications failed to ensure adequate fracture toughness, necessitating expensive recalls and legal settlements.

cluster_id: Wire drawing thesis project on superconducting wire production
display_name: Wire drawing thesis project on superconducting wire production

instances.name: Wire drawing with inadequate die reduction increments
instances.summary: Superconducting wire production using small 5-10% draw increments through expensive diamond dies resulted in poor microstructure, high strain-rate operation, and center-line cracking.
instances.key_quote: with five or 10% draws you get lousy Deltas okay you get a high Delta operation and you're allow to crack the center
instances.framework_lesson: Die reduction schedules must use sufficiently large increments (30-50% rather than 5-10%) to avoid strain-rate effects and centerline cracking in wire drawing.

instances.name: Wire drawing thesis project on superconducting wire production
instances.summary: As a bachelor student thesis project making superconducting wires via wire drawing, Eagar purchased $1,000 worth of diamond dies in 5% increment steps, but learned that small incremental draws (5-10%) produced poor homogeneity and high stress concentration (Delta), requiring larger draws of 30-50% instead.
instances.key_quote: I had a government research contract I went out and bought $1,000 worth of dies and I had them in 5% increments okay when well that was the wrong way to go
instances.framework_lesson: Small incremental deformation steps increase process stress concentration (Delta) and inhomogeneity; effective processing requires substantial single-pass reductions (30-50%) despite intuitive appeal of gradual steps.

cluster_id: Tree stand aluminum turnbuckle lawsuit
display_name: Tree stand aluminum turnbuckle lawsuit

instances.name: Tree stand turnbuckle lawsuit
instances.summary: A man fell from a tree stand when an aluminum cast aluminum turnbuckle failed; expert testimony was required to explain porosity defects to a jury in a product liability trial near White Plains, New York.
instances.key_quote: this guy fell from the tree stand hurt himself and the turnbuckle was broken so and someone looked on the fracture surface and they found four pores
instances.framework_lesson: Scanning electron microscope images can mislead juries by magnifying small defects; expert witnesses must translate microscopic evidence into relatable physical analogies to communicate actual material loss.

instances.name: Tree stand aluminum turnbuckle lawsuit
instances.summary: A person fell from an aluminum cast turnbuckle in a tree stand, and Eagar testified as an expert witness that the failure was due to overloading, not porosity defects in the casting.
instances.key_quote: i had a case once where a guy fell out of a tree stand in fact actually had a number of cases where people fought a tree stand but this one was an aluminum cast aluminum turnbuckle

cluster_id: New Alvin submersible cost overrun
display_name: New Alvin submersible cost overrun

instances.name: New Alvin submersible cost overrun
instances.summary: NSF-funded deep-sea research submersible project estimated at $6.5 million by Woods Hole but ultimately cost $37 million due to titanium price inflation and loss of domestic manufacturing infrastructure.
instances.key_quote: in 2007 when they're about to let the contract China just sort of blew open the metals Market everything like the price of titanium doubled and tripled
instances.framework_lesson: Cost estimation frameworks that assume stable material pricing and existing manufacturing infrastructure fail when global commodity markets shift and domestic industrial capacity has been lost.

instances.name: New Alvin titanium hull replacement (cost overrun)
instances.summary: NSF and Woods Hole commissioned a new titanium-hulled research submersible to replace aging Alvin; projected costs rose from $6 million to $50 million due to loss of manufacturing capability and commodity price inflation.
instances.key_quote: they had NSF had twenty five million dollars in their budget Woods Hole who was pushing this they said we can build a new one for six million dollars I said bull it'll cost you fifteen ok well I was closer the actual cost was 50 million
instances.framework_lesson: Loss of specialized military-industrial manufacturing capacity between 1970s and 1990s made titanium submarine hull production economically infeasible at predicted costs.

cluster_id: HY-130 Seawolf submarine welds
display_name: HY-130 Seawolf submarine welds

instances.name: HY-130 Seawolf submarine welds
instances.summary: Submarine welds made with HY-130 steel experienced hydrogen cracking because weld metal hardness exceeded Rockwell C40, caused by alloy composition at the high end of specification ranges.
instances.key_quote: the problem with the hy 130 seawolf welds i call them hy130 the weld metal was sort of an hy130 weld metal they were up there above rockwell c40 okay that's why they're getting hydrogen cracking
instances.framework_lesson: Welding specifications that allow composition ranges at the high end can inadvertently produce weld hardness levels that exceed safe limits for hydrogen-assisted cracking.

instances.name: HY-130 submarine hull program
instances.summary: The Navy developed HY-130 steel in the 1960s and spent $50 million (1960 dollars) on its development but could not practically weld it in the varying thicknesses required for full submarine construction; Eagar advised the Chief Engineer of the Navy against using it for the Sea Wolf-class submarines around 1990.
instances.key_quote: they were going to promise Congress around 1990 that the third or fourth ship in the sea wolf class they're going to start making hy-130 ships
instances.framework_lesson: A material may be weldable under laboratory conditions but fail to meet practical structural requirements when applied to complex real-world geometries with variable thicknesses and conditions.

cluster_id: Submarine hull perforation scenario
display_name: Submarine hull perforation scenario

instances.name: Submarine hull puncture and flooding scenarios
instances.summary: In submarine service, large holes in pressure hulls are catastrophic—by the time welded patches can be made on thick, high-pressure sections, flooding and sinking are inevitable; surface ships can more easily plug and repair damage.
instances.key_quote: on a submarine you get a hole and you probably lose the ship a big hole and you probably lose the ship just because by the time you could plug something that thick and that high pressure you've already flooded most of the thing
materials_or_systems: thick steel sections under high hydrostatic pressure

instances.name: Submarine hull perforation scenario
instances.summary: Engineering analysis of why submarine design requires more conservative welding standards than surface ships: a hull breach cannot be plugged in time before catastrophic flooding at depth.
instances.key_quote: on a submarine you get a hole and you probably lose the ship a big hole and you probably lose the ship just because by the time you could plug something that thick and that high pressure you've already flooded most of the thing
instances.framework_lesson: Submarine applications demand higher material toughness and weld quality than surface ships because the consequences of failure are absolute and unpluggable; this risk tolerance drives conservative specifications.

cluster_id: Spot weld safety chain failure
display_name: Spot weld safety chain failure

instances.name: Spot-welded clamp bolt failure and personnel safety
instances.summary: A spot-welded clamp bolt on a ship's hull failed under pressure and was ejected, creating a serious safety hazard; chains were subsequently added to all such welds to catch ejected bolts.

instances.framework_lesson: Spot welds on bolts subjected to line-up pressure can fail and eject the bolt dangerously; physical containment (chains) was added after incidents occurred.

instances.name: Spot weld safety chain failure
instances.summary: Workers removing bolted spot welds from pressurized structures were struck by explosive release of the weld, leading to safety chains being mandated on all similar structures.
instances.key_quote: if that's you have welded you spot weld and discredit bolt on the side of the pole which says I'm going to be removed right could have that weld fields that's under pressure because you will line the holes it'll show you know explode across the whole
instances.framework_lesson: Hazard controls (safety chains) become standard practice only after an accident occurs; the safety device exists because someone was injured.

cluster_id: Aircraft crankshaft bolt tensioning
display_name: Aircraft crankshaft bolt tensioning

instances.name: Aircraft crankshaft bolting
instances.summary: High-precision bolting system used in aircraft engine crankshafts where bolt tension must be controlled to within ±10% of stress for fatigue resistance.

instances.framework_lesson: Standard torque wrenches provide only ±30% accuracy; critical applications require precision measurement techniques (micrometer-based stretch measurement) to achieve the ±10% stress control needed for fatigue resistance.

instances.name: Aircraft crankshaft bolt tensioning
instances.summary: Critical airplane engine crankshaft bolts are precision-tensioned by measuring elongation with micrometers to achieve ±10% stress control instead of ±30% from torque methods.
instances.key_quote: they actually grind the tip of the bolt and the bottom of the bolt to an exact dimension and someone takes a micrometer and this thing is two point six two inches before they tension it and after it's two point six eight inches

cluster_id: Molybdenum bar rolling failure (Philips Elmet)
display_name: Molybdenum bar rolling failure (Philips Elmet)

instances.name: Molybdenum bar rolling failure (Philips Elmet)
instances.summary: Molybdenum bar rolled under high deformation conditions (high Delta) developed severe cracking and alligatoring with extensive residual stresses, causing the material to fracture into tooth-like pieces resembling a crocodile.
instances.key_quote: if you actually try to roll this and back off in his book on the next page has a picture of the bar mum bar that they're rolling and it alligators they're rolling this between two rolls that have semicircular cross-section and the thing's got tremendous residual stresses
instances.framework_lesson: High Delta deformation operations with inhomogeneous strain distribution generate uncontrolled residual stresses that cause catastrophic brittle cracking in refractory metals.

instances.name: Molybdenum rolling alligatoring
instances.summary: Philips Elmet experienced severe alligatoring and cracking when rolling molybdenum sheet for IBM computer interconnects in the 1980s due to residual stresses from inhomogeneous deformation.
instances.key_quote: they were rolling um this thing and it just split right down the middle okay so there are residual stresses in this material otherwise it wouldn't split
instances.framework_lesson: Improper selection of deformation reduction (Delta) for the forming operation induces residual stresses that cause splitting during processing.

cluster_id: Naval Surface Weapon Center baseball bat testing
display_name: Naval Surface Weapon Center baseball bat testing

instances.name: Naval Surface Weapons Lab aluminum bat impact study

cluster_id: Magnesium automobile wheels corrosion in road salt
display_name: Magnesium automobile wheels corrosion in road salt

instances.name: Magnesium wheels corrosion failure
instances.summary: Early magnesium wheels on automobiles developed pitting and white rust within a year despite protective coatings, leading manufacturers to switch to aluminum wheels.
instances.key_quote: they would start pitting after a year i mean they'd put clear coat on them and all this other stuff and they would still start pitting when they're out of magnesium
instances.framework_lesson: Magnesium's extreme galvanic activity combined with residual stresses from manufacturing makes it unsuitable for exposed automotive applications without stress-relief heat treatment.

instances.name: Magnesium automobile wheels corrosion in road salt
instances.summary: Magnesium wheels corroded rapidly when exposed to road salt in northern climates, leading manufacturers to switch to aluminum wheels despite the magnesium name.
instances.key_quote: they got to go through all that road salt in the northern climates and they corrode most of the make where's the most magnesium found in the world in the ocean
instances.framework_lesson: Material selection for automotive applications exposed to salt environments must account for chloride solubility; magnesium chloride is highly soluble, making magnesium unsuitable for exterior components.

cluster_id: 1980s refrigerator shelf failures
display_name: 1980s refrigerator shelf failures

instances.name: 1980s refrigerator shelf failures
instances.summary: Refrigerators with plastic interiors failed prematurely because manufacturers screwed shelves into plastic, which lacks sufficient toughness and creates stress concentrations that cause brittle fracture.
instances.key_quote: I would go and buy a nice refrigerator and within three years it was a piece of junk because they screwed the shelves into the plastic
instances.framework_lesson: Material substitution requires design methodology changes; plastic cannot be designed identically to steel, and stress concentrations in low-toughness materials cause premature failure.

instances.name: 1980s refrigerator shelf failures
instances.summary: Refrigerators manufactured in the 1980s failed within three years because plastic interior walls could not handle screwed-in shelves, which created stress concentrations causing brittle fracture and rust staining.
instances.key_quote: I would go and buy a nice refrigerator and within three years it was a piece of junk because they screwed the shelves into the plastic
instances.framework_lesson: Designers applied steel design principles to plastic without accounting for plastic's lower fracture toughness and stress concentration sensitivity; by the 1990s manufacturers learned to slide shelves in molded slots instead.

cluster_id: Chevrolet Vega engine removal for spark plug service
display_name: Chevrolet Vega engine removal for spark plug service

instances.name: Chevrolet Vega engine removal for spark plug service
instances.summary: The Chevrolet Vega's engine and chassis were designed independently; assembly required removing the entire engine to access spark plugs due to structural interference.
instances.key_quote: when I got he needed to change the spark plugs you had to remove the engine to change the spark plugs because someone had a little interference like the structure of the vehicle
instances.framework_lesson: Lack of design-for-manufacturability and cross-functional coordination between powertrain and body engineers results in poor serviceability and increased lifetime maintenance costs.

instances.name: Chevrolet Vega engine serviceability design flaw
instances.summary: The engine placement in the Vega frame made spark plugs inaccessible for maintenance without removing the entire engine due to blocking components.
instances.key_quote: when you needed to go change the spark plugs on a Chevy Vega you had to remove the engine because some of the other components were blocking it
instances.framework_lesson: Design reviews and manufacturing specifications must include serviceability and maintenance checks across the full assembly, not just individual component design.

cluster_id: Sea wall bolt failures
display_name: Sea wall bolt failures

instances.name: Sea wall bolt failures
instances.summary: High-strength steel bolts in coastal seawall structures experienced head fracture after six months due to hydrogen generation from galvanic corrosion in salt water.
instances.key_quote: i've had three or four times i've had someone come to me with a sea wall they built at some port and the heads of the bolts are coming off after six months
instances.framework_lesson: Galvanic coupling in marine environments can generate hydrogen through corrosion, causing brittle failure in high-strength fasteners even though no hydrogen was present at installation.

instances.name: Sea wall bolt head failures at ports
instances.summary: Bolt heads fractured and fell off within six months on sea walls at multiple port installations due to hydrogen generated by corrosion, initially misattributed to defective bolts.
instances.key_quote: i've had three or four times i've had someone come to me with a sea wall they built at some port and the heads of the bolts are coming off after six months what can they say oh must be defective bolts no it's corrosion

cluster_id: US Airways Flight 1549 (Sully)

instances.name: US Airways Flight 1549 (Sully)

cluster_id: Caroline Joseph corrosion testing project
display_name: Caroline Joseph corrosion testing project

instances.name: Caroline Joseph corrosion testing project
instances.summary: An undergraduate intern at an oil company in Houston was assigned to conduct corrosion tests on clad pipe for an oil or gas field project to determine whether to specify carbon steel or alloy steel based on field corrosiveness.
instances.key_quote: she was supposed to be looking at corrosion tests of clad pipe for they wanted to do a project an oil project in some country and she was supposed to be looking at the corrosive nature of this oil or gas field and determine should they use carbon or alloy steel

instances.name: Caroline Joseph oil/gas project corrosion case
instances.summary: An undergraduate intern evaluated whether clad pipe or carbon steel should be used in a corrosive oil/gas field project; she determined clad pipe was necessary but the project was cancelled due to local content and employment requirements in the host country.
instances.key_quote: she did determine that they really should use clad pipe it was a very corrosive product either getting out of the ground in that country but there were several other problems
instances.framework_lesson: Material selection decisions driven by technical merit (corrosion resistance) are overridden by externalities such as local manufacturing content requirements and local labor mandates.

cluster_id: Liquid Metal Technologies startup
display_name: Liquid Metal Technologies startup

instances.name: Liquid Metal Technologies
instances.summary: An MIT-affiliated venture proposed dissolving environmental waste in molten steel as a pollution solution, raised $130 million, but the concept failed to account for sodium in waste streams, which destroys steelmaking refractories.
instances.key_quote: there's an element that will destroy steel refractories called sodium do you think there's any sodium in most of the waste streams if you want to throw garbage of is sodium all over right it's a couple percent of the ocean
instances.framework_lesson: Promoters of novel technologies for environmental remediation failed to conduct adequate feasibility analysis regarding chemical compatibility between waste constituents and process equipment.

instances.name: Liquid Metal Technologies startup
instances.summary: An MIT-affiliated startup claiming to dispose of radioactive waste by transmuting nuclei in a steel bath received a $30 million Department of Energy contract; the company was indicted about three years later for fraudulent claims.
instances.key_quote: they were getting 30 million dollars from your government in my government part of our tax money and this is just you know how bad is the science right well what finally happened was about three years later they were indicted
instances.framework_lesson: Lack of technical due diligence and materials science expertise in government contracting allowed scientifically implausible proposals to receive substantial funding.

cluster_id: Rare earth element supply chain disruption
display_name: Rare earth element supply chain disruption

instances.name: Rare earth element supply chain disruption
instances.summary: China restricted rare earth exports used in starter motors, threatening global automotive industry and prompting consideration of reopening U.S. mines that had been closed due to lower prices.
instances.key_quote: how much would it cost to redesign all the starter motors on all the cars in the world everybody had to start paying these horrendous prices

instances.name: Rare earth supply disruption
instances.summary: China leveraged monopoly control of 97% of rare earth production to cut off supplies to Japanese electronics manufacturers Hitachi and Toshiba in 2010-2011, halting their ability to produce magnets for electronics.
instances.key_quote: in 2010 they basically told hitachi and uh and toshiba and all these people in japan they were not going to ship berths to them they could no longer make the electronics
instances.framework_lesson: Monopolistic control of critical materials by a single nation can create supply-chain vulnerability; engineers and manufacturers must develop alternative materials or diversify sources when facing such risks.

cluster_id: Chromic acid environmental and health impacts
display_name: Chromic acid environmental and health impacts

instances.name: Chromic acid environmental and health impacts
instances.summary: Chrome and chrome plating shop workers developed elevated cancer incidence from chromic acid exposure, leading to closure of chromium plating operations and a 15-year hiatus until environmentally controlled shops were redesigned.
instances.key_quote: they did a study and showed that they had a much higher incidence of cancer okay so they shut down the shops it wasn't until the mid-90s that ford built an environmentally protected sulfuric acid i mean a chromic acid plating shop
instances.framework_lesson: Workplace and environmental regulations lagged behind the discovery of carcinogenic effects of hexavalent chromium, requiring costly retrofitting of plating facilities.

instances.name: Chromic acid laboratory contamination (1970s)
instances.summary: Laboratory and manufacturing practice of rinsing glassware and equipment in chromic acid baths and draining directly to sewers was later recognized as hexavalent chromium environmental contamination.
instances.key_quote: if i did that today they'd probably handcuff me and take me away okay but we this was the 1970s early 70s 10 years later you know aaron brockovich kind of learned about chromic acid or hexavalent chrome
instances.framework_lesson: Industrial hygiene and environmental regulations lagged behind scientific understanding of hexavalent chromium toxicity; practices considered routine in the 1970s were later criminalized.

cluster_id: Quincy Shipyard barge cracking failure
display_name: Quincy Shipyard barge cracking failure

instances.name: Quincy Shipyard barge cracking failure
instances.summary: Barges built to carry number six bunker oil experienced cracking problems due to a rinse tank with pH of 2.3, as acidic as Coca-Cola, causing material degradation.
instances.key_quote: the rinse tank had a ph of 2.3 the rinse water had a ph of 2.3 that's as strong as coca-cola you know in terms of acidity um and so they had cracking problems
instances.framework_lesson: Inadequate maintenance and monitoring of cleaning tank chemistry led to unexpected corrosive damage; standards for rinse tank pH control were not sufficiently specified or enforced.

instances.name: Quincy Shipyard crude oil barge heating pipes
instances.summary: Brass heating pipes in barges transporting heavy crude oil developed stress-corrosion cracks; the failure was traced to rinse water with pH 2.3 that was not changed regularly.
instances.key_quote: the pH of their rinse water was 2.3 okay and it turns out this stuff that I just put up there you know it's a similar problem okay you don't change your rinse water
instances.framework_lesson: Manufacturing rinse-water specifications assume ideal conditions but do not mandate regular monitoring or maintenance; accumulated contaminants and pH drift can cause unexpected corrosion failure.

cluster_id: Samsung Galaxy Note 7 thermal runaway recalls
display_name: Samsung Galaxy Note 7 thermal runaway recalls

instances.name: Samsung Galaxy Note 7 thermal runaway recalls
instances.summary: Samsung recalled approximately 1.2 million Galaxy Note 7 phones due to lithium-ion battery thermal runaway causing fires and spontaneous ignition; company advised consumers not to charge beyond 60%.
instances.key_quote: Samsung's response you know when they recalled whatever 1.2 million phones or something was to tell that consumers don't charge it past sixty percent
instances.framework_lesson: Pushing energy density in battery design without adequate thermal management and safety margins creates field failures that require consumer behavior restrictions rather than material redesign.

instances.name: Samsung phone battery recall
instances.summary: Samsung recalled approximately 1.2 million phones due to thermal runaway risk in batteries, and responded by instructing consumers to limit charging to 60% to prevent overheating and combustion.
instances.key_quote: Samsung's response you know when they recalled whatever 1.2 million phones or something was to tell that consumers don't charge it past sixty percent so that's cut ties into to keep the temperature you know down
instances.framework_lesson: Energy density optimization in battery design created thermal runaway risk; mitigation required operational constraints rather than material redesign.

cluster_id: Navy topside weight reduction composite structures study
display_name: Navy topside weight reduction composite structures study

instances.name: Navy topside weight reduction composite structures study
instances.summary: During his master's thesis work, Eagar evaluated sandwich and composite structures as alternatives to monolithic aluminum for Navy ship topsides to reduce weight while maintaining structural performance.
instances.key_quote: could we use sandwich type structures or composite structures with different materials rather than just monolithic pieces of aluminum on the top side

instances.name: Navy topside weight reduction – sandwich composite structures
instances.summary: MIT master's thesis project examining sandwich and composite structures with aluminum foams to reduce Navy ship topside weight, which revealed maintenance and field-repair challenges despite good compression performance.
instances.key_quote: great novel boutique type materials but you have to think of how do you maintain it how would you repair it replace things like that
instances.framework_lesson: Material selection must account for field maintainability and repair logistics, not just mechanical properties and weight reduction.

cluster_id: 1988 nor'easter structural collapses (New Jersey/Pennsylvania)
display_name: 1988 nor'easter structural collapses (New Jersey/Pennsylvania)

instances.name: 1988 prefabricated building collapses during heavy snow
instances.summary: Prefabricated steel buildings designed in the mid-1980s with computer-optimized minimum-weight beams and reduced material thickness, intended to meet a 1.67 safety factor, collapsed during a major snowstorm because the design process assumed perfect construction geometry and symmetric loading that did not account for real-world imperfections.
instances.key_quote: they designed everything as if the building was perfectly erected and everything was fine until the big snow storm of 1988
instances.framework_lesson: Computer-aided design that optimizes material removal to nominal safety factors without accounting for construction tolerances, asymmetry, and real-world load distribution can eliminate the safety margin that existed in traditional uniform-section designs.

instances.name: 1988 nor'easter structural collapses (New Jersey/Pennsylvania)
instances.summary: A major snowstorm in 1988 caused widespread building collapses across New Jersey and eastern Pennsylvania because structures were designed to minimum code safety factors without accounting for actual snow loads exceeding historical records.
instances.key_quote: buildings were collapsing all over New Jersey in eastern Pennsylvania because they had bigger snow loads than they were used to having
instances.framework_lesson: Computer-aided optimization reduced safety factors from historical over-design (20-30% margins) to bare minimums specified in codes that were based on outdated historical snow load data rather than true worst-case conditions.

cluster_id: Nuclear submarine piping and ductwork coordination failure
display_name: Nuclear submarine piping and ductwork coordination failure

instances.name: Nuclear submarine piping and ductwork coordination failure
instances.summary: During nuclear submarine construction, electrical cables and ductwork were designed independently, leading to impossible-to-build conflicts discovered at the shipyard that doubled ship construction costs.
instances.key_quote: you get down in the middle of the ship had the shipyard and the guys say this is impossible I can't build it okay and that's why they would modify it on site it would double the cost of a ship
instances.framework_lesson: Sequential design without integrated spatial coordination leads to field modifications; virtual walkthroughs and CAD integration became necessary to catch conflicts before fabrication.

instances.name: Nuclear submarine piping and ductwork coordination failure
instances.summary: On nuclear submarines, electrical cables and ductwork were designed independently without coordination, requiring costly on-site modifications that doubled ship construction costs.
instances.key_quote: on nuclear submarine you'd have some guy designing the electrical cables and he puts him right in the middle of the ductwork and it would double the cost of a ship
instances.framework_lesson: Design standards lacked formal requirements for 3D spatial coordination between interdependent systems, leading to costly field rework and safety issues.

cluster_id: Johnson & Johnson electrocauterization device corrosion failure
display_name: Johnson & Johnson electrocauterization device corrosion failure

instances.name: Johnson & Johnson electrosurgical electrode corrosion problem (1980s)
instances.summary: Electrosurgical probes used for cauterization corroded rapidly when 100 volts were applied to steel and nickel alloy electrodes, threatening surgical effectiveness and safety.
instances.key_quote: I was working with johnson Johnson back in the 80s and they use electro pottery to basically cauterize a woman they just go in there with a couple of electrodes and 100 volts passable current through your skin
instances.framework_lesson: No metal electrode material could withstand anodic corrosion at 100 volts in the physiological environment; the solution required accepting disposable, corroding electrodes rather than designing for permanence.

instances.name: Johnson & Johnson electrocauterization device corrosion failure
instances.summary: Medical electrocauterization tweezers for blood vessel welding experienced severe anode pitting corrosion when using 100V DC, requiring material selection to manage corrosion limits imposed by electrochemical stability.
instances.key_quote: they started out with stainless steel and the pits were the tips were pitting terrible pitting on the anode because that's where the corrosion occurs
instances.framework_lesson: Material selection for electrochemical devices is fundamentally limited by bond strength and electrochemical potential; no material can exceed inherent pitting resistance thresholds regardless of other properties.

cluster_id: Warehouse fire protection system piping corrosion
display_name: Warehouse fire protection system piping corrosion

instances.name: Warehouse fire protection piping occlusion
instances.summary: A large warehouse fire protection system using steel piping suffered complete occlusion of two-inch diameter pipes by rust, requiring replacement of all steel piping in the system.
instances.key_quote: I got a better two inch diameter pipe maybe one a half inch completely occluded by my rust and this has been in a big warehouse I don't know how big and the they told me that all the pipes this is a huge warehouse everything was a polluted they were going to have to replace all the steel piping
instances.framework_lesson: Closed systems with oxygen present and oxygen makeup (from leaks or poor sealing) cause rapid internal corrosion in steel piping; the system design failed to ensure anaerobic conditions.

instances.name: Warehouse fire protection system piping corrosion
instances.summary: A large warehouse's steel fire protection piping corroded severely and required complete replacement due to an unrepaired water leak introducing continuous oxygen makeup to the supposedly closed system.
instances.key_quote: they have a leak you know kind of a stream of water that they had just go running into a drain they'd have it for a couple years okay they didn't fix it
instances.framework_lesson: Closed systems require maintenance discipline; even small leaks introducing makeup water with dissolved oxygen will cause catastrophic corrosion if left unaddressed.

instances.name: Gas-fired power plant water filter corrosion investigation

instances.key_quote: a gas-fired plant i think in georgia and they had 14 mega on water 14 mega ohm water is pretty clean okay it's very clean and they said oh it's someone says the chlorides in the water

cluster_id: Concorde maintenance and operational failures
display_name: Concorde maintenance and operational failures

instances.name: Concorde maintenance and operational failures

instances.name: Concorde maintenance and reliability

instances.framework_lesson: Operational scheduling and maintenance protocols were inadequate for the Concorde's demanding flight cycle, violating principles of preventive maintenance required by flight safety standards.

cluster_id: Australian aluminum bridge doubler plate failure
display_name: Australian aluminum bridge doubler plate failure

instances.name: Australian aluminum bridge doubler plate failure
instances.summary: A bridge in Australia failed approximately 50–60 years ago due to fatigue cracks initiated at doubler plate welds with wrapped connections and high residual stresses.
instances.key_quote: there's one in Australia that was a big surprise about 50 60 years ago to the designers of Steel and a lot of those details that I just showed you have come exactly out of the steel construction manual
instances.framework_lesson: Steel welding details applied directly to aluminum without accounting for aluminum's lack of a fatigue limit and higher sensitivity to residual stress concentrations caused unexpected failures.

instances.name: Australian bridge doubler plate fatigue failure
instances.summary: A bridge in Australia experienced fatigue cracks due to doubler plate design with wrapped welds and residual stresses, surprising designers 50–60 years before this lecture.
instances.key_quote: there's one in Australia that was a big surprise about 50 60 years ago to the designers of Steel
instances.framework_lesson: Fatigue-critical weld details from steel construction manuals apply to aluminum in more complex and dangerous ways; aluminum lacks a fatigue limit below which failure cannot occur.

cluster_id: Tributyl Tin Naval Anti-Fouling Paint Program
display_name: Tributyl Tin Naval Anti-Fouling Paint Program

instances.name: Tributyltin navy paint environmental contamination
instances.summary: The U.S. Navy applied tributyltin-based antifouling paint to ships to prevent barnacle growth, but the chemical leached into harbors and wiped out mollusk populations across U.S. harbors, prompting Congressional intervention to ban the practice.
instances.key_quote: the Navy discovered if you put tribal tin in the paint on ships you don't get any barnacles grow...they don't grow in the harbor parts per trillion of trib tin wiped out every mollusk in every Harbor in the United States
instances.framework_lesson: Military agencies claiming national security exemptions from environmental and safety standards can cause widespread harm; Congressional oversight is necessary to override such claims when civilian interests are endangered.

instances.name: Tributyl Tin Naval Anti-Fouling Paint Program
instances.summary: The U.S. Navy applied tributyl tin-based antifouling paint to ships from the 1970s–1980s, which killed mollusks throughout harbors at parts per billion concentrations, causing ecological damage and harming local fishing industries.
instances.key_quote: tributyl 10 added to the paint and this would kill any mollusk in the entire Harbor okay it was I mean parts per billion are parts per trillion and it would just wipe out all the mollusks
instances.framework_lesson: Naval anti-fouling paint regulations prioritized military operational needs (cost savings and barnacle prevention) over environmental and commercial fishing impacts; Congressional action was required to ban the practice.

cluster_id: Steel mill worker injuries (1930s-1940s bar rolling)
display_name: Steel mill worker injuries (1930s-1940s bar rolling)

instances.name: Steel mill worker injuries from hot steel rolling
instances.summary: Steel mill workers in the 1930s-1940s operating bar rolling equipment at high speeds would suffer traumatic amputation when hot steel bars caused equipment cobblers; the extreme heat cauterized wounds.
instances.key_quote: a piece of hot steel you know a mile long coming through at 40 m an hour and a guy was supposed to grab it with tongs and if anything happened you they called a cobble
instances.framework_lesson: Historical steel mill safety lacked mechanical guards and worker protection; modern mills now isolate operators in armored pulpits with protective glass.

instances.name: Steel mill worker injuries (1930s-1940s bar rolling)
instances.summary: Steel workers in the 1930s-1940s suffered severe injuries including severed limbs when hot steel bars being rolled at high speed encountered process problems called 'cobbles' and curved unexpectedly.
instances.key_quote: if anything happened you they called a cobble And the thing curved when it came out, it might just cut his arm off, but it's it cauterizes the wound at the same time
instances.framework_lesson: Lack of worker protective barriers and safety engineering in early steel mills led to systemic worker dismemberment; modern mills now use protected control rooms and armored glass.

cluster_id: SS Libra LNG vessel tail shaft failure
display_name: SS Libra LNG vessel tail shaft failure

instances.name: SS LNG Libra power loss incident

instances.key_quote: she had lost power fully loaded with LG uh going from Indonesia to somewhere in Japan and she just stopped dead in the water

instances.name: SS Libra LNG vessel tail shaft failure

instances.framework_lesson: Steel mills operating at maximum ingot capacity could not properly remove shrinkage pipe defects from the top third of ingots as required by practice, leading to catastrophic failures in service.

cluster_id: Pacific Gas and Electric pumped-storage hydroelectric project
display_name: Pacific Gas and Electric pumped-storage hydroelectric project

instances.name: Pacific Gas and Electric pumped-storage hydroelectric project
instances.summary: PG&E planned a pumped water storage facility in the High Sierras (early 1990s) to buffer nuclear reactor baseload power against peak evening electricity demand by pumping water between lakes at 8,000 and 6,000 feet elevation.

instances.summary: A 22-foot diameter steel water pipe failed catastrophically at a manway junction in the High Sierras, releasing 4 million horsepower of water, eroding a 100-foot section of canyon wall, and causing approximately $800 million in total losses including lost power production.

cluster_id: PG&E v. American Bridge lawsuit (Fresno, California)
display_name: PG&E v. American Bridge lawsuit (Fresno, California)

instances.name: PG&E Pipe Failure Litigation and Expert Testimony
instances.summary: After the pipe failure, PG&E was required by the California Public Utilities Commission to pursue litigation against American Bridge/U.S. Steel, claiming manufacturing defect; Eagar was hired as third metallurgist expert to analyze the hydrogen-induced crack in the manway weld.
instances.key_quote: the judge has said he can't have more than two we're just going to let you talk about the welding we want you to talk about how did this crack occur

instances.name: PG&E v. American Bridge lawsuit (Fresno, California)
instances.summary: Pacific Gas & Electric sued American Bridge (U.S. Steel division) for $100 million in damages from the pipeline failure; the case went to trial in Fresno where a jury ruled the failure was a foundational problem, not a welding defect, and American Bridge won and received $17 million in punitive damages for slander.
instances.key_quote: they had tried and now they go back to Public Utilities Commission and say well the jury decided it was a foundational problem our supports foundations for the support fight were inadequate they shifted and had nothing to do with hydrogen cracking
instances.framework_lesson: Expert testimony and forensic metallurgy can be influenced by financial incentives; metallurgists paid twice as much ($275/hour vs. $150/hour) argued the wrong technical conclusion, demonstrating how expert credibility becomes a market commodity.

cluster_id: Pressure vessel with internal rubber seal failure
display_name: Pressure vessel with internal rubber seal failure

instances.name: Pressure vessel with internal rubber seal failure
instances.summary: A pressure vessel with a factor of safety of four failed due to a rubber seal at the bottom that was not accounted for in the design, leading to crack propagation around the circumference under hydrostatic pressure.
instances.key_quote: on the inside they had a rubber uh seal in the bottom... they found at the very end that the crack was unzipping around the circumference
instances.framework_lesson: Design specifications that fail to account for all internal components and their effects on pressure distribution and corrosion resistance can undermine intended safety factors.

instances.name: Pressure vessel with rubber seal failure
instances.summary: A pressure vessel with a factor of safety of four failed when a crack grew circumferentially around a rubber seal at the bottom, which bridged the crack and prevented leaking, violating the 'leak before break' design principle.
instances.key_quote: the crack grew all around with the rubber bridging it so it didn't leak, and that's a concept I remember when I was a student the professor said well leak before break
instances.framework_lesson: Design standards for pressure vessels must account for unexpected sealing mechanisms (e.g., internal rubber membranes) that can prevent the intended leak-before-break failure mode and lead to catastrophic unzipping fractures.

cluster_id: Rolls Royce M250 BLISK (bladed disk) for helicopters

cluster_id: Siaki camshaft laser heat-treatment cracking failure
display_name: Siaki camshaft laser heat-treatment cracking failure

instances.name: Siaki camshaft laser heat-treatment cracking failure
instances.summary: A major automotive company (likely Ford) contracted Siaki to develop a laser-based surface hardening system for camshaft lobes, but the system failed catastrophically due to surface-only heating causing severe tensile residual stresses and surface cracking and spalling.
instances.key_quote: they heated the surface of this lobe ended up with very severe tensile residual stresses and started getting surface cracks and spalling the whole process was a disaster 10 million dollar machine was garbage
instances.framework_lesson: Surface-only heating from lasers produces compressive residual stress in the bulk beneath tensile stress at the surface, causing cracking; subsurface heating from electron beams deposits heat beneath the surface, inverting residual stress distribution favorably.

instances.name: Siaki camshaft electron beam heat-treatment success
instances.summary: After the laser camshaft hardening system failed, Siaki switched to an electron beam system which successfully hardened camshaft lobes with no cracking, achieving beneficial compressive residual surface stresses.
instances.key_quote: they switched to Electron Beam and they had no problem because Electron Beam actually deposits the Heat about four thousands of an inch deep and that meant the hottest area was just beneath the surface that's where my tensile residual stresses were I got compressive stresses on the very surface
instances.framework_lesson: Heating depth (subsurface vs. surface-only) determines residual stress distribution; electron beam's subsurface heating at ~0.004 inch depth inverts the stress state compared to laser surface heating, eliminating cracking.

cluster_id: Glass fiber humidity degradation in fiberglass manufacturing
display_name: Glass fiber humidity degradation in fiberglass manufacturing

instances.name: Glass fiber humidity degradation in fiberglass manufacturing
instances.summary: Freshly drawn glass fibers lose tensile strength rapidly when exposed to air humidity, degrading from 600-700 ksi to 400 ksi or lower within days unless epoxy-coated immediately.
instances.key_quote: if i pull a glass fiber brand new glass fiber aluminum borosilicate glass and i'm going to make it into fiberglass with the best strength you can buy this from corning it's called e-glass i had better coat that with the epoxy within 24 hours because the humidity in the air is going to degrade the strength
instances.framework_lesson: Glass fiber specifications must include moisture protection requirements or coating procedures; raw material properties decay rapidly in standard atmospheric conditions.

cluster_id: USS Nautilus heat exchanger design

instances.framework_lesson: Lack of rigorous boundary layer theory and computational tools forced conservative design margins that barely met operational requirements in tropical waters.

cluster_id: US Steel industry continuous casting adoption failure

instances.key_quote: people also see these same types of colors not just on titanium on stainless steels and about 10 or 15 years ago i used to get people coming through on a regular basis we're welding stainless steel piping for high purity semiconductor crystal growth piping

cluster_id: IBM stainless steel high-purity semiconductor piping rejection
display_name: IBM stainless steel high-purity semiconductor piping rejection

instances.name: IBM stainless steel high-purity semiconductor piping rejection
instances.summary: IBM rejected entire stainless steel piping systems for semiconductor crystal growth (used for gallium arsenide) worth $15,000 each due to surface contamination and interference colors visible on the welds.
instances.key_quote: ibm would purchase some gas system of stainless steel piping and they might pay 15 000 for this little gas train which is not very big and they would just cannibalize it and cut it open
instances.framework_lesson: High-purity semiconductor manufacturing imposed stricter contamination standards than the welding specification anticipated, requiring visual perfection beyond what the weld codes required.

cluster_id: Titanium welding porosity at aerospace contractor
display_name: Titanium welding porosity at aerospace contractor

instances.name: Titanium welding porosity at aerospace contractor
instances.summary: An aerospace manufacturer experienced porosity defects in titanium welds due to contamination from commercial-grade acetone and inadequate cleanliness protocols during degreasing.
instances.key_quote: get some reagent grade acetone don't just use regular commercial grade acetone as your degreaser or trichloroethylene whatever they were using
instances.framework_lesson: Material specifications and quality control procedures failed to mandate reagent-grade cleaning agents and white-glove handling protocols, allowing process drift and repeated failures.

cluster_id: General Electric electron beam titanium weld contamination
display_name: General Electric electron beam titanium weld contamination

instances.name: General Electric electron beam titanium weld contamination
instances.summary: General Electric's electron beam welds on titanium engine components that had passed inspection for 10 years suddenly began failing x-ray inspection due to contamination from commercial-grade acetone used in cleaning before vacuum electron beam welding.
instances.key_quote: we've been making these welds for years 10 years on this engine and we always used to pass but now we're flunking we're not passing the x-ray
instances.framework_lesson: Process specifications lacked explicit requirements for cleaning agent purity; reliance on undocumented 'standard practice' enabled gradual process drift toward cheaper commercial-grade materials that violated actual material requirements.

cluster_id: GE titanium ring resistance welding case
display_name: GE titanium ring resistance welding case

instances.name: GE titanium ring resistance welding case
instances.summary: General Electric attempted to weld two $25,000 titanium rings into a $70,000 assembly, but the weld failed due to contamination from acetone stored in a 55-gallon drum containing grease residue and dust in the non-clean welding shop environment.
instances.key_quote: they've got these two titanium rings that are both probably worth 25 000 a piece they're going to put them together and make a 70 000 part out of two twenty five thousand dollar parts if they can get a good weld
instances.framework_lesson: Industry ignores expert consulting advice until forced to change procedures; switching to reagent-grade acetone and proper surface cleaning eliminated defects entirely, saving $250,000 annually.

cluster_id: Second swashplate failure blamed on wrong cause
display_name: Second swashplate failure blamed on wrong cause

instances.name: Second swashplate failure blamed on wrong cause
instances.summary: A subsequent helicopter failure killed occupants and was initially attributed to the same swashplate bearing defect as the Japanese naval incident, but investigation later revealed a different root cause.
instances.key_quote: someone else blamed the next failure that occurred which killed a couple of people on that same cause even though it wasn't the same cause
instances.framework_lesson: Post-accident forensic analysis and measurement of failed components—even when nearly destroyed—can distinguish between different failure modes that produce similar symptoms, preventing misdiagnosis that leads to incorrect corrective actions.
instances.source_module: Codes and Standards Definitions and History

cluster_id: Manufacturing bearing closed-conformity defect discovery
display_name: Manufacturing bearing closed-conformity defect discovery

instances.name: Manufacturing bearing closed-conformity defect discovery
instances.summary: During inspection of six prototype slim-line bearings manufactured at high cost, one bearing exhibited vibration caused by a closed-conformity condition (ball radius larger than groove radius), which created sharp-contact stresses; military documentation recorded this defect but it was dismissed as a one-off anomaly rather than investigated for root cause.
instances.key_quote: they had manufactured six of them and one of them started creating some vibration and they took it off and looked at it and had what's called a closed conformity
instances.framework_lesson: A single-point-of-failure anomaly in a small sample was not escalated for investigation into manufacturing process control or tolerance stack-up in the housing, allowing the defect mode to persist in production.
instances.source_module: Codes and Standards Definitions and History

cluster_id: USS aircraft carrier steam system weld failure during Grenada invasion
display_name: USS aircraft carrier steam system weld failure during Grenada invasion

instances.name: USS aircraft carrier steam system weld failure during Grenada invasion
instances.summary: Bad welds in an aircraft carrier's steam system forced the vessel into emergency drydock repairs in Jacksonville during the invasion of Grenada, leaving the North Atlantic defenseless against potential Soviet attack.
instances.key_quote: aircraft carrier got pulled back into port because of some bad welds they just made on the steam system and this was right as they were starting the invasion of granada
instances.source_module: Codes and Standards Definitions and History

cluster_id: Caydon bearing defect and helicopter crash
display_name: Caydon bearing defect and helicopter crash

instances.name: Caydon bearing defect and helicopter crash
instances.summary: A defective bearing manufactured by Caydon failed in a Navy helicopter, killing two pilots and destroying a $30-40 million aircraft, triggering FBI criminal investigation, government takeover of the manufacturing facility, and subsequent civil litigation.
instances.key_quote: the government took over the caydon plant muskegon michigan okay and started making new bearings absolutely perfect to spec
instances.framework_lesson: Specifications and inspection protocols (even when multiplied 50-fold with government oversight) cannot guarantee in-service reliability if root causes in design conformity are not understood; legal pressure rather than engineering rigor drove settlement decisions.
instances.source_module: Codes and Standards Definitions and History

cluster_id: Replacement Caydon bearing failure
display_name: Replacement Caydon bearing failure

instances.name: Replacement Caydon bearing failure
instances.summary: A newly manufactured bearing, made under government emergency powers supervision and certified by both Caydon and US government inspectors as meeting all specifications, failed in the field within two months of deployment.
instances.key_quote: these bearings were better bearings none of these manufacturing defects and they put them into the helicopters and those new helicopters that have these new bearings and they had to manufacture a thousand of these okay so that's four million dollars or 40 million or something anyway whatever it is it was expensive but it's also done in a rush all these things uh go out there and guess what within two months one of them fails one of the good bearings fails but it was perfectly within spec
instances.framework_lesson: Meeting specification does not guarantee fitness for service; conformity type (open vs. closed contact geometry) is a fundamental design parameter that specifications alone cannot validate without specialized metallurgical forensics.
instances.source_module: Codes and Standards Definitions and History

cluster_id: Swashplate potato-chipping defect
display_name: Swashplate potato-chipping defect

instances.name: Swashplate potato-chipping defect
instances.summary: Investigation revealed that a helicopter swashplate had warped into a potato-chip shape, creating elliptical and non-uniform contact pressure that caused uneven wear patterns on the bearing races and ultimately bearing failure.
instances.key_quote: if you put a perfectly spherical bearing into a swashplate that has a potato chip shape and you now start thinking about what you have you now that potato chip ends up creating a minor axis in a major axis right it's effectively a little ellipse and it's going to get deep impressions on the minor axis and smaller impressions are the major axis
instances.source_module: Codes and Standards Definitions and History

instances.framework_lesson: Deformed tools showing signs of imminent failure were not removed from service until catastrophic injury occurred, indicating inadequate tool inspection and maintenance protocols.

cluster_id: Aluminum washer stress-corrosion cracking failure
display_name: Aluminum washer stress-corrosion cracking failure

instances.name: Aluminum washer stress-corrosion cracking failure
instances.summary: Aluminum T651 washers that were stress-relieved, then re-heat-treated without re-stress-relief, developed 8 ksi residual stresses exceeding the 5 ksi stress-corrosion cracking threshold and cracked in service.
instances.key_quote: they bought it stress relieved yeah okay but then you reintroduced residual stresses and so when you actually shipped it had 8 KSI residual stresses in it and stress corrosion cracking for that alloy is 5 KSI so they got cracking
instances.framework_lesson: Heat-treatment specifications must account for residual-stress reintroduction during secondary processing; stress-relief condition designation (T651) is negated by subsequent thermal cycles.

cluster_id: Welded steel crane component failure
display_name: Welded steel crane component failure

instances.name: Welded steel crane component failure
instances.summary: A 70-ton water bag fell from a crane whose welded steel component fractured in brittle mode due to unrelieved welding residual stresses equal to yield strength, injuring a worker beneath it.
instances.key_quote: I have a crane component that fractured and a 70 ton water bag fell on top of a man he hurt his back pretty badly um because they didn't stress relieve the welds which were about 2 in
instances.framework_lesson: Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code requires post-weld heat treatment for steel thicker than 1.5 inches specifically because unrelieved residual stresses cause brittle fracture; this component violated that standard.

cluster_id: US Navy submarine first-dive stress relief
display_name: US Navy submarine first-dive stress relief

instances.name: US Navy submarine first-dive stress relief
instances.summary: Nuclear submarines cannot be furnace-stress-relieved after assembly; instead, the first deep dive provides hydrostatic compression that mechanically stress-relieves all welds, with top shipyard management aboard to ensure quality.
instances.key_quote: you can't do that once you have all the equipment and the O-ring the Rubber and the plastic and everything else inside the submarine so how do they stress relieve it the first Deep dive you got hydrostatic compression and all those welds get stress relieved mechanically
instances.framework_lesson: When conventional stress-relief processes cannot be applied post-fabrication, the design and operational procedure must incorporate stress-relief mechanisms—here, hydrostatic pressure and crew accountability as quality assurance.

cluster_id: Trolley wire manufacturing by Conform process
display_name: Trolley wire manufacturing by Conform process

instances.name: Trolley wire manufacturing by Conform process
instances.summary: Phelps Dodge manufactured trolley wire using the Conform process, a hydraulic machine invented in Great Britain that kneads powder into solid wire through extreme plastic deformation (1000% equivalent strain).

instances.framework_lesson: Material properties (yield strength) limit the level of deformation achievable in a single processing step; softer materials tolerate higher strains than harder ones.

cluster_id: Printed circuit board solder metallurgical challenge
display_name: Printed circuit board solder metallurgical challenge

instances.name: Printed circuit board solder metallurgical challenge
instances.summary: Motorola, Intel, and other major manufacturers attempted to deposit liquid metal solder directly onto printed circuit boards but abandoned the effort because surface tension forces overwhelm gravity at millimeter scales, causing the metal to ball up rather than spread.
instances.key_quote: Motorola's tried it the intel strap you name the companies everybody's tried it 25 years ago and everyone gave up because properties of the metal in terms of surface tension are way different
instances.framework_lesson: Material properties (surface tension vs. gravity, Bond number scaling) were not adequately considered in initial process design; metallurgical physics constrains manufacturing feasibility independent of economic incentive.

instances.framework_lesson: High-intensity heat sources (lasers) create thermal conductivity limitations that constrain productivity; the inability to scale power input without surface evaporation prevents faster manufacturing rates.

cluster_id: General Electric CF6-6 engine fan rotor failure

instances.name: General Electric CF6-6 engine fan rotor failure

instances.key_quote: when the fan rotor fractured it resulted in the loss of all three hydraulic systems that controlled the flight controls so when that happened the hydraulic lines were severed or fractured
instances.framework_lesson: Macro-etch inspection was performed upstream of critical machining operations rather than after them, causing a hard alpha metallurgical defect to be missed; the FAA subsequently changed Quality Assurance and non-destructive evaluation processes in aircraft engine manufacturing.

cluster_id: Hard alpha inclusion in forged disc — non-destructive testing case study

instances.key_quote: the hard alpha metallurgical defect was formed in the material during manufacturer from the ingot from which the disc was forged all right the defect was missed not only by ultrasonic inspection it was missed by a macro inspection
instances.framework_lesson: Timing and sequencing of non-destructive inspection relative to machining operations is critical; performing macro-etch before final machining rather than after allowed defects to remain undetected, leading to regulatory changes in NDT protocols.

cluster_id: Wave solder bath contamination defects

instances.name: Wave solder bath contamination defects

instances.key_quote: if you pick up very much contamination you find that 50 parts per million of aluminum magnesium or zinc now zinc could be in a lot of things okay we use it for Corrosion Protection but it would easily get into that solder bath it'll form inter metallics and those inter metallics start to act like sand in the solder bath
instances.framework_lesson: Solder bath composition specifications must tightly control contaminant levels because even trace amounts (7–50 ppm) of certain elements form intermetallics that destroy wetting and create defects; routine recycling and reprocessing is required to maintain quality.

display_name: Solyndra solar cell company failure
