instances.summary: A U.S. solar cell manufacturer in Southern California that received $300-400 million in Department of Energy loan guarantees defaulted on federal loans and failed to commercialize solar technology.
instances.key_quote: the federal government lost default you know they defaulted on the federal guaranteed loans for four or five hundred million dollars
instances.framework_lesson: Government subsidies and loan guarantees cannot overcome fundamental economic barriers in manufacturing when the underlying process economics are not viable.
materials_or_systems: crystal growth and diamond saw cutting

cluster_id: Canadian solar cell manufacturing facility failure
display_name: Canadian solar cell manufacturing facility failure

instances.name: Canadian solar cell manufacturing facility failure
instances.summary: A large solar cell manufacturing facility in Canada that received $250 million in government investment went bankrupt due to uneconomical manufacturing processes.
instances.key_quote: i went up to a place in canada about 10 years ago that was a solar cell manufacturing thing the canadian government had plowed a quarter billion dollars into this place huge manufacturing facility for solar cells and they tanked
instances.framework_lesson: Large capital investments in manufacturing facilities for materials with unfavorable process economics will fail regardless of government funding unless the underlying process fundamentals are addressed.
materials_or_systems: crystal ingot growth and diamond saw cutting

cluster_id: New Hampshire sapphire and silicon crystal growth company
display_name: New Hampshire sapphire and silicon crystal growth company

instances.name: New Hampshire sapphire and silicon crystal growth company
instances.summary: A New Hampshire company built expensive facilities to grow large-grain sapphire and silicon crystals, but planned to sell the technology to China where low-cost labor and government subsidies would undercut competitors.
instances.key_quote: they're going to sell the technology to make these large grain sapphire large grain silicon to the chinese are then going to put their low-cost labor into it with their government subsidies and everything else and they're going to wipe out all the other people
instances.framework_lesson: Advanced materials ventures fail when they ignore geopolitical and cost-structure realities; government subsidies in competing nations can eliminate domestic competitive advantage.

cluster_id: Gary Wennick conductive polymers research talk
display_name: Gary Wennick conductive polymers research talk

instances.name: Gary Wennick conductive polymers research talk
instances.summary: Professor Gary Wennick promoted polyacetylene as a replacement for copper in electric motors and generators based on superior specific electrical conductivity, but the selection criterion was irrelevant to actual motor and generator design practice.
instances.key_quote: he said these conductive polymers have a specific electrical conductivity that means the electrical conductivity which is the inverse of resistance divided by the density that is better than copper
instances.framework_lesson: Material selection based on a single improved property metric can mislead if that metric is not the actual design-controlling parameter; engineers must verify the relevance of the property to the application.

cluster_id: Aluminum motors vs. copper generators
display_name: Aluminum motors vs. copper generators

instances.name: Aluminum motors vs. copper generators
instances.summary: In the 1980s, Eagar questioned why large generators continued to use copper despite aluminum having twice the specific electrical conductivity, revealing that marketed material properties do not always align with actual design requirements.
instances.key_quote: if specific electrical conductivity is the parameter of interest in the design of this material why am i still using copper in these million dollar machines
instances.framework_lesson: Marketing of specific material properties can obscure the true performance metrics needed for actual design; engineers must validate whether advertised advantages address actual design constraints.

cluster_id: Conductive polymers hype cycle
display_name: Conductive polymers hype cycle

instances.name: Conductive polymers hype cycle
instances.summary: Electrically conductive polymers were heavily promoted in the 1980s-1990s as replacements for copper in power applications, but failed to displace copper in large generators and motors despite superior specific conductivity.
instances.key_quote: i was still reading that all through the 1990s how this electrically conductive polymers was going to take over the copper industry in the world don't think so folks
instances.framework_lesson: Superior specific properties on paper do not guarantee market adoption when other design constraints (thermal management, processing, cost at scale) are not addressed.

cluster_id: Structural ceramics for automotive and turbine engines
display_name: Structural ceramics for automotive and turbine engines

instances.name: Structural ceramics for automotive and turbine engines
instances.summary: In the early 1980s, researchers spent billions on fine ceramic research for automotive and aircraft turbine engines, but ceramics were never adopted for these applications due to brittleness and lack of fracture toughness.
instances.key_quote: we're not going to have structural ceramics because they don't have fracture toughness look at that piece of silicon the little flat piece that's got the chips on it
instances.framework_lesson: High-temperature strength alone is insufficient for structural applications; fracture toughness and damage tolerance are non-negotiable design requirements.

cluster_id: High-temperature superconductivity for high-field magnets and levitated trains
display_name: High-temperature superconductivity for high-field magnets and levitated trains

instances.name: High-temperature superconductivity for high-field magnets and levitated trains
instances.summary: A faculty member in Iowa's department claimed in the mid-1990s that high-field magnets and levitated trains would be deployed within five years using newly discovered high-temperature superconductors; this prediction failed to materialize.
instances.key_quote: this faculty member because he had research in high temperature superconductors he told me we would be riding on levitated trains within five years well it's now about 14 years about 17 years later now and i still haven't been able to get on one yet
instances.framework_lesson: Fundamental material discoveries do not automatically translate into practical engineering systems without solving engineering problems beyond material properties (cost, reliability, system integration).

cluster_id: Continuous casting and steel industry capacity collapse
display_name: Continuous casting and steel industry capacity collapse

instances.name: Continuous casting and steel industry capacity collapse
instances.summary: The shift to continuous casting in the 1970s increased yield from 65% to 97%, doubling steel productivity from 1980–1990 and creating excess global capacity that crashed steel prices and bankrupted mills.
instances.key_quote: the change to continuous casting which went from in the 1970s went from 65 yield to 97 yield all of a sudden the world had 30 percent more steel making capacity than they needed and the price of steel dropped
instances.framework_lesson: Radical improvements in process efficiency can destabilize entire industries if capacity is not managed; mills that sold equipment to competitors at scrap prices after bankruptcy later faced competition from that same used equipment.

cluster_id: Nylon oil pan design and manufacturing trade-offs
display_name: Nylon oil pan design and manufacturing trade-offs

instances.name: Nylon oil pan design and manufacturing trade-offs
instances.summary: Engineers redesigned steel/aluminum engine oil pans using nylon polyamide 6 reinforced with glass fiber, achieving 60% weight reduction but requiring much closer screw spacing due to lower stiffness, increasing fastener and assembly costs.
instances.key_quote: the nylon is going to go for four dollars a pound six dollars a pound the steel or aluminum is going to go for the aluminum go for two dollars a pound the steel will go for a buck a pound
instances.framework_lesson: Lower material modulus in composites requires higher fastener density to maintain seal integrity, offsetting weight savings through higher assembly labor and part costs.

cluster_id: Langley helicopter supersonic blade tip experiment
display_name: Langley helicopter supersonic blade tip experiment

instances.name: Langley helicopter supersonic blade tip experiment
instances.summary: NASA researchers at Langley Virginia attached rocket motors to a helicopter to push it to 240 mph forward speed, causing the advancing blade tips to exceed sonic velocity and creating instabilities.
instances.key_quote: they actually flew it with the rocket motors pushing it forward to like 240 miles an hour and so the blade tips had gone supersonic on the advancing blade tip
instances.framework_lesson: The instabilities created by supersonic blade-tip speeds cannot be engineered around; they represent a fundamental physical limit that drove the need for alternative designs like the tilt-rotor.

cluster_id: ATM touchscreen wear failure
display_name: ATM touchscreen wear failure

instances.name: ATM touchscreen wear failure
instances.summary: Indium tin oxide coatings on automatic teller machine touchscreens wore out where users repeatedly pressed buttons with their fingernails in the same corner location.
instances.key_quote: you ever go into an automatic teller and down where they have the button in the same corner and people hit it with their fingernail and it wears out

cluster_id: Damascus sword quenching practice
display_name: Damascus sword quenching practice

instances.name: Damascus sword quenching practice
instances.summary: Medieval Damascus swordsmiths quenched swords in living slaves rather than water, believing this practice gave their blades superior killing power against enemies.
instances.key_quote: the best swords were not just quenched in water they were quenched in a slave and not a dead slave but a live slave and that gave them power against the enemies

cluster_id: Titanium superplastic forming adoption
display_name: Titanium superplastic forming adoption

instances.name: Titanium superplastic forming adoption
instances.summary: Aerospace industry rapidly adopted titanium superplastic forming technology in less than 15 years from its rediscovery in the early 1960s, making aircraft structural parts with complex rib-stiffened construction by the mid-1970s.
instances.key_quote: people were forming out of titanium super plastic parts by the mid 70s that's one of the quickest adoptions of Technology less than 15 years of any technology I can think of

cluster_id: Titanium aircraft part isothermal forging case study
display_name: Titanium aircraft part isothermal forging case study

instances.name: Titanium aircraft part isothermal forging case study
instances.summary: An aircraft part with complex rib stiffening was redesigned from conventional forging to isothermal super plastic forming, reducing weight from 154 kg to the final 28 kg part with 45 kg net material savings.
instances.key_quote: conventional forging would have been something in the white outline here and the super plastically formed part would be something that would be in the gray part and the actual machine part weight is 28 kg the formed weight for conventional was 154

cluster_id: Americas Cup racing yacht hardware failure and redesign
display_name: Americas Cup racing yacht hardware failure and redesign

instances.name: Americas Cup racing yacht hardware failure and redesign
instances.summary: A consulting case where increasing wall thickness of a thin-walled aluminum component from 3mm to 4mm doubled fracture toughness and corrosion stress-corrosion cracking threshold, converting a catastrophic failure mode to a controlled leak.
instances.key_quote: the solution to his problem was it was only that was like only an eighth of an inch thick to go from three millimeters thick to four millimeters thick to increase the wall a little bit because we're going to get less strength but we could double our fracture toughness
instances.framework_lesson: Design philosophy in high-performance racing requires materials and structures on the edge of failure; acceptance of controlled failure modes (leak before break) is preferable to brittle fracture.

cluster_id: Beryllium toxicity discovery at MIT
display_name: Beryllium toxicity discovery at MIT

instances.name: Beryllium toxicity discovery at MIT
instances.summary: Beryllium berylliosis was discovered in the MIT area (corner of Mass Ave near railroad tracks) when workers exposed to beryllium powder developed fatal pulmonary nodules; 10% of population has genetic predisposition.
instances.key_quote: this was discovered right up here on the corner of mass ave okay not where we have building 35 but just on the corner of other side of the railroad tracks i believe that's where it is
instances.framework_lesson: Beryllium's exceptional strength-to-weight ratio makes it ideal for aerospace applications, but its severe occupational health hazard (berylliosis in genetically susceptible workers) severely restricts its use and drives up costs.

cluster_id: Magnesium car fire from insurance fraud
display_name: Magnesium car fire from insurance fraud

instances.name: Magnesium car fire from insurance fraud
instances.summary: Junked cars with magnesium wheels burned uncontrollably in poor neighborhoods when torched for insurance fraud, reaching approximately 4000 degrees due to magnesium's extreme reactivity.
instances.key_quote: you go to one of the poor areas of town where they torch cars on this part on the sidewalk to get the insurance money okay your car's a junker but it's got mag wheels okay uh so you torch it and the magnesium you know throw some gasoline on a car and on some magnesium and the fire will get hot enough magnesium will ignite and you get a really hot fire

cluster_id: Magnesium weld stress-corrosion cracking from humidity
display_name: Magnesium weld stress-corrosion cracking from humidity

instances.name: Magnesium weld stress-corrosion cracking from humidity
instances.summary: Fusion-welded magnesium alloys experienced stress-corrosion cracking from atmospheric humidity within two days if residual stresses were not relieved by stress-relief heat treatment.
instances.key_quote: if you haven't stress relieved it just the humidity in the air will cause stress corrosion cracking okay so that's why most things are castings because castings don't have residual stresses
instances.framework_lesson: Welded magnesium requires mandatory post-weld stress relief due to vulnerability of residual stresses to atmospheric moisture-induced cracking.

cluster_id: Dow Chemical magnesium price cartel (1946–1975)
display_name: Dow Chemical magnesium price cartel (1946–1975)

instances.name: Dow Chemical magnesium price cartel (1946–1975)
instances.summary: A doctoral thesis by Joel Clark's student in the mid-1970s found statistical evidence that magnesium and aluminum prices tracked perfectly from 1946 to 1975, suggesting an illegal price-fixing cartel dominated by Dow Chemical's near-monopoly on magnesium production.
instances.key_quote: you can track the price from 1946 all the way up to 1975 and the magnesium and aluminum prices tracked perfectly with one another and you can do statistics and correlation coefficients there is no economic reason for that to be true other than an illegal cartel

cluster_id: Jet engine turbine blade hot corrosion
display_name: Jet engine turbine blade hot corrosion

instances.name: Jet engine turbine blade hot corrosion
instances.summary: Aircraft turbine blades with nickel-chrome-aluminum coating experienced accelerated corrosion during engine startup when engines were not preheated on the runway, causing rapid sulfidation failure.
instances.year_or_era: six or seven or eight years ago
instances.key_quote: when you take off you stay in this region for five or ten minutes okay as you can see you're now doing your pre-heating up in the air
instances.framework_lesson: Operating procedures must account for thermodynamic corrosion windows; skipping ground preheating to save fuel exposed engines to peak corrosion rates during climb.

cluster_id: Destroyer steam line stress corrosion cracking (Gulf War)
display_name: Destroyer steam line stress corrosion cracking (Gulf War)

instances.name: Destroyer steam line stress corrosion cracking (Gulf War)
instances.summary: A naval destroyer experienced failure of a 3000 psi steam line during the Gulf War due to stress corrosion cracking caused by use of the wrong alloy material.
instances.key_quote: that destroyer going in the gulf war and they lost their 3000 psi steam line because of stress grows and cracking because it was the wrong alloy
instances.framework_lesson: Alloy selection for high-pressure steam systems must account for stress corrosion cracking susceptibility; wrong material specification resulted in critical system failure.

cluster_id: Alaska Pipeline brittle fracture risk assessment
display_name: Alaska Pipeline brittle fracture risk assessment

instances.name: Alaska Pipeline brittle fracture risk assessment
instances.summary: Late-1970s risk assessment of the trans-Alaska pipeline for potential running brittle fractures in pressurized oil pipeline, with discussion of catastrophic economic consequences and fracture arrest mechanisms.
instances.key_quote: if you got two million barrels a day coming down from Prudhoe Bay to Anchorage back then it might have been thirty dollars a barrel that's 60 million dollars a day
instances.framework_lesson: Economic incentives for pipeline operators to prevent fractures far exceed regulatory or environmental pressures; brittle fracture in high-flow systems creates uncontrollable running cracks spanning miles due to sustained pressurization ahead of decompression wave.

cluster_id: Japanese low-sulfur A36 steel market disruption
display_name: Japanese low-sulfur A36 steel market disruption

instances.name: Japanese low-sulfur A36 steel market disruption
instances.summary: Mid-1970s competitive disruption: Japanese steelmakers sold low-sulfur A36 plate on U.S. West Coast at standard price, undercutting U.S. mills that charged 10% premium for low-sulfur processing.
instances.key_quote: when the Japanese started selling Garden variety A36 plate on the west coast that was low sulfur for no increase in price okay in the mid 70s because the United States we're getting a 10 premium
instances.framework_lesson: Newly constructed Japanese integrated steel mills incorporated low-sulfur processing into standard manufacturing, making premium pricing for low-sulfur steel untenable and exposing U.S. industry's higher production costs.

cluster_id: Pipeline brittle fracture and crack propagation
display_name: Pipeline brittle fracture and crack propagation

instances.name: Pipeline brittle fracture and crack propagation
instances.summary: High-pressure gas pipelines experienced running brittle fractures that propagated for 30 miles or more, requiring industry solutions like crack arrestors and toughness improvements.
instances.key_quote: they have had in smaller pipelines like six inch pipeline they've had cracks run for 30 miles okay now you don't even store enough pipe for a 30 mile fracture
instances.framework_lesson: Pipeline safety standards underwent decades of creeping increases in required toughness (from 10 to 80 foot-pounds) as engineers learned that base material toughness alone was insufficient to prevent running fractures.

cluster_id: Iran hostage rescue helicopter limitations
display_name: Iran hostage rescue helicopter limitations

instances.name: Iran hostage rescue helicopter limitations
instances.summary: Failed or limited helicopter rescue operation during the 1979 Iranian embassy takeover revealed that conventional helicopters are too loud and slow for covert insertion.
instances.key_quote: the Iranians took over the American Embassy in 1979 or whatever and they tried to rescue some of them and the helicopters weren't successful helicopters tend to announce their arrival they're fairly noisy
instances.framework_lesson: Conventional helicopter acoustic signature and speed limitations drove military demand for tilt-rotor designs (XV-15, V-22 Osprey) with lower tip speeds and greater forward speed.
materials_or_systems: rotor tip speed and noise generation

cluster_id: Boeing Airstream specification over-specification
display_name: Boeing Airstream specification over-specification

instances.name: Boeing Airstream specification over-specification
instances.summary: Boeing specified a safety requirement for an Airstream trailer that was overly conservative, requiring components to prevent even minor air leaks that posed no realistic harm.
instances.key_quote: everyone was afraid to go and tell Boeing that their spec was stupid it was stupid but no one was going to tell them that
instances.framework_lesson: Engineers and suppliers will not challenge overly conservative specifications from dominant customers even when they know the specification is unnecessary and wasteful.

cluster_id: Plumber MAPP gas torch facial injury incident
display_name: Plumber MAPP gas torch facial injury incident

instances.name: Plumber MAPP gas torch facial injury incident
instances.summary: A plumber working on a water pipe while lying on his stomach, smoking a cigar and talking on a cell phone, used a lit MAPP gas torch as a hammer, causing an eight-foot flame blast to his face; a woman and her granddaughter witnessed the incident from a block away.
instances.key_quote: she sees this eight foot blast of flame shooting right up in his face as he's looking in there with his lit cigar and his cell phone that he's talking on

cluster_id: MAPP gas cylinder drop and bending incidents
display_name: MAPP gas cylinder drop and bending incidents

instances.name: MAPP gas cylinder drop and bending incidents
instances.summary: Plumbers have repeatedly dropped or thrown lit MAPP gas cylinders during work, causing deformation; cylinders bent at 25-30 degrees can crack despite robust wall thickness.
instances.key_quote: a guy will be doing his plumbing and he'll reach over and he misses he has it lit on the table and he reaches over and he doesn't grab low enough he grabs here and they pick you know or whatever and they throw it to the ground
instances.framework_lesson: Portable pressure vessels designed with high safety factors can still fail when subjected to mechanical abuse during typical field use.

cluster_id: Fire sprinkler head misuse as coat hook
display_name: Fire sprinkler head misuse as coat hook

instances.name: Fire sprinkler head misuse as coat hook
instances.summary: Hotel guests and maintenance personnel hang clothing and objects from fire sprinkler head adapters, damaging the precision mechanism and potentially preventing the sprinkler from activating during a fire.
instances.key_quote: it turns out people love to you know take the coat hanger and hang it up from they figure this is a handy hook to coat hang things from it turns out this is actually a Precision piece of equipment
instances.framework_lesson: Codes and standards for safety equipment must account for foreseeable misuse and user behavior, not just nominal operation.

cluster_id: Improved lightning-resistant flexible gas tubing development
display_name: Improved lightning-resistant flexible gas tubing development

instances.name: Improved lightning-resistant flexible gas tubing development
instances.summary: Manufacturers developed improved corrugated stainless steel tubing with carbon-filled black plastic insulation and later aluminum-sheet construction to withstand lightning strikes; this took approximately 10 years and resulted in a billion feet of inadequate product already in service.
instances.key_quote: it took them about 10 years to come up with something that won't burn down your house okay in the meantime there's a billion feet of this out here out there and they're got two or three hundred fires a year
instances.framework_lesson: When a design flaw is discovered in a widely deployed product, the lengthy process of developing and deploying a safe replacement means years of continued hazard exposure and failures.

cluster_id: Surge arrestor product defect and fire hazard
display_name: Surge arrestor product defect and fire hazard

instances.name: Surge arrestor product defect and fire hazard
instances.summary: A surge arrestor product with inadequate lightning strike tolerance caused 200-300 fires per year over approximately 10 years, and inferior versions were sold at 8% less cost despite being 50 times less safe.
instances.key_quote: they're got two or three hundred fires a year from this so bulking up for the safety standards in this case because they didn't have a good enough safety standard
instances.framework_lesson: Inadequate initial safety standards for lightning strike tolerance allowed unsafe products to be manufactured and distributed widely before safer iterations were developed.

cluster_id: Bullseye glass fire hazard
display_name: Bullseye glass fire hazard

instances.name: Bullseye glass fire hazard
instances.summary: Bullseye glass used in colonial New England homes could focus sunlight and burn down buildings if the geometry happened to be favorable.
instances.key_quote: the sun's rays come in there and just burn your home down if you do it fairly uniformly

cluster_id: Sango Bath (Saint-Gobain) founding and long-term strategy
display_name: Sango Bath (Saint-Gobain) founding and long-term strategy

instances.name: Sango Bath (Saint-Gobain) founding and long-term strategy
instances.summary: Saint-Gobain was founded in 1688 making polished glass mirrors for the French aristocracy and developed into a 400-year-old company by making long-term business decisions rather than quarterly stock-price decisions.
instances.key_quote: they make a business decision according to our cash on what will make them and keep them in business a hundred years from now

cluster_id: Pittsburgh Plate Glass mile-long factory
display_name: Pittsburgh Plate Glass mile-long factory

instances.name: Pittsburgh Plate Glass mile-long factory
instances.summary: Pittsburgh Plate Glass built a mile-long manufacturing facility in the 1800s where molten glass was poured onto metal tables, rolled, and polished continuously because the material was too brittle to handle after casting.
instances.key_quote: the factory was a mile long just because you want to have the process flow go that way

cluster_id: Ford Motor Company and Libby Owens Ford float glass development
display_name: Ford Motor Company and Libby Owens Ford float glass development

instances.name: Ford Motor Company and Libby Owens Ford float glass development
instances.summary: Ford Motor Company partnered with Libby Owens Ford in the 1920s to develop a continuous plate glass process to supply glass for mass-produced automobiles, improving on earlier batch polishing methods.
instances.key_quote: they'd pull it out in a continuous stream onto a metal table with rollers and they've made roll plate glass just like they did before but now it was a continuous process

cluster_id: Light bulb manufacturing process comparison test
display_name: Light bulb manufacturing process comparison test

instances.name: Light bulb manufacturing process comparison test
instances.summary: Expert consulting case where Eagar tested properly annealed Corning-manufactured light bulbs versus hand-blown Korean specialty bulbs to demonstrate the effect of improper tempering on water-drop impact resistance.
instances.key_quote: you go to one of these hand blown glasses and apparently I never i've still to this day i never got to see the video which you take a little dropper you put one drop of water on it Wow blows up because of the residual stresses
instances.framework_lesson: Proper manufacturing controls and annealing procedures are essential structural specifications; overseas hand-manufacturing without these controls created product liability failures.

cluster_id: MRAP vehicle armor selection and development
display_name: MRAP vehicle armor selection and development

instances.name: MRAP vehicle armor selection and development
instances.summary: The US Army tested six different materials (titanium, steel, aluminum, and others) for MRAP armored personnel carrier sides and found that six layers of 2-inch-thick glass outperformed all alternatives against RPG threats.
instances.key_quote: they did a study and they use titanium they steal they used aluminum they tried six different materials and the only one that worked for the one that worked the best was six layers of 2 inch thick glass
instances.framework_lesson: Counter-intuitive material selection emerged from systematic comparative testing; glass proved superior to traditional armor materials for specific threat signatures.

cluster_id: Professor Omen's chemically tempered glass demonstration
display_name: Professor Omen's chemically tempered glass demonstration

instances.name: Professor Omen's chemically tempered glass demonstration
instances.summary: A glass expert demonstrated the strength of chemically tempered glass by taking an 18-inch-long, 3-4 inch-wide piece that was bowed 2 inches high and flattening it by hand without fracture.
instances.key_quote: professor omen who taught me and who was a glass expert had a piece of glass that was about 18 inches wide or 18 inches long three or four inches wide and actually was bowed about two inches high and he could take that with his bare hand and he could just flatten it

cluster_id: Medical implant polymer certification freeze
display_name: Medical implant polymer certification freeze

instances.name: Medical implant polymer certification freeze
instances.summary: The FDA grandfathered in polymers used in medical implants in the early 1970s, and no new plastics have been certified for human implant use since then because the regulatory qualification process (requiring hamster, dog, and monkey studies) is too expensive and time-consuming for manufacturers to pursue.
instances.key_quote: there has not been another plastic certified for use in the human body since the early 70s because the law basically set down the specifications of what you had to do to certify a new material
instances.framework_lesson: Specifications intended to ensure safety can stifle innovation by imposing prohibitive certification costs that lock in older, inferior materials indefinitely.

cluster_id: 60 Wall Street (JP Morgan building) air conditioning riser pipe failure
display_name: 60 Wall Street (JP Morgan building) air conditioning riser pipe failure

instances.name: 60 Wall Street (JP Morgan building) air conditioning riser pipe failure
instances.summary: A 40-story New York City office building's welded riser pipes for air conditioning were found to have poor weld penetration after 10 years of operation, discovered during inspection and leading to a $27 million dispute over repair responsibility.
instances.key_quote: they found the welds were lousy okay well of course they're lousy if you're on a rush job and your guys up there hanging off an I-beam with no protection around them
instances.framework_lesson: Visual inspection alone is insufficient for structural welds under pressure; rapid erection schedules and lack of on-site inspector presence incentivize poor workmanship that passes visual tests but fails internal penetration requirements.

cluster_id: Oil refinery pipeline inspection and lawsuit
display_name: Oil refinery pipeline inspection and lawsuit

instances.name: Oil refinery pipeline inspection and lawsuit
instances.summary: An oil company discovered one leaking pipe out of 600 installed lines in a refinery expansion and decided to remove and replace all 600 pipes, turning a $100,000 inspection problem into a $60 million lawsuit.
instances.key_quote: they found one leaking pipe out of 600 and they decided that they were going to rip out everything they had already put into their Refinery and a three or four million dollar thing which could have been solved by a hundred thousand dollars worth of inspection uh turned into a 60 million dollar lawsuit
instances.framework_lesson: Overreaction to discovered defects without engineering evaluation of actual risk or necessity of replacement can create liability; specifications should define the scope of remediation, not leave it to reactive management decisions.

cluster_id: Boeing 747 cleaning specification failure
display_name: Boeing 747 cleaning specification failure

instances.name: Boeing 747 cleaning specification failure
instances.summary: Boeing engineers wrote an overly strict cleaning specification for aircraft components that required expensive procedures; when followed correctly, defect rates improved dramatically, but the specification itself was poorly conceived and wasteful.
instances.key_quote: somewhere that Boeing had written a stupid Spec just because you can find a flaw doesn't mean the flaw is harmful
instances.framework_lesson: A specification written by inexperienced engineers without domain knowledge in fracture mechanics or non-destructive testing can mandate wasteful procedures that don't improve actual safety.

cluster_id: Stainless steel hot water tank bleach corrosion failure
display_name: Stainless steel hot water tank bleach corrosion failure

instances.name: Stainless steel hot water tank bleach corrosion failure
instances.summary: A food processing plant's stainless steel hot water tank corroded under insulation due to bleach used for sanitation, eventually failing catastrophically and killing multiple people.
instances.key_quote: the vessel looked like you know a dried riverbed in terms of all the cracks that were all over it until it blew up and killed a couple of people
instances.framework_lesson: Inspection requirements exist because people have been hurt; inspections must include checking under insulation where hidden corrosion can occur unseen.

cluster_id: Massachusetts lemon-law case against General Motors
display_name: Massachusetts lemon-law case against General Motors

instances.name: Massachusetts lemon-law case against General Motors
instances.summary: Eagar personally won a lemon-law case against General Motors in Massachusetts in the 1980s over a defective vehicle.
instances.key_quote: I did take a car in an aluminum at once back in the 80s and won against General Motors

cluster_id: David Taylor Model Basin aluminum replacement program
display_name: David Taylor Model Basin aluminum replacement program

instances.name: David Taylor Model Basin aluminum replacement program
instances.summary: Mid-1980s U.S. Navy engineering effort at David Taylor Model Basin (Annapolis) to replace aluminum superstructures with high-strength steel waffle construction across the fleet.
instances.key_quote: back in the mid 80s at David Taylor Annapolis this place that is closed now someone said earlier they were doing a lot of stuff to replace aluminum superstructures with waffle steel so it wouldn't Catch Fire
instances.framework_lesson: Two high-profile naval fires led the Navy to allocate approximately $100 million in research and development to eliminate flammable aluminum from capital ship design.

cluster_id: SPF/DB titanium aerospace applications
display_name: SPF/DB titanium aerospace applications

instances.name: SPF/DB titanium aerospace applications
instances.summary: The Air Force invested significantly in super-plastic forming and diffusion bonding of titanium to create complex aircraft components with fine grain structures and excellent mechanical properties.
instances.key_quote: super plastic forming diffusion bonded um was something that um the Air Force put a lot of money into after they realized that they could form titanium this way

cluster_id: CONFORM superconductor experiment
display_name: CONFORM superconductor experiment

instances.name: CONFORM superconductor experiment
instances.summary: Eagar attempted to use CONFORM process to manufacture superconductors approximately 30 years prior to the lecture (early 1980s) but the process did not work well for that application.
instances.key_quote: I actually tried to do conform to make some superconductors back 30 years ago and it didn't work very well

cluster_id: Copper-niobium alloy CONFORM die failure
display_name: Copper-niobium alloy CONFORM die failure

instances.name: Copper-niobium alloy CONFORM die failure
instances.summary: Eagar attempted to apply CONFORM process to copper-niobium alloy mixtures but repeatedly broke the dies because niobium strengthened the material excessively, making it impossible to design a steel die that could withstand the process.
