cluster_id: North American CONFORM machine (Rhode Island)
display_name: North American CONFORM machine (Rhode Island)

instances.name: North American CONFORM machine (Rhode Island)
instances.summary: A North American company located in Rhode Island attempted to commercialize and sell CONFORM machines in the early 1970s; Eagar conducted research visits to this facility because of its proximity to MIT.
instances.key_quote: the North American uh company that was trying to sell conform was located down here in P Rhode Island okay so it was close enough that I could go do some research down there

cluster_id: Watertown Arsenal closure
display_name: Watertown Arsenal closure

instances.name: Watertown Arsenal closure
instances.summary: The Watertown Arsenal, a historic weapons manufacturing facility built before the Civil War and used to make cannonballs, closed approximately 20 years before this 2012 lecture (around 1992).
instances.key_quote: it was it died in the first round of bra but it was built before the Civil War to make weapons for the Army they made cannon balls there during the Civil War

cluster_id: Faberware Millennium cookware hydrogen cracking
display_name: Faberware Millennium cookware hydrogen cracking

instances.name: Faberware Millennium cookware hydrogen cracking
instances.summary: Expensive stainless steel pots cracked during shipment to Japan due to hydrogen embrittlement introduced during Teflon coating application in an argon-hydrogen atmosphere.
instances.key_quote: we make these very expensive pots called Millennium cookware and we've shipped them to Japan and lo and behold when they get to Japan they're cracked
instances.framework_lesson: Metallurgical literature conflated stress corrosion cracking and hydrogen embrittlement without distinguishing their electrochemical mechanisms (anode vs. cathode), masking the true failure mode.

cluster_id: Macondo blowout and near-misses
display_name: Macondo blowout and near-misses

instances.name: Macondo blowout and near-misses
instances.summary: The Deepwater Horizon Macondo well failure in the Gulf of Mexico, with approximately four additional near-miss failures in bolted connections for undersea oil production over 15-20 years.
instances.key_quote: they didn't want another Macondo failure did you know we've had about four near-miss Macondo failures in addition to the Macondo in the last 15 or 20 years
instances.framework_lesson: Regulatory oversight by the Department of the Interior requires systematic review of bolted connection design in subsea oil production to prevent catastrophic failures.

cluster_id: Connecticut hockey rink concrete failure
display_name: Connecticut hockey rink concrete failure

instances.name: Connecticut hockey rink concrete failure
instances.summary: A concrete hockey rink poured in August collapsed when black polyethylene cooling pipes failed due to excessive heat from the exothermic concrete curing reaction.
instances.key_quote: I had a hockey rink that was put in Connecticut in August and they used polyethylene black polyethylene tubing and they head of the tubing all laying there in the rink to pour the concrete on and of course the hot summer day the black was hot they poured the concrete on top and as it takes it concrete it's not a very good thermal conductor and so it basically heated up the pipes and they collapsed inside the concrete the whole thing was Chuck had rebuild it
instances.framework_lesson: Concrete curing generates significant heat; thermal management during placement in hot climates must account for exothermic reactions that can degrade embedded systems.

cluster_id: 1922 additive manufacturing weld-buildup on cast iron
display_name: 1922 additive manufacturing weld-buildup on cast iron

instances.name: 1922 additive manufacturing weld-buildup on cast iron
instances.summary: An early example of additive manufacturing found in a 1922 Welding Journal article showing weld beads laid down on cast iron to build up pads, though with cracking issues.
instances.key_quote: I found in the welding Journal of 1922 this additive manufactured part whereas people were laying weld beads down on cast-iron weld beats there are little cracks on either side of this thing but they were essentially building up pads and doing additive manufacturing

cluster_id: Erin Brockovich hexavalent chromium case
display_name: Erin Brockovich hexavalent chromium case

instances.name: Erin Brockovich hexavalent chromium case
instances.summary: Hexavalent chromium (Cr6+) used as a cleaning agent for aluminum and glassware is carcinogenic, as dramatized in the Erin Brockovich legal case.
instances.key_quote: chromic that's cr cro3 that's hexavalent chrome that's aaron brockovich that causes cancer
instances.framework_lesson: Historical lab practice of using chromic acid without proper environmental controls was later found to pose serious health and environmental risks, leading to modern EPA restrictions on discharge.

cluster_id: MIT undergraduate lab chromic acid disposal practice
display_name: MIT undergraduate lab chromic acid disposal practice

instances.name: MIT undergraduate lab chromic acid disposal practice
instances.summary: MIT's undergraduate laboratory historically maintained a large vat of chromic oxide for cleaning glassware, with researchers directly dipping equipment and draining residue down the sink without protective equipment or environmental controls.
instances.key_quote: they had a hood right there and they had a sink and it was a big vat of chromic oxide just sat there and when you cleaned your glassware you just take the tongs and you dip it in the chromic oxide and then you drain it in the sink
instances.framework_lesson: Laboratory safety and environmental practices have become far more stringent; practices that were routine in the 1970s-80s would now immediately trigger EPA and fire department response.

cluster_id: Fluke reference standard redefinition (1991 SI volt change)
display_name: Fluke reference standard redefinition (1991 SI volt change)

instances.name: Fluke reference standard redefinition (1991 SI volt change)
instances.summary: Fluke's precision reference voltage standards, sold for tens of thousands of dollars, became inaccurate when the international definition of the volt was redefined in 1991, affecting accuracy at the sixth decimal place.
instances.key_quote: a change in the way they defined the volt in 1991 and it turns out it made a difference in the six decimal plat place of what a volt is and all of a sudden fluke standard uh reference cell which they sold for tens of thousands of dollars uh was It was supposed to be accurate to so many parts in a year and it was no longer accurate
instances.framework_lesson: Standards bodies can redefine fundamental units retroactively, invalidating expensive precision instruments built to prior specifications; industry must track SI definition changes.

cluster_id: Tar Sands Pipeline Failure (Canada)
display_name: Tar Sands Pipeline Failure (Canada)

instances.name: Tar Sands Pipeline Failure (Canada)
instances.summary: A 6-inch steel pipe in Canadian tar sands operation was repaired with plain carbon steel instead of 1% chrome-molybdenum steel; sulfur corrosion thinned the wall to paper thickness, causing a pressure rupture and fire that cost approximately one billion dollars.
instances.key_quote: about 3 years later the thing had thinned down from like a quarter inch wall to a wall that was 177,000 about four sheets of paper and the pressure it blew open started a fire that cost a billion dollars
instances.framework_lesson: Positive materials identification specifications and quality control procedures failed to catch material substitution; material properties directly determine corrosion resistance in sour-service environments.

cluster_id: Chemical Plant/Refinery Pipe Mislabeling Survey
display_name: Chemical Plant/Refinery Pipe Mislabeling Survey

instances.name: Chemical Plant/Refinery Pipe Mislabeling Survey
instances.summary: Quality control audit in chemical plants and refineries found that approximately 6% of all steel pipes in service were mislabeled as to their actual steel type.
instances.key_quote: in some chemical plants a chemical refinery I've seen data of different steel pipes and they've gone in and done a very careful positive materials identification and 6% of all the pipes are mislabeled as to what type of Steel they are
instances.framework_lesson: Positive materials identification is critical in chemical service because material type directly determines corrosion resistance; labeling alone cannot substitute for actual material verification.

cluster_id: US Navy Destroyer brass fitting failure (Second Gulf War)
display_name: US Navy Destroyer brass fitting failure (Second Gulf War)

instances.name: US Navy Destroyer brass fitting failure (Second Gulf War)
instances.summary: A destroyer lost its 3000 psi pneumatic air system due to stress corrosion cracking in a brass fitting made from the wrong alloy specification, forcing the ship to withdraw from combat operations.
instances.key_quote: it turns out another student in the class was working in the Philadelphia Supply Depot about the same time and when they found that it was the wrong brass alloy it was not the correct alloy that was specified by navc for this application
instances.framework_lesson: Specification and supply-chain verification failures: the correct alloy was specified but 80% of the stock in the Philadelphia Supply Depot warehouse contained the wrong material, demonstrating the need for PMI (positive material identification) enforcement.

cluster_id: Canadian billion-dollar corrosion problem
display_name: Canadian billion-dollar corrosion problem

instances.name: Canadian billion-dollar corrosion problem
instances.summary: A material selection or corrosion-related failure in Canada resulted in approximately one billion dollars in damage.
instances.key_quote: it created a billion dollar problem up in Canada it created a ship that had to come offline in the middle of an an assault
instances.framework_lesson: Proper material identification, measurement, and specification enforcement are critical; failures to verify material properties and alloy composition can result in catastrophic field failures and enormous financial losses.

cluster_id: Hostelite toilet valve stress corrosion cracking
display_name: Hostelite toilet valve stress corrosion cracking

instances.name: Hostelite toilet valve stress corrosion cracking
instances.summary: Brass water closet valves made from a copper alloy failed by stress corrosion cracking in chlorine-laden Boston air, causing water jets to shoot through bathroom ceilings for 20 years.
instances.key_quote: for the next 20 years we had exploding toilets they made little um water closet valves out of these things and the thing would just fall apart particularly if people put you know the little blue things in the water closet
instances.framework_lesson: Engineers failed to anticipate that a commodity material introduction would encounter aggressive chloride environments and aggressive sterilizing chemicals that would trigger stress corrosion cracking.

cluster_id: Tom Eagar's residential water pipe failure and gas pipe damage incident
display_name: Tom Eagar's residential water pipe failure and gas pipe damage incident

instances.name: Tom Eagar's residential water pipe failure and gas pipe damage incident
instances.summary: Tree roots growing since the 1930s ruptured a corroded steel water service line at Eagar's house; during emergency repair, the backhoe operator discovered and damaged an adjacent buried gas pipe.
instances.key_quote: the guy in the backhoe jumps out of the backhoe takes some gum out of his mouth jumps in a hole and sticks a sticks of gum on a hole in the pipe the gas pipe that was next to the water pipe
instances.framework_lesson: Multiple buried utilities with different failure modes (corrosion, mechanical damage, root intrusion) create cascading hazards; loose practices for locating and protecting gas lines during excavation pose safety risks.

cluster_id: Historical Boston water-gas street lighting infrastructure
display_name: Historical Boston water-gas street lighting infrastructure

instances.name: Historical Boston water-gas street lighting infrastructure
instances.summary: Boston used wooden logs gun-drilled and later cast-iron pipe to convey water-gas (produced from coal and water) for street lighting in the 1800s and early 1900s.
instances.key_quote: the first thing they did they just took wooden logs and they gun drilled them what's gun drilling did i ask you anybody about gun drilling before

cluster_id: Residential gas pipeline explosions from plastic pipe use
display_name: Residential gas pipeline explosions from plastic pipe use

instances.name: Residential gas pipeline explosions from plastic pipe use
instances.summary: Homes have exploded from gas leaks when plastic pipes were initially used for gas distribution in the 1980s, motivating the gas association to develop safer polyethylene pipe designs.
instances.key_quote: we're worried about leaking water as they do gas okay there have been cases where homes have blown up we started using plastic pipes in the 1980s

cluster_id: Army ammunition dump lightning strike
display_name: Army ammunition dump lightning strike

instances.name: Army ammunition dump lightning strike
instances.summary: U.S. Army ammunition dumps have been struck by lightning and destroyed, motivating the Army's interest in reliable lightning protection systems.
instances.key_quote: why is the army interested in lightning protection they've had a few ammo dumps go in a lightning storm over the years get a nice lightning bolt right through your your ammo and you all of a sudden you don't have any ammo left

cluster_id: Silk screws for pediatric jaw fractures
display_name: Silk screws for pediatric jaw fractures

instances.name: Silk screws for pediatric jaw fractures
instances.summary: Researchers at Tufts University developed silk screws for use in children's jaw fractures that dissolve in the body after healing, eliminating need for surgical removal.
instances.key_quote: they put the bones together with silk screws and the silk screws eventually just dissolve in the body after they've done their job holding the bone together
instances.framework_lesson: FDA grandfather clause for pre-1970s materials creates regulatory barrier to material innovation in medical implants, forcing engineers to work with legacy materials despite better alternatives.

cluster_id: Metal plate and screw bone fixation stress concentration problem
display_name: Metal plate and screw bone fixation stress concentration problem

instances.name: Metal plate and screw bone fixation stress concentration problem
instances.summary: Medical implants using stiff metal plates and screws to fix broken bones create stress concentrations at the material interface due to modulus mismatch with bone tissue.
instances.key_quote: a high modulus material next to a low modulus material in a structure gives you a stress concentration not because of a notch but just because of the differences in the materials
instances.framework_lesson: Current metal fixation design is known to be suboptimal but persists because metals provide necessary strength; material modulus matching was not historically prioritized in implant specifications.

cluster_id: Nickel-aluminum precipitation hardening alloy development
display_name: Nickel-aluminum precipitation hardening alloy development

instances.name: Nickel-aluminum precipitation hardening alloy development
instances.summary: Eagar developed a nickel-aluminum alloy with precipitation hardening in the early 1980s for the Navy, achieving 50% higher hardness than prior materials, but the sponsoring company decided there was no real application and abandoned the project.
instances.key_quote: i went off in 1984 to tokyo for the navy came back afterwards and said well what happened have we have you patented it and they said well uh we decided there wasn't a real application for it

cluster_id: Sterling silver lightweight dinnerware alloy
display_name: Sterling silver lightweight dinnerware alloy

instances.name: Sterling silver lightweight dinnerware alloy
instances.summary: After the nickel-aluminum project failed, Eagar developed a lithium-tin-silver alloy for lightweight sterling silver dinnerware with the same precipitation-hardening principle, but Reed & Barton rejected it because wealthy customers want heavy silverware to display wealth; the alloy found niche success in baby cups with 20% weight reduction.
instances.key_quote: reed martin said well no one wants lightweight sterling silver dinnerware they want people to know that they are rich and they can afford a lot of silver

cluster_id: Chrome-molybdenum tube cracking at Boston Navy Yard
display_name: Chrome-molybdenum tube cracking at Boston Navy Yard

instances.name: Chrome-molybdenum tube cracking at Boston Navy Yard
instances.summary: A chrome-molybdenum tube over time developed brittle topologically close-packed phases (sigma, p, mu, laves phases) after approximately 10,000 hours at temperature, causing cracking; the Navy required inconel welding instead of stainless steel to mitigate the problem.
instances.key_quote: the stainless steel probably after 10 000 hours formed this brittle phase and the things would crack okay so it's a general problem
instances.framework_lesson: Codes and material specifications did not account for long-term thermal exposure (tens of thousands of hours) causing precipitation of brittle intermetallic phases.
materials_or_systems: brittle intermetallic phases (sigma, p, mu, laves)

cluster_id: Westinghouse Bettis inconel electron beam weld cracking specification
display_name: Westinghouse Bettis inconel electron beam weld cracking specification

instances.name: Westinghouse Bettis inconel electron beam weld cracking specification
instances.summary: Westinghouse Bettis, which manufactures nuclear reactor components for the Navy, employed an unusual welding code allowing cracks up to approximately 1 mm in length in inconel electron beam welds, because liquation cracking (formation of liquid at the fusion boundary and solidification grain boundary) is a persistent problem in nickel-base alloys that cannot be fully eliminated.
instances.key_quote: they had a minimum size it had a maximum size of the crack it i think it couldn't be more than about a millimeter in length
instances.framework_lesson: Welding codes for high-strength nickel-base alloys in nuclear applications had to permit small cracks as a practical compromise because liquation cracking is inherent to the metallurgy and cannot be prevented.

cluster_id: Nickel alloy coating crack specification case
display_name: Nickel alloy coating crack specification case

instances.name: Nickel alloy coating crack specification case
instances.summary: A coating specification for nickel-base alloys allowed cracks up to approximately 1 millimeter in length to pass inspection.
instances.key_quote: the only coat i ever knew that would allow you to have cracks in your welds and pass the spec they had a minimum size it had a maximum size of the crack
instances.framework_lesson: A specification that permitted cracks in welds created a false sense of acceptability for defects prone to liquation cracking under thermal cycling.

cluster_id: Monel electron beam weld consulting case
display_name: Monel electron beam weld consulting case

instances.name: Monel electron beam weld consulting case
instances.summary: Eagar examined multi-pass electron beam welds in Monel with 30-degree overlap for an application (possibly Vetis), which experienced liquation cracking in the heat-affected zone due to grain boundary segregation.
instances.key_quote: they had to make an electron beam weld with a 30 overlap in this particular thing and you get a grain boundary film that melts
instances.framework_lesson: Multi-pass electron beam welds in Monel can create grain boundary films that partially melt and segregate alloy content, causing intergranular liquation cracks that do not self-heal.

cluster_id: Cast nickel-base alloy dendritic segregation cracking
display_name: Cast nickel-base alloy dendritic segregation cracking

instances.name: Cast nickel-base alloy dendritic segregation cracking
instances.summary: A cast nickel-based alloy weld experienced centerline cracking due to severe dendritic segregation, with alloy content concentrated at dendrite boundaries and rejected in the dendrite centers, creating high thermal stress during cooling.
instances.key_quote: you have a highly more highly alloyed structure and you can see you heat it up to weld it and the cooling the stresses from cooling of the weld crack things

cluster_id: Rensselaer Polytechnic transverse strength test (stainless/nickel alloy)
display_name: Rensselaer Polytechnic transverse strength test (stainless/nickel alloy)

instances.name: Rensselaer Polytechnic transverse strength test (stainless/nickel alloy)
instances.summary: A standard bend test (RPI Varestrength test) on stainless steel or nickel-based alloy welds demonstrated that liquation cracks open during transverse bending because the cracks remain partially liquid at test temperature.
instances.key_quote: you basically you can see when you applied the pressure and you just open up the cracks okay because the cracks were partially liquid okay at the time

cluster_id: Stainless steel corrosion failures
display_name: Stainless steel corrosion failures

instances.name: Stainless steel corrosion failures
instances.summary: Despite stainless steel comprising only 2% of steel production, it accounts for 30% of all material failures because users misunderstand its properties and remove the protective chromium oxide layer through contamination or improper handling.
instances.key_quote: stainless steels are two percent of all steels which makes them about two percent of all metals they constitute 30 percent of all the problems and failures
instances.framework_lesson: Marketing and naming created false consumer expectations that 'stainless' meant 'stain-free' and required no maintenance, leading to widespread field failures when protective oxide layers were compromised.

cluster_id: New Haven to Boston trolley wire decision
display_name: New Haven to Boston trolley wire decision

instances.name: New Haven to Boston trolley wire decision
instances.summary: Tom Eagar was given 48 hours to decide whether to accept six million dollars of aluminum ingot material for trolley wire on a railroad extension, which he rejected due to excessively large grain size.
instances.key_quote: i got a phone call saying you have to tell us by christmas three days later whether we can accept six million dollars of this material
instances.framework_lesson: Very pure copper alloys develop large grain structures that compromise strength; impurities must be added (e.g., 0.2% silver) to suppress grain growth and maintain mechanical properties.

cluster_id: Military low-level radar platform molybdenum-copper composite
display_name: Military low-level radar platform molybdenum-copper composite

instances.name: Military low-level radar platform molybdenum-copper composite
instances.summary: Military aircraft flying at sonic velocities near ground level used pulsed electron-beam radar amplifiers that would melt copper; engineers solved this by bonding a 2mm molybdenum layer to copper via transient liquid phase diffusion bonding to dissipate heat while maintaining thermal conductivity.
instances.key_quote: you would melt copper with the heat intensity of that pulsed electron beam but you won't melt molybdenum but you need the thermal conductivity of copper so they make a composite

cluster_id: San Diego wildfires and utility transmission line lawsuits
display_name: San Diego wildfires and utility transmission line lawsuits

instances.name: San Diego wildfires and utility transmission line lawsuits
instances.summary: A major lawsuit or settlement (three to four hundred million dollars) was brought against a California utility after transmission lines sagged and touched ground during summer heat, allegedly starting wildfires that burned much of southern California.
instances.key_quote: they had some tremendous wildfires in san diego and it would just burn down all that part of southern california well it turns out they finally some people finally got a big one a big three or four hundred million dollar lawsuit
instances.framework_lesson: Transmission line sag calculations must account for thermal expansion under peak summer air-conditioning load; failure to maintain adequate ground clearance creates fire hazard and massive liability.

cluster_id: Welded titanium stent tip failure
display_name: Welded titanium stent tip failure

instances.name: Welded titanium stent tip failure
instances.summary: A medical device company brought Tom Eagar a welded titanium stent (10–15 years old at time of lecture) because of problems in the welding of the tip, though he does not recall the specific failure mode.
instances.year_or_era: Late 1990s or early 2000s (described as 10–15 years old at 2014 lecture date)
instances.key_quote: they had some problem on the welding of the tip there so it came to me i don't even remember what the problem was

cluster_id: Powdered beryllium discovery in MIT laboratory
display_name: Powdered beryllium discovery in MIT laboratory

instances.name: Powdered beryllium discovery in MIT laboratory
instances.summary: Assistant professor found a bottle of powdered beryllium in Nick Grant's retired laboratory on the third floor of Building 8, requiring environmental health services disposal; the material had likely been stored since the 1940s.
instances.key_quote: he found a little plastic bottle that said be on it and he came rushing into my office we found i found powdered beryllium

cluster_id: Golf club metallic woods cracking failure
display_name: Golf club metallic woods cracking failure

instances.name: Golf club metallic woods cracking failure
instances.summary: High-end golf club heads made from metallic materials would crack after approximately six hits during use.
instances.key_quote: they were making metallic woods or you know golf ball golf heads and they would get about six shots before they would all crack and everything okay

cluster_id: Soviet Titanium Aircraft Program (Docket 3)
display_name: Soviet Titanium Aircraft Program (Docket 3)

instances.name: Soviet Titanium Aircraft Program (Docket 3)
instances.summary: Soviet aircraft fuselage full of cracks despite having resources to build it, illustrating how political decisions to leapfrog technology can proceed even when engineering shows fundamental problems.
instances.key_quote: no one's going to tell the top brass that even if we build it the docket 3 personal defense is full of cracks

cluster_id: WWII Welded Merchant Vessel Structural Failures (Fleet-Wide)
display_name: WWII Welded Merchant Vessel Structural Failures (Fleet-Wide)

instances.name: WWII Welded Merchant Vessel Structural Failures (Fleet-Wide)
instances.summary: Of 6000 Liberty-class ships built, roughly 1000–1100 experienced major structural failures with 6–7 complete breakups, revealing systemic fracture problems in welded steel design.
instances.key_quote: I've got 6,000 chefs the number that had serious structural problems was huge but like a thousand eleven hundred ships and major structural failures six or seven broke up completely
instances.framework_lesson: Design codes specified tensile strength but ignored fracture toughness and ductile-brittle transition; wartime production volumes exposed the gap.

cluster_id: White Oak Naval Particle Beam Program
display_name: White Oak Naval Particle Beam Program

instances.name: White Oak Naval Particle Beam Program
instances.summary: US Navy's White Oak facility in Maryland spent approximately $250 million developing a relativistic particle beam weapon system intended to defend against cruise and anti-ship missiles during the Cold War.
instances.key_quote: the US Navy white oak Maryland was developing spent a quarter billion dollars developing particle beam with us now this was to be a relativistic electron being millions of electron volts

cluster_id: Boeing Friction-Stir Welding Machine Investment
display_name: Boeing Friction-Stir Welding Machine Investment

instances.name: Boeing Friction-Stir Welding Machine Investment
instances.summary: Boeing invested $10 million to build a friction-stir welding machine for aircraft structures because welded joints are lighter than bolted alternatives.
instances.key_quote: boeing went out and spent 10 million dollars building friction-stir welding machine for some of their structures because a welded joint is always lighter than a bolted joint

cluster_id: Philippine gold-mining crushing machine failure
display_name: Philippine gold-mining crushing machine failure

instances.name: Philippine gold-mining crushing machine failure
instances.summary: A four-story-tall crushing machine used for gold mining in the Philippines failed due to steel lamellar cracking in the base plate, but an inspector's 237-paragraph report blamed laminations despite five inspection teams finding none.
instances.key_quote: there's a guy there's oh this mill this crushing machine that is four stories tall in the Philippines that was whether mining for gold it was all because of the steel had was at my military
instances.framework_lesson: Engineering reports can perpetuate outdated failure modes (laminations) as explanations even after the root cause has been solved through improved steel manufacturing practices.

cluster_id: Small Aircraft Wing Cracking
display_name: Small Aircraft Wing Cracking

instances.name: Small Aircraft Wing Cracking
instances.summary: Small aircraft developed unexpectedly large cracks (6-8 inches) in wing structures that were not predicted by design due to complex twisting modes that are difficult to evaluate and inaccessible for inspection.
instances.key_quote: you know in some small aircraft structures you can't really see inside the wings and therefore they don't really get inspected and then all of a sudden you know you have cracks six inches eight inches long that were not predicted by Design
instances.framework_lesson: Inaccessible structures with unpredictable failure modes require higher safety factors and preventive inspection access rather than reliance on analytical strength alone.

cluster_id: High-speed boat propeller shaft failure
display_name: High-speed boat propeller shaft failure

instances.name: High-speed boat propeller shaft failure
instances.summary: A four-inch diameter propeller shaft on a high-speed boat in Florida fractured and failed during the owner's second season of use, causing the propeller to fall off during operation.
instances.key_quote: it went only to uh I think it was a season and a half so he was into his second season and the prop fell off was he was on a ride so he was really surprised
instances.framework_lesson: Safety factors of 1.2 are inadequate when fatigue crack initiation is not controlled; surface scars and stress concentrations were not properly accounted for in the design.

cluster_id: Bridge truss collapse with gusset plate failure
display_name: Bridge truss collapse with gusset plate failure

instances.name: Bridge truss collapse with gusset plate failure
instances.summary: A bridge with two main side trusses connected by gusset plates collapsed after approximately 40 years of service due to undersized gusset plates, corrosion, added lanes, and pavement loads that consumed all safety factor.
instances.key_quote: if we start with a safety factor of two but we miss out on the thickness right from day one on the erection drawing we don't have a safety factor of two anymore it's less than that
instances.framework_lesson: Fatigue assessments that evaluate only members and ignore joints provide false confidence; gusset plate design was not adequately covered in inspector training until after the collapse.

cluster_id: Food processing stainless steel tank stress corrosion cracking
display_name: Food processing stainless steel tank stress corrosion cracking

instances.name: Food processing stainless steel tank stress corrosion cracking
instances.summary: A heated pressurized stainless steel tank in a food processing plant failed by unexpected stress corrosion cracking initiated from the outside due to chlorides in the jacket insulation, causing brittle failure despite a design safety factor of four.
instances.key_quote: even though I started with that factor of safety of four which would typically be with respect to yield stress I can have stress corrosion cracking add a load okay one-fifth of that yield stress
instances.framework_lesson: Design codes assume ductile failure modes and yield-based safety factors, but unexpected failure modes like stress corrosion cracking can eliminate safety margin entirely and cause brittle fracture instead of leak.

cluster_id: Washington DC government steam piping drip leg connection failure
display_name: Washington DC government steam piping drip leg connection failure

instances.name: Washington DC government steam piping drip leg connection failure
instances.summary: A steam piping system serving government buildings in Washington DC had an improperly detailed drip leg connection that collected condensate, with an unusual welded cap design that created unanticipated stress concentration.
instances.key_quote: the connection here at that drip leg was built in an unusual way so it had the proper material thickness but I'll show you the detail of that one so you get an idea for what may have happened

cluster_id: Pressure vessel drip leg weld failure
display_name: Pressure vessel drip leg weld failure

instances.name: Pressure vessel drip leg weld failure
instances.summary: A pressure vessel's drip leg connection failed due to improper weld design that concentrated tensile stress at the weld root, causing failure under transient water hammer loads.
instances.key_quote: what they had the weld in this corner and when you pressurize this design detail it just wants to expand so you're cracking open those welds
instances.framework_lesson: Codes specify proper cap geometry and weld placement to distribute stress evenly; poor detail design can eliminate safety factors despite adequate wall thickness.

cluster_id: Blowout preventer investigations (4-5 cases)
display_name: Blowout preventer investigations (4-5 cases)

instances.name: Blowout preventer investigations (4-5 cases)
instances.summary: Multiple forensic investigations of blowout preventers on drilling rigs, where pressure bursts typically allow the well to self-seal but occasionally result in total loss of well integrity.
instances.key_quote: I had four or five well blowout investigation and a lot of times nobody gets hurt it's a lot of money lost or it's a lot of environmental damage
instances.framework_lesson: Lack of prescriptive API safety factor standards for blowout preventers has resulted in relatively low reliability valves that fail to seal under extreme conditions.

cluster_id: Small jet wing assembly fatigue testing
display_name: Small jet wing assembly fatigue testing

instances.name: Small jet wing assembly fatigue testing
instances.summary: Wings for small jets were tested through cyclic deflection up to one foot or more at the tip, including an extreme final cycle to failure, to evaluate fatigue and seal/fuel tank integrity.
instances.key_quote: the wings for small Jets being moved up and down so they help where the fuselage is with all the bolts on and just move the tip of the wing at least a foot up and down
instances.framework_lesson: Full-scale assembly-level testing is required in aerospace to account for integration effects like seal behavior and fuel tank integrity under extreme deflection that calculation alone cannot predict.

cluster_id: ATV exhaust and water ingress assembly failures
display_name: ATV exhaust and water ingress assembly failures

instances.name: ATV exhaust and water ingress assembly failures
instances.summary: ATVs experienced reliability problems when heat from exhaust and water ingress during operation were not adequately accounted for in design calculations.
instances.key_quote: you have the exhaust so you have the heat and you you're going to have water coming in when you're running the units um these are not things that are easily taking into account with the computer
instances.framework_lesson: Calculation-based design misses integration-level failure modes; full-scale and field testing of complete assemblies is necessary to identify problems in smaller recreational products.

cluster_id: Helicopter blade spar stress evaluation case
display_name: Helicopter blade spar stress evaluation case

instances.name: Helicopter blade spar stress evaluation case
instances.summary: Engineering evaluation of aluminum helicopter rotor blade spars subjected to combined centrifugal and aerodynamic loads, using strain gauges to measure actual stress levels and determine fatigue life requirements.
instances.key_quote: they put strain gauges right there actually close to the rotor well just before they had the stiffeners so they put a strain gauge there find out where the stress level is
instances.framework_lesson: Fatigue design requires actual measured stress data from representative flight conditions rather than theoretical estimates, and safety factors must account for the nonlinear relationship between stress level and cyclic life on S-N curves.

cluster_id: Preloaded bolt connection in heavy crane or bearing application
display_name: Preloaded bolt connection in heavy crane or bearing application

instances.name: Preloaded bolt connection in heavy crane or bearing application
instances.summary: Analysis of preloaded bolts in crane and bearing applications showing how preload reduces stress amplitude by a factor of six or seven compared to unpre-loaded connections.
instances.key_quote: if I don't calculate all of this if I don't go through this method how am I going to know which safety factor to use it will have to be 10
instances.framework_lesson: Generic codes specifying a single safety factor (e.g., five) for bolts do not account for preload effects; detailed assembly analysis is required to justify lower factors and avoid over-design.

cluster_id: Building and nuclear facility bolt inspection protocol
display_name: Building and nuclear facility bolt inspection protocol

instances.name: Building and nuclear facility bolt inspection protocol
instances.summary: Inspection method using direct tension indicator sensors (DTIs) that collapse when a specific preload force is reached, used in building and nuclear facility construction to verify proper bolt installation.
instances.key_quote: for a nuclear facility they have these sensors that collapses when you reach a certain load dtis or direct tension indicator and so the inspectogens can just go around and see that all the sensors are put in
instances.framework_lesson: Codes for critical structures (nuclear facilities) now mandate field verification of bolt preload through instrumented sensors to prevent under-tensioning failures.

cluster_id: Snowmobile frame and weld design case
display_name: Snowmobile frame and weld design case

instances.name: Snowmobile frame and weld design case
instances.summary: Design of a recreational snowmobile frame using a factor of safety of three, with the concern that welds must also meet the same factor of safety or the overall design is compromised.
instances.key_quote: if I start with tree is that gonna be enough well you know it depends on this it depends on what's the variability and that safety factor of tree so did I look at all the welds
instances.framework_lesson: A single factor of safety applied to the nominal material strength is insufficient if all components (especially welds) are not analyzed with equal rigor and margin.

cluster_id: Trailer design joint failure example
display_name: Trailer design joint failure example

instances.name: Trailer design joint failure example
instances.summary: Incomplete description of a trailer design where failure modes at joints were not analyzed early in the design process, serving as a cautionary example.
instances.key_quote: you do want to think about the joints very early on because you know stupid example of that is I built a trailer that has all the

cluster_id: Bridge forensic consulting case
display_name: Bridge forensic consulting case

instances.name: Bridge forensic consulting case
instances.summary: Tom Eagar was part of a consulting team investigating a bridge failure or structural issue approximately four years after the bridge's construction.
instances.key_quote: it was different types of people but it was related to the design team that originally put the bridge together
instances.framework_lesson: Original design documentation and designer knowledge can be lost or unavailable during forensic investigation when the original organization has been dismantled, complicating failure analysis.

cluster_id: Allied Signal amorphous metal foil patent dispute
display_name: Allied Signal amorphous metal foil patent dispute

instances.name: Allied Signal amorphous metal foil patent dispute
instances.summary: Allied Signal (later Honeywell) discovered rapidly solidified amorphous metal foil with superior magnetic properties but failed to secure patent rights when a University of Pennsylvania professor they consulted with, without a nondisclosure agreement, patented the material's most important property.
instances.key_quote: they didn't have a nondisclosure agreement with him he owned the patent rights and the University of Pennsylvania owned the patent rights to that material so they had to buy him back
instances.framework_lesson: Failure to use nondisclosure agreements in collaborative research resulted in loss of patent control and substantial financial liability.

cluster_id: Chrysler lightweight aluminum vehicle design
display_name: Chrysler lightweight aluminum vehicle design

instances.name: Chrysler lightweight aluminum vehicle design
instances.summary: Chrysler designed an all-aluminum body vehicle with a 1.6-liter engine but failed to optimize the overall system; they could have achieved greater fuel savings by also redesigning the engine to 1.2 liters instead of simply substituting materials.
instances.key_quote: when they went to the lightweight aluminum they didn't really get as good a bang for the buck on the fuel savings because they were using an engine there was one point six liters
instances.framework_lesson: Material substitution without system-level redesign fails to capture full efficiency gains; optimization requires holistic design from scratch.

cluster_id: Aluminum vehicle lightweight design failure
display_name: Aluminum vehicle lightweight design failure

instances.name: Aluminum vehicle lightweight design failure
instances.summary: A manufacturer switched a vehicle to all-aluminum construction but failed to redesign the engine, missing optimal fuel savings because they kept a 1.6-liter engine instead of downsizing to 1.2 liters.
instances.key_quote: they didn't really get as good a bang for the buck on the fuel savings because they were using an engine there was one point six liters if they actually had a 1.2 liter engine for this small car they could have gotten even more fuel savings
instances.framework_lesson: Material substitution alone without whole-system optimization fails to deliver full performance benefits; engineers must redesign all components together.

cluster_id: KitchenAid refrigerator successful design
display_name: KitchenAid refrigerator successful design

instances.name: KitchenAid refrigerator successful design
instances.summary: By 1990, KitchenAid successfully redesigned plastic refrigerator interiors by using sliding slot joints instead of threaded fasteners, eliminating stress concentrations and achieving long service life.
instances.key_quote: by 1990 i bought a kitchenaid which i think i still have at least I think I know it who has it still in use they designed the plastic sheet metal not cheap metal but cheap plastic so that the shelves basically slid into a slot like a drawer
instances.framework_lesson: Material changes require corresponding design changes in joining methods; sliding constraints eliminate stress concentrations superior to threaded joints in plastics.

cluster_id: Lead exposure in Trail, British Columbia
display_name: Lead exposure in Trail, British Columbia

instances.name: Lead exposure in Trail, British Columbia
instances.summary: Children in Trail, British Columbia developed developmental and cognitive disabilities from chronic airborne lead oxide exposure over a century-long lead and zinc smelting operation.
instances.key_quote: Trail British Columbia it's been a his ink smelting community for over a hundred years and there's lots of lead oxide in the air and so children in trail British Columbia have been growing up breathing lead

cluster_id: Copper-to-copper crimp connector adoption in residential plumbing
display_name: Copper-to-copper crimp connector adoption in residential plumbing

instances.name: Copper-to-copper crimp connector adoption in residential plumbing
instances.summary: The plumbing industry shifted from soldered copper joints to mechanical crimp connectors with rubber gaskets to reduce fire risk, increasing cost and installation complexity.
instances.key_quote: basically used a crimp on connector a mechanical crimp on with a rubber gasket so it's copper to copper with a rubber gasket and each one of those fittings costs like thirty or forty bucks
instances.framework_lesson: A regulation designed to eliminate fire risk (banning soldering) created an externality: shift to more expensive mechanical fittings, reflecting unintended consequences of material/process standards.

cluster_id: US Navy nuclear submarine steel pipe corrosion (30-year ship life)
display_name: US Navy nuclear submarine steel pipe corrosion (30-year ship life)

instances.name: US Navy nuclear submarine steel pipe corrosion (30-year ship life)
instances.summary: Navy ships using rigorous carbon steel pipe experienced severe corrosion after ~25 years, approaching end of 30-year service life; officers had to continuously repair leaks throughout ship operation.
instances.key_quote: they used to use rigorous steel pipe which would corrode away after about 25 years the ship had a 30 year life and you just I remember one officer I was a lieutenant junior and when she graduated from the Academy her job on the ship was to fix the leaks
instances.framework_lesson: Material selection based on initial cost without accounting for in-service corrosion and life-cycle maintenance creates unreliable platforms; extended service requirements demand corrosion-resistant alternatives.

cluster_id: Corrugated stainless steel gas pipe lightning strike fires
display_name: Corrugated stainless steel gas pipe lightning strike fires

instances.name: Corrugated stainless steel gas pipe lightning strike fires
instances.summary: Corrugated stainless steel tubing with polyethylene coating used for household gas distribution fails catastrophically when struck by lightning, causing approximately 100 home fires per year.
instances.key_quote: the lightning just like the yellow tubing is not there if the lightning strikes your structure you'll blast a hole on the inside 10 times the diameter the little hole in the end and it burns down about a hundred homes a year
instances.framework_lesson: The polyethylene insulation coating adequate for household electrical current (30,000 volts rating) provides no protection against millions of volts from lightning strikes, and the product standard did not account for lightning exposure.
materials_or_systems: corrugated stainless steel tubing (ten thousandths inch thick)

cluster_id: Graphite pressure vessel failure with sulfuric acid
display_name: Graphite pressure vessel failure with sulfuric acid

instances.name: Graphite pressure vessel failure with sulfuric acid
instances.summary: Graphite pressure vessels used to contain concentrated sulfuric acid are brittle and prone to catastrophic failure when they rupture under pressure.
instances.key_quote: when the thing goes boom and it's full of sulfuric acid it's not a good day okay

cluster_id: MBTA Thermite Rail Welding at Rosendale
display_name: MBTA Thermite Rail Welding at Rosendale

instances.name: MBTA Thermite Rail Welding at Rosendale
instances.summary: The MBTA used thermite welding to join train tracks together at a site in Rosendale, Massachusetts, producing intense sparks and molten iron.
instances.key_quote: I went down to Rosendale once to watch this okay about 30 years ago um the MBTA was welding two train tracks together

cluster_id: Navy Ship Aluminum-Steel Transition Joints - Galvanic Corrosion
display_name: Navy Ship Aluminum-Steel Transition Joints - Galvanic Corrosion

instances.name: Navy Ship Aluminum-Steel Transition Joints - Galvanic Corrosion
instances.summary: The U.S. Navy uses explosive-bonded aluminum-to-steel transition joints in ship superstructures to reduce weight, but the galvanic couple between aluminum and steel in seawater causes corrosion failure.
instances.key_quote: the only problem you then have is you now put a galvanic couple of aluminum and iron into a seawater environment and it corrodes okay it corrodes just great
instances.framework_lesson: Material compatibility in specific chemical environments must be designed into composite joints; galvanic couples in seawater require protective measures beyond structural bonding.

cluster_id: Power Plant Heat Exchanger Tube Sheet Joining
display_name: Power Plant Heat Exchanger Tube Sheet Joining

instances.name: Power Plant Heat Exchanger Tube Sheet Joining
instances.summary: Utility power plants use explosive welding to rapidly join thousands of tubes into massive tube sheets in heat exchangers; the technique creates reliable seals and prevents crevice corrosion that would otherwise destroy multi-million-dollar equipment.
instances.key_quote: one thing that seals it very quickly they just put a charge in here and they taper the holes and they essentially have the tube inside the in here and they have a detonation thing in here they go slamming it against there

cluster_id: Incandescent light bulb development (Edison/Langmuir)
display_name: Incandescent light bulb development (Edison/Langmuir)

instances.name: Incandescent light bulb development (Edison/Langmuir)
instances.summary: Edison's early incandescent light bulbs using carbonized horse hair failed after less than 10 hours; Langmuir at GE discovered that filling with argon gas instead of vacuum extended tungsten filament life from 10 to 1000+ hours by preventing vaporization.
instances.key_quote: Edison had invented the incandescent light bulb but they didn't last very long like less than 10 hours which was getting kind of pricey to use

cluster_id: NASA Mercury space program graphite lubricant failure
display_name: NASA Mercury space program graphite lubricant failure

instances.name: NASA Mercury space program graphite lubricant failure
instances.summary: NASA used graphite as a lubricant in early Mercury spacecraft, but at ultra-high vacuum (10^-10 atmospheres) in space, oxygen and CO2 desorbed from graphite basal planes, leaving only strong carbon-to-carbon bonds that seized the mechanism.
instances.key_quote: they Ed graphite as the lubricant in the early Mercury space shots when they went up into space and they got down to very low pressures like 10us 10 atmospheres and at those pressures you could strip the oxygen and the CO2 off the basal planes of the graphite
instances.framework_lesson: Lubricant behavior depends critically on adsorbed surface films; vacuum removes those films and changes material properties in unexpected ways.

cluster_id: NASA cold welding research initiative
display_name: NASA cold welding research initiative

instances.name: NASA cold welding research initiative
instances.summary: Following early spaceflight problems with materials sticking and mechanisms failing in vacuum, NASA funded research into cold welding phenomena and organized a 1967 conference on adhesion and cold welding in space environments.
instances.key_quote: because of that NASA got very interested in cold welding and they actually started funding research and after what was Alan Shepard was 1959 or something anyway he was the first American in space but you know so 8 years later they're now having a conference to talk about how things stick together in space

cluster_id: Ballpark Franks hot dog cooker failure
display_name: Ballpark Franks hot dog cooker failure

instances.name: Ballpark Franks hot dog cooker failure
instances.summary: A stainless steel steam cooker at a hot dog manufacturing facility in Everett suddenly began failing and crumbling after years of reliable service because replacement parts were specified as 304 stainless steel instead of the original 316, which lacked molybdenum needed to resist chloride corrosion from salt in hot dogs.
instances.key_quote: the new replacement parts were just literally crumbling in the higher salt content that's the most dramatic example i've ever seen of parts falling apart in 304 versus 316
instances.framework_lesson: Material specification changes in replacement parts without verification of corrosion resistance requirements can cause sudden failures in systems that had performed reliably for years.
instances.source_module: Codes and Standards Definitions and History

cluster_id: Desalination pump bearing failure
display_name: Desalination pump bearing failure

instances.name: Desalination pump bearing failure
instances.summary: A company manufacturing stainless steel desalination pumps for Middle Eastern markets specified 316 material for the pumps but used 302 stainless steel for small bearing-holding inserts, causing bearing failures within one year and destroying the company's reputation in the desalination industry.
instances.key_quote: within a year the bearing was falling apart okay that because these little inserts held the screws that held the bearing together and the company basically it ended up destroying the company's reputation in the desalination business
instances.framework_lesson: Inconsistent material specifications across components in a single assembly can cause localized corrosion failures even when primary materials are correctly specified.
instances.source_module: Codes and Standards Definitions and History

cluster_id: Woods Hole house spray foam fire
display_name: Woods Hole house spray foam fire

instances.name: Woods Hole house spray foam fire
instances.summary: A house in Woods Hole caught fire after spray polyurethane foam insulation was installed incorrectly in the eaves, causing overheating and ignition due to inadequate oxygen access and thermal runaway.
instances.key_quote: this guy actually woods hole attic as he was rebuilding his head down his house that year last year burned down because of when they're installed this is the fire
instances.framework_lesson: Spray foam insulation standards and installation instructions can be inadequate; thermal runaway occurs when foam is installed with improper ventilation, and standards may not account for installation error scenarios.
instances.source_module: Codes and Standards Definitions and History

cluster_id: Concorde supersonic transport operations and economics
display_name: Concorde supersonic transport operations and economics

instances.name: Concorde supersonic transport operations and economics
instances.summary: The Concorde supersonic aircraft operated uneconomically despite its technical capability, as it could only fly supersonic over water and faced noise complaints, making routes between major cities unfeasible.
instances.key_quote: there are only certain routes they made it uneconomical to new york between basically and so you can take root muta and things people complaining about breaking the solution
instances.source_module: Codes and Standards Definitions and History

cluster_id: Alberta refinery pipe hydrostatic test failure
display_name: Alberta refinery pipe hydrostatic test failure

instances.name: Alberta refinery pipe hydrostatic test failure
instances.summary: A newly fabricated refinery pipeline system failed during hydrostatic testing after welding, developing a crack that caused a major contractual and financial dispute between the builder and pipe supplier.
instances.key_quote: when they're doing a hydra test the thing after they've welded every water in and they pressurize so a performance crack in the and they condemned based on this okay we want our six million dollars back
instances.framework_lesson: The dispute arose because people do not know how to read a standards statement in plain English, creating disagreement over whether the crack met condemnation criteria under the applicable standard.
instances.source_module: Codes and Standards Definitions and History

cluster_id: Paper slitter knife company evaluation
display_name: Paper slitter knife company evaluation

instances.name: Paper slitter knife company evaluation
instances.summary: Eagar evaluated a small Canadian company (ca. 1984) that had developed a new brazing and heat-treating process for paper-cutting knives, achieving 6× tool life, but the improvement threatened to collapse a $25M annual market.
instances.key_quote: you can take a $25 million business and turn it into a $3 million business or a $4 million business because it has four times to life
instances.framework_lesson: Extending consumable tool life without growing market demand reduces total revenue; durability improvements in commodity products require identification of new applications to justify the investment.

cluster_id: Diamond tool wear in steel machining
display_name: Diamond tool wear in steel machining

instances.name: Diamond tool wear in steel machining
instances.summary: Diamond cutting tools, despite being the hardest material, failed prematurely when machining steel because carbon diffused from the diamond into the steel at cutting temperatures around 1,000°C.
instances.key_quote: at 1000 C carbon starts the diamond starts diffusing into the steel so what they use is cubic boron nitride
instances.framework_lesson: Material hardness alone does not predict cutting tool performance; chemical wear mechanisms (carbon dissolution) can dominate over abrasive wear, requiring selection of chemically inert tools like cubic boron nitride for steel.

cluster_id: Silicon carbide cutting tool development for road surface wear
display_name: Silicon carbide cutting tool development for road surface wear

instances.name: Silicon carbide cutting tool development for road surface wear
instances.summary: Improved centered silicon carbide cutting tools extended tip life from one hour to one full night shift, dramatically reducing tool-change downtime that had caused significant worker idle time.
instances.key_quote: that was a big problem because now look at the productivity you're losing all those workmen standing around while some mechanics changing the tools out
instances.framework_lesson: Material performance improvements must be evaluated against maintenance-cycle constraints; once a tool lasts one full shift, further improvements face market barriers because maintenance windows already exist.

cluster_id: Technetium lab data credibility case
display_name: Technetium lab data credibility case

instances.name: Technetium lab data credibility case
instances.summary: An SEM/EDS analysis of nails from the Kentucky school fire reported 133% technetium content, demonstrating the need to question suspicious analytical data.
instances.key_quote: they did a sem EDS analysis and it found 133% technum and I said oh we can solve this case... this is where again you got to question the data right
instances.framework_lesson: Expert analysis must critically evaluate implausible results; impossibly high elemental percentages should trigger immediate skepticism about data quality.

cluster_id: Leech and Garner consulting retainer
display_name: Leech and Garner consulting retainer

instances.name: Leech and Garner consulting retainer
instances.summary: Tom Eagar took a $5,000 annual consulting retainer from Leech and Garner company in Attleboro, Massachusetts in 1978, which enabled him to purchase his first house.
instances.key_quote: I had gotten a consulting job with a company down here in Attleboro Massachusetts called leech and Garner and leech and Garner uh they put me on a $5,000 year retainer

cluster_id: Attleboro brass-rat jewelry company ring theft via river
display_name: Attleboro brass-rat jewelry company ring theft via river

instances.name: Attleboro brass-rat jewelry company ring theft via river
instances.summary: A jewelry manufacturer in Attleboro discovered missing rings and found that employees were throwing them out windows for retrieval from a stream running through town; the company purchased mineral rights to police the river.
instances.key_quote: they were missing whole rings and they found out that there's a river runs right through the middle a little stream runs right through the middle of Attleboro people were throwing rings out the windows and then on weekends they go die for gold in the river

cluster_id: Attleboro jewelry plant toilet smuggling
display_name: Attleboro jewelry plant toilet smuggling

instances.name: Attleboro jewelry plant toilet smuggling
instances.summary: At an Attleboro jewelry manufacturer, a female employee who was fired continued to help steal rings by flushing them down the toilet despite company efforts to account for all gold inventory.
instances.key_quote: they fired one of these guys they caught him and they were still missing some rings and they could not figure out what it was turns out he had a girlfriend and she worked in the same plant and just despite the company she was flushing them down the toilet

cluster_id: Leech and Garner floor drain gold recovery
display_name: Leech and Garner floor drain gold recovery

instances.name: Leech and Garner floor drain gold recovery
instances.summary: A Leech and Garner engineer discovered that gold was flowing into the casting shop floor drain unfiltered; when he installed a filter, the first week yielded $110,000 of gold, and the company subsequently had to excavate decades of accumulated gold from the drain.
instances.key_quote: he decided to go in and put a filter in the grain drain he took $110,000 of gold out in the first week it was just going down the drain

cluster_id: Plasma torch discovery by Bob Gage
display_name: Plasma torch discovery by Bob Gage

instances.name: Plasma torch discovery by Bob Gage
instances.summary: Bob Gage at Union Carbide's Indianapolis labs in the 1950s serendipitously discovered plasma arc processing by drilling a hole in a water-cooled copper plate used for studying gas tungsten arcs, observing the plasma flame shoot through the hole.
