cluster_id: Central Artery tunnel project (Boston)
display_name: Central Artery tunnel project (Boston)

instances.name: Central Artery tunnel project (Boston)
instances.summary: A major Boston infrastructure tunnel project had its budget increase from an original $2 billion estimate to $17 billion, and was designed by one of Eagar's MIT classmates who proposed the depression of the central artery.
instances.key_quote: before they built the central artery the big tunnel here in boston they cost 17 billion dollars original estimate of 2 billion it's done by one of my classmates an mit engineer

cluster_id: Spot welding excessive current test
display_name: Spot welding excessive current test

instances.name: Spot welding excessive current test
instances.summary: A spot welding experiment at high current (2000 amps) resulted in excessive plasma jet pressure that sprayed molten metal across the room due to the force relationship varying with the square of current.
instances.key_quote: the story on friday of the spot welding where they tried to go to two thousand amps if i square to two thousand as opposed to squaring two 200 i got tremendous jet pressures and it just sprayed molten metal all over the room

cluster_id: High-amperage spot welding metal spray incident
display_name: High-amperage spot welding metal spray incident

instances.name: High-amperage spot welding metal spray incident
instances.summary: An attempt to increase spot welding current to 2000 amperes resulted in excessive plasma jet pressure that sprayed molten metal uncontrollably across the room.
instances.key_quote: if i square to two thousand as opposed to squaring two 200 i got tremendous jet pressures and it just sprayed molten metal all over the room

cluster_id: Titanium submarine welding transfer instability
display_name: Titanium submarine welding transfer instability

instances.name: Titanium submarine welding transfer instability
instances.summary: Gas metal arc welding of titanium produced unstable metal transfer due to stationary cathode spots on the titanium surface that created upward-directed plasma jets interfering with droplet deposition.
instances.key_quote: in titanium you get very stable plasma jets coming off the surface we've done high speed movies of them and you just blow the drops away

cluster_id: USS Patrick Henry hull degradation
display_name: USS Patrick Henry hull degradation

instances.name: USS Patrick Henry hull degradation
instances.summary: The attack submarine USS Patrick Henry accumulated dozens of holes and cutouts in its hull over 30 years of service requiring continuous repairs, demonstrating the maintenance burden of submarine hulls.
instances.key_quote: they showed the patrick henry which had just finished its life and it was about to be blown up off the coast of virginia and sunken to the bottom of the atlantic and showed a picture of all the cutouts in the hull that it had in the previous 30 years and it looked like a patchwork quilt
instances.framework_lesson: Composite submarine designers failed to account for real submarine maintenance requirements—there is no internal space to move equipment through large hatch doors, only tight cutouts where specific components are located.

cluster_id: Composite submarine repairability failure
display_name: Composite submarine repairability failure

instances.name: Composite submarine repairability failure
instances.summary: Aerospace composite engineers proposed composite submarine designs with large hatch doors for equipment removal, unaware that composite materials cannot be cut and repaired like metal hulls without destroying structural integrity.
instances.key_quote: the composite guys came in and they had certain great big hatch doors in their composite design and i raised my hand and said have you ever seen a nuclear submarine
instances.framework_lesson: Composite aerospace engineers lacked domain knowledge of submarine architecture and maintenance practices; composite materials' inability to be locally repaired made them unsuitable for submarine hulls despite initial DARPA advocacy.

cluster_id: Arc blow magnetization in shipyard welding
display_name: Arc blow magnetization in shipyard welding

instances.name: Arc blow magnetization in shipyard welding
instances.summary: Welders were unable to produce sound welds in large steel structures due to uncontrolled magnetic arc blow caused by magnetized steel and improper grounding configurations, requiring field demagnetization before welding could proceed.
instances.key_quote: there have been plenty of cases where people cannot welders cannot make a good weld period unless you go in and de-magnetize the steel
instances.framework_lesson: Grounding position and uncontrolled magnetic fields from the welding circuit itself can render skilled welders unable to control arc behavior; this phenomenon is poorly documented in welding handbooks despite its practical prevalence.

cluster_id: Daniel Rosenthal's escape from Nazi Germany and development of Rosenthal equation
display_name: Daniel Rosenthal's escape from Nazi Germany and development of Rosenthal equation

instances.name: Daniel Rosenthal's escape from Nazi Germany and development of Rosenthal equation
instances.summary: Jewish mathematician Daniel Rosenthal fled Germany through the Netherlands and Morocco, arriving at MIT by 1940 where he developed the traveling heat source solution for welding known as the Rosenthal equation.
instances.key_quote: he was jewish i don't remember if he's originally from switzerland or he did go through the netherlands in his flight from germany in 1939 and he ended up in morocco for a year and by 1940 he was here

cluster_id: Niels Christensen's hospitalization during welding research
display_name: Niels Christensen's hospitalization during welding research

instances.name: Niels Christensen's hospitalization during welding research
instances.summary: Norwegian researcher Niels Christensen was laid up in a hospital bed for a year during his work on welding flux research and extension of the Rosenthal formula.
instances.key_quote: there's guy niels christensen from norway who came along and professor christensen basically extended the rosenthal formula to i don't know if i can find it here we go to dimensionless form so here's nils christensen distribution of temperatures in arc welding he was at the royal institute in trondheim norway at the time he had some accident was laid up in a hospital bed for a year

cluster_id: Annealed glass door fatality
display_name: Annealed glass door fatality

instances.name: Annealed glass door fatality
instances.summary: People were killed when walking through annealed glass doors that shattered into long sharp shards because they did not realize they were walking through glass.
instances.key_quote: it breaks it shatters and it ends up with these long sharp things that have killed people when they walk through they don't realize they're walking through a glass door
instances.framework_lesson: Safety codes were changed to require either tempered or laminated glass in doors after fatalities from uncontrolled shattering of annealed glass.

cluster_id: Four-inch and eight-inch IED escalation in Iraq
display_name: Four-inch and eight-inch IED escalation in Iraq

instances.name: Four-inch and eight-inch IED escalation in Iraq
instances.summary: Insurgents in Iraq escalated from 4-inch diameter steel-pipe IEDs with shaped charges (200 kilojoules) to 8-inch versions (1.6 megajoules) that could destroy Humvees completely, forcing emergency development of armored vehicles.
instances.key_quote: they went to eight inches and eight inches goes as the square so it had four times the energy... they went to 1.6 Mega joules that went below a Humvee into smithereens

cluster_id: Brooklyn Navy Yard shaped-charge discovery
display_name: Brooklyn Navy Yard shaped-charge discovery

instances.name: Brooklyn Navy Yard shaped-charge discovery
instances.summary: U.S. Navy discovered the shaped-charge principle accidentally in the 1880s at Brooklyn Navy Yard during explosives work, finding that a copper dish focused by explosive energy could cut through steel.
instances.key_quote: The U.S Navy discovered this in the 1880s at the Brooklyn Navy Yard why mistake while they were just doing some explosives work and they found they could impress um the or they could cut through just say we'll cut through steel

cluster_id: Three-tank RPG penetration test (1985)
display_name: Three-tank RPG penetration test (1985)

instances.name: Three-tank RPG penetration test (1985)
instances.summary: Army conducted a battlefield test in which a single RPG penetrated through six layers of armor across three stacked tanks, demonstrating the inadequacy of existing tank armor and prompting crash development of new armor types within a year.
instances.key_quote: they lined up three tanks on a Battlefield probably down at Aberdeen and they shot an RPG and it went through six layers of armor through you know both sides of all three tanks

cluster_id: 1985 RPG armor penetration demonstration
display_name: 1985 RPG armor penetration demonstration

instances.name: 1985 RPG armor penetration demonstration
instances.summary: Army doctoral student at Watertown Arsenal witnessed three tanks lined up at Aberdeen Proving Ground being penetrated by a single RPG round through all three vehicles, shocking military leadership into emergency armor development.
instances.key_quote: they lined up three tanks on a Battlefield probably down at Aberdeen and they shot an RPG and it went through six layers of armor through both sides of all three tanks
instances.framework_lesson: Existing armor specifications provided no protection against emerging RPG technology; within two years composite layered armor with ceramic barriers were developed to defeat the threat.

cluster_id: 1984-1986 composite armor development cycle
display_name: 1984-1986 composite armor development cycle

instances.name: 1984-1986 composite armor development cycle
instances.summary: Military armor and anti-armor technology evolved in rapid succession: in 1984 no armor could stop RPGs; by 1986 new composite armor with ceramic layers defeated all existing penetrators, demonstrating an iterative materials-design arms race.
instances.key_quote: in like 1984 there was no armor that could not be penetrated by 1986 there was no propellant that could penetrate the modern armor but the modern armor was a composite

cluster_id: Corning float glass dual-surface manufacturing
display_name: Corning float glass dual-surface manufacturing

instances.name: Corning float glass dual-surface manufacturing
instances.summary: Corning developed a proprietary welding process to join two sheets of float glass so that both external surfaces are the superior air-side surface, while burying the defective tin-bath side in the interior, enabling thin high-strength transparent screens.
instances.key_quote: they basically take two sheets of float glass and they weld them together so the outside surface on both sides is float glass and the bad surfaces the tin surfaces are buried
instances.framework_lesson: Screen glass specifications require both surface flatness and moisture resistance; the tin-bath side of float glass fails both, necessitating a capital-intensive ($250k) welding solution.

cluster_id: Corian countertop thermal cracking
display_name: Corian countertop thermal cracking

instances.name: Corian countertop thermal cracking
instances.summary: A Dupont Corian polymer countertop cracked due to thermal stress when a hot frying pan was placed on it without thermal protection; cooling from the heated state induced tensile stress that caused fracture.
instances.key_quote: by thermal expansion it was in compression but when it cooled back down we got cracks okay so it turns out Dupont came out with um an improved Corian about three years later
instances.framework_lesson: Material selection for kitchen countertops must account for thermal cycling and residual stress states; early Corian formulation was inadequate for typical kitchen use conditions.

cluster_id: Williams Tower elevator marble finishes
display_name: Williams Tower elevator marble finishes

instances.name: Williams Tower elevator marble finishes
instances.summary: The 70-story Williams Tower near Houston features 12 different elevators clad with 12 different marble types from Europe; the anecdote illustrates marble's aesthetic appeal but also its vulnerability to damage through sealed protective coatings.
instances.key_quote: one of my favorite set of elevators in the country is the Williams Tower in uh just west of Houston it's the tallest building on the Beltway around Houston at the nine o'clock position

cluster_id: Alcoa monopoly and price fixing with Dow Chemical
display_name: Alcoa monopoly and price fixing with Dow Chemical

instances.name: Alcoa monopoly and price fixing with Dow Chemical
instances.summary: Alcoa and Dow Chemical maintained a documented price-fixing cartel from 1945 to at least 1975, with Alcoa controlling 100% of bauxite supply worldwide and agreeing to stay out of magnesium if Dow stayed out of aluminum.
instances.key_quote: Alcoa and Dao had to have fixed prices after World War II and Alcoa said we won't go into the Magnesium business if you stayed out of the aluminum business

cluster_id: Farberware cooking pot thermal warping
display_name: Farberware cooking pot thermal warping

instances.name: Farberware cooking pot thermal warping
instances.summary: A multi-layer cookware pot with aluminum base bonded to stainless steel pan would warp severely if the bond were 100% complete due to thermal expansion mismatch during heating to 600-700°C.
instances.key_quote: if it was 100% the difference between aluminum and steel when you go up 6 or 700° would cause warping of that pan
instances.framework_lesson: Dissimilar metal joints require deliberately incomplete bonding to accommodate differential thermal expansion; 100% bonding causes failure in thermal cycling.

cluster_id: Ceramic coating on turbine blades thermal stress
display_name: Ceramic coating on turbine blades thermal stress

instances.name: Ceramic coating on turbine blades thermal stress
instances.summary: Gas turbine blades coated with zirconia ceramic experience severe thermal gradients (500°C temperature differential between hot gas-exposed center and cooler stator-attached base) that risk ceramic spalling and flaking.
instances.key_quote: you got a big hot spot in the middle and then you got cool spots down here how do you keep the ceramic from just forming a dried riverbed pattern and flaking off
instances.framework_lesson: Material selection for thermal barrier coatings must match thermal expansion coefficients; zirconia was chosen over alumina specifically because its expansion coefficient closely matches superalloy substrates.

cluster_id: Motorola Defense Division classified large-format chip bonding
display_name: Motorola Defense Division classified large-format chip bonding

instances.name: Motorola Defense Division classified large-format chip bonding
instances.summary: Motorola's defense division fabricated 1–2 inch silicon chips bonded to substrates for classified military applications in the mid-1990s, pushing beyond normal thermal and strain limits.
instances.key_quote: back in the mid 90s the defense department didn't make chips that were 2 in or an inch on a side or 2 Ines on a side but this was all super classified military stuff

cluster_id: Rocky Flats activated diffusion bonding of maraging steel
display_name: Rocky Flats activated diffusion bonding of maraging steel

instances.name: Rocky Flats activated diffusion bonding of maraging steel
instances.summary: Rocky Flats nuclear weapons facility (O'Brien, Rice, and Olson) developed activated diffusion bonding of high-strength maraging steel (200 KSI) by depositing silver interlayers to avoid property degradation at elevated bonding temperatures.
instances.key_quote: at Rocky Flats where they used to do things to make nuclear warheads and they had to do things that were sort of offbeat and they could afford to do things no one else could afford to do
instances.framework_lesson: Standard diffusion bonding temperatures destroy the martensitic microstructure of high-strength steels; activated diffusion bonding via soft metal interlayers enables bonding below the property-degradation temperature.

cluster_id: 3D-printed solder droplet circuit boards research
display_name: 3D-printed solder droplet circuit boards research

instances.name: 3D-printed solder droplet circuit boards research
instances.summary: A 20-year research effort funded by Eagar to develop 3D printing of circuit boards using liquid metal solder droplets, involving MIT mechanical engineering professor Sonin, which ultimately remained unfinished despite hundreds of millions in industry spending.
instances.key_quote: tens and hundreds of millions of dollars have been spent trying to do this with liquid metals and it just it has not yet been perfected after 20 years
instances.framework_lesson: Young's equation, the standard equilibrium model for wetting, is oversimplified and does not apply to non-equilibrium transient droplet conditions; applying it to dynamic 3D printing scenarios produces indeterminate mathematical problems.

cluster_id: Lead-tin solder obsolescence and lead-free transition
display_name: Lead-tin solder obsolescence and lead-free transition

instances.name: Lead-tin solder obsolescence and lead-free transition
instances.summary: Industry spent hundreds of millions of dollars over 15 years attempting to replace lead-tin solder with lead-free alternatives for environmental reasons, but no replacement has matched lead-tin's wetting speed and performance.
instances.key_quote: nothing wets as well or as fast as lead tin solder no one has found been able to develop anything and hundreds of millions of dollars have been spent by industry and government
instances.framework_lesson: Environmental regulations forced material substitution before equivalent performance alternatives were engineered, leaving the industry dependent on an inferior material.

cluster_id: 1960s soldering manual surface oxidation study
display_name: 1960s soldering manual surface oxidation study

instances.name: 1960s soldering manual surface oxidation study
instances.summary: A soldering manual from the 1960s demonstrated that copper surfaces oxidize rapidly; a freshly cleaned surface wets in half a second, but after 24 hours in steam oxidation increases wetting time to over 10 seconds.
instances.key_quote: if you let it 24 hours in Steam unplated no coating fresh copper boy you go from half a second to more than 10 seconds to get the basic solder to take off that copper oxide tarnish
instances.framework_lesson: Military specifications requiring soldering within one hour of cleaning were established based on this empirical data showing that surface oxidation severely degrades wetting performance.

cluster_id: VAX 9000 bonding failure
display_name: VAX 9000 bonding failure

instances.name: VAX 9000 bonding failure
instances.summary: Digital Equipment Corporation's VAX 9000 computer project suffered near-zero yield due to poor bond quality in tape-automated bonding, caused by a 3-4 week delay between inner and outer lead bonding in Arizona, violating the one-hour cleanliness rule.
instances.key_quote: well I told you cleanliness is Next to Godliness and you should Bond within an hour 3 or 4 weeks is more than an hour okay they were having terrible problems with Bond quality
instances.framework_lesson: Manufacturing specifications that mandate bonding within one hour of cleaning were violated by multi-week delays, demonstrating the critical importance of process timing in solid-state bonding.

cluster_id: Digital Equipment Corporation bankruptcy
display_name: Digital Equipment Corporation bankruptcy

instances.name: Digital Equipment Corporation bankruptcy
instances.summary: Digital Equipment Corporation went bankrupt by the mid-1990s primarily due to the failed VAX 9000 mainframe project and strategic missteps, including rejection of RISC architecture as a business direction.
instances.key_quote: they essentially killed digital equipment they went bankrupt by the mid99s because of that vax 9000 project primarily and a few other poor choices

cluster_id: US Navy Charpy impact specification evolution
display_name: US Navy Charpy impact specification evolution

instances.name: US Navy Charpy impact specification evolution
instances.summary: The US Navy originally specified 15 foot-pounds of Charpy impact energy but experienced failures; the Coast Guard later increased the requirement to 20 foot-pounds as a safety factor.
instances.key_quote: they made it 15 foot pounds and by 1960 the Coast Guard had decided well we should put a bigger safety factor than just 15 foot pounds will make it 20 foot pounds
instances.framework_lesson: Initial safety specifications were inadequate to prevent service failures; regulatory agencies had to increase toughness requirements retroactively.

cluster_id: LNG tanker steel development for cryogenic service
display_name: LNG tanker steel development for cryogenic service

instances.name: LNG tanker steel development for cryogenic service
instances.summary: A steel company developed a welded steel for LNG tanker hulls to support large aluminum spheres; the steel had to maintain toughness at minus 60 degrees Fahrenheit despite the ductile-brittle transition of steel.
instances.key_quote: they were building LNG tankers you know these great big tankers steel ships that have great big aluminum spheres and in order to hold that sphere you have to have a cylinder that you put the sphere on and that had have toughness at minus 60 degrees Fahrenheit
instances.framework_lesson: Low-temperature service requires steels designed to avoid brittle fracture; fine-grain microstructure is the primary solution for achieving both strength and toughness.

cluster_id: Ice fishers dynamite accident
display_name: Ice fishers dynamite accident

instances.name: Ice fishers dynamite accident
instances.summary: Ice fishers in Minnesota or Wisconsin attempted to break holes in ice using dynamite; a dog retrieved one of the sticks and ran back toward the pickup truck, causing the explosives to detonate near the vehicle.
instances.key_quote: they threw the stick of dynamite out there to blow a hole in the ice and the dog went and picked up the state went running after the stick of dynamite he was used to playing catch with sticks right so he comes running back for the dynamite these guys run and the dog doesn't know what to do so he gets underneath the pickup truck and he in the pickup truck go blowing up

cluster_id: Prudhoe Bay offshore ice islands
display_name: Prudhoe Bay offshore ice islands

instances.name: Prudhoe Bay offshore ice islands
instances.summary: Civil engineers, including MIT-affiliated designers, engineered man-made islands for the Prudhoe Bay, Alaska oil fields in water 100 feet deep; the islands exploited natural ice formation with sloped rock walls to allow expanding ice to build up above sea level.
instances.key_quote: they started building the oil fields in Prudhoe Bay in Alaska and essentially they built man made islands to go out there into the ocean 100 feet deep they built low islands and in fact those islands were designed by a group of civil engineers who used to be here at MIT

cluster_id: Dynamite ice-fishing accident
display_name: Dynamite ice-fishing accident

instances.name: Dynamite ice-fishing accident
instances.summary: A person attempted to drill a hole in ice using dynamite instead of a drill, resulting in the person and pickup truck being blown up.
instances.key_quote: pickup truck and he in the pickup truck go blowing up and so better not drink too much bill beer just go ahead and drill your hole in the ice don't try to blow it with dynamite

cluster_id: Arizona forklift container failure
display_name: Arizona forklift container failure

instances.name: Arizona forklift container failure
instances.summary: A forklift container holding 50 tons of pipe broke during lifting, causing millions of dollars in damage; metallurgical analysis showed ductile bending before fracture, indicating overload rather than material defect.
instances.year_or_era: approximately one year before Spring 2016 lecture (2015)
instances.key_quote: I looked at the photos and the steel bent before it broke so the steel is ductile I knew right there it was an overload failure

cluster_id: Unnamed shaft fatigue failure
display_name: Unnamed shaft fatigue failure

instances.name: Unnamed shaft fatigue failure
instances.summary: A shaft with a known defect was shipped despite being rejected; a fatigue crack grew from the defect over five years and the shaft broke, causing loss of power in a critical application.
instances.key_quote: they shipped the shaft with a defect and five years later the fatigue crack grew from the defect and the shaft broke into and they lost all power in a critical application
instances.framework_lesson: Engineers failed to apply known fracture mechanics analysis to predict fatigue crack growth from an identified defect, resulting in catastrophic failure.

cluster_id: Wright brothers' aircraft engine
display_name: Wright brothers' aircraft engine

instances.name: Wright brothers' aircraft engine
instances.summary: The Wright brothers used precipitation-hardened aluminum-copper alloy in their engine at Kitty Hawk, which remains in the Smithsonian today as an example of early advanced materials engineering.
instances.key_quote: the wri brothers use nanotechnology in their engine okay at Kittyhawk it was an aluminum copper alloy that had been precipitation hardened

cluster_id: General Electric Appliance Park manufacturing
display_name: General Electric Appliance Park manufacturing

instances.name: General Electric Appliance Park manufacturing
instances.summary: General Electric's Appliance Park negotiates bulk steel purchases (washing machines and refrigerators) at commodity carbon steel prices.
instances.key_quote: I'm General Electric Appliance Park where they make washing machines and refrigerators or I'm General Motors they're going to negotiate prices

cluster_id: Hot shortness from iron sulfides (pre-1880s forging)
display_name: Hot shortness from iron sulfides (pre-1880s forging)

instances.name: Hot shortness from iron sulfides (pre-1880s forging)
instances.summary: Steel containing high sulfur formed liquid iron sulfides on grain boundaries during hot working, causing the material to crumble during rolling or forging until manganese was added to tie up the sulfur.
instances.key_quote: if you have very much silicon or very much sulfur you got a real problem because the iron sulfur diagram has a really deep eutectic below the hot working temperature where you're going to forge that steel and so you'd form liquid iron sulfides on the grain boundaries and they call it hot shortness or hot tearing
instances.framework_lesson: Early steelmakers did not understand phase diagrams or the thermodynamic basis for hot shortness; adding manganese empirically solved the problem before the metallurgical theory was fully understood.

cluster_id: Brooklyn-to-Japan shipping crack failure
display_name: Brooklyn-to-Japan shipping crack failure

instances.name: Brooklyn-to-Japan shipping crack failure
instances.summary: Welded steel components shipped from Brooklyn to Japan developed delayed hydrogen cracks during transit that were not visible until arrival, traced to martensite formation from deformation in TRIP steel.
instances.key_quote: they'd ship them out of brooklyn and by the time they got to japan they had cracks so they brought these actually some of the cracks have extended over time

cluster_id: Idaho National Engineering Lab nuclear reactor component cracking
display_name: Idaho National Engineering Lab nuclear reactor component cracking

instances.name: Idaho National Engineering Lab nuclear reactor component cracking
instances.summary: A carbon steel nuclear reactor component preheated to 150°F on Friday cooled completely over the weekend, eliminating the preheat benefit and causing hydrogen cracking when welding resumed Monday morning.
instances.key_quote: we did that on friday oh okay and it cooled down over the weekend so there was no effect of preheat
instances.framework_lesson: Preheat specifications must account for cooling during idle time; a Friday preheat provides no metallurgical benefit if the component cools to ambient temperature before welding resumes.

cluster_id: Japanese HSLA shipbuilding steel development
display_name: Japanese HSLA shipbuilding steel development

instances.name: Japanese HSLA shipbuilding steel development
instances.summary: Japanese shipbuilders developed high-strength low-alloy (HSLA) commercial steels for shipbuilding that required no preheat, even for one-inch-thick plate, saving millions in fabrication costs compared to traditional alloy steels.
instances.key_quote: the japanese were making commercial steels for shipbuilding at the time that required no preheat so they might have one inch thick plate that in the old days with an alloy steel you had to preheat to 200 degrees now they didn't have to preheat it at all
instances.framework_lesson: Material metallurgy innovation can eliminate preheat requirements entirely, reducing production costs dramatically without sacrificing structural integrity.

cluster_id: Electric Boat HY80 weld forensics
display_name: Electric Boat HY80 weld forensics

instances.name: Electric Boat HY80 weld forensics
instances.summary: Examination of an actual one-inch thick HY80 weld specimen from Electric Boat showing heat-affected zone structure and backing bar design in a highly restrained joint.
instances.key_quote: this is an actual weld in one inch thick hy 80 made by electric boat back up about 30 or 35 years ago this is an actual weld in one inch thick hy 80 made by electric boat

cluster_id: General Motors carpet adhesive failure in assembly plant
display_name: General Motors carpet adhesive failure in assembly plant

instances.name: General Motors carpet adhesive failure in assembly plant
instances.summary: Carpeting adhesive failed repeatedly in a GM assembly plant; a shop steward with a philosophy degree identified that a nearby water-jet cutter robot was creating contaminating mist, violating the cleanliness principle required for adhesive bonding.
instances.key_quote: we're gluing down the carpeting on the cars okay on the inside of the cars and we've had consultants coming in here and it just it wrinkles the adhesives are not working
instances.framework_lesson: Understanding fundamental principles like cleanliness in adhesive bonding can solve manufacturing failures that specialized consultants miss.

cluster_id: Carpeting adhesion failure at unspecified facility
display_name: Carpeting adhesion failure at unspecified facility

instances.name: Carpeting adhesion failure at unspecified facility
instances.summary: Carpeting repeatedly failed to stay down due to inadequate adhesive performance caused by dust and water contamination from poor ventilation.
instances.key_quote: particles and water everywhere... he got some screens and convinced them to have some ventilation to pull all that stuff aside and all of a sudden the carpeting started staying down
instances.framework_lesson: Basic principle of adhesive bonding—cleanliness—was being overlooked; proper ventilation and particle control were the solution rather than material substitution.

cluster_id: Tank explosion at Pennzoil Oil City refinery
display_name: Tank explosion at Pennzoil Oil City refinery

instances.name: Tank explosion at Pennzoil Oil City refinery
instances.summary: A woman welder was killed and thrown across a river when a corroded riveted steel tank from the 1920s exploded during welding at an old Pennsylvania refinery.
instances.key_quote: an old riveted steel tank from the 1920s that had blown up and it was corroded around the bottom
instances.framework_lesson: Aging infrastructure with decades of cumulative corrosion and environmental contamination created hazardous conditions; economic incentives (cleanup liability) kept unsafe facilities operating.

cluster_id: Pennzoil plant environmental regulation case
display_name: Pennzoil plant environmental regulation case

instances.name: Pennzoil plant environmental regulation case
instances.summary: A manufacturing plant was kept open despite making no economic sense because of environmental regulations, illustrating how regulatory externalities override pure cost optimization.
instances.key_quote: i told you about the pennzoil plant and why they kept it open even though it made no economic sense made a lot of environmental sense made no economically okay but because of some regulation
instances.framework_lesson: Environmental regulations as externalities force engineering decisions that pure economic optimization would reject.

cluster_id: Soviet oil pipelines in Siberia
display_name: Soviet oil pipelines in Siberia

instances.name: Soviet oil pipelines in Siberia
instances.summary: Oil pipelines in Siberia were gushing leaks in the early 1990s, left unfixed because the post-Soviet population prioritized food and survival over environmental remediation.
instances.key_quote: you've got oil pipelines in siberia they're just gushing out oil in the leaks why don't you fix them and he said well in 1985 when we first had glasnost and people in the soviet union became very interested in the environment but today six or seven years later they're really just interested in eating
instances.framework_lesson: Engineering priorities and environmental standards depend on population wealth and Maslow's hierarchy—subsistence needs override environmental concerns.

cluster_id: U.S. iron ore depletion—Mesabi Range
display_name: U.S. iron ore depletion—Mesabi Range

instances.name: U.S. iron ore depletion—Mesabi Range
instances.summary: The United States exhausted its cheap iron ore reserves in the Mesabi Range of Minnesota during World War II through intensive mining, losing a cost advantage in steelmaking.
instances.key_quote: the united states used to have them they called it wasabi range up in minnesota but we world war ii we basically you know dug it all out

cluster_id: Pittsburgh air pollution and steel mills
display_name: Pittsburgh air pollution and steel mills

instances.name: Pittsburgh air pollution and steel mills
instances.summary: Steel mills in Pittsburgh produced severe air pollution in the early 20th century, making it impossible to wear white clothing outdoors due to soot and smog.
instances.key_quote: people in pittsburgh couldn't wear a white shirt for a whole day if they went outside the air was so bad in pittsburgh
instances.framework_lesson: The environmental crisis in Pittsburgh's steel industry directly prompted the Environmental Protection Act of 1972, establishing regulatory frameworks for industrial emissions.

cluster_id: Blood diamonds civil war
display_name: Blood diamonds civil war

instances.name: Blood diamonds civil war
instances.summary: During a civil war in Angola, separatists took control of diamond mines, creating conflict diamonds used to fund armed conflict.
instances.key_quote: they're having a civil war in angola and some of the separatists were basically taking over the diamond mines

cluster_id: Venezuelan oil boiler failure
display_name: Venezuelan oil boiler failure

instances.name: Venezuelan oil boiler failure
instances.summary: Stainless steel boilers fired with high-vanadium Venezuelan crude oil experienced rapid failure when vanadium pentoxide formed a low-melting eutectic that destroyed the chromium oxide protective scale.
instances.key_quote: the protective chromium oxide scale on the stainless steel when they fired their boilers with this oil containing vanadium just melted away the chromium oxide protective scale for high temperatures and the whole thing just destroyed overnight
instances.framework_lesson: Industry specifications (API) were developed retroactively to limit vanadium content in crude oils after field failures demonstrated the corrosion mechanism.

cluster_id: Tar sands vanadium pentoxide furnace problem
display_name: Tar sands vanadium pentoxide furnace problem

instances.name: Tar sands vanadium pentoxide furnace problem
instances.summary: A furnace used to remove vanadium from crude oil in Alberta tar sands had to be constructed from exotic high-temperature materials because standard alloys corroded rapidly under vanadium pentoxide attack at 2000°C.
instances.key_quote: there's only two materials you can use that won't be corroded by vanadium pentoxide and one is some ceramic which is of course very brittle you have to run this furnace to get rid of the vanadium in like 2000 degrees centigrade
materials_or_systems: titanium alloy or aluminum-titanium alloy internals

cluster_id: Glass furnace reversing door failure
display_name: Glass furnace reversing door failure

instances.name: Glass furnace reversing door failure
instances.summary: A glass factory's regenerative furnace suffered production loss when a reversing door valve mechanism became stuck and could not close, causing uncontrolled overheating of the refractory brickwork.
instances.key_quote: one of them got hung up they couldn't close it and they just started overheating the brickwork they couldn't close it they had to close it within five or six hours couldn't do it had to shut the whole thing down and it takes about three four weeks to start it back up

cluster_id: Steel mill valve failure and furnace shutdown
display_name: Steel mill valve failure and furnace shutdown

instances.name: Steel mill valve failure and furnace shutdown
instances.summary: A worn-out valve in a steel furnace got stuck and could not be closed, forcing the mill to shut down for three to four weeks and causing significant production losses.
instances.key_quote: one of them got hung up they couldn't close it and they just started overheating the brickwork they couldn't close it they had to close it within five or six hours

cluster_id: Austrian BOF development post-WWII
display_name: Austrian BOF development post-WWII

cluster_id: Water injection hazard in BOF copper lances
display_name: Water injection hazard in BOF copper lances

cluster_id: Copper checks from continuous caster startup

cluster_id: Boeing door product center cost accounting failure
display_name: Boeing door product center cost accounting failure

instances.name: Boeing door product center cost accounting failure
instances.summary: Boeing's aircraft door manufacturing center operated as a cost center with no actual price per unit, obscuring that doors cost approximately $50,000 each while management had no awareness of true manufacturing costs.

cluster_id: 3/8 inch rebar rolling mill productivity case
display_name: 3/8 inch rebar rolling mill productivity case

instances.name: 3/8 inch rebar rolling mill productivity case
instances.summary: A steel rolling mill company increased output of 3/8 inch rebar by splitting the hot steel strand into multiple strands and slowing the mill speed, achieving 4x output with 2x productivity while gaining market dominance.

cluster_id: Jet engine test pilot casualties (WWII-early jet era)
display_name: Jet engine test pilot casualties (WWII-early jet era)

instances.key_quote: we killed a lot of test pilots until chuck yeager brought speed

cluster_id: Turbine blade creep failure at grain boundaries
display_name: Turbine blade creep failure at grain boundaries

instances.name: Turbine blade creep failure at grain boundaries
instances.summary: Early jet engine turbine blades failed in service through creep along grain boundaries, which were discovered to be the weakest regions under stress at high temperature.
instances.key_quote: they fail along the brain boundaries in fact i thought he had a picture here he has a picture of metal failure at the grain boundaries
instances.framework_lesson: Conventional casting grain structure was inadequate for high-temperature creep resistance; directional solidification was required to align grain boundaries parallel to stress.

cluster_id: Air-cooled turbine blade development (Pratt Whitney)
display_name: Air-cooled turbine blade development (Pratt Whitney)

cluster_id: CMSX4/CMSX5 rhenium alloy pigtails in MIT lab
display_name: CMSX4/CMSX5 rhenium alloy pigtails in MIT lab

instances.name: CMSX4/CMSX5 rhenium alloy pigtails in MIT lab

cluster_id: United Technologies niobium alloy development program
display_name: United Technologies niobium alloy development program

cluster_id: Weld defect forensic cutting analysis (unnamed case)
display_name: Weld defect forensic cutting analysis (unnamed case)

instances.name: Weld defect forensic cutting analysis (unnamed case)
instances.summary: A customer complained about an imperfect weld, so Eagar cut it into 14 pieces to investigate; fracture mechanics analysis showed the porosity defect was insignificant and did not affect structural integrity.
instances.key_quote: I had a problem and I went in and I cut the weld up in 14 pieces and I found a little pour in there well first of all do fracture mechanics and see if the PO makes a difference
instances.framework_lesson: Visual or detected weld defects must be evaluated via fracture mechanics rather than automatically rejected; historical evidence and code limits exist because similar small defects have performed acceptably for decades.

cluster_id: Helicopter engine loss lawsuits
display_name: Helicopter engine loss lawsuits

instances.name: Helicopter engine loss lawsuits

instances.key_quote: there's lawsuits and everything because they didn't have for momentum sometimes it was because you're supposed to get out of that area of no you know going straight up or hovering you're not supposed to hang in those areas where you can't auto rotate safely

cluster_id: Undermatching weld metal field trials
display_name: Undermatching weld metal field trials

instances.name: Undermatching weld metal field trials
instances.summary: Over approximately 25 years prior to the 1990s, multiple costly testing programs (~millions of dollars each, approximately every 15 years) were undertaken to determine if undermatching filler metals could be used in structural welding.
instances.key_quote: I haven't seen another millions of dollars worth of testing for about 25 years but before that about every 15 years someone would go out and spend millions of dollars trying to find out if they could use undermatching filler metal

cluster_id: Steel blade demonstration in teaching forge
display_name: Steel blade demonstration in teaching forge

instances.name: Steel blade demonstration in teaching forge
instances.summary: A teaching demonstration at MIT's forge shows how untempered martensite becomes so brittle that a quenched steel bar shatters when struck with a hammer.
instances.key_quote: he'll heat it up and he can bend it 90 de or 180 Dees and then he quenches it in water and he hits it and makes sure nobody's looking that direction everybody's got safety glasses on he can shatter the bar

cluster_id: Eastern Pennsylvania bicycle handlebar failure
display_name: Eastern Pennsylvania bicycle handlebar failure

instances.name: Eastern Pennsylvania bicycle handlebar failure
instances.summary: A 21-year-old bicycle stunt rider became a paraplegic when 4130 steel handlebars fractured during a jump; the steel had been electroplated without proper hydrogen bake-out, causing embrittlement that initiated a brittle crack that grew by fatigue.
instances.key_quote: he landed on his head and now he's a paraplegic okay so I get the handlebars 4130 steel turns out it was pretty sure it was normalized wasn't special strength
instances.framework_lesson: The electroplating shop and mom-and-pop bicycle manufacturer did not follow ASM specifications requiring hydrogen bake-out within 1–4 hours of plating, leaving the material embrittled despite correct steel selection.

cluster_id: Frank Whittle turbine engine development
display_name: Frank Whittle turbine engine development

instances.name: Frank Whittle turbine engine development

cluster_id: Johns Hopkins APL sonar tow cable pad-eye failure
display_name: Johns Hopkins APL sonar tow cable pad-eye failure

instances.name: Johns Hopkins APL sonar tow cable pad-eye failure
instances.summary: A welded steel pad-eye attachment on a Navy research ship failed when towing a classified submarine-detection object, and the fractured pad-eye struck a crew member in the head, causing brain injuries; the crew member subsequently filed suit.
instances.key_quote: a guy got hit in the head by piece of metal while he's a board ship out in the Pacific is a research ship being operated by Johns Hopkins and it was towing on a cable some little object
instances.framework_lesson: Expert analysis must account for dynamic cable loading (stretch under swell-induced time delays) to determine whether a hole deforms before a weld fails; intuitive forensic thinking can reach the wrong conclusion without quantitative load analysis.
materials_or_systems: one-inch thick steel plate with one-inch hole
instances.source_module: How to be a Successful Engineer

cluster_id: Deep-sea cable towing failure case
display_name: Deep-sea cable towing failure case

instances.name: Deep-sea cable towing failure case
instances.summary: A subsea object being towed 2,000 feet below the surface experienced cable stretch and load concentration during ten-foot ocean swells, creating risk of hole deformation in the towed apparatus.
instances.key_quote: when the ship goes up to the top of the swell the cable has to stretch because this is way down in the bottom there's a time delay here the mass of this thing that they're towing is not going to follow the swells
instances.framework_lesson: Dynamic cable loading from surface wave action on subsurface towed objects requires transient stress analysis beyond static design calculations.
materials_or_systems: elastically stretched cable under cyclic ocean loading

cluster_id: 2008 financial crisis / derivatives collapse
display_name: 2008 financial crisis / derivatives collapse

instances.name: 2008 financial crisis / derivatives collapse
instances.summary: The derivatives market, built on mathematical models derived from the Black-Scholes method, grew from $1 trillion to $50 trillion by 2008, leading to systemic financial collapse when banks defaulted and could not honor derivative obligations.
instances.key_quote: the amount of derivatives in 2004 in the world was $1 trillion the amount of derivatives in 2008 at the time of the collapse was $50 trillion
instances.framework_lesson: Mathematical models and trading frameworks for derivative risk failed to account for systemic contagion and liquidity collapse when counterparties could not meet obligations.

cluster_id: Cruise missile graphite engine
display_name: Cruise missile graphite engine

instances.name: Cruise missile graphite engine
instances.summary: Graphite-based jet engines for cruise missiles operated successfully despite material brittleness by accepting short operational duration and relying on protective coatings.
instances.key_quote: you make a jet engine with no imperfections in there larger than half or one-tenth the size of human hair it'll run at Great temperatures and you know we've already done that it's called a cruise missile
instances.framework_lesson: Brittle material performance in high-temperature applications is viable if critical flaw size is tightly controlled and service life is short enough that coating degradation remains acceptable.

cluster_id: Dan Rather CBS building collapse
display_name: Dan Rather CBS building collapse

instances.key_quote: one of the beams which is there like this in tension just broke and it like a house of cards or dominoes all the other beams came down one guy broke a leg
instances.framework_lesson: Rebar in concrete structures was being tack-welded without consideration of high-carbon steel's brittleness, converting ductile connections into brittle fracture points.

cluster_id: Aluminum 7075 stress-corrosion cracking in aircraft structures
display_name: Aluminum 7075 stress-corrosion cracking in aircraft structures

instances.name: Aluminum 7075 stress-corrosion cracking in aircraft structures
instances.summary: Older 7075 aluminum alloy formulations used in aircraft wings were susceptible to stress-corrosion cracking; replaced with improved 7050 alloy with better corrosion resistance and strength.
instances.key_quote: the old alloy they did over temper and it was susceptible to stress corrosion cracking nowadays they developed a more stress corrosion cracking resistant alloy
instances.framework_lesson: Material specification evolution was driven by service failures in stress-corrosion environments; modern alloys now achieve both better strength and better environmental resistance.

cluster_id: Shipyard Navy welding humidity specification enforcement
display_name: Shipyard Navy welding humidity specification enforcement

instances.name: Shipyard Navy welding humidity specification enforcement
instances.summary: Navy specifications prohibited welding in shipyards when humidity exceeded 80%, resulting in operational shutdowns during high-humidity conditions to prevent hydrogen-related weld defects.
instances.key_quote: if they're above eighty percent of community okay you were a new per dose how many days did you shut down operations of welding because you're over eighty percent humidity
instances.framework_lesson: Codes and specifications must account for moisture absorption in atmospheric conditions to prevent hydrogen embrittlement in high-strength welds.

cluster_id: Department of Energy 9% nickel steel qualification effort
display_name: Department of Energy 9% nickel steel qualification effort

instances.name: Department of Energy 9% nickel steel qualification effort
instances.summary: The Department of Energy has spent 40 years attempting to qualify 9% nickel steel and other advanced steels for higher-temperature nuclear reactor applications, but widespread adoption remains hesitant due to insufficient operational database.
instances.key_quote: the Department of Energy's tried to qualify 9% nickel steals, they've been doing this for 40 years and people are still somewhat hesitant
instances.framework_lesson: New material qualification for critical infrastructure requires not just laboratory testing but operational experience with full-scale prototypes; theoretical qualification is insufficient without field experience.

cluster_id: Alvin and Sea Cliff deep-ocean research submersible development
display_name: Alvin and Sea Cliff deep-ocean research submersible development

instances.framework_lesson: Purposeful design and construction of research prototypes can serve dual functions: advancing scientific capability while building industrial experience with novel materials and processes for future military systems.

cluster_id: Aluminum 6061 weld cracking with improper filler wire selection
display_name: Aluminum 6061 weld cracking with improper filler wire selection

instances.name: Aluminum 6061 weld cracking with improper filler wire selection
instances.summary: Welding 6061 aluminum with 1100 filler wire and other suboptimal filler wire combinations resulted in centerline cracks and circumferential cracking, demonstrating the critical importance of matched filler wire composition selection.
instances.key_quote: if you don't think the right combinations of filler wire so it's actually harder when someone says come to me if they come to me say I want to repair something
instances.framework_lesson: Aluminum welding requires full chemical composition knowledge of both base and filler metals; unlike steel where standards allow broader specification, aluminum demands precise matching to avoid solidification cracking.

cluster_id: C-1 / Sea Wolf submarine welding failure
display_name: C-1 / Sea Wolf submarine welding failure

instances.name: C-1 / Sea Wolf submarine welding failure
instances.summary: Submarine welded with HY-130 filler wire instead of specified HY-100, causing hydrogen embrittlement due to inadequate hydrogen control during assembly.
instances.key_quote: they thought they were welding hy 100 they thought they'd had a filler wire that would be in hy 100 filler wire because the range of variability that you got something more like an 8 1 130 filler wire they needed tighter hydrogen control they didn't have that
instances.framework_lesson: Material specifications with wide variability ranges in filler wire composition can lead to uncontrolled properties if process controls (hydrogen management) are not correspondingly tightened.

cluster_id: All-aluminum automobile body cost analysis (early 1990s)

instances.key_quote: the cost of the steel frame the unibody frame was 500 and the all aluminum was about $1,000
instances.framework_lesson: Industry hype about material substitution ignored manufacturing cost realities and the long design-to-production cycle (3–5 years design plus 2 years for die fabrication) required to introduce new vehicles at scale.

cluster_id: Watertown Arsenal Civil War shot tower
display_name: Watertown Arsenal Civil War shot tower

instances.name: Watertown Arsenal Civil War shot tower
instances.summary: The Army Arsenal at Watertown attempted to produce cannonballs by dropping molten cast iron from towers to achieve spherical solidification, limited by incomplete understanding of heat transfer.
instances.key_quote: during the Civil War they actually tried to build towers over there to drop Cannonball molten cast iron for cannonballs and see if they would hit the ground and solidify they didn't know a lot about heat transfer in the Civil War

cluster_id: Civil War cannonball drop towers
display_name: Civil War cannonball drop towers

instances.name: Civil War cannonball drop towers
instances.summary: The U.S. Army attempted to create spherical cannonballs by dropping molten cast iron from towers during the Civil War, but the towers were theoretically infeasible and all attempts resulted in splats on the ground.
instances.key_quote: they realized that the tower they had to have was about 50 miles tall okay to get the heat transfer and they also didn't know that there is the ratio of surface tension forces to gravity forces
instances.framework_lesson: Engineers lacked dimensionless number analysis and understanding of surface tension-to-gravity ratios needed to predict whether droplets would form spheres versus ellipsoids below critical size.

cluster_id: U.S. Navy titanium submarine program decision
display_name: U.S. Navy titanium submarine program decision

instances.name: U.S. Navy titanium submarine program decision
instances.summary: The U.S. Navy decided not to develop titanium submarines despite Soviet advances, recognizing the unfavorable cost-benefit ratio of building one titanium submarine versus ten steel submarines, though Congress later demanded an impossible shift to composite submarine construction.
instances.key_quote: you could build one titanium sub for 10 steel Subs so you have to ask yourself would you rather have 10 steel subs or one titanium sub
instances.framework_lesson: Engineering decisions cannot ignore economic and strategic trade-offs; pursuing advanced materials without cost-benefit analysis can lead to inferior overall capability.

cluster_id: Aircraft engine crankcase stud failure and warranty repair
display_name: Aircraft engine crankcase stud failure and warranty repair

instances.name: Aircraft engine crankcase stud failure and warranty repair
instances.summary: A mechanic over-torqued a 3/8 inch steel stud in an aluminum aircraft engine housing, stripping threads and causing the stud to pull out; the manufacturer conducted failure testing on the repair using helical coil inserts with different weld metal compositions.
instances.key_quote: they had repaired one in someone's torquing with the stud and it's supposed to torque to 204 foot inch pounds and the mechanics always using a torque wrench here you never tighten anything on a aircraft engine less you have a calibrated torque wrench okay
instances.framework_lesson: Proper torque specification and calibrated tools are critical for fatigue-loaded aircraft components; improper application leads to thread stripping and potential in-service failures.

cluster_id: Independent consultant patent liability and indemnification

instances.key_quote: if anyone ever sues you will treat me just like one of your employees... because I don't want to see hundreds of thousands of dollars in legal costs... my name is on the patent and I could be sued
instances.framework_lesson: Independent consultants named on patents face personal liability and must secure professional liability insurance at time of design and carry it through the product's life, or negotiate corporate indemnification.
