instances.name: Car elevator loading failure
instances.summary: A car elevator in a storage garage failed when a forklift with a much smaller wheel span was loaded entirely on one side instead of centered, causing structural failure not anticipated by the design code.
instances.key_quote: somebody came with a forklift fully loaded that was much light and much smaller wheel span so they were able to put all the load on one side as opposed to more or less in the middle and that came to be a problem
instances.framework_lesson: Elevator design codes assume centered loading and do not account for off-center placement of heavy point loads; designers must either tighten assumptions or add capacity for eccentricity.

cluster_id: Custom-built trailer design and field testing
display_name: Custom-built trailer design and field testing

instances.name: Custom-built trailer design and field testing
instances.summary: Eagar designed and built a 8-foot by 4-foot trailer with a tongue and cross-members over 8 years of use; discovered that cross-member sizing was insufficient when weight was placed toward the front rather than centered on wheels, and that torsional loading from puddles and uneven terrain required stronger welded corner connections than initial steady-load analysis predicted.
instances.key_quote: when i put the weight here then instead of all going into the suspension some of it goes on the ball so i can put a little bit more weight so when i designed it i think i put the weight right around here and that it made this member here too weak the cross member
instances.framework_lesson: Trailer design lacks industry codes or standards; designers must infer loads empirically through field use and consult experienced builders to account for dynamic torsional stresses from terrain that steady-state load analysis misses.

cluster_id: Trailer overload with firewood (3000 lb vs. 2000 lb design)
display_name: Trailer overload with firewood (3000 lb vs. 2000 lb design)

instances.name: Trailer overload with firewood (3000 lb vs. 2000 lb design)
instances.summary: Eagar loaded approximately 3000 pounds of firewood into a trailer designed for 2000 pounds; the structure deformed slightly but did not fail catastrophically because the welded corner connections were designed with compression-side welds only, allowing the tension-side member to slip under load rather than fracture.
instances.key_quote: i put on probably about three thousand pounds in it instead of two thousand pound at some point i was not going really fast but i knew it was all full of uh firewood
instances.framework_lesson: Welded connection design detail (compression-side welds vs. tension-side) can inadvertently create a fail-safe mechanism that allows controlled deformation rather than brittle failure under overload.

cluster_id: Custom firewood trailer design and overload incident
display_name: Custom firewood trailer design and overload incident

instances.name: Custom firewood trailer design and overload incident
instances.summary: Eagar designed and built a one-ton capacity trailer that he overloaded with approximately three thousand pounds of firewood, which deformed slightly but did not fail catastrophically due to his deliberate welding strategy.
instances.key_quote: at some point this location deformed a little bit uh but i've put on probably about three thousand pounds in it instead of two thousand pound
instances.framework_lesson: By intentionally placing welds only in compression zones and avoiding welds in tension zones, the designer avoided the code-conservative assumption that creates unnecessary fatigue risk at weld discontinuities in tension.

cluster_id: Homemade trailer wheel-to-fender contact issue
display_name: Homemade trailer wheel-to-fender contact issue

instances.name: Homemade trailer wheel-to-fender contact issue
instances.summary: During operation at loads above 2500 pounds, the wheels of the custom trailer began to contact the fenders when traversing bumps, forcing Eagar to raise the fenders to maintain clearance for additional weight.
instances.key_quote: just here where the fenders are when you go above 2500 pounds you go through some bumps and the wheels hit the defenders

cluster_id: Ladder with unauthorized welds
display_name: Ladder with unauthorized welds

instances.name: Ladder with unauthorized welds
instances.summary: A ladder specified on drawing as 24 feet was built from 16-foot members with undocumented welds added by fabricator, creating quality defects in tension zones.
instances.key_quote: we've seen cases people have ladders that on the drawing it just says 24 feet ladder and then it turns out somebody put some welds in it because they only built it out of 16 foot long members
instances.framework_lesson: Design specifications must explicitly prohibit welds in tension zones and define inspection requirements, or fabricators will improvise without quality control.

cluster_id: Custom race car trailer design (author's project)
display_name: Custom race car trailer design (author's project)

instances.name: Custom race car trailer design (author's project)
instances.summary: Tom Eagar designed and built a custom 15-foot trailer by extending an original 12-foot tongue, requiring analysis of fatigue under cyclic loading and optimization of beam transitions.
instances.key_quote: i made the tongue longer after i built it started as a 12 foot member and i made it to 15 feet so in the end here it's a smaller beam a transition from a bigger beam to a smaller beam
instances.framework_lesson: Practical fabrication constraints often prevent theoretical optimization; designers must balance welded connections, fabrication cost, and weight against analytical ideals.
materials_or_systems: bolted and welded joints in tension and shear

cluster_id: Nuclear facility structural frame hole-drilling incident
display_name: Nuclear facility structural frame hole-drilling incident

instances.name: Nuclear facility structural frame hole-drilling incident
instances.summary: A contractor drilled holes into welded structural tubes in a nuclear seismic-resistant frame without engineering approval, creating stress concentrators in members designed to work in plasticity.
instances.key_quote: the contractor came and put in some tubings for just conduits for the electrical into the main frame that had to be resistant to seismic deformation and if you have holes in it it's not the same
instances.framework_lesson: Design codes embed safety factors and assumptions about member integrity; field modifications that violate those assumptions require complete re-evaluation and cannot simply be repaired with standard solutions like plug welds.

cluster_id: Plug-weld repair of seismic frame (rejected solution)
display_name: Plug-weld repair of seismic frame (rejected solution)

instances.name: Plug-weld repair of seismic frame (rejected solution)
instances.summary: An attempt to repair stress-concentration holes in a seismic-resistant frame by plug-welding them was rejected because the welding residual stresses would increase crack initiation risk under cyclic plasticity.
instances.key_quote: don't plug weld them because you're actually going to make the situation worse welding will add residual stresses to your situation to the point where when you get the plasticity you're going to be more likely to initiate cracking
instances.framework_lesson: Residual stress from welding repairs can violate design assumptions in structures intended to deform cyclically and absorb energy, making conventional repair methods counterproductive.

cluster_id: Florida tree stand welding failure
display_name: Florida tree stand welding failure

instances.name: Florida tree stand welding failure
instances.summary: A man was killed in Florida when a tree stand failed due to improper weld orientation that placed the joint in tension rather than compression.
instances.key_quote: i had a case down in florida where a guy you talked about designing your trailer well i can i had the exact same thing in the tree stand and kill the guy
instances.framework_lesson: Design guidance on weld orientation (compression vs. tension loading) is fundamental and omission can be fatal; engineers must consider load path direction relative to joint orientation.

cluster_id: Tractor-trailer frame weld failures
display_name: Tractor-trailer frame weld failures

instances.name: Tractor-trailer frame weld failures
instances.summary: Heavy tractor-trailer frames have experienced repeated weld failures on their steel frames, leading to code prohibition against field welding on these structures.
instances.key_quote: you are not allowed by code to weld on that frame that's steel frame too many trailers have to be at the well given only
instances.framework_lesson: Codes restrict welding on safety-critical structures (trailer frames) because of repeated historical failures; bolted connections, though heavier and weaker, are required for inspectability and reliability.

cluster_id: Boston warehouse fire protection system corrosion
display_name: Boston warehouse fire protection system corrosion

instances.name: Boston warehouse fire protection system corrosion
instances.summary: A large warehouse fire protection system in Boston became completely clogged with rust over 10-20 years due to a quarter-inch leak that converted the closed system into an open system, continuously introducing fresh oxygen.
instances.key_quote: they had clogged up all the arteries because it wasn't a closed system it was an open system and they wanted to know how i knew well i knew because the principle is you can't get corrosion unless you got free oxygen

cluster_id: Arizona warehouse roof corrosion failure
display_name: Arizona warehouse roof corrosion failure

instances.name: Arizona warehouse roof corrosion failure
instances.summary: A newly constructed warehouse in Arizona experienced catastrophic corrosion of galvanized steel roof hangers at 40,000 to 80,000 thousandths per inch per year due to trapped moisture creating a wet-dry cycling environment.
instances.key_quote: the fastest carbon steel corrosion rate i've ever seen you could do sewage you could do chlorides you could do anything you want this was the best it was in arizona
instances.framework_lesson: Arizona construction practices did not account for rare but destructive rainfall events; the moisture barrier design failed to anticipate trapped humidity combined with day-night thermal cycling accelerating corrosion.

cluster_id: Casting vessel fragment ejection incident
display_name: Casting vessel fragment ejection incident

instances.name: Casting vessel fragment ejection incident
instances.summary: A 16-ton piece was ejected a quarter mile from a casting facility, creating a public safety hazard that alarmed local officials despite minimal media coverage.
instances.key_quote: the 16 ton piece landed a quar mile away if it had landed on somebody's house the news might have picked it up but safety officials the fire department the police department the mayor's office they were not happy
instances.framework_lesson: Quality control improvements in material mills, heat treatment understanding, water treatment protocols, and stress calculation methods were subsequently implemented to prevent recurrence.

cluster_id: Bell Labs zone melting semiconductor purification (1950s)
display_name: Bell Labs zone melting semiconductor purification (1950s)

instances.name: Bell Labs zone melting semiconductor purification (1950s)
instances.summary: Technician Jan Schafmeister at Bell Labs developed zone melting technique to achieve 6-9 nines purity in silicon and germanium for early semiconductor manufacturing by repeatedly melting and directionally solidifying ingots.
instances.key_quote: a technician at Bell Labs named fan he had a high school diploma but he certainly didn't have a college degree and he was the one who said well let's just keep melting it in One Direction and sweep all the impurities to one end

cluster_id: Single-crystal turbine blades (GE/Pratt & Whitney)
display_name: Single-crystal turbine blades (GE/Pratt & Whitney)

instances.name: Single-crystal turbine blades (GE/Pratt & Whitney)
instances.summary: GE and Pratt & Whitney developed single-crystal investment casting turbine blades grown in gradient furnaces over 10 hours to eliminate dendrites and improve high-temperature performance in jet engines.
instances.key_quote: General Electric and Pratt their casting shops use about 16 or 24 on a investment casting sprew and they grow it as a single crystal in a gradient furnace it takes about 10 hours
instances.framework_lesson: Economic justification for high-cost manufacturing: single-crystal blades costing $6,000 each were justified because they allowed engines to use half the fuel over their lifetime.

cluster_id: Cold heading tool wear and surface cracking (pre-WWII)
display_name: Cold heading tool wear and surface cracking (pre-WWII)

instances.name: Cold heading tool wear and surface cracking (pre-WWII)
instances.summary: Cold heading operations for fasteners and parts experienced severe tool wear and surface cracking due to frictional welding between steel parts and forging dies, limiting production to only a few die passes.
instances.key_quote: you can get welding between there and if you keep on going you're going to get surface cracking on your part and you're also going to wear out your tooling very quickly
instances.framework_lesson: Post-WWII discovery of phosphate coating and stearate lubricant solutions showed that boundary lubrication conditions were not controlled in earlier specifications.

cluster_id: German superplasticity research (1938, lost in WWII)
display_name: German superplasticity research (1938, lost in WWII)

instances.name: German superplasticity research (1938, lost in WWII)
instances.summary: German researchers published a paper in 1938 on superplasticity in metal forging, but the work was lost and ignored due to World War II.
instances.key_quote: isothermal forging is something that was really developed by Pratt and Whitney based on something that was developed in 1938 in Germany but was lost because of World War II

cluster_id: German superplastic tensile bar (1934)
display_name: German superplastic tensile bar (1934)

instances.name: German superplastic tensile bar (1934)
instances.summary: A 1934 German research sample demonstrated extreme superplasticity in a bismuth-tin alloy, achieving 7× elongation at near-zero strain-rate sensitivity.
instances.key_quote: it's 1934 the German this is a picture from the German paper of a tensil bar of super plastic material bisou tin tic alloy and there it is you know um a lot of stretch

cluster_id: Backov aluminum-zinc candy dish forming
display_name: Backov aluminum-zinc candy dish forming

instances.name: Backov aluminum-zinc candy dish forming
instances.summary: Backov used superplastic aluminum-zinc alloy with 500% elongation to form a complex candy dish in a single draw operation, eliminating the 4–5 drawing operations normally required in sheet-metal forming.
instances.key_quote: with a super plastic material that has 500% elongation you can do it in a single draw okay that is the reinvention of super plasticity after the germ is in 1934

cluster_id: Beer and soda can drawing operations
display_name: Beer and soda can drawing operations

instances.name: Beer and soda can drawing operations
instances.summary: Industrial beer and soda can manufacturing requires multiple drawing operations (2–6 stages) due to low strain-rate sensitivity in standard metals, and die tooling costs ~$2 million even in modern production.
instances.key_quote: the do tries to make a beer can run for about $2 million even today big machine very complex uh but a lot of work's been done on it

cluster_id: Nickel-based turbine disc isothermal forging case study
display_name: Nickel-based turbine disc isothermal forging case study

instances.name: Nickel-based turbine disc isothermal forging case study
instances.summary: A superalloy turbine disc was produced by isothermal forging from 160–126 lb starting material into a 15 lb finished part in a single operation, drastically reducing machining chips compared to conventional forging that would generate 235 lbs of scrap from a 250 lb blank.
instances.key_quote: you would ordinarily have to start with a 250 lb of a $100 an hour uh $100 a pound alloy and to make a 15lb part and you would generate 235 lbs of chips in Machining

cluster_id: Wire drawing defects in extrusion
display_name: Wire drawing defects in extrusion

instances.name: Wire drawing defects in extrusion
instances.summary: Wire drawing and extrusion operations produce internal cracks and surface defects when lubrication, contact angle, or reduction parameters are not optimized.
instances.key_quote: just something as simple and has been going on for 4,000 years is drawing wires through a die you got to know what you're doing or you can just turn out junk

cluster_id: Cold welding and fracture in metal forming
display_name: Cold welding and fracture in metal forming

instances.name: Cold welding and fracture in metal forming
instances.summary: When insufficient lubricant is applied during metal forming operations, the material cold welds to the tool surface, and fracture occurs if local strain exceeds approximately 30%.
instances.key_quote: if you don't have enough lubricant you actually start cold welding the material to the surface if you get more than 30% local strain it breaks

cluster_id: Roll flattening limit in sheet rolling
display_name: Roll flattening limit in sheet rolling

instances.name: Roll flattening limit in sheet rolling
instances.summary: When rolling sheet metal, the ratio of sheet thickness to roll diameter cannot exceed approximately 1:500, or elastic spring-back of the roll prevents any deformation of the sheet.
instances.key_quote: at a ratio of sheet thickness to roll diameter of about 500 you can no longer get any deformation the thing will just go through there without deforming the sheet at all

cluster_id: Reynolds Aluminum foil manufacturing
display_name: Reynolds Aluminum foil manufacturing

instances.name: Reynolds Aluminum foil manufacturing
instances.summary: Reynolds Aluminum company developed aluminum foil rolling (pack rolling) to package cigarettes with moisture-barrier properties after the company founder realized he needed a hermetic seal for his cigarette products.
instances.key_quote: Reynolds Aluminum was started by the guy who was making the cigarettes and wanted to have something that wouldn't lose or pick up moisture so he needed a hermetic type of seal

cluster_id: Alcoa air knife implementation failure
display_name: Alcoa air knife implementation failure

instances.name: Alcoa air knife implementation failure
instances.summary: MIT researcher identified a $10,000 air-knife solution to eliminate water-induced gauge variation at Alcoa's Davenport mill, but Alcoa management refused to implement it due to 'not invented here' corporate culture.
instances.key_quote: Alcoa at that time this is 1990 was a big time not invented here okay if it wasn't invented at Alcoa it was garbage there was no value to it
instances.framework_lesson: Organizational culture and incentive structures can prevent adoption of external solutions even when cost-benefit analysis strongly favors implementation.

cluster_id: LIGO gravitational wave detector corrosion failure
display_name: LIGO gravitational wave detector corrosion failure

instances.name: LIGO gravitational wave detector corrosion failure
instances.summary: Mice urinated and defecated on stainless steel components of the 4-kilometer LIGO ultra-high vacuum system, causing corrosion and a leak after 12 years of operation.
instances.key_quote: some mice had built a nest and they had been urinating and defecating on the stainless steel and the stainless steel corroded and here's this billion dollar facility and a few mice have jeopardized this facility

cluster_id: MIT Millstone radar dome structural fabrication
display_name: MIT Millstone radar dome structural fabrication

instances.name: MIT Millstone radar dome structural fabrication
instances.summary: Lincoln Laboratory converted a physics department radar dome into a 300 GHz space-imaging radar system requiring aluminum structures the size of a football field with tolerances within 3mm over 100 yards, creating unprecedented welding and fabrication challenges.
instances.key_quote: we actually had to find shops that would and kind of come up with fabrication techniques that would make it possible
instances.framework_lesson: Standard welding shop tolerances and procedures were inadequate for precision space-systems applications; specialized fabrication techniques had to be developed to meet extreme accuracy requirements.

cluster_id: Nuclear submarine hull steel (HY-80)
display_name: Nuclear submarine hull steel (HY-80)

instances.name: Nuclear submarine hull steel (HY-80)
instances.summary: Eagar received a sample of HY-80 high-strength steel used in nuclear submarine hulls from Generator Dynamics approximately 30-35 years before this 2013 lecture, representing advanced materials in undersea vehicle construction.
instances.key_quote: this is a piece of steel from generator dynamics it's hy80 it's a high strength submarine hull steel and it's a weld that they gave me when i was down there about 30 35 years ago

cluster_id: ESSO Manhattan
display_name: ESSO Manhattan

instances.name: ESSO Manhattan
instances.summary: Welded steel merchant vessel from WWII Liberty ship program that sustained fracture damage, illustrated in Navy investigation of brittle fracture failures.
instances.key_quote: the esso manhattan this one oh what'd i do something on these buttons oh it's showing up there

cluster_id: Microsurgery instrument material selection consulting case
display_name: Microsurgery instrument material selection consulting case

instances.name: Microsurgery instrument material selection consulting case
instances.summary: Consultant recommended sapphire (aluminum oxide) tubes and rods instead of steel for surgical instruments to achieve higher stiffness and wear resistance while enabling novel marketing through color-doping as rubies and emeralds.
instances.key_quote: you could use aluminum oxide use sapphire you can dope it with a little chrome and you can call it ruby and sell tell you the mds that you're selling them ruby instruments

cluster_id: Nuclear submarine disaster
display_name: Nuclear submarine disaster

instances.name: Nuclear submarine disaster
instances.summary: A nuclear submarine suffered a catastrophic failure causing crew deaths, which shut down the entire U.S. nuclear submarine program for 2-3 years.
instances.key_quote: the guys on the ship on a board were listening to this on the you know the screams of the men as they were just slowly you know going down to their death
instances.framework_lesson: The disaster led to creation of SUBSAFE manufacturing and quality control standards that were 30 years ahead of American and world industry practice.

cluster_id: Electron beam deposition on naval propellers
display_name: Electron beam deposition on naval propellers

instances.name: Electron beam deposition on naval propellers
instances.summary: MIT conducted research using electron beam deposition to build up nickel-manganese-aluminum bronze propellers and stainless steel structures, but encountered solidification defects and unmanageable residual stresses that cracked samples beyond half-inch thickness.
instances.key_quote: it turns out sometimes you end up getting solidification defects surface tension defects and they tend to propagate and it's hard to get rid of them

cluster_id: Shaft rebuilding in shipyards with residual stress failures
display_name: Shaft rebuilding in shipyards with residual stress failures

instances.name: Shaft rebuilding in shipyards with residual stress failures
instances.summary: Naval shipyard practice of rebuilding worn tail shafts by machining and re-welding caused bearing failures in service due to unrelieved residual stresses from multi-pass weld buildup, requiring preheat and postweld stress-relief heating.
instances.key_quote: you put on more than about a quarter of an inch and you're going to have tremendous residual stresses which will cause the thing the bearing to fail in service unless you relieve the residual stresses

cluster_id: Pratt & Whitney laser additive manufacturing R&D (Office of Naval Research)
display_name: Pratt & Whitney laser additive manufacturing R&D (Office of Naval Research)

instances.name: Pratt & Whitney laser additive manufacturing R&D (Office of Naval Research)
instances.summary: In the mid-1970s, Pratt & Whitney conducted a proprietary ONR-funded research program attempting to build turbine discs using high-power lasers, but the process produced unacceptable residual stresses and extremely slow deposition rates (15 lbs/day) that proved economically unviable.
instances.key_quote: back in their um like 90 76 or 77 when they first had some high power lasers they tried to build up things and I remember there was a research contract at pratton Whitney from The Office of Naval Research
instances.framework_lesson: Early additive manufacturing attempts failed to achieve sufficient deposition rates and residual stress control, yet successive generations of researchers repeated the same approach without learning from documented failures.

cluster_id: Wyoming gas plant heat exchanger explosion and failure
display_name: Wyoming gas plant heat exchanger explosion and failure

instances.name: Wyoming gas plant heat exchanger explosion and failure
instances.summary: A critical cryogenic aluminum heat exchanger failed in an active gas processing plant in Wyoming, causing an explosion that incapacitated a quarter-billion-dollar project, with a 70-week procurement lead time for replacement equipment.
instances.key_quote: I have a failure right now in a gas plant has in uh in Wyoming uh they had an explosion and they lost a critical heat exchanger and this heat exchanger the lead time is 70 weeks

cluster_id: Naval Research Lab HY80 laser welding study
display_name: Naval Research Lab HY80 laser welding study

instances.name: Naval Research Lab HY80 laser welding study
instances.summary: A 25 kW laser welding study at Naval Research Lab found that laser welding of HY80 steel produced weld metal with better mechanical properties than the base material by selectively vaporizing oxide inclusions.
instances.key_quote: the laser was coming in and it was essentially selectively vaporizing the oxide inclusions in the steel oxides and sulfides and just blast casting them away as vapor and leaving behind a pure more refined weld metal

cluster_id: Coal gasifier heavy section steel welding project
display_name: Coal gasifier heavy section steel welding project

instances.name: Coal gasifier heavy section steel welding project
instances.summary: A multi-hundred-million-dollar national project to build coal gasifiers and weld heavy section steels encountered persistent beam stability issues when attempting electron beam welding of thick pressure vessels.
instances.key_quote: spent a couple hundred million dollars of a national project to try to build these coal gasifiers and weld heavy section Steels

cluster_id: Electron beam welding defect repair failure
display_name: Electron beam welding defect repair failure

instances.name: Electron beam welding defect repair failure
instances.summary: Navy electron beam welding project for 30ft diameter pressure vessels 6-8 inches thick experienced beam instability every 10-15 minutes, producing defects that could not be reliably repaired due to lack of effective deep-seated defect repair techniques.
instances.key_quote: if you're going to weld one of these 30ft diameter pressure vessels 6 or 8 in thick it takes about an hour to go around and typically you'll have some hiccup in the beam every 10 or 15 minutes
instances.framework_lesson: No engineering solution was developed for reliable repair of deep-seated defects in thick sections welded with high-energy processes.

cluster_id: Illinois laser-welded aluminum window frames manufacturing
display_name: Illinois laser-welded aluminum window frames manufacturing

instances.name: Illinois laser-welded aluminum window frames manufacturing
instances.summary: A plant in Illinois manufactured 300 million feet per year of laser-welded aluminum double-pane window frames using 10 high-power lasers in production, exploiting the reflectivity properties of aluminum to achieve oscillating melt/keyhole transitions.
instances.key_quote: about 30 years ago the largest application in the world for laser welding was laser welding of aluminum and a plant in Illinois um they were turning out 300 million feet a year

cluster_id: Mitsubishi Heavy Industries spherical stainless steel reactor vessel welding
display_name: Mitsubishi Heavy Industries spherical stainless steel reactor vessel welding

instances.name: Mitsubishi Heavy Industries spherical stainless steel reactor vessel welding
instances.summary: Mitsubishi Heavy Industries built a specialized 2-3 story building-sized vacuum chamber to electron beam weld hundreds of 2-inch thick spherical stainless steel vessels for a nuclear reactor or physics experiment, completing each vessel in approximately 10 minutes.
instances.key_quote: Mitsubishi heavy Industries had to make a bunch of spherical stainless steel vessels that were 2 in thick and they built a chamber that was about five time well it wasn't five times it's probably about the vacuum chamber was about two or three times the size of this room

cluster_id: Laser-welded aluminum window frames (circa 1980)
display_name: Laser-welded aluminum window frames (circa 1980)

instances.name: Laser-welded aluminum window frames (circa 1980)
instances.summary: Double-pane window manufacturers used laser welding on aluminum frames, deliberately creating inherently unstable, porous welds that naturally oscillated to allow air pressure breathing between panes.
instances.key_quote: they made a series of defective welds inherently defective because of the properties of aluminum and the reflectivity of aluminum and the physics of the beam interaction
instances.framework_lesson: The physics of laser-aluminum interaction (high reflectivity causing beam oscillation) was leveraged as a feature rather than treated as a failure mode; conventional reliability thinking would have rejected the process.

cluster_id: Kodak film plant exclusion from Japan (late 1970s–1980s)
display_name: Kodak film plant exclusion from Japan (late 1970s–1980s)

instances.name: Kodak film plant exclusion from Japan (late 1970s–1980s)
instances.summary: Kodak requested permission from the Japanese government to build a photographic film manufacturing plant in Japan but never received a yes-or-no answer, effectively blocking market entry and allowing Fujifilm to establish dominant global position.
instances.key_quote: the Japanese government just never answered kodak's request of can we build a plant they're still waiting for an answer
instances.framework_lesson: Non-technical trade barriers and regulatory delay can reshape competitive landscape and undermine a dominant firm's ability to maintain market leadership.

cluster_id: Kodak manufacturing inefficiency in monopoly era (mid-1990s)
display_name: Kodak manufacturing inefficiency in monopoly era (mid-1990s)

instances.name: Kodak manufacturing inefficiency in monopoly era (mid-1990s)
instances.summary: Kodak had spent decades in a semi-monopolistic environment and built custom stainless-steel conveyors and elevators for routine material handling rather than purchasing standard equipment, exemplifying wasteful spending habits developed under artificial competition-free conditions.
instances.key_quote: instead of going out and buying some conveyor you know conveyor tables to move the boxes and then a little elevator to move them up to the next level Kodak designed and built out of stainless steel not carbon steel their own conveyors and elevators
instances.framework_lesson: Organizations sheltered from genuine competition develop pathologically wasteful engineering and procurement practices that become fatal liabilities once competition arrives.

cluster_id: General Motors and Ford minivan spot-weld fatigue failures
display_name: General Motors and Ford minivan spot-weld fatigue failures

instances.name: General Motors and Ford minivan spot-weld fatigue failures
instances.summary: During the mid-1980s lightweighting effort, GM and Ford spot-welded high-strength steel in minivans assuming fatigue strength would scale with base-metal strength; instead spot welds failed after a few years of service, causing rattles and customer complaints.
instances.key_quote: people buying minivans in the mid 80s were unhappy when all you know after a couple years they started hearing extra rattles because the spot welds were fading
instances.framework_lesson: Design assumptions that fatigue strength of welded joints scales proportionally with base-metal ultimate strength are incorrect; high-strength steel offers no fatigue advantage at a weld stress concentration.

cluster_id: Historical bridge and building fatigue failures (1930s-1960s)
display_name: Historical bridge and building fatigue failures (1930s-1960s)

instances.name: Historical bridge and building fatigue failures (1930s-1960s)
instances.summary: Between the 1930s and 1960s, welded bridges and buildings experienced fatigue failures because engineers did not account for weld stress concentrations in design; failures were discovered through full-scale vibration testing of prototypes.
instances.key_quote: people have gone out and made these things and broken them they were doing this all the time back in the 1930s through the 1960s
instances.framework_lesson: Early fatigue design codes did not properly account for weld notch effects; empirical full-scale testing revealed failures before codes incorporated stress-concentration factors for welded joints.

cluster_id: Egyptian-Israeli Tank War
display_name: Egyptian-Israeli Tank War

instances.name: Egyptian-Israeli Tank War
instances.summary: The 1973 Egyptian-Israeli conflict was used as a case study at Harvard Business School to teach military and strategic decision-making through historical analysis.
instances.key_quote: this tank war the egyptian-israeli tank war of net was in 1973 or whatever and how the Israelis wiped out the Egyptians in the three days or whatever

cluster_id: West Grafton forging press I-beam crack
display_name: West Grafton forging press I-beam crack

instances.name: West Grafton forging press I-beam crack
instances.summary: A 10-foot crack developed in one of six support I-beams of the world's largest forging press in the early 1980s, originating from a fillet-welded attachment block that created a stress concentration and fatigue crack.
instances.key_quote: in the early 80s the one in west grafton developed a crack and it developed a crack in one of the six support beams now this thing is a big world's largest forging press
instances.framework_lesson: The attachment block created an unrecognized stress concentration that initiated fatigue cracking; the design did not adequately account for secondary structural features as crack initiation sites.

cluster_id: High-speed helicopter drive shaft hydrogen cracking
display_name: High-speed helicopter drive shaft hydrogen cracking

instances.name: High-speed helicopter drive shaft hydrogen cracking
instances.summary: High-speed drive shafts in high-strength steel cracked in service after the supply center applied etched identification codes via chemical etching, which introduced hydrogen and created delayed brittle fractures.
instances.key_quote: they had stamped or etched a unique identifier code onto this high-speed drive shaft and that's what started to decrease they did an actual experiment during the investigation they did like 50 of them put 50 of these things through and they crack etched in 25 more and it was 100 every etched one
instances.framework_lesson: Material identification and serialization programs introduced a manufacturing process (chemical etching) incompatible with high-strength steel, causing hydrogen-assisted cracking; no materials compatibility review was performed.

cluster_id: WWII riveted vs. welded ship brittle fracture comparison
display_name: WWII riveted vs. welded ship brittle fracture comparison

instances.name: WWII riveted vs. welded ship brittle fracture comparison
instances.summary: WWII-era all-welded ships suffered long-running brittle fractures that would not occur in pre-WWII riveted ships because rivets interrupted crack propagation, allowing cracks to run for great distances in welded construction.
instances.key_quote: it was the all-welded construction in world war ii and before that we always riveted chips together and if you had a brittle fracture we had brittle fractures in world war one but they only ran the length of the plate you get to the end of the plate where the plate stops and you got rivets and the crack stops okay it's when you get to all welded construction that all of a sudden you can get a crack run for great distances
instances.framework_lesson: The transition from riveted to all-welded ship construction eliminated the natural crack arresters provided by rivet holes, allowing catastrophic crack propagation that riveted designs would have arrested.

cluster_id: 30-mile pipeline brittle fracture
display_name: 30-mile pipeline brittle fracture

instances.name: 30-mile pipeline brittle fracture
instances.summary: A small-diameter buried gas pipeline experienced a catastrophic brittle fracture crack that ran for 30 miles, demonstrating the severity of unchecked crack propagation in all-welded systems.
instances.key_quote: there have been cases i've heard of cases that there were small diameter pipelines i don't know if they're 12 inch or whatever where a crack would start and run for 30 miles
instances.framework_lesson: All-welded pipelines without crack arresters can experience runaway brittle fractures spanning tens of miles; gas pipelines are particularly vulnerable because decompression cannot relieve stress at the crack tip.

cluster_id: IMT hot isostatic press vessel failure
display_name: IMT hot isostatic press vessel failure

instances.name: IMT hot isostatic press vessel failure
instances.summary: A 60-inch diameter, 300-ton forged hot isostatic press vessel made by a Japanese steel company fractured catastrophically at a stress concentration, breaking into ~70 pieces; a 5-ton fragment crushed the operator's chair at 2 a.m., narrowly missing him.
instances.key_quote: when this thing let go it let go with a crack right here that stress concentration from the outside they designed this on a super computer
instances.framework_lesson: The vessel was designed using 1980s supercomputer thermal analysis that did not account for transient heat flow and corrosion-hydrogen embrittlement, reducing the critical flaw size from 15 inches to a much smaller value.

cluster_id: Large forging fracture failure (unnamed consulting case)
display_name: Large forging fracture failure (unnamed consulting case)

instances.name: Large forging fracture failure (unnamed consulting case)
instances.summary: A 200-ton high-quality nickel-chrome-moly-vanadium forging fractured catastrophically, dropping a 5-ton piece that crushed an operator's chair during night shift operations.
instances.key_quote: a five-ton piece landed and crushed the chair of the operator it occurred at two o'clock in the morning he was about two or three feet away from it when the five-ton piece crushed his chair
instances.framework_lesson: Quality control specifications based on test coupons cooled differently than the actual component can mask material deficiencies; heat treatment procedures must ensure representative testing of the full mass.

cluster_id: Air Force Buy-to-Fly Ratio in Aircraft Manufacturing
display_name: Air Force Buy-to-Fly Ratio in Aircraft Manufacturing

instances.name: Air Force Buy-to-Fly Ratio in Aircraft Manufacturing
instances.summary: The Air Force used the buy-to-fly ratio metric in the 1980s-90s to track material waste in aerospace manufacturing, where some parts required 100:1 ratios of starting material to finished part weight due to subtractive manufacturing.
instances.key_quote: the Air Force they actually used to use it a lot in the '80s and 90s it's how many pounds of metal you buy in Ingot form or Billet form and in order to fly something how much does the part weigh
instances.framework_lesson: Excessive material waste in aerospace manufacturing prompted the Air Force to invest billions in near-net-shape manufacturing in the 1990s to reduce scrap losses.

cluster_id: Boeing aluminum wing manufacturing waste
display_name: Boeing aluminum wing manufacturing waste

instances.name: Boeing aluminum wing manufacturing waste
instances.summary: Boeing wing fabrication from plate stock exhibits a buy-to-fly ratio of approximately 20:1 due to extensive machining away of unwanted material.
instances.key_quote: when I talked about the Boeing Wings they start with plate and then machine away all the material they don't want and the B toly ratio on that aluminum Wing is probably something on the order of 20 to1

cluster_id: German plastic injection molding machine shaft forging lap
display_name: German plastic injection molding machine shaft forging lap

instances.name: German plastic injection molding machine shaft forging lap
instances.summary: A 20-foot long forged steel shaft for a plastic injection molding machine developed a fatigue crack after 1.5 years in service, originating from a forging lap defect approximately 1 inch long and 0.25 inches deep.
instances.key_quote: Germans had forged 20ft long shaft for a plastic injection molding machine and it had been machined and everything went into service and about a year and a half later had a big fatigue crack and it had a forging lap
instances.framework_lesson: Forging lap defects with oxidized surfaces are unmistakable due to oxide and decarbonization, yet may pass initial inspection and cause failures in rotating applications under fatigue.

cluster_id: Gold tubing manufacturer dies redesign (Boston area)
display_name: Gold tubing manufacturer dies redesign (Boston area)

instances.name: Gold tubing manufacturer dies redesign (Boston area)
instances.summary: A Boston-area gold company had empirically designed dies for deep-drawing gold tubing with illogical reduction steps (9% to 40%) causing inconsistent failure rates; Eagar redesigned them with logical progression.
instances.key_quote: some of the dies had like a less than I think one of them had a 9% reduction and another one had a 35% reduction there's no logic whatsoever
instances.framework_lesson: Dies designed by trial and error without engineering calculation of deformation progression can produce high failure rates and cracking, while seemingly light passes at 9% reduction can set up harmful residual stresses.

cluster_id: Shaul Steel mini-mill business model
display_name: Shaul Steel mini-mill business model

instances.name: Shaul Steel mini-mill business model
instances.summary: Shaul Steel, founded by MIT graduate Gordon Forward, pioneered the mini-mill concept in the mid-1970s by using cheaper scrap steel ($100/ton) instead of blast-furnace cast iron ($200/ton), gaining a $100/ton cost advantage.
instances.key_quote: Gordon realized in the mid 70s that it cost $200 a ton to make cast iron in the steel mill but you could buy scrap iron at the time scrap steel for $100 a ton

cluster_id: Leaded brass plumbing fittings lawsuit
display_name: Leaded brass plumbing fittings lawsuit

instances.name: Leaded brass plumbing fittings lawsuit

instances.key_quote: there have been hundreds of millions of dollars worth of settlements in las vegas and some other places and there's still billions of dollars worth of lawsuits because some material scientists on the west coast have determined that this type of brass the leaded brass which we've been using for about a hundred years is defective
instances.framework_lesson: Litigation over predicted five-year failure timelines in systems already operational for 20 years suggests misalignment between material science predictions and actual service performance.

cluster_id: Kresge Auditorium lead roof

cluster_id: US Army materials study

instances.key_quote: the us army did a million dollar study to figure out this is about 15 or 20 years ago what was the material of the future for the us army

cluster_id: High-nickel pipeline steel proposal
display_name: High-nickel pipeline steel proposal

instances.key_quote: they were going to use all the world's nickel per year in building that pipeline okay even just two percent of nickel in this alloying with the steel was going to consume all the nickel in the world
instances.framework_lesson: Material selection must account for global resource availability and production capacity, not just performance properties.

cluster_id: 1930s all-aluminum Duesenberg automobiles
display_name: 1930s all-aluminum Duesenberg automobiles

cluster_id: IBM summer materials science course (late 1970s)

cluster_id: MIT School of Engineering strategy retreat (circa 1990)

instances.key_quote: we spent about a day in little breakout groups we can finally came up with a what was I still think is a very good definition and i'll give it to you later but you know what about 10 years ago i asked the assistant dean donna said Vicki for a copy of it because I didn't have that little report she barely even remembered it
instances.framework_lesson: Institutional outputs from costly, high-level strategic meetings are often filed away and lost, failing to drive lasting organizational change.

cluster_id: Space Shuttle O-ring failure
display_name: Space Shuttle O-ring failure

instances.name: Space Shuttle O-ring failure

instances.key_quote: there was one chance in 10,000 of the Space Shuttle failing on launch okay and then when it failed on launch Fineman was part of the blue ribbon panel
instances.framework_lesson: NASA's probabilistic safety analysis methodology failed to capture actual failure modes; engineering judgment and ground testing revealed gaps in the analytical framework.

cluster_id: NASA missile and shuttle launch reliability
display_name: NASA missile and shuttle launch reliability

instances.name: NASA missile and shuttle launch reliability
instances.summary: Feynman noted that four percent of missiles shot since 1947 had failed on launch, and the 25th shuttle flight represented a statistically expected failure within that rate, not an anomaly.
instances.key_quote: four percent of the missiles that they had shot since 1947 or whatever had failed on launch and he also pointed out this was the 25th shuttle flight four percent
instances.framework_lesson: Media and public misunderstand statistical probability in small samples; a failure on the 25th flight was within expected rates from historical data, not an unexpected event.

cluster_id: Air Force turbine blade repair
display_name: Air Force turbine blade repair

instances.key_quote: the Air Force built a pair of a jet engines and this is a turban way that tips and worn off and they built up by welding 25 10 passes that's one millimeter so they built up about less than a centimeter

cluster_id: Boeing 777 brake certification testing
display_name: Boeing 777 brake certification testing

instances.name: Boeing 777 brake certification testing
instances.summary: During Boeing 777 certification, aircraft brakes were tested to withstand extreme stopping loads from landing a 450-ton aircraft at 100 mph, with allowance for carbon-carbon composite brake fires lasting up to three minutes before fire crew arrival.
instances.key_quote: the certification process of the 77 Boeing 777 and they basically had to land the aircraft in stop it in such and such a distance and then the brakes could catch fire but they have had at least three minutes before the fire crew can put it out
instances.framework_lesson: Certification standards must account for realistic emergency response times and allow acceptable degradation (fire) of critical components during worst-case landing scenarios.

cluster_id: Stainless steel pot cracking incident
display_name: Stainless steel pot cracking incident

cluster_id: Hot isostatic pressing vessel explosion
display_name: Hot isostatic pressing vessel explosion

instances.name: Hot isostatic pressing vessel explosion
instances.summary: A large 250-ton hot isostatic pressing vessel, approximately 22 inches thick with threaded end caps, suffered catastrophic brittle fracture and shattered into 70 pieces averaging five tons each during startup in the mid-1980s; the design was validated only for steady-state operation without transient thermal stress analysis.
instances.key_quote: one of these vessels blew up by grabs here north end over sent broke the whole thing in brittle fracture into 70 pieces in average of five tons
instances.framework_lesson: Finite element analysis was performed only for steady-state operating conditions; transient startup thermal stresses and resulting residual stress patterns were not analyzed, leading to unexpected tensile stresses at stress-concentration features.
materials_or_systems: welded construction with threaded end caps

cluster_id: LNG vessel pressure vessel failure
display_name: LNG vessel pressure vessel failure

instances.name: LNG vessel pressure vessel failure
instances.summary: An LNG vessel's large water-cooled pressure vessel failed catastrophically due to combined transient thermal stresses, improper heat treatment, hydrogen embrittlement from corrosion, and flawed fracture toughness testing methodology.
instances.key_quote: they had high stresses from the transient thermal stresses they had high-minded for micro structure from importantly treatment whatever okay and it turns out the krogan
instances.framework_lesson: Design standards for large pressure vessels did not account for transient thermal stresses during startup, and testing protocols allowed substitute coupons to pass while actual vessel material remained untested.

cluster_id: LNG vessel heat treatment coupon substitution and testing failure
display_name: LNG vessel heat treatment coupon substitution and testing failure

instances.name: LNG vessel heat treatment coupon substitution and testing failure
instances.summary: During fracture toughness qualification testing of LNG vessel plate, the manufacturer substituted small coupons for the required prolongation test pieces, which cooled faster and achieved higher toughness values, masking poor properties in the actual 200-ton vessel material.
instances.key_quote: the smaller pieces cool with a faster rate than the ramp in 200 countries and they've passed on that external teeth whereas the original one that was cooling in st. raid didn't pass
instances.framework_lesson: Material qualification standards must enforce use of representative specimen geometry and cooling rates to prevent surrogate test results from masking actual in-service material degradation.

cluster_id: Insurance policy gap in pressure vessel coverage
display_name: Insurance policy gap in pressure vessel coverage

instances.name: Insurance policy gap in pressure vessel coverage
instances.summary: A multi-million-dollar pressure vessel failure went uninsured due to a policy exclusion gap when Hartford Steam Boiler modified their standard coverage and a broker failed to notice the change, creating a situation where neither the boiler insurance nor the all-risk policy covered the loss.
instances.key_quote: the brokers who are some of this multi-million dollar policy decided to let Hartford steam boiler change their policy so I didn't quite mesh one of the things was missing
instances.framework_lesson: Commercial insurance practices for industrial machinery require careful coordination between boiler/machinery policies and all-risk policies; policy modifications by insurers can create unintended coverage gaps.

cluster_id: Insurance policy exclusion dispute
display_name: Insurance policy exclusion dispute

instances.name: Insurance policy exclusion dispute
instances.summary: A major financial company nearly faced a multi-billion-dollar liability claim after a broker allowed a client (Parker) to rewrite their insurance policy, inadvertently excluding coverage for a loss that had traditionally been covered.
instances.key_quote: you let Parker rewrite their policy and excluded this one area that is traditionally been covered but the travelers I'll say something good about two hours the head underwriter traveler says doesn't matter we are going to pay for this loss
instances.framework_lesson: Insurance contracts and exclusions must be carefully monitored; failure to track policy revisions can expose companies to liability disputes and require careful underwriter judgment to resolve.

cluster_id: Forensic investigation consulting case (unnamed)
display_name: Forensic investigation consulting case (unnamed)

instances.framework_lesson: Expert witnesses and forensic investigators must be aware that clients may limit their scope of investigation to control narrative and liability exposure, regardless of technical findings.

cluster_id: Steam boiler explosions

cluster_id: NASA X-33 space plane program
display_name: NASA X-33 space plane program

instances.name: NASA X-33 space plane program
instances.summary: NASA's attempt to develop the X-33 space plane on a 30-month schedule from design through fabrication to test flight resulted in a $1.3 billion program failure.
instances.key_quote: NASA tried to develop the x33 space plane on a 30mon schedule okay from design through fabrication to test flight and the whole $1.3 billion program was a flop
instances.framework_lesson: Compressed development schedules cannot overcome fundamental research unknowns; attempting accelerated prototyping without adequate time for problem-solving in research and development leads to project failure.

cluster_id: Weld defect consulting case — welded components with undiscovered flaws
display_name: Weld defect consulting case — welded components with undiscovered flaws

instances.name: Weld defect consulting case — welded components with undiscovered flaws
instances.summary: A customer ordered welded components to specification but discovered defects in the welds after delivery, leading to a contractual dispute about responsibility and safety.
instances.key_quote: someone says we ordered these welded components and we ordered it to this standard and we've now for whatever reason we had some problem or some went in and did something and we found that there's defects in our welds
instances.framework_lesson: Welding codes apply higher safety factors (3.3 vs. 1.67 for base material) because welds inherently contain defects; a weld can be defined as a continuous defect surrounded by good material, yet purchase orders sometimes do not account for this reality.

cluster_id: Shipyard fillet weld measurement dispute
display_name: Shipyard fillet weld measurement dispute

instances.name: Shipyard fillet weld measurement dispute
instances.summary: A maritime administration program developed a laser-based automatic inspector for fillet welds that could measure to ±0.001 inch accuracy, but revealed human inspectors could only achieve ±0.020 inch accuracy, leading to contractual disputes over weld sizes that were previously immeasurable.
instances.key_quote: we're now as we improve our measurement techniques we get into more fights okay when I was a an undergraduate student working in the lab
instances.framework_lesson: Advancing measurement capability creates new contractual disputes when specifications are written based on the limits of prior measurement technology, not actual structural requirements.

cluster_id: Bath Ironworks and Electric Boat shipyard conflicts
display_name: Bath Ironworks and Electric Boat shipyard conflicts

instances.name: Bath Ironworks and Electric Boat shipyard conflicts
instances.summary: Recurring contractual disputes between ship customers and yards (Bath Ironworks, Newport News, Electric Boat) over whether detected weld defects meet allowable specifications and who pays for additional inspection requested after contract award.

cluster_id: Soup ship fillet weld measurement dispute

cluster_id: Romanian steel pipe shortage purchase

cluster_id: Subsea pipeline tool fragment defect
display_name: Subsea pipeline tool fragment defect

instances.name: Subsea pipeline tool fragment defect
instances.summary: A broken tool fragment became lodged in the wall of a seamless steel pipe during manufacturing, passed multiple hydrostatic tests and inspection due to its location and the wedge-shaped plug, but leaked during final hydro testing at the ocean floor after the plug dislodged, requiring a $4 million recovery and repair operation.
instances.key_quote: it cost them $4 million to get it out of the ocean bring it back and the delay in the project and everything else
instances.framework_lesson: Hydrostatic testing at 40% yield strength can pass pipes with severe wall defects (up to 50% loss) that would fail more stringent non-destructive tests, creating a gap in detection capability.

cluster_id: High-strength steel cookware hydrogen cracking

cluster_id: WWI Army truck and vehicle repair

cluster_id: WWI Navy ship reconditioning
display_name: WWI Navy ship reconditioning

cluster_id: Tantalum-vanadium diffusion bonding brittle intermetallic

instances.name: Tantalum-vanadium diffusion bonding brittle intermetallic

instances.framework_lesson: Dissimilar-material bonding requires prediction of intermetallic formation; insertion of an intermediate material (niobium) that does not form brittle phases can solve the problem.

cluster_id: Navy submarine transformer solder joint failure

instances.key_quote: they put in the furnace so at four million dollars worth of transformers sitting there they analyze it and find that it's lead tin eutectic which is the most common solder alloy for electronics at the time
instances.framework_lesson: Manufacturing process control failed to enforce correct alloy specification; the wrong solder was used without detection until the thermal stress test revealed the error, requiring post-hoc metallurgical analysis to salvage the order.

cluster_id: Automotive sheet metal stretcher strain defects

instances.name: Automotive sheet metal stretcher strain defects

instances.key_quote: you'd have these ugly looking stretcher strain in your sheet metal where it had locally necked down that's why the steel companies and all the researchers were spending so much money learning about this
instances.framework_lesson: Steel chemistry control—removal of nitrogen from molten steel eliminated the upper and lower yield points and Lüders strain, eliminating the aesthetic defect and scrap loss in automotive sheet metal.
