display_name: Arc strike hydrogen cracking

instances.name: Arc strike hydrogen cracking
instances.summary: Accidental arc strikes on steel during welding create stress concentrations that initiate hydrogen-induced cracks radiating from the heat-affected zone.
instances.key_quote: Arc strikes are they may be cosmetically unappealing but they can actually be harmful and so many of the codes will require that you grind off the arc strikes
instances.framework_lesson: Welding codes now mandate removal of arc strikes despite their seeming cosmetic insignificance, because they are real crack initiation sites under residual stress.

cluster_id: Sulfide stress corrosion cracking in high-strength low-alloy steel
display_name: Sulfide stress corrosion cracking in high-strength low-alloy steel

instances.name: Sulfide stress corrosion cracking in high-strength low-alloy steel
instances.summary: High-strength low-alloy steel exposed to hydrogen sulfide gas exhibits accelerated hydrogen-induced cracking, with failure times varying dramatically by temperature: less than 2 hours at 25°C but up to 20 hours at lower temperatures where hydrogen diffusion is slower.
instances.key_quote: this actually comes from sulfide stress cracking in a high strength low alloy steel this is hydrogen sulfide uh sulfide stress corrosion cracking turns out sulfide stress corrosion cracking is merely uh another form of hydrogen cracking
instances.framework_lesson: Hydrogen cracking behavior in corrosive service (H2S environments) is temperature-dependent; codes must account for the fact that lower temperatures extend time-to-failure, giving a false sense of safety.

cluster_id: Electroplated bicycle tube fatality
display_name: Electroplated bicycle tube fatality

instances.name: Electroplated bicycle tube fatality
instances.summary: A bicyclist became a paraplegic after a hydrogen crack formed in an electroplated steel tube, which grew as a fatigue crack under service loads.
instances.key_quote: if they had plated the bicycle tube and gotten it into a 375 de Fahrenheit furnace which is the standard temperature within an hour they wouldn't have formed a crack and the boy wouldn't have the bicyclist wouldn't have become a paraplegic
instances.framework_lesson: Post-electroplating stress relief is critical to prevent hydrogen cracking in high-strength steels; failure to follow this standard practice resulted in catastrophic injury.

cluster_id: World War II ship welding hydrogen cracking lessons
display_name: World War II ship welding hydrogen cracking lessons

instances.name: World War II ship welding hydrogen cracking lessons
instances.summary: During WWII ship construction, the Navy discovered that low-hydrogen welding electrodes must be kept warm and dry to prevent delayed hydrogen cracking in welds.
instances.key_quote: we learned in the 1940s uh when we were doing welding of ships for World War II we learned you need to dry out these electrodes by keeping them warm
instances.framework_lesson: Wartime production exposed the need for strict electrode storage and handling protocols; post-weld hydrogen diffusion time became a critical control in naval and high-strength steel welding codes.

cluster_id: WWII ship welding moisture control
display_name: WWII ship welding moisture control

instances.name: WWII ship welding moisture control
instances.summary: During World War II ship construction, welders discovered that moisture in electrodes caused welding problems, leading to the adoption of electrode drying protocols in the 1940s.
instances.key_quote: when we were doing welding of ships for World War II we learned you need to dry out these electrodes by keeping them warm
instances.framework_lesson: Welding standards had to incorporate moisture control procedures for electrodes based on operational failures during mass production.

cluster_id: Nautilus submarine hydrogen cracking during construction
display_name: Nautilus submarine hydrogen cracking during construction

instances.name: Nautilus submarine hydrogen cracking during construction
instances.summary: During the mid-1950s construction of the USS Nautilus, the first nuclear submarine, delayed hydrogen cracks occurred in welded stiffener structures when high-strength HY80 steel was used without adequate hydrogen control, with cracks forming 8 hours after welding.
instances.key_quote: some guy welding at night would hear this piece that had been welded during the day and it be a big boom like a rifle shot okay a crack a hydrogen crack just ran through the part they had made that day 8 hours earlier
instances.framework_lesson: Early nuclear submarine construction lacked proper electrode storage and hydrogen control procedures for high-strength steels, leading to delayed cracking failures that forced adoption of stricter welding controls.

cluster_id: Excavator high-strength steel welding failures
display_name: Excavator high-strength steel welding failures

instances.name: Excavator high-strength steel welding failures
instances.summary: Inadequate hydrogen control during welding of excavators using 100 KSI high-strength steel resulted in fatalities among welders.
instances.year_or_era: modern era (reference to 30 years prior to 2014)
instances.key_quote: these excavators a lot of them are using 100 KSI strength material and stupid if you don't kind of control your hydrogen on a high strength steel and I've seen people lose their lives because of it
instances.framework_lesson: Field welding of high-strength construction equipment without proper hydrogen control procedures has resulted in catastrophic failures and loss of life.

cluster_id: 1950s submarine hydrogen cracking
display_name: 1950s submarine hydrogen cracking

instances.name: 1950s submarine hydrogen cracking
instances.summary: Submarine construction with egg-crate stiffeners experienced delayed hydrogen cracking failures when welds made during the day cracked at night, with hydrogen diffusing to crack tips over 8 hours, producing loud rifle-shot-like sounds.
instances.key_quote: some guy welding at night would hear this piece that had been welded during the day and it be a big boom like a rifle shot okay a crack a hydrogen crack just ran through the part
instances.framework_lesson: Poor hydrogen control in welding electrodes and lack of strict moisture management protocols allowed hydrogen to diffuse into steel after welding, causing delayed cracking failures that codes had not adequately addressed.

cluster_id: Boston Molasses Flood
display_name: Boston Molasses Flood

instances.name: Boston molasses disaster
instances.summary: A large structure storing molasses ruptured in Boston, killing people and demonstrating the need for design standards on liquid-storage vessels in populated areas.
instances.key_quote: when we had the uh the molasses disaster in boston very long time ago um i don't think that led to a border and pressure vessel code where it led people to realize we can't have structures that are storing large quantities of liquid in a city without having any control over how it's designed
instances.framework_lesson: The disaster prompted the creation of pressure vessel codes to regulate the design and strength of large liquid-storage structures in populated areas.

cluster_id: Regional jet wing flap heating system design case
display_name: Regional jet wing flap heating system design case

instances.name: Regional jet wing flap heating system design case
instances.summary: A Canadian aircraft manufacturer redesigned a heated wing-leading-edge component from titanium to aluminum and then considered casting it in titanium to reduce cost while maintaining safety certification.
instances.key_quote: they had these phd guys that were putting sensors and sending the thing up in the air and running it to have some idea of the temperature but they never really came up with this is the maximum value it's never going to see any higher temperature than that
instances.framework_lesson: Uncertainty in operating temperature (from variable engine bleed-air heating) forced material and design selection toward higher-temperature-resistant alloys despite the cost and manufacturing complexity tradeoff.

cluster_id: Aircraft wing casting substitution project
display_name: Aircraft wing casting substitution project

instances.name: Aircraft wing casting substitution project
instances.summary: Boeing replaced a machined titanium structural component with a casting to reduce manufacturing waste, requiring extensive non-destructive testing and full-scale wing fatigue testing to validate equivalence.
instances.key_quote: it took a long time for the aircraft people to say fine we can fly with castings and there were a lot of obstacles in the way
instances.framework_lesson: Casting materials require manufacturing reliability inspection (100% defect screening) and higher safety factors than wrought materials, even when material properties are superior, because hidden defects from mold contamination cannot be predicted by standard property testing.

cluster_id: Landing gear torsional cracking failure
display_name: Landing gear torsional cracking failure

instances.name: Landing gear torsional cracking failure
instances.summary: A heavy steel landing gear component developed unexpected rapid crack growth during service-level testing due to unanalyzed torsional loading that dominated over the analyzed bending and tension modes.
instances.key_quote: because of a torsional effect on one of the structural component that they never really analyzed for torsion they did the tension the bending on it they never really thought that torsion would become a dominant mode
instances.framework_lesson: Incomplete loading analysis in complex assemblies can fail to identify dominant failure modes, and late-stage discovery of such modes creates expensive redesign cascades affecting aircraft weight and cost baseline.

cluster_id: Unnamed aerospace rib structure design failure
display_name: Unnamed aerospace rib structure design failure

instances.name: Unnamed aerospace rib structure design failure
instances.summary: A critical aerospace component (rib structure) failed in a specific loading mode that was not anticipated during initial design, requiring late-stage redesign decisions involving material changes and cost-weight tradeoffs.
instances.key_quote: you knew you screwed up in a very specific loading mode that you didn't expect in a full assembly
instances.framework_lesson: Component-level testing cannot fully predict failure modes in full assemblies with complex multi-directional loading (bending, torsion, shear) that were not analyzed beforehand.

cluster_id: Building extension snow drift loading design case
display_name: Building extension snow drift loading design case

instances.name: Building extension snow drift loading design case
instances.summary: A building addition at different height (e.g., Costco warehouse extension) created unanticipated snow drift loads that structural designers must account for via prescriptive code requirements based on elevation difference and proximity to edges.
instances.key_quote: if you are designing a roof as a structural engineer i don't suggest you do it if you're not a structural engineer but um you have to go by very specific requirements for the location
instances.framework_lesson: Snow load codes are highly prescriptive and location-specific, requiring engineers to account for drift created by building geometry changes and mechanical systems that may not be obvious in initial design.

cluster_id: Steel joist warehouse collapses
display_name: Steel joist warehouse collapses

instances.name: Steel joist warehouse collapses
instances.summary: Nine steel joist collapses examined over a one-year period, with eight traced to welding defects at critical connections rather than design inadequacy.
instances.key_quote: out of nine different collapses that i've looked at over about a one year period there was only one job that there were they didn't have any welding issue
instances.framework_lesson: Codes specify inspection criteria for welds, but these criteria are not consistently enforced in industry practice, creating a gap between design demand and actual capacity.

cluster_id: Tomato greenhouse/warehouse weld failure
display_name: Tomato greenhouse/warehouse weld failure

instances.name: Tomato greenhouse/warehouse weld failure
instances.summary: A large tomato greenhouse/warehouse experienced weld failures where low-contact-angle fillet welds pulled away from the cord rather than failing in the base metal.
instances.key_quote: that's a big warehouse for tomatoes greenhouse that i worked on and same story where you look at this well and we talked a little bit about convexity
instances.framework_lesson: Insufficient weld fusion at low contact angles creates a hidden failure mode where the weld peels off rather than tearing metal, requiring either bilateral welds or explicit acceptance criteria.

cluster_id: Single-sided eccentric weld on rectangular tube connection
display_name: Single-sided eccentric weld on rectangular tube connection

instances.name: Single-sided eccentric weld on rectangular tube connection
instances.summary: A joist connection to a rectangular tube was welded only on one side and off-center to the tube, creating eccentricity, stress concentration, and no redundancy or inspection access.
instances.key_quote: they had this weld connecting it here off centered from that rectangular tube and they had no welds on the opposite side you can see here there's this little amount that's coming off here the paint's coming off
instances.framework_lesson: Design-for-inspectability and design-for-redundancy are capacity-side factors that codes do not quantify; reliance on welder expertise without redundancy creates unquantifiable risk.

cluster_id: Shaft designed to fail within 400 hours
display_name: Shaft designed to fail within 400 hours

instances.name: Shaft designed to fail within 400 hours
instances.summary: A shaft was deliberately designed with a safety factor less than one to fail within 400 hours of service, creating very low effective safety factors over a 10-year design life.
instances.key_quote: we had that popular shaft example that was designed to fail within 400 hours and therefore you know the fake safety factor for 10 year life is less than one
instances.framework_lesson: Initial safety factors do not reflect true safety margins over the actual service life when degradation, maintenance failures, or manufacturing defects occur in practice.

cluster_id: Iron sulfide embrittlement in early steel production
display_name: Iron sulfide embrittlement in early steel production

instances.name: Iron sulfide embrittlement in early steel production
instances.summary: Early steelmakers could not produce good steel until finding Czechoslovak ore with high manganese content, because manganese sulfide is the only element that will tie up sulfur impurities at iron's melting point; without it, liquid iron sulfide at grain boundaries causes rolling failure.
instances.key_quote: you end up getting liquid iron sulfide at the grain boundaries you try to heat it up to roll it and it's trying to roll a Slurpee
instances.framework_lesson: Material composition control and impurity chemistry are critical to processability; sulfur content and manganese availability constrained early steelmaking geography.

cluster_id: MIT basement stainless steel carbon control technology
display_name: MIT basement stainless steel carbon control technology

instances.name: MIT basement stainless steel carbon control technology
instances.summary: A graduate student at MIT developed doctoral research on carbon reaction control in Building 8 during the 1950s, which when commercialized transformed the stainless steel industry by enabling reproducibly low-carbon steels and reducing costs.
instances.key_quote: the technology to make very low carbon steels which is very important in the stainless steel industry was invented in the basement of building eight here okay in the 1950s
instances.framework_lesson: Carbon control technology was essential to stainless steel corrosion resistance, and academic metallurgical research directly enabled commercial transformation of the industry.

cluster_id: Medieval sword tempering practice
display_name: Medieval sword tempering practice

instances.name: Medieval sword tempering practice
instances.summary: During the Crusades, blacksmiths would quench hot steel by running it through a living slave's body to achieve superior hardness and killing properties.
instances.key_quote: during the crusades the best quenching the one that gave it the i don't know what it gave it but they used to take a living slave and they would quench the hot steel by running it through the slave

cluster_id: European steel quality problems (1870s-1880s)
display_name: European steel quality problems (1870s-1880s)

instances.name: European steel quality problems (1870s-1880s)
instances.summary: European steelmakers in the 1870s-1880s faced quality problems due to high phosphorus and sulfur content from their coal sources, while American producers had access to cleaner Pennsylvania coal.
instances.key_quote: people found that just straight iron and carbon wasn't good enough in the 1870s and 1880s particularly in europe where they had a lot of phosphorus in their steel and they had a fair amount of sulfur which came from the coal

cluster_id: 19th-century European steel phosphorus and sulfur brittleness
display_name: 19th-century European steel phosphorus and sulfur brittleness

instances.name: 19th-century European steel phosphorus and sulfur brittleness
instances.summary: European steelmakers in the 1870s-1880s produced brittle steel due to high phosphorus and sulfur content from poor coal quality, causing brittleness at grain boundaries.
instances.key_quote: sulfur and steel can be a real problem because it forms a liquid iron sulfide you end up with not a solid piece of steel but something sort of like a slurpee
instances.framework_lesson: Steelmakers discovered that manganese addition was necessary to combat sulfur embrittlement, establishing manganese as an essential alloying element for steel quality.

cluster_id: Welding industry manganese litigation
display_name: Welding industry manganese litigation

instances.name: Welding industry manganese litigation
instances.summary: In the 1980s-1990s, plaintiff attorneys filed approximately 100,000 lawsuits against the welding industry claiming welders' neurological disorders were caused by manganese exposure from welding fumes, costing the industry over $100 million in legal defense.
instances.key_quote: the welding industry was spending over a hundred million dollars in legal fees a few years ago to defend themselves against what would be a hundred thousand cases
instances.framework_lesson: The litigation conflated manganism (manganese poisoning from excessive exposure) with idiopathic Parkinson's disease; subsequent scientific proof that manganism affects different brain regions than Parkinson's vindicated the industry, but only after enormous legal expenditure.

cluster_id: Eagar manganese deposition expert witness testimony
display_name: Eagar manganese deposition expert witness testimony

instances.name: Eagar manganese deposition expert witness testimony
instances.summary: Eagar testified as an expert witness defending the welding industry in a manganese litigation case in Danville, Illinois, where he explained manganese is essential for health and present in common foods.
instances.key_quote: i actually helped defend the welding industry on those back in the 90s i was in danville illinois

cluster_id: General Motors Linden New Jersey locating pin failure
display_name: General Motors Linden New Jersey locating pin failure

instances.name: General Motors Linden New Jersey locating pin failure
instances.summary: GM's Linden, New Jersey plant produced significant scrap due to failure to maintain a locating pin in the manufacturing process, which could have been fixed by replacing a two-dollar part.
instances.key_quote: you just replace this little two dollar locating pin which is just not pulling it out with pliers and hammering in a new one in and the guy wouldn't would have been able to put the part on there each time
instances.framework_lesson: Maintenance engineers failed to inspect and maintain critical components, resulting in scrap rates of several percent when they should have been achieving parts-per-million defect levels.

cluster_id: Car fleet starting failures (maintenance case)
display_name: Car fleet starting failures (maintenance case)

instances.name: Car fleet starting failures (maintenance case)
instances.summary: A factory fleet of cars frequently failed to start; management changed policy from towing to pushing, which motivated workers to pressure engineers to fix the root cause.
instances.key_quote: new manager came in says no more tow motors if it doesn't start you push okay and so all of a sudden the hourly workers are now pushing cars 150 yards out to the far away parking lot and they didn't like that
instances.framework_lesson: Manufacturing defect rates reflect what management is willing to accept; when the burden of defects is shifted to workers, pressure to eliminate root causes increases.

cluster_id: Toyota supplier quality crisis (1990s)
display_name: Toyota supplier quality crisis (1990s)

instances.name: Toyota supplier quality crisis (1990s)
instances.summary: A parts supplier to Toyota, Honda, GM, and Ford received an urgent call from Toyota at 11 a.m. demanding the supplier appear at the plant at 1 p.m. to address a quality problem with supplied parts.
instances.key_quote: they got a call from Toyota that morning ok at 11 o'clock and they said we got a problem with your parts be up here at 1:00 o'clock well his office was two hours away from the Toyota plant
instances.framework_lesson: Japanese manufacturers treated supplier quality problems collaboratively, working with suppliers to fix issues, contrasting with the adversarial approach used by American auto companies which threatened litigation.

cluster_id: Waltham welding plant Navy issue
display_name: Waltham welding plant Navy issue

instances.name: Waltham welding plant Navy issue
instances.summary: A Navy contractor in Waltham faced a welding problem that shut down the plant; Eagar determined it was a minor issue and wrote documentation for the Navy, getting them back in operation by Friday.
instances.key_quote: this is a tempest in a teapot and I won't go through this one of my welding courses like I described what the real problem was

cluster_id: Upton Sinclair meatpacking industry exposure and Food and Drug Act
display_name: Upton Sinclair meatpacking industry exposure and Food and Drug Act

instances.name: Upton Sinclair meatpacking industry exposure and Food and Drug Act
instances.summary: Upton Sinclair's book exposing unsanitary conditions in the meat-packing industry prompted Congress to pass the Food and Drug Act of 1906.
instances.key_quote: Upton Sinclair wrote a book about how bad the meat producing industry was and got Congress to write the Food and Drug Act of 1906
instances.framework_lesson: Industrial failures in sanitation and manufacturing control led to federal legislation mandating food and drug quality standards.

cluster_id: Government helicopter design approval and manufacturer liability protection
display_name: Government helicopter design approval and manufacturer liability protection

instances.name: Government helicopter design approval and manufacturer liability protection
instances.summary: A Supreme Court decision or early-1990s law established that defense contractors (Bell, Sikorsky, McDonnell Douglas) cannot be sued for design defects if the government approved the design in writing, though manufacturing defects remain actionable.
instances.key_quote: there's a law in the early 90s or Supreme Court decision where you cannot sue bell or Sikorsky or McDonnell Douglas on a design defect theory because the government designed it
instances.framework_lesson: Government assumption of design responsibility shifts liability from manufacturers to the state, incentivizing contractors to follow approved specifications exactly.

cluster_id: Thixotropic/semi-solid casting patent suppression at MIT
display_name: Thixotropic/semi-solid casting patent suppression at MIT

instances.name: Thixotropic/semi-solid casting patent suppression at MIT
instances.summary: MIT licensed a semi-solid casting technology to a company that deliberately kept it off the market for 10-15 years as competitive suppression, causing MIT to overhaul its patent licensing policy in the mid-1980s.
instances.key_quote: they licensed it to a company that sat on the patent for about 10 or 15 years and never did anything with the technology and this is actually why MIT changed its whole patent position
instances.framework_lesson: MIT's original patent licensing terms lacked march-in rights, allowing licensees to suppress competing technologies; the failure led MIT to implement mandatory march-in clauses requiring licensees to commercialize or lose exclusive rights.

cluster_id: MIT patent licensing failure - Rio casting technology
display_name: MIT patent licensing failure - Rio casting technology

instances.name: MIT patent licensing failure - Rio casting technology
instances.summary: MIT licensed a patented Rio casting technology to a company that sat on the patent for 10-15 years without commercializing it, deliberately keeping it out of the marketplace as a competitive threat.
instances.key_quote: they licensed it to a company that didn't want to see it come to Market and they're you know because it was competition for them so the company licensed it to keep it out of the marketplace
instances.framework_lesson: MIT's failure to include march-in rights in early patent licenses allowed licensees to suppress innovations; this led MIT to change its entire patent policy in the mid-1980s to include march-in rights for abandoned licenses.

cluster_id: Early jet engine turbine blades (1940s-1950s)
display_name: Early jet engine turbine blades (1940s-1950s)

instances.name: Early jet engine turbine blades (1940s-1950s)
instances.summary: Gas turbine engines developed in the 1940s-50s used polycrystalline cast blades or machined steel compressor blades, which had limited high-temperature capability compared to later directionally-solidified and single-crystal designs.
instances.key_quote: in the late 40s that's when Jets actually the this the Germans uh had some um experimental Jets at the very end of World War II jet aircraft um but after World War II we started building Jets and those jet engines it was just poly crystallin cast turbine blades
materials_or_systems: polycrystalline cast nickel alloy turbine blades

cluster_id: Modern single-crystal turbine blade casting (core shift defect)
display_name: Modern single-crystal turbine blade casting (core shift defect)

instances.name: Modern single-crystal turbine blade casting (core shift defect)
instances.summary: Single-crystal turbine blades with ceramic cores used in modern jet engines suffer from core shift defects during casting that can eliminate wall thickness, requiring 100% CAT scan inspection and scrapping up to 30% of production.
instances.key_quote: if you have a little bit of core shift you've got a ceramic core in the middle of that thing when you're growing this thing and you get a little core shift of 5 or 10,000s and you got no wall left

cluster_id: US Army M16 firing pin semi-solid steel injection molding program
display_name: US Army M16 firing pin semi-solid steel injection molding program

instances.name: US Army M16 firing pin semi-solid steel injection molding program
instances.summary: The US Army funded a 1970s program to injection mold semi-solid steel for M16 firing pins, but it failed because mold materials could not withstand the required temperatures, making the process uneconomical despite successfully producing parts.
instances.key_quote: The Army gave them millions of dollars back in the 1970s to see if they could injection mold a semi-solid steel okay and this is actually injection molding you're pressing the semisolid material into the cavity

cluster_id: NASA deep-space mission plutonium-fueled thermoelectric generators with iridium containment
display_name: NASA deep-space mission plutonium-fueled thermoelectric generators with iridium containment

instances.name: NASA deep-space mission plutonium-fueled thermoelectric generators with iridium containment
instances.summary: NASA launches iridium-shelled plutonium heat sources aboard rockets for deep-space missions to outer planets and Pluto, risking environmental contamination of the Atlantic Ocean if launch fails, using iridium because it maintains integrity at 3000°C and withstands plutonium's long radioactive decay.
instances.key_quote: they take a thermoelectric generator just a semiconductor and you have to heat one side and cool the other side it's easy to cool things out there in Far space okay you just expose it to the you know have a radiator and let it radiate out to the cold

cluster_id: MIT classroom copper pipe theft
display_name: MIT classroom copper pipe theft

instances.name: MIT classroom copper pipe theft
instances.summary: Copper pipes worth $88,000 were stolen from a classroom heating and cooling system during renovation; surveillance camera footage identified a contractor who had been brought in during normal hours and returned in off-hours to steal the materials.
instances.key_quote: someone was talking about they had lost $88,000 worth of copper pipe which actually nowadays is not that much okay cuz copper prices have gone way up

cluster_id: Vancouver World's Fair gold coin
display_name: Vancouver World's Fair gold coin

instances.name: Vancouver World's Fair gold coin
instances.summary: A 36-inch diameter, 3/4-inch thick 14-karat gold coin valued at $4-5 million was fabricated by casting 6-inch wide gold bars and welding them together without distortion for display at the 1986 Vancouver World's Fair.
instances.key_quote: we cut cast 3/4 in thick 6in wide gold 14 karat gold bars and then I had to weld them together okay without Distortion and make a 36 in diameter coin

cluster_id: Hazlet steel band rupture with molten copper
display_name: Hazlet steel band rupture with molten copper

instances.name: Hazlet steel band rupture with molten copper
instances.summary: At the Hazlet Company continuous casting operation in Burlington, Vermont, rupture of the thin steel band during copper casting creates a hazard of molten copper contacting water, generating steam explosions.
instances.key_quote: it's a real mess if you break one of these bands and you now have molten copper in contact with water and you generate Steam and explosions and other things

cluster_id: Continuous casting of steel strip (50-year unsolved challenge)
display_name: Continuous casting of steel strip (50-year unsolved challenge)

instances.name: Continuous casting of steel strip (50-year unsolved challenge)
instances.summary: For 50 years, the steel industry has attempted to develop continuous casting of thin steel strip but has been unable to succeed, unlike the proven process for copper.
instances.key_quote: people have been trying to make continuously cast steel strip for 50 years and no one's really been able to do it they still make it as a 10in continuously cast plate and they roll that down
instances.framework_lesson: Steel's melting temperature of 2600°F versus copper's thermal conductivity advantage makes the 600°F difference a critical barrier to adopting the same continuous casting method.

cluster_id: Reading Pennsylvania copper plant deoxidation process
display_name: Reading Pennsylvania copper plant deoxidation process

instances.name: Reading Pennsylvania copper plant deoxidation process
instances.summary: A copper refining plant in Reading, Pennsylvania uses tree trunks immersed in molten copper baths to deoxidize the metal through hydrocarbon combustion, an ancient technology still in use.
instances.key_quote: there's one in Reading Pennsylvania that I went through once I had a student do a thesis down there um they actually use green uh tree trunks the lots of sap in them

cluster_id: Providence catenary wire test track failure
display_name: Providence catenary wire test track failure

instances.name: Providence catenary wire test track failure
instances.summary: Six miles of defective catenary wire installed in Providence despite explicit Amtrak orders not to use the material; when a test train ran down the track, the wire sparked visibly due to surface waviness, confirming the material was unsuitable.
instances.key_quote: they ran a train down it was just a 4th of July all over the place um but uh that was their big mistake because it wasn't clear that I could back up with data
instances.framework_lesson: Field testing can provide undeniable evidence of failure where laboratory data alone may be disputed; direct observation trumps expert assessment when the specification itself is contested.

cluster_id: Ford Aerostar axle fatigue failures
display_name: Ford Aerostar axle fatigue failures

instances.name: Ford Aerostar axle fatigue failures
instances.summary: Ford discovered in the mid-1980s that high-strength steel axles on the Aerostar van were failing by fatigue at welds, contrary to their expectation that higher strength would improve fatigue life.
instances.key_quote: they were finding that people's axles were fatiguing not a good thing okay on the rear axles and so they had a huge program in the late 80s to look at fatigue strength of wells at ford
instances.framework_lesson: Design engineers at Ford did not understand that fatigue strength of welded joints does not benefit from increased base material strength, a principle known to bridge and construction engineers for 40 years prior.

cluster_id: Ford aluminum welding fatigue problem
display_name: Ford aluminum welding fatigue problem

instances.name: Ford aluminum welding fatigue problem
instances.summary: Ford discovered 30 years prior (circa 1984) that welding aluminum structures caused loss of high-strength material advantages due to weld toe stress concentrations.
instances.key_quote: the surprise for a lot of people particularly ford 30 years ago was that when you weld it you lose all your advantage of high strength

cluster_id: Guthrie weld toe fatigue research
display_name: Guthrie weld toe fatigue research

instances.name: Guthrie weld toe fatigue research
instances.summary: British Welding Institute researcher Guthrie demonstrated in the 1960s that perfect weld toe profiles and TIG dressing techniques could recover fatigue strength in high-strength welded steels.
instances.key_quote: guthrie back in the 60s kind of made part of his career at the british welding institute was showing that you could get that fatigue strength with higher strength steels

cluster_id: Caterpillar earthmoving machinery fatigue study
display_name: Caterpillar earthmoving machinery fatigue study

instances.name: Caterpillar earthmoving machinery fatigue study
instances.summary: Caterpillar, which employs 5,000 welders in Peoria, Illinois, conducted a major multi-year proprietary program to develop welding procedures that improve fatigue strength in fatigue-loaded earthmoving equipment to enable use of higher-strength steels.
instances.key_quote: caterpillar was having a big study because their earth moving machinery is fatigue loaded right big heavy welds fatigue loaded and they were trying to get welding procedures that would give you a nice profile that would improve the fatigue strength

cluster_id: Coast Guard cutter bracket undermatching case
display_name: Coast Guard cutter bracket undermatching case

instances.name: Coast Guard cutter bracket undermatching case
instances.summary: Coast Guard cutters used undermatched filler metal (lower strength) on high-strength steel brackets for beams where corrosion resistance and toughness were prioritized over full strength matching.
instances.key_quote: these brackets on the coast guard cutters because they had plenty of weld area they were welding a high strength steel because they needed a deflection of the beam but they had plenty of weld area holding it together so you can go to lower strength
materials_or_systems: stainless steel welds for corrosion resistance

cluster_id: US Navy superaustenitic submarine material evaluation
display_name: US Navy superaustenitic submarine material evaluation

instances.name: US Navy superaustenitic submarine material evaluation
instances.summary: The US Navy evaluated replacing HY-100 steel with non-magnetic superaustenitic stainless steel for submarines to reduce magnetic signature detection, but abandoned the approach due to cost and changing threat assessment.
instances.key_quote: 10 or 15 years ago the us navy was thinking built about getting rid of hy100 and building a super austenitic stainless steel submarine because it's non-magnetic

cluster_id: Alcoa Tennessee labor violence (1910)
display_name: Alcoa Tennessee labor violence (1910)

instances.name: Alcoa Tennessee labor violence (1910)
instances.summary: Alcoa aluminum smelting plant in Knoxville brought in Pinkerton guards during a labor strike around 1910, resulting in shooting deaths of union workers; the incident remained a source of bitter worker memory decades later.
instances.key_quote: back around 1910 or something they had a strike and alcohol brought in the Pinkerton guards and someone among the Pinkerton guards shot at the people who were the Union and a couple of people died

cluster_id: Alcoa Tennessee 1980s strike and worker conditions
display_name: Alcoa Tennessee 1980s strike and worker conditions

instances.name: Alcoa Tennessee 1980s strike and worker conditions
instances.summary: During a late-1980s strike at Alcoa Tennessee, management was brought in to run the plant; a worker revealed in a management meeting that the strike was prompted by intolerable sanitary conditions, including a wooden toilet seat that caused bleeding.
instances.key_quote: I get this big cut from the splinters on the wooden toilet seat I'm bleeding I do my business and I reach to find the toilet paper no toilet paper gentlemen this is what this strike is about
instances.framework_lesson: Management failure to listen to worker grievances and provide basic workplace sanitation drives labor unrest.

cluster_id: General Motors Chevy Blazer welding problem (Linden, New Jersey)
display_name: General Motors Chevy Blazer welding problem (Linden, New Jersey)

instances.name: General Motors Chevy Blazer welding problem (Linden, New Jersey)
instances.summary: A GM manufacturing plant in Linden, New Jersey producing Chevy Blazers experienced poor arc welds on door hinges; investigation revealed the electrical ground was contaminated with oil from the stamping process, preventing proper electrical contact.
instances.key_quote: I walk in we go walk up to the machine it's break time and I go up and look at it and his oil all over the electrical ground
instances.framework_lesson: Cross-process contamination (oil from stamping remaining on parts for welding) was not controlled, compromising electrical ground quality in the welding operation.

cluster_id: Power plant strike at Boston Edison (Everett)
display_name: Power plant strike at Boston Edison (Everett)

instances.name: Power plant strike at Boston Edison (Everett)
instances.summary: During a strike at a Boston Edison power plant in Everett, management brought in downtown engineers to operate the facility; one engineer unfamiliar with the equipment was confronted by a plant foreman when he expressed amazement at the size of the boiler he had designed.
instances.key_quote: he walks in and he says boy it sure is big as he looks up at this huge boiler and the foreman who's one of the regular workers in the plant says well you designed it
instances.framework_lesson: Engineers who design equipment may lack practical understanding of operating conditions and scale; frontline workers possess critical operational knowledge that management ignores.

cluster_id: Manufacturing plant electrical grounding and welding defects
display_name: Manufacturing plant electrical grounding and welding defects

instances.name: Manufacturing plant electrical grounding and welding defects
instances.summary: A manufacturing plant experienced poor weld quality and electrical grounding failures due to inadequate degreasing of stamped metal parts, worn locating pins in fixtures, and lack of maintenance procedures.
instances.key_quote: you can't get in a good electrical ground through oil I said well in that bout that time the hourly worker runs the Machine comes up

cluster_id: Alaska highway asphalt degradation
display_name: Alaska highway asphalt degradation

instances.name: Alaska highway asphalt degradation
instances.summary: Severe winter cycles in Fairbanks and northern Alaska cause asphalt to crack and fail due to extreme thermal expansion and contraction, making road maintenance economically unfeasible.
instances.key_quote: in Alaska you get a much above Fairbanks and they don't even bother to pave the roads because you'd have to rebuild the road every spring
instances.framework_lesson: No existing material can survive the severe thermal cycling regime in extreme arctic climates, requiring alternative infrastructure approaches.

cluster_id: University of Alaska asphalt research program
display_name: University of Alaska asphalt research program

instances.name: University of Alaska asphalt research program
instances.summary: Research program to develop improved asphalt formulations for southern Alaska due to the enormous cost of road repairs caused by severe winter conditions.
instances.key_quote: they had a big program for the southern half of Alaska to get better asphalt's because they spend a tremendous amount of money repairing those roads

cluster_id: Canadian aluminum-silicon carbide composite casting development
display_name: Canadian aluminum-silicon carbide composite casting development

instances.name: Canadian aluminum-silicon carbide composite casting development
instances.summary: A Canadian company developed an inexpensive casting technique to embed silicon carbide particles in aluminum, generating significant industry interest in the late 1970s-early 1980s.
instances.key_quote: back 30 40 years ago 35 years ago someone up in Canada developed a technique to mix silicon carbide in with aluminum and cast it
instances.framework_lesson: Composites that gain one property (strength) through reinforcement often lose critical properties in other domains (toughness, formability, corrosion resistance, cost), limiting practical applicability.

cluster_id: Fiberglass truck bed trial at coal mine
display_name: Fiberglass truck bed trial at coal mine

instances.name: Fiberglass truck bed trial at coal mine
instances.summary: Ford-developed fiberglass and resin-molded composite truck beds were tested alongside steel beds at a coal mine; the composite beds outperformed steel by not cracking or denting, causing the coal company to refuse to return them.
instances.key_quote: they gave some steel truck beds and some fiberglass truck beds and they were supposed to put them in service for six months and send them back but the companies the coal companies didn't want to give back the fiber of the resin mold and composite beds
instances.framework_lesson: Superior material performance in field trials may be overridden by cost considerations in high-volume production, leading to compromise solutions like plastic liners on steel beds.

cluster_id: New England highway bridge rebar corrosion
display_name: New England highway bridge rebar corrosion

instances.name: New England highway bridge rebar corrosion
instances.summary: Rebar corrosion in concrete bridge decks causes concrete spalling and potholes, requiring bridge deck replacement every ~20 years throughout New England highways.
instances.key_quote: the rebar in the concrete rusted the rust expands causes the concrete to fall and you end up with potholes on your highways if someone hits them at 70 miles an hour they'll have a wreck
instances.framework_lesson: Standard steel rebar lacks corrosion resistance in salt/moisture environments; attempted solutions (stainless steel, plastic coatings, nickel-base rebar) face cost or material availability constraints.

cluster_id: Longfellow Bridge guardrail perforation
display_name: Longfellow Bridge guardrail perforation

instances.name: Longfellow Bridge guardrail perforation
instances.summary: Cast iron and steel handrails and guardrails on the Longfellow Bridge in Boston have corroded through completely after ~100 years of exposure to acid rain and snow melt.
instances.key_quote: snow in New England is acid rain it's just frozen acid rain and when it melts its ph 5.5 and it will start eating away that steel and you see the fence rails the guardrails to keep you from falling into the to the Charles are all rusted away all the way through perforated after a hundred years

cluster_id: Nuclear submarine welding hydrogen embrittlement cracks
display_name: Nuclear submarine welding hydrogen embrittlement cracks

instances.name: Nuclear submarine welding hydrogen embrittlement cracks
instances.summary: During submarine construction in Groton, Connecticut, uncontrolled hydrogen during welding of high-strength pressure vessels causes delayed brittle cracks (hydrogen embrittlement) that propagate suddenly with audible bangs.
instances.key_quote: they're welding nuclear submarines down in Groton Connecticut and if they don't control the hydrogen while they're welding these big egg-crate type of constructions guys will be welding during the day and the guys at night who are welding will hear a bang like a rifle shot in the shop
instances.framework_lesson: Hydrogen control during welding of high-strength steels is critical; ASTM specifications mandate stress-relief baking at 375°F post-weld to diffuse hydrogen before it accumulates at imperfections and causes delayed cracking.

cluster_id: Groton Connecticut submarine welding hydrogen embrittlement failures
display_name: Groton Connecticut submarine welding hydrogen embrittlement failures

instances.name: Groton Connecticut submarine welding hydrogen embrittlement failures
instances.summary: During night-shift welding of high-strength pressure vessel egg-crate structures for nuclear submarines, workers heard sharp rifle-shot-like bangs as brittle cracks suddenly propagated through steel welds due to hydrogen embrittlement.
instances.key_quote: they're welding nuclear submarines down in Groton Connecticut and if they don't control the hydrogen while they're welding and these big egg-crate type of constructions to make a you know high-strength pressure vessel guys will be welding during the day and the guys at night who are welding will hear a bang like a rifle shot in the shop
instances.framework_lesson: Hydrogen embrittlement in high-strength steels requires active control during welding operations; without hydrogen management, delayed cracking can occur hours after welding is complete.

cluster_id: Steel-toe boot heat treating tank stress corrosion cracking / hydrogen embrittlement
display_name: Steel-toe boot heat treating tank stress corrosion cracking / hydrogen embrittlement

instances.name: Steel-toe boot heat treating tank stress corrosion cracking / hydrogen embrittlement
instances.summary: A newly installed heat-treating tank at a shop near an airport, filled with sodium nitrate salt solution for making high-strength steel-toe boots, developed cracks in the welds; initial diagnosis of stress corrosion cracking was later corrected to hydrogen embrittlement through polarity reversal testing.
instances.key_quote: they were putting in a big heat training tank and it was all welded construction and they were filling it up like a swimming pool about 50 yards long and about five yards wide and three yards deep and all of a sudden it started leaking there were cracks
instances.framework_lesson: Published metallurgical literature can conflate hydrogen embrittlement with stress corrosion cracking; cathodic polarity testing is a diagnostic tool that can distinguish the two mechanisms and reveal the true failure mode.
materials_or_systems: hydrogen embrittlement vs. stress corrosion cracking

cluster_id: Bolt hydrogen embrittlement cracking dispute
display_name: Bolt hydrogen embrittlement cracking dispute

instances.name: Bolt hydrogen embrittlement cracking dispute
instances.summary: A bolt that had cracked in service was examined and debated between Tom Eagar and another metallurgist (Doctrine Palmar) regarding whether failure was due to hydrogen embrittlement or stress corrosion cracking.
instances.key_quote: doctrine palmar sent around a piece of bolt that had cracked in service and then he and I are still debating about whether it's hydrogen embrittlement or stress corrosion cracking
instances.framework_lesson: Hydrogen embrittlement and stress corrosion cracking are mechanistically distinct (cathode vs. anode) but are often conflated in practice; proper diagnosis requires understanding the electrochemical environment and crack location.

cluster_id: Steel worker arm amputation at Saint Elizabeth's Hospital
display_name: Steel worker arm amputation at Saint Elizabeth's Hospital

instances.name: Steel worker arm amputation at Saint Elizabeth's Hospital
instances.summary: Steel workers in Bethlehem, Pennsylvania suffered traumatic arm amputations from hot steel, with cauterization preventing bleeding as the steel sliced through tissue.
instances.key_quote: some guy get his arm cut off by a piece of hot steel just sliced through your arm like butter but there's no bleeding because it cauterized as it goes

cluster_id: U.S. Navy vibratory stress relief research program
display_name: U.S. Navy vibratory stress relief research program

instances.name: U.S. Navy vibratory stress relief research program
instances.summary: The U.S. Navy spent over a million dollars investigating whether vibratory stress relief could replace thermal stress relief on pressure vessels and concluded it does not work.
instances.key_quote: the u.s navy spent over a million dollars trying to prove whether vibratory stress relief works they don't use it so what do you think they concluded
instances.framework_lesson: Unproven alternative stress-relief methods can be marketed to engineers despite lack of validation; regulatory spending is required to definitively reject pseudoscientific approaches.

cluster_id: British Welding Institute vibratory stress relief evaluation
display_name: British Welding Institute vibratory stress relief evaluation

instances.name: British Welding Institute vibratory stress relief evaluation
instances.summary: The British Welding Institute conducted a multi-million dollar study for oil companies attempting to validate vibratory stress relief as a cost-saving alternative to thermal stress relief.
instances.key_quote: the british welding institute spent several million dollars for the oil companies trying to cut if you could do it cost us hundreds of millions of dollars to do stress relief every year
instances.framework_lesson: The economic pressure to reduce post-weld heat treatment costs incentivizes industry to pursue unvalidated alternatives, necessitating independent research institutions to test claims.

cluster_id: Aluminum company vibratory stress relief proposal (2014)
display_name: Aluminum company vibratory stress relief proposal (2014)

instances.name: Aluminum company vibratory stress relief proposal (2014)
instances.summary: A 10-billion-dollar-per-year aluminum company recently approached the instructor seeking endorsement to use vibratory stress relief on welded components.
instances.key_quote: a guy just emailed me two weeks ago from an aluminum company and they're trying to convince this 10 billion a year aluminum company to use vibratory stress relief
instances.framework_lesson: Even large, well-resourced manufacturers can be persuaded by sales claims to adopt unproven stress-relief methods in preference to established thermal practices.

cluster_id: Pressure vessel explosion from leftover electrode stub
display_name: Pressure vessel explosion from leftover electrode stub

instances.name: Pressure vessel explosion from leftover electrode stub
instances.summary: A pressure vessel exploded after operation because a welder left a three-inch electrode stub on the shop floor; another welder used it two days later, creating a hydrogen crack that led to vessel failure.
instances.key_quote: they have had pressure vessels and pressure vessel shops blow up because some guy left a three inch stub on the ground and some other guy came along two days later and welded with it and created a hydrogen crack
instances.framework_lesson: Electrode moisture control requires procedural enforcement (stub counting, time limits, baking oven returns) because individual welder discipline cannot be relied upon; ASME code inspectors now revoke shop licenses if stubs are found on the floor.

cluster_id: Submarine hydrogen cracking consulting case
display_name: Submarine hydrogen cracking consulting case

instances.name: Submarine hydrogen cracking consulting case
instances.summary: Eagar investigated hydrogen cracking in submarine welds and determined the root cause was dirty welding wire from lubricant residue, with smaller diameter wire having higher surface-to-volume ratio accumulating more contaminants.
instances.key_quote: my conclusion was it was dirty wire okay this is a milliliters hydrogen for 100 grams of weld rutile electrodes high hydrogen basic

cluster_id: Submarine scuttling practice (Newport News)
display_name: Submarine scuttling practice (Newport News)

instances.name: Submarine scuttling practice (Newport News)
instances.summary: U.S. Navy used to scuttle decommissioned submarines off Newport News and Nags Head, burying them in the ocean including their nuclear reactors, a practice later discontinued.
instances.key_quote: you used to blow them up off of newport news uh or nags head you know that story right that's actually sort of the story in hunt for reg right october

cluster_id: Sylvester and Associates embezzlement fraud
display_name: Sylvester and Associates embezzlement fraud

instances.name: Sylvester and Associates embezzlement fraud
instances.summary: Bruce Sylvester, owner of a nondestructive testing and diving consulting firm, created fake invoices and submitted them to banks to obtain loans, embezzling approximately half a million dollars from his mother's company.
instances.key_quote: bruce sylvester was making fake invoices submitting them to the bank to get loans from the bank based on his accounts receivables but this was for work that never existed finally about 1983 they caught up with bruce

cluster_id: North Sea oil platform collapse
display_name: North Sea oil platform collapse

instances.name: North Sea oil platform collapse
instances.summary: An offshore oil platform leg developed a 50-foot corrosion crack that went nearly all the way around a 20-foot diameter leg, causing the platform to collapse; a diving survey two weeks prior had failed to detect the crack.
instances.key_quote: they had just done a diving survey to look for cracks two weeks before so do you think they missed it yeah okay that was one of the problems
instances.framework_lesson: Commercial divers lack adequate incentive and oversight to perform thorough inspections in harsh conditions, leading to missed critical defects.

cluster_id: North Sea platform hatch failure and flooding
display_name: North Sea platform hatch failure and flooding

instances.name: North Sea platform hatch failure and flooding
instances.summary: An oil rig capsized after one leg failed and another leg flooded due to an open hatch, introducing unintended water weight that contributed to the tipping over.
instances.key_quote: you lost one leg and then you had another leg got flooded because they left a hatch open or something okay and so when it tipped over or something you had all this water on the inside that it wasn't supposed to be bearing that weight
instances.framework_lesson: Safety protocols for compartment isolation and hatch closure must be enforced; failure to close compartments converts a containable failure into a catastrophic one.

cluster_id: Petrobras oil rig failure (Brazil)
display_name: Petrobras oil rig failure (Brazil)

instances.name: Petrobras oil rig failure (Brazil)
instances.summary: An offshore oil rig operated by Petrobras listed at 45 degrees for three days in calm weather due to an open hatch, resulting in a half-billion-dollar loss but no fatalities.
instances.year_or_era: late 1980s or early 1990s (four years after North Sea incident)
instances.key_quote: four years later off brazil petrobras had a similar failure and there's nice pictures for three days in the nice sun sunny weather there's this i used to have well i actually have it on one of my old computers this half billion dollar oil rig out there listing at 45 degrees
instances.framework_lesson: The same failure mode repeated at another operator despite prior documented lessons, indicating poor industry knowledge transfer and adherence to safety standards.

cluster_id: Mass General subway stop welding investigation
display_name: Mass General subway stop welding investigation

instances.name: Mass General subway stop welding investigation
instances.summary: Eagar consulted on a welding problem at the Mass General subway stop, requiring assessment of manganese-to-sulfur ratios in older 1900-era steels.
instances.year_or_era: 2000s (during his teaching period; original steel from ~1900)
instances.key_quote: i did work on this problem bethlehem steel owned shipyards when i was in the research lab and they wanted a weld over primer although i did work on this problem i would also when i was doing some of this stuff if it's a 1900 piece of steel i would also start worrying about you know the manganese to sulfur ratio
instances.framework_lesson: Vintage steel grades with high sulfur content require special attention to manganese ratios in modern welding; historical composition specs (written in 1920s-1940s) may not reflect current best practices.

cluster_id: Welding over paint corrosion problem
display_name: Welding over paint corrosion problem

instances.name: Welding over paint corrosion problem

instances.key_quote: people have been trying to do this for 50 years knowing to solve the problem and i didn't solve it either

cluster_id: Bath Iron Works welding-over-paint trials
display_name: Bath Iron Works welding-over-paint trials

instances.name: Bath Iron Works welding-over-paint trials

instances.key_quote: i've you know i was up here at bath iron works they were about eight years ago they were trying to do it

display_name: David Taylor Model Basin titanium welding limitations

instances.name: David Taylor Model Basin titanium welding limitations

display_name: Pratt & Whitney turbine blade

display_name: NIST competitiveness vs. productivity misunderstanding

cluster_id: MIT Food Nutrition Department closure
display_name: MIT Food Nutrition Department closure

instances.name: MIT Food Nutrition Department closure
instances.summary: An MIT provost (later CIA head) unilaterally closed the Food Nutrition Department without notice, killing a department that included pioneering food canning research by Samuel Prescott.
instances.year_or_era: during Eagar's time as MIT student (1970s-1980s era)
instances.key_quote: he got killed by a provost who didn't make president because he killed this department without bothering to tell them he was going to do it

cluster_id: British iron industry wood shortage crisis
display_name: British iron industry wood shortage crisis

instances.name: British iron industry wood shortage crisis
instances.summary: 16th-17th century England faced deforestation crisis as iron smelting, shipbuilding, and glassmaking competed for charcoal, forcing politicians to pass timber-protection laws that exempted their own regions and ultimately caused iron industry collapse in the Weald by 1615.
instances.key_quote: the shortage of wood was so serious a further act was passed for vetting the felony of trees within 22 miles of the thames river within four miles of the great forest of the weld
instances.framework_lesson: Political exemptions to resource-protection regulations undermined their stated conservation intent and accelerated the very crisis they aimed to prevent.

cluster_id: Coal mining water-table pumping efficiency problem
display_name: Coal mining water-table pumping efficiency problem

instances.name: Coal mining water-table pumping efficiency problem
instances.summary: Coal mine operators needed to determine whether water-pumping costs for draining flooded coal seams exceeded the value of extracted coal, prompting James Watt's thermodynamic efficiency studies of pumps.
instances.key_quote: the owners of the coal mines wanted to know whether they were spending more money on the pumps to get the water out than the coal that they're getting bringing out

cluster_id: Engine blade rejuvenation via hot isostatic pressing (HIP)
display_name: Engine blade rejuvenation via hot isostatic pressing (HIP)

instances.name: Engine blade rejuvenation via hot isostatic pressing (HIP)

instances.key_quote: we found if you did hide isostatic pressing you could take an old blade and rejuvenate it with a fancy heat treatment and so it didn't become as big a problem as some people thought

cluster_id: 1952 T2 tanker fracture off Cape Cod
display_name: 1952 T2 tanker fracture off Cape Cod

cluster_id: LNG tanker ship construction failure and abandonment
display_name: LNG tanker ship construction failure and abandonment

instances.key_quote: they built five or six of these ships at a cost of 19 mid-70s one and a half billion dollars or whatever all underwritten by us taxpayers dollars insured and they found when they put the foam insulation in the ship's they screwed up

cluster_id: Brazilian helicopter rotor spar failure litigation
display_name: Brazilian helicopter rotor spar failure litigation

instances.name: Brazilian helicopter rotor spar failure litigation
instances.summary: A helicopter rotor spar failed in Brazil; initial blame was placed on surface grinding scratches, but Eagar's forensic analysis using fracture mechanics proved the actual cause was stress-corrosion cracking of high-strength aluminum, leading to corrected service bulletins.
instances.key_quote: metallurgically on the surface it was stress corrosion cracking the problem was not that they had sanded the surface they hadn't repainted it well enough to protect it from stress corrosion cracking of a high-strength aluminum
instances.framework_lesson: Initial failure hypotheses based on visible surface damage were wrong; quantitative fracture mechanics analysis revealed the true root cause and corrected erroneous service bulletins.

cluster_id: Aluminum automotive applications (1990s Ford Taurus vs. Toyota Camry)
display_name: Aluminum automotive applications (1990s Ford Taurus vs. Toyota Camry)

cluster_id: Mexican airline jet turbine blade sulfidation
display_name: Mexican airline jet turbine blade sulfidation

cluster_id: Helicopter bearing precision manufacturing
display_name: Helicopter bearing precision manufacturing

instances.name: Helicopter bearing precision manufacturing
instances.summary: World's largest helicopter bearings required 42-inch width tolerance of ±5 thousandths of an inch, necessitating measurement tools bathed in cooling oil to maintain thermal equilibrium with the workpiece.
instances.key_quote: i've worked on some helicopter bearings on some of the world's largest helicopters 42 inch bearing half inch raceway and it was 42 inches wide it was supposed to be 42 inches plus or minus 5 thousandths
instances.framework_lesson: Precision tolerance specifications must account for thermal expansion coefficients; measuring instruments must be maintained at identical temperature as the part being measured.

cluster_id: Shipyard thermal distortion during construction
display_name: Shipyard thermal distortion during construction

instances.name: Shipyard thermal distortion during construction
instances.summary: Shipyard construction had to account for solar heating causing differential bow in steel plates, affecting tolerances during assembly even at quarter-inch precision over hundred-yard spans.
instances.key_quote: in a shipyard they have to worry about when the sun when the sun comes out from behind the clouds because the heat on that side of the ship will cause the whole ship to bow
instances.framework_lesson: Large-scale precision manufacturing must accommodate environmental thermal loading, not just mechanical process variables.

cluster_id: Tekken test development for Japanese railways
display_name: Tekken test development for Japanese railways

instances.name: Tekken test development for Japanese railways
instances.summary: Japanese railway engineers in the 1970s-80s developed the Tekken (railroad) test to assess weldability of steel used in subway and transportation infrastructure construction.
instances.key_quote: japanese were building lots of railroads in the still in the 70s and 80s for transportation subways and things like that and that was the tekken test
materials_or_systems: small test plates (1 inch or half inch)

cluster_id: Lost foam casting invention and commercialization
display_name: Lost foam casting invention and commercialization

instances.summary: An artist from Boston invented lost foam casting by sculpting styrofoam, surrounding it with sand, and pouring molten metal to let the foam evaporate; MIT's Professor Flemings helped perfect the technique into an industrial process now used for most General Motors cast iron engine blocks.
instances.key_quote: he comes in and he had decided to sculpt something in styrofoam and then pour the sand around the styrofoam and then pour the molten metal in and just let the styrofoam evaporate from the hot metal

cluster_id: Turbine blade casting development at Pratt & Whitney
display_name: Turbine blade casting development at Pratt & Whitney

cluster_id: Pittsburgh aluminum explosive removal service

cluster_id: Electric arc furnace water-cooled pipe rupture and molten steel explosion
display_name: Electric arc furnace water-cooled pipe rupture and molten steel explosion

instances.name: Electric arc furnace water-cooled pipe rupture and molten steel explosion
instances.summary: A high-power electric arc furnace (150 MW) with water-cooled steel pipe walls suffered a rupture from arc erosion through degraded ceramic coating; molten steel and water contact caused a violent explosion that killed six workers.
instances.key_quote: the Arc jumped from the bundle to the wall because there is a gap in the ceramic coating and had a hole this big almost the diameter and they had an explosion uh the metal erupted turns to VAP turns to a foam and six guys died

cluster_id: MIT Lincoln Lab Quadulan radar dome bogie replacement
display_name: MIT Lincoln Lab Quadulan radar dome bogie replacement

instances.name: MIT Lincoln Lab Quadulan radar dome bogie replacement
instances.summary: A Cold War-era radar dome facility in the Pacific was running at 3,000% of its design life due to continuous classified military operation; a steel bogie wheel assembly needed replacement but had a one-year casting lead time, forcing a machine-from-plate alternative with stringent non-destructive testing requirements.
instances.key_quote: one of my first uh consulting jobs back in the 70s was MIT Lincoln lab came in and they said we have this radar dome in quadulan in the Pacific and it turns out they had built this for some scientific purposes but it just happened to be located in the perfect position whenever the Soviets were sending a satellite up into space
instances.framework_lesson: ASM ultrasonic testing specifications for heavy plate (above 6 inches) permitted defects up to 12 inches in diameter as acceptable, which may be inadequate for critical load-bearing components.

cluster_id: Caltech rapid solidification metallic ribbon research
display_name: Caltech rapid solidification metallic ribbon research

instances.name: Caltech rapid solidification metallic ribbon research
instances.summary: In the 1960s, researchers at Caltech developed rapid solidification technology to produce thin ribbons of amorphous iron-boron alloys by pouring molten metal onto a spinning copper wheel, achieving solidification rates of one million degrees per second and eliminating dendrite formation.
instances.key_quote: in the 1960s the guy at Caltech took some iron Boron Alloys uh that they're actually basically just brazing Alloys um but they melt at a fairly low temperature sort of like cast iron and he took a spinning copper wheel spinning at uh I don't remember how many surface feet per minute but it's it was like going around like 60

cluster_id: Bulk metallic glass commercialization effort
display_name: Bulk metallic glass commercialization effort

instances.name: Bulk metallic glass commercialization effort
instances.summary: Starting in the late 1980s, researchers developed bulk metallic glasses by adding metalloids to iron-based systems, enabling larger castings up to golf-ball size; however, consolidation of thin sheets into bulk material proved unsolvable for decades, and the only successful commercial product was metal-wood golf clubs.
instances.key_quote: in the late 80s came up with bulk metallic glasses he started throwing all kinds of other metalloids from that part of the periodic table in with the iron and actually it wasn't just iron it was iron buril Alloys originally

cluster_id: Rapid solidification powder consolidation (30-year research cycle)
display_name: Rapid solidification powder consolidation (30-year research cycle)

instances.name: Rapid solidification powder consolidation (30-year research cycle)
instances.summary: The research community spent hundreds of millions of dollars over 30 years attempting to consolidate rapidly solidified metal powders into bulk solids but failed to develop a commercially viable process.
instances.key_quote: that's what they said about rap rapidly solidified powders 30 years ago and we spent hundreds of millions of dollars and got nowhere
instances.framework_lesson: Engineers focused on demonstrating proof-of-concept properties in small samples without planning for scaled manufacturing and consolidation, leading to perpetual research funding cycles that never reached commercialization.

cluster_id: Trolley wire (Boston-New Hampshire connection)
display_name: Trolley wire (Boston-New Hampshire connection)

cluster_id: Army wonder armor scaling failure
display_name: Army wonder armor scaling failure

instances.name: Army wonder armor scaling failure
instances.summary: The Army developed a composite armor material that performed excellently against small-caliber bullets in laboratory tests but could not be scaled up to plate size for practical applications.
instances.key_quote: they can make little things that you can shoot little bullets at okay and it stops little bullets just great but you'll never stop the big stuff CU you can't make it big how do you scale it up
instances.framework_lesson: Materials that demonstrate excellent properties at small scale may have fundamental scaling constraints that prevent transition to production-relevant geometries.

cluster_id: Doctoral exam vacuum reverberatory furnace design error
display_name: Doctoral exam vacuum reverberatory furnace design error

instances.name: Doctoral exam vacuum reverberatory furnace design error
instances.summary: A doctoral candidate designed a reverberatory furnace to operate in vacuum while including a flame burner, which is physically impossible since flames require oxygen.
instances.key_quote: so there he had this flame shooting right into the vacuum he had a picture of it shooting flame shooting into the vacuum and uh I was grading this question Professor Clark who was in charge
instances.framework_lesson: Basic thermodynamic and combustion principles must constrain furnace design; students must validate fundamental feasibility before detailed engineering.

cluster_id: Friction stir welding development for aluminum aircraft structures
display_name: Friction stir welding development for aluminum aircraft structures

instances.name: Friction stir welding development for aluminum aircraft structures
instances.summary: Boeing invested $10 million in a friction stir welding facility to join aluminum plates without melting, achieving excellent properties with minimal distortion for aircraft applications.
instances.key_quote: Boeing put in a $10 million facility to weld aluminum plates well for the last 20 years people have been trying to do steel

cluster_id: Electric Boat welding humidity specification violation
display_name: Electric Boat welding humidity specification violation

instances.name: Electric Boat welding humidity specification violation
instances.summary: Navy specification prohibits welding on days exceeding certain humidity levels to prevent hydrogen cracking in steel, but the specification is violated 80% of the time at shipyards because they are located near water.
instances.key_quote: that napi spec is violated 80% of the days that they're welding okay in fact you could weld 20% of the time at electric boat or any other Shipyard because there's a shipyards tend to be located next to the a lot of water
instances.framework_lesson: Engineering specifications are written without practical reality in mind; they become systematically ignored when compliance is physically or economically infeasible.

cluster_id: Helicopter rotor containment specification
display_name: Helicopter rotor containment specification

instances.name: Helicopter rotor containment specification
instances.summary: FAA and military specifications require a shell around helicopter engine compartments to contain rotor fragments, but no material can practically stop a rotor without armor so heavy the helicopter cannot fly.
instances.key_quote: there is no material that can stop a rotor from going through and well there are materials but you're talking about armor so if you want to put 2-in armor plate around your engine in case your engine and 2in armor plate that's fine it's just you can't get your helicopter off the ground
instances.framework_lesson: Standards are adopted without verification of feasibility; when standards cannot be met, they are universally ignored rather than revised.

cluster_id: Whipple gas turbine invention
display_name: Whipple gas turbine invention

instances.key_quote: this guy Whipple who designed the first gas turban in England in the late 30s he says it's good thing he didn't know that this scientific fact was true or he never would have tried

cluster_id: Barge machine turnings fire
display_name: Barge machine turnings fire

instances.name: Barge machine turnings fire
instances.summary: A barge transporting old machine turnings to a steel mill for scrap caught fire, demonstrating the difficulty of extinguishing metal powder fires.
instances.key_quote: someone was transporting a bunch of old machine turnings in a big barge and they're going to take them to the steel mill for scrap and they the barge caught on fire it's not easy to put out of metal fire okay

cluster_id: NYC titanium heat exchanger fire
display_name: NYC titanium heat exchanger fire

instances.name: NYC titanium heat exchanger fire
instances.summary: Workers cutting a titanium heat exchanger in a basement with oxy-acetylene torches in lower Manhattan post-9/11 created fine titanium powders that ignited; spraying water on the fire intensified it by generating hydrogen, requiring specialized extinguishing agent.
instances.key_quote: they were cutting up this huge titanium heat exchanger flame cutting it um well when you do that you create some fine powders not of all some of which maybe completely combusted but maybe some others didn't
instances.framework_lesson: Workers without English-language safety training did not understand that water application to reactive metal fires generates hydrogen gas and intensifies combustion; industrial metal fire hazards require specialized suppression methods unavailable at most locations.

cluster_id: New York basement titanium fire and lawsuit
display_name: New York basement titanium fire and lawsuit

instances.name: New York basement titanium fire and lawsuit
instances.summary: A titanium fire in a New York City basement burned for three days before fire department was called; firefighters' response was allegedly inadequate, leading to a lawsuit against the fire department despite their use of specialized metal-fire extinguishing agent.
instances.key_quote: they have a small little hydrogen explosion in the basement uh doesn't do a lot of terrible damage but does a few million dollars worth of damage and so now the people are suing the fire department in New York
instances.framework_lesson: Liability frameworks may unfairly penalize emergency responders when operators delay calling for help and then blame responders for inadequate control of a fire they themselves had been managing for days.

cluster_id: Ed Metzbower laser welding of HY-80 steel
display_name: Ed Metzbower laser welding of HY-80 steel

instances.name: Ed Metzbower laser welding of HY-80 steel
instances.summary: Naval Research Laboratory researcher Ed Metzbower discovered that laser welding of HY-80 steel produced weld metal cleaner than the base metal because the superheating from the laser beam vaporized inclusions in the weld.
instances.key_quote: he was welding hy8 steel with a laser and he went in he found that the weld metal was cleaner than the base metal that he was welding and he figured out that what was happening is the superheating from the laser beam in the weld metal was vaporizing all the inclusions

cluster_id: Air Force titanium powder prototype facility (Wyman Gordon)
display_name: Air Force titanium powder prototype facility (Wyman Gordon)

instances.key_quote: they were trying to build a $10 million prototype facility and actually I think they may have been successful they wanted to make titanium powders for aircraft parts that were very clean

cluster_id: Velcro invention (serendipitous discovery)
display_name: Velcro invention (serendipitous discovery)
