cluster_id: Ship bolt tightness checks and propeller bolts
display_name: Ship bolt tightness checks and propeller bolts

instances.name: Ship bolt tightness checks and propeller bolts
instances.summary: Ships conduct bolt tightness checks every four to five years on critical joints like propellers, sometimes reusing and retorquing bolts rather than replacing them entirely, affecting long-term reliability.
instances.key_quote: I think every like four or five years on ships they do both tightness checks yeah but they didn't completely remove it they just kind of go in and retorque

cluster_id: Swedish naval ship Visby hull structural problems
display_name: Swedish naval ship Visby hull structural problems

instances.name: Swedish naval ship Visby hull structural problems
instances.summary: The Swedish Visby corvette, built from aluminum and composite materials, experienced hogging (sagging between wave peaks) and structural failure in heavy seas due to insufficient stiffness.
instances.key_quote: the visby does has problems with hogging and other things if you know what hogging is you know basically a beam between the waves right in Heavy Seas the ship just becomes a beam between the waves well composits and aluminum just don't have the stiffness
instances.framework_lesson: Low-modulus materials (aluminum, composites) cannot achieve the structural stiffness required for long-span naval hulls in heavy sea conditions, despite cost and weight advantages.

cluster_id: Catalytic converter cold-start emissions problem
display_name: Catalytic converter cold-start emissions problem

instances.name: Catalytic converter cold-start emissions problem
instances.summary: Automotive catalytic converters take 2–3 minutes to reach operating temperature after cold start, causing vehicles to emit high levels of pollutants during the warm-up period and fail state emissions tests if tested immediately after starting.
instances.key_quote: most of your admissions problems are when you first start up the vehicle the Catalyst isn't working when the catalyst is cold when you start up the vehicle all your emissions are going right out the back until you heat up the Catalyst
instances.framework_lesson: Thermal mass design trade-offs affect both emissions compliance and real-world pollution; lighter, faster-heating catalysts became necessary to meet cold-start regulatory requirements.
materials_or_systems: ceramic or stainless steel catalyst support

cluster_id: Army gun barrel erosion and coating programs
display_name: Army gun barrel erosion and coating programs

instances.name: Army gun barrel erosion and coating programs
instances.summary: The Army has attempted for over a century to apply vapor-deposited structural coatings and electroplated chromium to the interior of gun barrels to resist erosion from advanced propellants, though chromium remains the preferred solution due to cost.
instances.key_quote: the Army's been trying to do that for gun barrels for years they've been trying to put Vapor deposited structural coatings on the inside of gun barrels because they have propellants that will wipe out the gun barrel after one shot

cluster_id: ALVIN submarine pressure hull cost overrun
display_name: ALVIN submarine pressure hull cost overrun

instances.name: ALVIN submarine pressure hull cost overrun
instances.summary: NSF commissioned study to assess feasibility of building a new ALVIN deep submersion vehicle; initial Woods Hole estimate of $6.5 million was found to be severely underestimated, ultimately costing approximately $40 million due to lack of titanium fabrication expertise and industrial capacity in the United States.
instances.key_quote: the greatest risk in meeting the cost estimate of a new hov is the cost of fabricating the pressure hull, the two million dollars for the pressure hole was the biggest problem
instances.framework_lesson: Loss of industrial capacity and expertise in specialized materials (titanium pressure hull fabrication) since the 1970s meant cost estimates failed to account for rebuilding dormant manufacturing capability and training.

cluster_id: Deep-sea titanium submersible pressure hull cost overrun
display_name: Deep-sea titanium submersible pressure hull cost overrun

instances.name: Deep-sea titanium submersible pressure hull cost overrun

instances.key_quote: the final cost as i understand it was about 40 million tom eager thought he was being very generous by going up by 250 percent over woods hole
instances.framework_lesson: Specifications and cost estimation failed to account for the loss of industrial titanium expertise since the 1970s when Navy funding ended, leading to vendors unfamiliar with the material submitting unrealistic bids.

cluster_id: Titanium fire extinguishing with water
display_name: Titanium fire extinguishing with water

instances.key_quote: when you do that you get titanium oxide plus hydrogen and so you actually end up creating a bigger flame because you get hydro you're generating hydrogen when you spray water on titanium that's burning

cluster_id: Aluminum deep-diving submersible stress corrosion cracking
display_name: Aluminum deep-diving submersible stress corrosion cracking

instances.name: Aluminum deep-diving submersible stress corrosion cracking
instances.summary: The Aluminaut, an all-aluminum deep-diving submersible built by the Aluminum Association in the 1960s-70s, experienced stress corrosion cracking in its high-strength aluminum pressure hull.
instances.key_quote: they have built all aluminum deep diving submersibles the aluminum association built the illuminate back in the 1960s or 70s you have to use high strength aluminum and that has stress corrosion cracking problems
instances.framework_lesson: High-strength aluminum's susceptibility to stress corrosion cracking in seawater environments makes it unsuitable for deep-pressure hull applications despite offering weight savings.

cluster_id: B1 bomber program titanium surplus repurposing

cluster_id: Cape Cod sailboat anodized aluminum corrosion failure
display_name: Cape Cod sailboat anodized aluminum corrosion failure

instances.framework_lesson: The anodizing process specification must distinguish between two competing requirements: sealing for corrosion resistance versus leaving porous for adhesive bonding; failure to seal negates corrosion protection.

cluster_id: Food and Drug Act polymer restrictions (medical devices)
display_name: Food and Drug Act polymer restrictions (medical devices)

instances.framework_lesson: Overly stringent regulatory requirements can stifle innovation by making certification costs prohibitively high, forcing designers to use decades-old materials despite better alternatives.
instances.source_module: Codes and Standards Definitions and History

instances.summary: A Midwest specialty vehicle manufacturer made aluminum 200-gallon water tanks for cement trucks at 55 PSI, excluded themselves from ASME pressure vessel codes and DOT regulation, omitted reinforcement plates around cut holes, and the tanks fatigued in service.

instances.framework_lesson: Regulatory gaps and code exclusions—citing low pressure thresholds and non-critical vehicle use—allowed a manufacturer to avoid both federal DOT oversight and ASME boiler code requirements, leading to fatigue failures that good engineering practice would have prevented.
instances.source_module: Codes and Standards Definitions and History

instances.framework_lesson: State law requires pressure vessels to meet ASME code, but without visible code stamps or certified-shop requirements, illegal vessels evade inspection and repair by uncertified welders, creating catastrophic risk.

cluster_id: China glass bridge high-heel impact fracture
display_name: China glass bridge high-heel impact fracture

instances.name: China glass bridge high-heel impact fracture
instances.summary: A woman wearing high heels impacted and fractured one layer of a multi-layer glass panel on a pedestrian bridge in China, demonstrating the need for design redundancy in glass structures.
instances.key_quote: they had a glass one of the one area one of the areas they had fracture and the reason they had the fracture was because they did not tell the people walking on there you couldn't wear high heels
instances.framework_lesson: Design specifications for impact resistance on glass structures must account for realistic user behavior and localized stress concentrations; redundancy mitigates single-point failures.

cluster_id: Bob Rose light bulb classroom demonstration
display_name: Bob Rose light bulb classroom demonstration

instances.name: Bob Rose light bulb classroom demonstration
instances.summary: Professor Bob Rose demonstrated that freshly manufactured glass light bulbs can be bounced without breaking, but after several hours of classroom exposure they become brittle and shatter due to oxygen diffusion and surface micro-crack formation.
instances.key_quote: right when after it's manufactured the glass he would be able to bounce it and it wouldn't break because right after it's manufactured you don't have the attack of the air at the surface of the glass
instances.framework_lesson: Glass strength is time-dependent and degradation is driven by kinetic diffusion of atmospheric species; specifications must account for service duration and environmental exposure, not just initial properties.

cluster_id: London conical building solar focusing incident
display_name: London conical building solar focusing incident

instances.name: London conical building solar focusing incident
instances.summary: A conical glass-façade building in London designed by a renowned architect focused sunlight like a lens onto a street location below, creating temperatures hot enough to melt car hood paint and destroy parked vehicles.
instances.year_or_era: early 2000s (referenced as 'a couple years ago' in 2016 lecture)
instances.key_quote: at one area across the street you know two blocks down they were actually getting temperatures so hot it was melting the SCAR hoods and it was like super hot what was happening was with the building the design of the building acted like a lens or a mirror and it focused the sunlight at one spot
instances.framework_lesson: Building façade design geometry must account for solar reflection and focusing effects during the design phase, or unintended thermal hazards will emerge after construction and the location becomes a known danger zone.

cluster_id: Multi-building repeated thermal stress glass failures by same architect
display_name: Multi-building repeated thermal stress glass failures by same architect

instances.name: Multi-building repeated thermal stress glass failures by same architect
instances.summary: A designer known for conical building designs experienced glass curtain wall thermal stress failures on two to three separate buildings due to sunlight-induced stress.
instances.key_quote: he this designer who actually designed it has had this issue on two or three buildings and the designer was known for his you know making conical type buildings
instances.framework_lesson: Thermal stress glass fracture from solar angle and building orientation requires case-by-case analysis; repeated failures on similar designs by the same architect indicate a systematic design methodology gap.

cluster_id: Single-side glass fracture asymmetry in high-rise buildings
display_name: Single-side glass fracture asymmetry in high-rise buildings

instances.name: Single-side glass fracture asymmetry in high-rise buildings
instances.summary: Building operators discovered that one side of a high-rise experienced 50 broken glass panels while the opposite side had none, traced to differential solar heating and thermal stress on the sun-facing façade.
instances.year_or_era: 2000s-2010s (referenced as case studies in design discussions)
instances.key_quote: on some buildings and there there's been case studies people say why do I have a building and in I have 50 broken panels on this side and no broken panels on the backside and more often than not the answer is well when the Sun comes up and it hits it you're getting thermal stress breakage
instances.framework_lesson: Thermal stress breakage is orientation- and latitude-dependent; design analysis must account for solar angle variation and resulting asymmetric heat distribution across façade, or unexpected failure clusters will emerge in service.

cluster_id: Nickel sulfide inclusion spontaneous glass breakage
display_name: Nickel sulfide inclusion spontaneous glass breakage

instances.name: Nickel sulfide inclusion spontaneous glass breakage
instances.summary: Tempered glass panels with trapped nickel sulfide inclusions break spontaneously in service years after installation due to phase-transformation-induced volume expansion and tensile stress.
instances.key_quote: you can have glass in service for 10 years and it can be fine and then one day it just breaks
instances.framework_lesson: Float glass manufacturing process limitations (thickness constraints due to diffusion kinetics) allow inclusions to remain trapped at mid-thickness, creating latent failure modes that bypass initial quality inspection.

cluster_id: Laminated automotive safety glass delamination
display_name: Laminated automotive safety glass delamination

instances.name: Laminated automotive safety glass delamination
instances.summary: Multi-layer safety glass in automobiles delaminates at edges when adhesion between glass and polyvinyl butyral (PVB) interlayer is inadequate, causing frosted appearance and progressive failure.
instances.key_quote: if the adhesion isn't Prop done properly one of the layers will start peeling away and you'll get this frosted or glazed appearance when you have delamination
instances.framework_lesson: The pummel test (hammer-impact adhesion test) is performed after-the-fact rather than as a process control specification, allowing defectively bonded glass to reach service.

cluster_id: Bullet-resistant glass installation failure investigation
display_name: Bullet-resistant glass installation failure investigation

instances.name: Bullet-resistant glass installation failure investigation
instances.summary: A site had bullet-resistant glass with nicks and impact indications; Eager was consulted to determine if the glass was defective or if the damage was within acceptable limits for the product.
instances.key_quote: they wanted to know if there was a issue with the glass because they had a couple areas where there was Nick's in the glass and slight areas where the bulletproof glass you know had indications that something hit it
instances.framework_lesson: Bullet-resistant glass specifications do not guarantee imperviousness to all damage; scratches and breaks can occur within normal use and still meet performance requirements.

cluster_id: Navy TLP bonding consulting case
display_name: Navy TLP bonding consulting case

instances.name: Navy TLP bonding consulting case
instances.summary: Eagar conducted tensile testing and materials analysis on brazed copper components for a Navy application, demonstrating grain growth and orange peel effects from high-temperature brazing, then provided expert testimony to justify cost savings without compromising product quality.
instances.key_quote: I got a small consulting fee by doing some tests and writing a letter to that they could give to the Navy and tell them a scientific reason why sometimes you can save money and get a better product at the same time
instances.framework_lesson: Government procurement standards assumed cost and quality were mutually exclusive; engineering analysis demonstrated they could be optimized simultaneously.

cluster_id: Elevator cable brittle fracture investigation
display_name: Elevator cable brittle fracture investigation

instances.name: Elevator cable brittle fracture investigation
instances.summary: An investigator examined brittle wire failures in an elevator cable and suspected hydrogen embrittlement but did not observe intergranular fracture, a common indicator.
instances.key_quote: he had looked at the failures and they were somewhat brittle failures on some of the wires and one of the things you always suspect when you have brittle failures in steel is hydrogen embrittlement
instances.framework_lesson: Absence of intergranular fracture does not rule out hydrogen embrittlement in high-strength deformed steel like piano wire, where grain morphology prevents this characteristic feature.

cluster_id: Classified aircraft hydrogen embrittlement case
display_name: Classified aircraft hydrogen embrittlement case

instances.name: Classified aircraft hydrogen embrittlement case

instances.key_quote: this other thing which is basically a very high strength steel similar to what they use in aircraft landing gear and I won't it wasn't an aircraft landing gear it was an aircraft application I can't tell you more about it right now
instances.framework_lesson: Hydrogen embrittlement in high-strength steels does not always produce intergranular fracture; approximately 2% of cases lack this characteristic feature, and investigators must consider chemistry changes rather than relying solely on fractography.

cluster_id: California texting and driving fatal collision

cluster_id: 1940s welded ship cracking
display_name: 1940s welded ship cracking

instances.framework_lesson: Engineering codes and practice were not prepared for the fracture behavior of welded structures at scale; welding of critical structures required new understanding of brittle fracture.

cluster_id: USA Today gas pipeline corrosion article

cluster_id: Massachusetts gas explosion forensic investigation

instances.name: Federal gas pipeline explosion investigation (Mississippi)
instances.summary: A gas pipeline explosion in Mississippi during construction triggered federal reporting requirements to the Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration (PHMSA), with investigation costs reaching hundreds of thousands of dollars.
instances.key_quote: i had an explosion in mississippi a few years ago when they actually when they were building it anything happens like that happens the utility has to give a report to the it's now part of the transportation or tsa
instances.framework_lesson: Federal regulatory frameworks require utilities to fund comprehensive accident investigations even when root cause is determined, prioritizing regulatory documentation over cost-effectiveness.

cluster_id: Pensacola, Florida gas distribution leakage
display_name: Pensacola, Florida gas distribution leakage

instances.name: Pensacola, Florida gas distribution leakage
instances.summary: Pensacola, Florida gas utility loses 4.5% of all purchased gas through pipeline leaks in the distribution system, with this leakage rate being typical across similar infrastructure.
instances.key_quote: pensacola florida cannot account for four and a half percent of all the gas they buy it just leaks out into the ground and that's a typical value
instances.framework_lesson: Economic optimization theory suggests that fixing leaks at reasonable cost would increase profit margin, but utilities operate with aging infrastructure where repair costs exceed benefit.

cluster_id: Speaker's home gas service sleeving (three-quarter inch pipe)
display_name: Speaker's home gas service sleeving (three-quarter inch pipe)

instances.name: Speaker's home gas service sleeving (three-quarter inch pipe)
instances.summary: During methane detection monitoring in the speaker's neighborhood 15 years prior, methane was found in soil near the speaker's home and a neighbor's home, triggering plastic sleeve installation in aging 1930s-era gas service lines.
instances.key_quote: when they did this 15 years ago at my house and another house about four houses down they found methane in the soil you know a foot and a half deep they said ah-ha so they go and they sleeve it

cluster_id: New Jersey asphalt plant gas pipeline explosion
display_name: New Jersey asphalt plant gas pipeline explosion

instances.name: New Jersey asphalt plant gas pipeline explosion
instances.summary: A gas pipeline ruptured beneath an asphalt plant in New Jersey (circa 1995); investigation revealed the pipeline had been damaged by heavy earth-moving equipment and excavation activity that the plant operators denied had ever occurred, with a buried 1988 Ford Bronco discovered in the excavation crater as evidence.
instances.key_quote: they found a completely buried 1988 ford bronco so how did that get there if no one had ever dug a hole there okay well i guess you know someone had just parked their ford bronco there
instances.framework_lesson: Regulatory oversight and incident investigation can be hampered when operators provide false records; physical forensic evidence may be the only way to establish actual site history.

cluster_id: Wyoming liquefied natural gas storage facility release
display_name: Wyoming liquefied natural gas storage facility release

instances.name: Wyoming liquefied natural gas storage facility release
instances.summary: A liquefied natural gas storage facility in Wyoming experienced a catastrophic cryogenic release captured on surveillance video; the gas cloud traveled approximately 500 yards before finding an ignition source and creating a flame that rushed back toward the release point.
instances.key_quote: you can see the release on the surveillance video and you can see this white cloud of cryogenic you know liquid and gas it just spreads out and you can see it go off this the screen and it's about four or five seconds later you can see the flame rushing back

cluster_id: Consulting work on BP oil rig failure (unnamed)
display_name: Consulting work on BP oil rig failure (unnamed)

cluster_id: Boeing 747 C-5A competition
display_name: Boeing 747 C-5A competition

instances.summary: Boeing lost the Air Force competition to build a massive military transport aircraft (won by Lockheed Martin's C-5A Galaxy), leading Boeing to repurpose their investment by developing the 747 as a commercial aircraft.
instances.key_quote: Boeing had lost the competition had invested billions of dollars of their own money and they'd have said well why don't we make a you know a commercial air transport

cluster_id: Aluminum-silicon carbide composite welding project
display_name: Aluminum-silicon carbide composite welding project

instances.name: Aluminum-silicon carbide composite welding project
instances.summary: A company requested electron beam welding of aluminum-silicon carbide composites, but the project was fundamentally flawed because fusion welding would form aluminum carbide, which reacts with humidity and causes cracking.
instances.key_quote: when you melt a silicon carbide in the presence of aluminum what you're doing in a fusion well you're going to form aluminum for carbide and aluminum for carbide reacts with the humidity in the air and cause cracking
instances.framework_lesson: Engineering teams failed to recognize that composite material constraints (silicon carbide particles) make certain repair processes (fusion welding) fundamentally incompatible with the base material chemistry.

cluster_id: Qantas airline composite delamination
display_name: Qantas airline composite delamination

instances.name: Qantas airline composite delamination
instances.summary: A Qantas airline aircraft experienced adhesive failure and delamination in a reinforced composite structure, highlighting problems with adhesion in composite sandwich construction.
instances.key_quote: the Qantas airline okay reinforced composites so they learn about the problem of adhesion

cluster_id: Engine nacelle structural failures (Boeing 787/777)
display_name: Engine nacelle structural failures (Boeing 787/777)

instances.name: Engine nacelle structural failures (Boeing 787/777)
instances.summary: Nacelle structures on large commercial aircraft experienced failures under the extreme loads (100,000+ pounds of thrust) that must be transferred from engines to wings, revealing structural complexities not initially appreciated.
instances.key_quote: I've seen some failures of some of the cells and you got pieces of high-strength steel that are three inches square and you see some of the accidents and these stuff is twisted

cluster_id: PEX pipe lightning perforation fires
display_name: PEX pipe lightning perforation fires

instances.name: PEX pipe lightning perforation fires
instances.summary: PEX plastic plumbing pipe, introduced in 1998, melted and perforated when lightning struck homes, causing internal wall fires and burning down approximately 400-500 homes by 2001.
instances.key_quote: they brought it out to Market in 1998 and by 1999 they started hearing reports of homes burning down that had the stuff in it
instances.framework_lesson: Engineers spent six years reviewing plumbing codes and standards for approval but failed to consider lightning codes and natural disaster hazards, missing a critical failure mode.
instances.source_module: Codes and Standards Definitions and History

cluster_id: Post-9/11 building egress code revisions
display_name: Post-9/11 building egress code revisions

instances.name: Post-9/11 building egress code revisions
instances.summary: After 9/11, building egress codes were revised to allow occupants to exit buildings faster and reduce fire propagation risk.
instances.key_quote: after 9911 they revised a lot of the building egress code so people could get out of buildings faster so the building the fire wouldn't bring the building down as quickly
instances.framework_lesson: Catastrophic events expose deficiencies in life-safety codes, prompting regulatory updates to improve evacuation speed and survival rates.
instances.source_module: Codes and Standards Definitions and History

cluster_id: Aircraft landing gear and turbine engine shaft vacuum arc melting
display_name: Aircraft landing gear and turbine engine shaft vacuum arc melting

instances.name: Aircraft landing gear and turbine engine shaft vacuum arc melting
instances.summary: Aircraft landing gear, transmission shafts, and turbine engine shafts undergo triple vacuum arc remelting to eliminate inclusions that act as crack initiation sites.
instances.key_quote: all the crankshafts and all the Piston engines and aircrafts have to be triple vacuum AR melted now because vacuum AR melting essentially Burns away the inclusions
instances.framework_lesson: Billions of dollars were invested in vacuum arc melting technology after recognition that oxide and nitride inclusions from conventional steelmaking initiate fatigue cracks in critical aircraft components; inclusion standards were established.

cluster_id: SS Great Britain propeller shaft forging
display_name: SS Great Britain propeller shaft forging

instances.name: SS Great Britain propeller shaft forging
instances.summary: James Nasmyth and Isambard Kingdom Brunel used a steam hammer to forge one of the largest wrought-iron propeller shafts of the era (30 inches diameter) for the SS Great Britain ship, employing V-shaped anvils to prevent center cracking through triaxial compression.
instances.key_quote: 1839 they had studied back often they knew all about Delta and they were that's why they were using their v-shaped anvils right
instances.framework_lesson: Engineers in 1839 already understood and applied the principle that three-sided compression prevents internal tensioning and cracking during large forging operations.

cluster_id: Hot slag worker injury hazard
display_name: Hot slag worker injury hazard

instances.name: Hot slag worker injury hazard
instances.summary: Workers at a forging facility operated in pairs scooping and throwing approximately 1000°F hot slag into dumpsters in a repetitive piston-like motion, with an occupational hazard of one worker throwing hot slag into the other's face; the worker anecdote suggests that workers who caused such injuries likely did not continue employment.
instances.key_quote: the guys who do throw it in the other guy's face they probably don't work there anymore cuz they may be dead

cluster_id: Isothermal forging sample misappropriation
display_name: Isothermal forging sample misappropriation

instances.name: Isothermal forging sample misappropriation

instances.key_quote: that sample should be in the Smithsonian I recovered it from The Graduate student's desk they were using it as an ashtray

cluster_id: Vietnam-era Naval jet engine crashes and rebuilds
display_name: Vietnam-era Naval jet engine crashes and rebuilds

instances.key_quote: we got all these crashes coming back from Vietnam and we'd have to rebuild the engines

cluster_id: Titanium beta-phase forging shear band failure
display_name: Titanium beta-phase forging shear band failure

instances.name: Titanium beta-phase forging shear band failure
instances.summary: Forging of all-beta titanium microstructure resulted in localized shear bands and coarse, non-uniform microstructure instead of uniform deformation.
instances.key_quote: you try to form it in the all beta look what happens you get Shear bands localized shearing and all this other material
instances.framework_lesson: Forging parameters must account for crystal structure phase; alpha-beta titanium deforms uniformly while all-beta phase exhibits catastrophic shear localization.

display_name: Polymer extrusion mixing problem (blue/white resin)

instances.summary: A chemical engineer challenged students to explain why blue resin inserted in the center of a polymer extrusion ended up on the outside after extrusion; the puzzle was solved by recognizing inhomogeneous flow and nose-folding rather than diffusion.

cluster_id: Connecticut cement truck steering failure
display_name: Connecticut cement truck steering failure

instances.name: Connecticut cement truck steering failure

instances.key_quote: he had been using the muriatic acid to clean off the underside of his truck and he had been dissolving away his gears okay some of the stuff was leaking through the seals

cluster_id: MIT compressed air tank inspection lapse (Massachusetts)
display_name: MIT compressed air tank inspection lapse (Massachusetts)

instances.name: MIT compressed air tank inspection lapse (Massachusetts)
instances.summary: Regular National Board inspections of MIT's compressed air tanks ceased when Massachusetts changed its inspection laws, leaving potentially corroded vessels in service without oversight.
instances.key_quote: something changed in Massachusetts laws and that never gets done anymore so when you're standing around a big compressor tank at MIT be careful
instances.framework_lesson: Regulatory changes can inadvertently eliminate critical safety inspection regimes, leaving aging pressurized vessels unmonitored even if originally built to code.

cluster_id: Pratt & Whitney military jet engine design defect lawsuit (1992)
display_name: Pratt & Whitney military jet engine design defect lawsuit (1992)

instances.name: Pratt & Whitney military jet engine design defect lawsuit (1992)
instances.summary: In 1992, the Supreme Court ruled that manufacturers cannot be sued for design defects in military hardware because the government (NavAir, Air Force) is the designer; Pratt & Whitney was protected from design defect suits.
instances.key_quote: in 1992 the supreme court ruled that you can't sue pratt and whitney for an engine design defect you consume for a manufacturing defect but you can't sue them for a design defect
instances.framework_lesson: Military procurement doctrine shields manufacturers from design liability when government design teams approve specifications, creating a legal framework that differs from commercial product liability.

cluster_id: Black iron pipe corrosion rate field observation
display_name: Black iron pipe corrosion rate field observation

instances.name: Black iron pipe corrosion rate field observation
instances.summary: Eagar observed that carbon steel piping aboard ships typically lasts 25–30 years before requiring replacement due to corrosion, establishing a practical design lifetime for naval vessels.
instances.key_quote: those of you have been aboard ship and you have carbon steel piping how long does it last about 25 or 30 years before you really need to sort of get rid of the whole thing
instances.framework_lesson: The 25–30 year carbon steel piping lifetime became a limiting constraint on ship service life; when NAVSEA required 50-year ship lifespans, materials specifications had to shift to more corrosion-resistant alloys.

cluster_id: PEX (cross-linked polyethylene) brass fitting failures
display_name: PEX (cross-linked polyethylene) brass fitting failures

instances.key_quote: pex actually the pex tubing is fine some people are blaming the fittings there's a billion dollars worth of lawsuits out in las vegas and the west coast on pex fittings brass fittings

cluster_id: Consultant's daughter polybutylene condo replacement lawsuit (Raleigh, NC)
display_name: Consultant's daughter polybutylene condo replacement lawsuit (Raleigh, NC)

instances.key_quote: i had a daughter who about 19 1998 bought a condo down in raleigh north carolina and it had polybutylene and she knew about it and she got in just under the wire of the class action suit and they paid her twenty five hundred dollars to have all the fittings replaced

cluster_id: 1980s Delrin toilet failures
display_name: 1980s Delrin toilet failures

cluster_id: Las Vegas brass fitting corrosion litigation
display_name: Las Vegas brass fitting corrosion litigation

instances.key_quote: some attorneys in las vegas got a about a quarter billion dollar settlement for corrosion of some of the brass fittings and so now there's a feeding frenzy by attorneys claiming that this causes leaks

cluster_id: Atomic bomb recovery off Spain
display_name: Atomic bomb recovery off Spain

instances.name: Atomic bomb recovery off Spain
instances.summary: U.S. Air Force dropped an atomic warhead off the coast of Spain; the Alvin submersible was instrumental in locating it before Soviet recovery attempts.
instances.key_quote: the Alvin found the um atom bomb that the Air Force had dropped off Spain right and the Soviets were out looking for it we were out looking for it

cluster_id: Shipyard radiography safety and inspection protocol shift
display_name: Shipyard radiography safety and inspection protocol shift

instances.name: Shipyard radiography safety and inspection protocol shift
instances.summary: Around 1996-97, Navy directive permitted ultrasonic testing (UT) as alternative to X-ray in shipyards; within one year all commercial shipyards (Electric Boat, Bath Iron Works, etc.) switched to UT to eliminate costly radiation safety protocols.
instances.key_quote: if you've been in a shipyard with x-rays is you have to have about 30 people around to be able to take a for one guy to do an x-ray you have to have about 30 guards to make sure no rats come rushing into the area
instances.framework_lesson: Inspection codes that mandate specific methods without performance-based alternatives drive industry away from technically superior options when safety protocols become administratively burdensome.

cluster_id: Naval shipyard radiography eye injury incident
display_name: Naval shipyard radiography eye injury incident

instances.name: Naval shipyard radiography eye injury incident
instances.summary: A worker's eye was damaged during radiography operations at a Naval shipyard approximately 15 years before the lecture, leading to regulatory restrictions on when X-ray inspections could be performed.
instances.key_quote: 15 years ago somebody got their te went blink yeah that's interesting because Newport ship building almost exclusively does it
instances.framework_lesson: An isolated eye injury led to overly broad regulatory restrictions limiting radiography to day shifts only, even though the restriction was disproportionate to the actual hazard.

cluster_id: Navy surface ship and submarine hull construction practices
display_name: Navy surface ship and submarine hull construction practices

instances.name: Navy surface ship and submarine hull construction practices
instances.summary: Navy shipyards adopted HSLA steels instead of HY-80 for surface ships, eliminating preheat requirements and saving tens of millions of dollars per ship, while submarine construction remained conservative with higher-strength steels requiring extensive preheat.
instances.key_quote: tens of millions of dollars per ship because of preheat okay and you can kind of see how hsla seals can save tens of millions of dollars per ship and so the surface people use them and the substance because some people are conservative
instances.framework_lesson: Conservative engineering practices based on historical testing (explosion bulge tests from the 1950s-60s) can persist even when newer materials demonstrate adequate toughness, creating unnecessary cost and risk trade-offs between vessel types.

cluster_id: Explosion bulge test (Pelini underwater weld testing)
display_name: Explosion bulge test (Pelini underwater weld testing)

instances.name: Explosion bulge test (Pelini underwater weld testing)
instances.summary: Underwater explosive shock-wave testing of welds developed in the 1950s-60s to assess toughness and deformation resistance of welded joints, particularly for submarine applications.
instances.key_quote: they basically take it under water and they make a weld and they hit it with an explosive charge and they see if the thing will deform
instances.framework_lesson: Historical toughness data from controlled explosive testing established conservative design practices for high-pressure vessels where weld failure would be catastrophic.

cluster_id: Welder recertification burden in oil and construction (early 1990s)
display_name: Welder recertification burden in oil and construction (early 1990s)

instances.name: Welder recertification burden in oil and construction (early 1990s)
instances.summary: Experienced welders working across multiple employers were forced to pay $500+ per recertification even after decades of experience, creating unnecessary economic burden in mobile workforce industries.
instances.key_quote: the guy's been welding for 30 years you know and it's costing 500 every time to certify him or more so the american welding society says we will certify welders
instances.framework_lesson: Employer-based qualification systems failed to recognize portable skills, prompting creation of a portable welder certification standard (AWS card system) to streamline industry mobility.

cluster_id: Misapplication of structural welding code to heavy sections (early 1990s–2000s)
display_name: Misapplication of structural welding code to heavy sections (early 1990s–2000s)

instances.name: Misapplication of structural welding code to heavy sections (early 1990s–2000s)
instances.summary: Engineers attempted to apply the structural welding code (designed for thin sections up to ~2 inches) to eight-inch-thick steel, risking inadequate procedure specifications.
instances.key_quote: some people go to the structural welding code and said i'm going to use this for eight inch thick steel uh-uh this is for structural welding which is rarely over two inches thick
instances.framework_lesson: Pre-qualified procedures and codes written for common thicknesses in bridges and buildings create a false sense of universal applicability; engineers must check scope limits before adopting a procedure for heavier sections.

cluster_id: Pennsylvania forging press repair
display_name: Pennsylvania forging press repair

instances.name: Pennsylvania forging press repair
instances.summary: A 17-inch-thick steel forging press required repair using stick electrode welding with approximately 400 weld passes, representing the intensive multi-pass welding techniques used during wartime.
instances.key_quote: this is a well a 17 inch thick piece of steel it was part of the forging press they were trying to repair in Pennsylvania some time ago

cluster_id: Trailer weld failure sailboat accident
display_name: Trailer weld failure sailboat accident

instances.name: Trailer weld failure sailboat accident
instances.summary: A home-built sailboat trailer broke down a Nova Scotia highway when a critical weld joint failed; the failure had been caused by someone welding over a bolt that was positioned between angle irons without proper preparation.
instances.key_quote: someone took a bolt between two of the angle irons and stuck it in there and welded over the bodies and it was his boat is cruising down with no brakes
instances.framework_lesson: Improper welding technique—welding over foreign objects and bolts in stress-critical joints—can create catastrophic failures in structural components.

cluster_id: WWII ship construction slugging welds
display_name: WWII ship construction slugging welds

instances.name: WWII ship construction slugging welds
instances.summary: During rapid WWII ship construction, exhausted welders used a dangerous shortcut called 'slugging' where multiple electrodes were dropped into a groove and welded over, creating critical flaws in hull welds.
instances.key_quote: sometimes they were tired and they would slug the welds about slugging a well this movie is he on big thing like this on a cruiser a battleship
instances.framework_lesson: Lack of inspection standards and quality control allowed welder fatigue and cost-cutting fabrication methods to produce hidden critical flaws in primary structure.

cluster_id: Boat trailer weld failure on highway
display_name: Boat trailer weld failure on highway

instances.name: Boat trailer weld failure on highway
instances.summary: A homemade boat trailer broke apart on a highway when a welder improperly welded over a bolt that had been placed between angle irons, causing catastrophic failure and the boat to become uncontrolled.
instances.key_quote: someone took a bolt between two of the angle irons and stuck it in there and welded over the bodies

cluster_id: Nuclear carrier piping misalignment and repair
display_name: Nuclear carrier piping misalignment and repair

instances.name: Nuclear carrier piping misalignment and repair
instances.summary: During initial shipyard construction, pipes were jacked into alignment and welded under stress; years later these pipes required complete refabrication because the stress-induced misalignment could not be corrected by simple rewelding.
instances.key_quote: when you we have a lot of corrosion issues with carrier nuclear piping that we're dealing with right now so like back in the shipyard when they built the thing they would Jack a pipe you know it didn't line up so you Jackie O nothing right he's up to and weld it
instances.framework_lesson: Welding components under mechanical jacking stress to force alignment creates long-term reliability and repairability problems that manifest years later.

cluster_id: Boeing aircraft rivet hole fit-up specification
display_name: Boeing aircraft rivet hole fit-up specification

instances.name: Boeing aircraft rivet hole fit-up specification
instances.summary: Boeing engineers discovered that applying excessive force during rivet hole alignment on aircraft skin caused fatigue failures due to locked-in residual stresses, leading to a strict 5-pound maximum force specification.
instances.key_quote: they're so worried about the key and residual stresses on the skin of that aircraft they basically the structural engineer sitting in manufacturing you cannot apply more than 5 pounds of force
instances.framework_lesson: General specifications must be tailored to specific applications; a single force limit cannot account for different failure modes in aircraft skin versus naval hulls.

display_name: Come-along pulling injury in shipyard

cluster_id: New York City underground copper wire capacity crisis
display_name: New York City underground copper wire capacity crisis

instances.name: New York City underground copper wire capacity crisis
instances.summary: In the early 1970s, New York City faced a capacity crisis in underground tunnels and manholes because there was insufficient room to route all the copper wire needed for telephone conversations and electrical power distribution to skyscrapers.
instances.key_quote: there was not enough room in the manholes or the tunnels under New York to carry the amount of copper wire that was necessary to carry all the telephone conversations and the electrical power

cluster_id: High-temperature superconductors hype and failure to solve power transmission
display_name: High-temperature superconductors hype and failure to solve power transmission

instances.name: High-temperature superconductors hype and failure to solve power transmission
instances.summary: In 1989, high-temperature superconductors were promoted as the solution to power distribution problems in major cities, but failed to deliver because achieving high critical temperature caused unacceptable degradation in critical current density and required unachievable pinning centers.
instances.key_quote: we knew that a huge critical temperature increase was going to translate to real problems in trying to get any decent critical current and you can't make a magnet without putting current through it
instances.framework_lesson: Materials scientists must understand the interrelationships between critical properties; optimizing one parameter (temperature) can fatally compromise another (current density).

cluster_id: Nigerian cement shipping crisis
display_name: Nigerian cement shipping crisis

instances.name: Nigerian cement shipping crisis
instances.summary: Nigeria ordered approximately 200 shiploads of cement during an oil-boom period under a corrupt government, but the ships sat idle in harbor unable to unload due to strikes and logistical failures, causing the cement to set up from humidity and rendering the vessels unusable.
instances.key_quote: there were 200 ships sitting in the Nigerian Harbor full of cement waiting to unload because they had a strike or something and all these ships are going to be junk

cluster_id: Shoe factory boiler explosion (Brockton, Massachusetts)
display_name: Shoe factory boiler explosion (Brockton, Massachusetts)

instances.name: Shoe factory boiler explosion (Brockton, Massachusetts)
instances.summary: A boiler exploded at a shoe factory in Brockton, Massachusetts around 1905, prompting the ASME to develop the Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code.
instances.key_quote: it was a shoe factory down here in Brockton Massachusetts a boiler blew up Kapow and around 1905 or something like that and the ASME decided we're getting tired of this
instances.framework_lesson: Catastrophic boiler failures led to the creation of standardized codes and safety factors to replace ad-hoc design practices.

cluster_id: Early steamboat boiler explosions (Ohio River)
display_name: Early steamboat boiler explosions (Ohio River)

instances.name: Early steamboat boiler explosions (Ohio River)
instances.summary: Steamboat boilers exploded on the Ohio River, leading Congress to appropriate funds for the first US government-sponsored research contract in 1836 to investigate boiler failures.
instances.key_quote: the first research contract ever led by the US government was in 1836 or something some-some steamboat on the Ohio River blew up and Congress actually appropriated some money for someone to go and figure out why these boilers were blowing up
instances.framework_lesson: Repeated catastrophic boiler explosions necessitated government intervention and formal scientific investigation to establish design principles and safety factors.

cluster_id: Offshore Gulf of Mexico near-miss incidents post-2000
display_name: Offshore Gulf of Mexico near-miss incidents post-2000

instances.name: Offshore Gulf of Mexico near-miss incidents post-2000
instances.summary: Three additional near-miss failures in offshore Gulf of Mexico structures since 2000, several involving bolting failures, that did not result in major oil losses like the BP disaster.

instances.framework_lesson: Bolting technology in marine environments emerged as a critical safety concern requiring systematic study and standardization across the offshore drilling industry.

cluster_id: Nuclear reactor bolt failures
display_name: Nuclear reactor bolt failures

instances.name: Nuclear reactor bolt failures
instances.summary: Early 1980s nuclear reactor experienced bolt failures; Eagar performed forensic fracture analysis on an unknown bolt and determined it suffered low-cycle fatigue at 600°F with variable amplitude loading.
instances.key_quote: they said we're gonna send you a bolt and we're not going to tell you anything about it we want to know what you can tell us about it from just looking at it
instances.framework_lesson: Fracture surface analysis (striations, coloration, crack morphology) can reveal operating temperature, stress history, and fatigue mechanism without historical documentation.

cluster_id: Offshore structure bolt failures

instances.key_quote: on the offshore structures torque ranges plus or minus thirty percent and they wonder why they have failures when they put these things down in the ocean
instances.framework_lesson: Industry preference for simple torque-wrench methods with ±30% tolerance over proven stretch-control or ultrasonic methods leads to preventable subsea structure failures.

cluster_id: Alaska Pipeline Welding Union Control
display_name: Alaska Pipeline Welding Union Control

instances.name: Alaska Pipeline Welding Union Control
instances.summary: The Tulsa-based pipeline welding union has maintained monopolistic control over manual pipeline welding globally since the 1976 Alaskan pipeline project, preventing adoption of automated welding technology despite superior quality available since 1976.
instances.key_quote: the tulsa union will make sure everybody knows they had the technology to do automated welding in 1976 on the alaskan pipeline probably could have come up with better quality than manual welders built it manually and they still do 40 years later
instances.framework_lesson: Union control and informal agreements (not in written contracts) can prevent adoption of superior welding technology globally, with exceptions only where the union lacks enforcement power such as former Soviet Union or China.

cluster_id: Navy aluminum ship cracking

instances.framework_lesson: Aluminum structures require fundamentally different joint design and stress concentration mitigation than steel; applying steel design practices incurs significant safety factor penalties.

cluster_id: Titanium anhydrous ammonia explosions
display_name: Titanium anhydrous ammonia explosions

instances.name: Titanium anhydrous ammonia explosions
instances.summary: Early titanium rocket program components failed catastrophically when exposed to anhydrous ammonia with low moisture content, causing stress corrosion cracking and explosions at launch pads.
instances.key_quote: titanium will react with anhydrous ammonia and cause stress corrosion cracking and they had some really exciting explosions they were using some of this with some of the rocket programs early on and blow up some of the launch pads
instances.framework_lesson: Engineers selected titanium based on its superior corrosion resistance without anticipating that anhydrous ammonia, lacking protective moisture, would cause titanium nitride and hydride formation leading to failure.

cluster_id: Automotive spot welding aluminum inspection
display_name: Automotive spot welding aluminum inspection

instances.name: Automotive spot welding aluminum inspection
instances.summary: An automotive engineer proposed testing spot-welded aluminum joints by striking them with a hammer to destruction as an inspection method, which was criticized as counterproductive and likely to introduce damage.
instances.key_quote: he said well all we need to do is we'll make our welds and then we'll come in and we'll hit the thing really hard with a hammer and if it breaks it's not good enough
instances.framework_lesson: Destructive testing by hammer blow can introduce new damage that obscures the true weld quality and causes premature failure at one-tenth the expected strength.

cluster_id: GE Lynn jet engine turbine disk friction welding
display_name: GE Lynn jet engine turbine disk friction welding

instances.name: GE Lynn jet engine turbine disk friction welding
instances.summary: General Electric friction-welds titanium turbine disks worth $25,000 each to create $70,000 assemblies for jet engines, with critical need for first-pass success because poor welds result in total scrap loss.
instances.key_quote: they friction well two twenty five thousand dollar parts to make a seventy thousand dollar part and they really want it to be a good friction weld the first time because otherwise you end up with fifty thousand dollars worth of scrap
instances.framework_lesson: High-value friction welds require extensive instrumentation of force, pressure, and displacement to ensure reproducibility when post-weld inspection is difficult or impossible.

cluster_id: Ball bearing precision manufacturing
display_name: Ball bearing precision manufacturing

instances.name: Ball bearing precision manufacturing
instances.summary: High-precision ball bearing manufacturing requires temperature and humidity-controlled facilities with specialized micrometer equipment because dimensional measurements are sensitive to thermal changes of 2°F or less.
instances.key_quote: the micrometers to measure the balls for very Precision ball bearings are the size of a small lathe okay and they are in a room that is temperature controlled and humidity controlled cuz a 2° Fahrenheit change in temperature will screw up your measurement
instances.framework_lesson: Precision tolerancing below 50 millionths of an inch requires specification of measurement temperature and environmental controls, not just dimensional tolerance values.

display_name: Orange peel surface defect in sheet metal forming

instances.name: Orange peel surface defect in sheet metal forming
instances.summary: Large grain sizes in sheet metal cause non-uniform surface deformation that produces a rough, orange-peel texture, degrading surface quality beyond design tolerances.
instances.key_quote: later on we'll talk about orange peel and how large grains don't deform uniformly at the surface and you will get a Surface roughness that can be a lot more than that it's called Orange Peel because it has the texture of an orange

display_name: Brewery filling line consulting project

display_name: Alcoa beverage can production line

cluster_id: Detroit radiator tube stamping consulting case
display_name: Detroit radiator tube stamping consulting case

instances.key_quote: this is something else that I probably consulted on 25 years ago um in Detroit I think the guy was an MIT Alum but the parts they were making were radiators

cluster_id: Wire drawing centerline cracking (2011 aluminum alloy)
display_name: Wire drawing centerline cracking (2011 aluminum alloy)

instances.name: Wire drawing centerline cracking (2011 aluminum alloy)
instances.summary: High-Delta wire drawing of 2011 free-machining aluminum alloy induced internal centerline fractures in round wire due to inhomogeneous deformation leaving an undeformed central plug under tension.
instances.key_quote: internal fracture in a round wire induced by high Delta drawing two 2011 free Machining aluminum alloy so this is aluminum alloy and you can see the deformation
instances.framework_lesson: Excessive reduction ratio (Delta of 3-4) causes insufficient deformation at the wire center, creating a tensile stress state that induces centerline cracking.

cluster_id: Extruded steel rod centerline cracking
display_name: Extruded steel rod centerline cracking

instances.name: Extruded steel rod centerline cracking
instances.summary: Centerline cracks observed in extruded steel rods, a classic example of inhomogeneous deformation defects documented by Don Blick (graduate of MIT Materials Science and former VP of Research at Bethlehem Steel).
instances.key_quote: this is uh Centerline cracks and extruded steel rods from Don blicked who's Don blicked well he's a graduate of this department but it was my first boss he was vice president of research at betham steel
instances.framework_lesson: Extrusion processes with improper die design or reduction ratios produce residual tensile stresses along the centerline, initiating cracks.

cluster_id: High-nitrogen steel wire deformation cracking
display_name: High-nitrogen steel wire deformation cracking

instances.name: High-nitrogen steel wire deformation cracking
instances.summary: High-nitrogen steel wire underwent axis-symmetric deformation in a low-Delta operation (circa 1958) showing tension development at the centerline due to inhomogeneous compression, initiating localized shear slip.
instances.key_quote: look at this little arrow point right there that's intention it actually started to slip and Shear you see those that little arrow point right there that's in tension at that location
instances.framework_lesson: Even low-Delta deformation operations can concentrate tensile stresses at the center when compression fails to penetrate, causing centerline tension cracks.

cluster_id: Helicopter bearing failure and crash (Stratford)
display_name: Helicopter bearing failure and crash (Stratford)

instances.name: Helicopter bearing failure and crash (Stratford)
instances.summary: A helicopter crashed in Stratford, killing two pilots, after bearing failure caused by distorted aluminum housing from an out-of-square milling machine; the bearing manufacturer was blamed and sued despite the root cause being the helicopter company's defective machining.
instances.key_quote: they had a milling machine that wasn't Square itself it was making distorted aluminum housings they knew it they hit it and they passed the buck to the bearing manufacturer
instances.framework_lesson: Specifications and conformity standards for bearing geometry cannot detect distortion in the housing that deforms the bearing during operation; root-cause analysis requires examining the entire assembly, not just the bearing component in isolation.

cluster_id: Bearing conformity misidentification investigation
display_name: Bearing conformity misidentification investigation

instances.name: Bearing conformity misidentification investigation
instances.summary: During forensic investigation of bearing failures across multiple helicopters, physical wear patterns (hourglass vs. barrel shapes on 188 of 192 balls) allowed definitive determination that the bearings had the correct conformity type, redirecting investigation toward the housing rather than the bearing itself.
instances.key_quote: I had 192 balls and I could actually after that incident I could measure 188 of them and they were all hourglass so what's the chance I had the wrong conformity zero folks
instances.framework_lesson: Physical evidence from failure analysis (wear patterns) can definitively rule out specification non-conformity and redirect investigation to system-level integration issues.

cluster_id: Precision measurement methodology in bearing manufacture
display_name: Precision measurement methodology in bearing manufacture

instances.name: Precision measurement methodology in bearing manufacture
instances.summary: Manufacture of a 42-inch diameter bearing with 0.002-inch tolerance required precision measurement using a micrometer mounted on an oil-bath equipped machine tool to maintain thermal equilibrium between the measuring instrument and workpiece.
instances.key_quote: they would put it in the machine with the oil bath that was doing the Machining of this 42 inch diameter half inch square race so it was at the same temperatures
instances.framework_lesson: Engineering drawings and specifications must define the temperature at which tolerances apply, and measurement methodology must ensure the measuring instrument and workpiece are in thermal equilibrium to achieve stated accuracy.

cluster_id: Military helicopter bearing failure and fleet grounding
display_name: Military helicopter bearing failure and fleet grounding

instances.name: Military helicopter bearing failure and fleet grounding
instances.summary: A military helicopter fleet was grounded after bearing failures; the FBI took over quality control at the bearing manufacturer in Michigan, suspecting specification violations, but investigation revealed the bearings met spec and the failure was due to improper conformity in the bearing geometry causing seizure during frictional heating.
instances.key_quote: the whole fleet was grounded and this had risen all the way up to the Pentagon and people were saying if you know if we have some problem you know someone attacks us we don't have any mh-53s available
instances.framework_lesson: Interface failures between components from different suppliers meeting their individual specifications highlight the gap between part-level spec compliance and system-level fit; tolerances of one part in ten thousand require clean-room measurement at controlled temperature to verify conformance.

cluster_id: Helicopter swash plate milling machine degradation
display_name: Helicopter swash plate milling machine degradation

instances.key_quote: they scrapped a three-year-old machine everybody throws out three million dollar machines after three years and we were trying to find out what happened to this milling machine if we'd gotten that milling machine and found that the bed was potato chipped

cluster_id: 1973 Honda Civic corrosion failure
display_name: 1973 Honda Civic corrosion failure

instances.name: 1973 Honda Civic corrosion failure
instances.summary: The original 1973 Honda Civic had severe corrosion and rust-through failures within one year, giving Honda a terrible reputation for reliability.
instances.key_quote: in 1973 they came out with a Honda Civic and it would rust through in one year it had terrible corrosion resistance
instances.framework_lesson: Honda's failure to design for corrosion resistance cost them their reputation in the 1970s, but they subsequently improved corrosion protection beyond Toyota and rebuilt their brand through materials and design improvements.

cluster_id: Boston hot-work fire-watch regulation and building cost impact
display_name: Boston hot-work fire-watch regulation and building cost impact

instances.name: Boston hot-work fire-watch regulation and building cost impact
instances.summary: Boston and Massachusetts OSHA regulations require a fire-watch (often a paid fireman) during any hot work including soldering, significantly increasing construction costs by requiring 2–3 people to stand watch while one person works.
instances.key_quote: in construction this is one of the reasons construction whether it's bridges and buildings has gotten so expensive because you generally have two or three people standing around watching one person work
instances.framework_lesson: Safety regulations mandating fire watch during hot work created a cost externality in construction that incentivized alternative materials and assembly methods (mechanical fittings instead of soldering) to eliminate the hot work entirely.

cluster_id: Plastic gas can fire forensics case
display_name: Plastic gas can fire forensics case

instances.name: Plastic gas can fire forensics case
instances.summary: A plastic gas can survived a major fire, but when examined three years later after being sealed and stored, the gasoline had completely diffused through the plastic walls and evaporated.
instances.key_quote: I had a situation once where someone was interested there was a big fire and someone had a plastic gas can that was survived the fire and they wanted to know how much gas had been poured out of this gas can
instances.framework_lesson: Designers switching from lead-coated steel to plastic for gas tanks underestimated permeability as a failure mode; gasoline diffuses completely through plastic over time.

cluster_id: EPA gasoline permeability regulations and Ford gas tank design
display_name: EPA gasoline permeability regulations and Ford gas tank design

instances.name: EPA gasoline permeability regulations and Ford gas tank design
instances.summary: The EPA discovered that plastic gas tanks on 30 million cars were losing 1% of all gasoline in the U.S. as volatile organic vapors through permeability, prompting regulatory action requiring multi-layer composite tanks.
instances.key_quote: the EPA came along said oh we're losing 1 percent of all the gasoline in the country as volatile organic hydrocarbons into the air by just the permeability through all these 30 million cars
instances.framework_lesson: Initial plastic gas tank designs ignored permeability constraints; EPA regulations forced manufacturers to adopt seven-layer composite structures to meet emission and permeability requirements.

instances.key_quote: one of the breweries was advertising I think it was Coors that they had plastic beer bottles okay yeah oh they had to do a lot of work make sure you didn't taste like plastic

display_name: 1980s aluminum beverage can design optimization using supercomputers
