instances.key_quote: what they did in the 1980s they were using supercomputers to design the aluminum cans it's a huge it's a multi multi-billion dollar industry

cluster_id: Gas distribution pipeline corrosion and leaks
display_name: Gas distribution pipeline corrosion and leaks

instances.name: Gas distribution pipeline corrosion and leaks
instances.summary: Half a million miles of oil and gas pipe in the United States, much 60-70 years old, are corroding significantly, resulting in approximately 5% of distributed gas being lost and requiring continuous leak-detection and repair operations.
instances.key_quote: in this country and we've got you know something like a half million miles of major pipe for oil and gas in this country much of which is 60 to 70 years old and is corroding big-time

cluster_id: Plastic-coated gas pipe material substitution
display_name: Plastic-coated gas pipe material substitution

instances.name: Plastic-coated gas pipe material substitution
instances.summary: In the 1980s, the American Gas Association promoted thermally welded plastic-coated gas pipe as a solution to widespread pipeline corrosion, resulting in three decades of plastic pipe installation across residential and commercial systems.
instances.key_quote: back in the 1980s the American Gas Association decided the solution was plastic and so this is a piece of gas pipe
instances.framework_lesson: Switching to new materials (plastic coatings) without full understanding of long-term failure modes created unforeseen maintenance and leak-detection burdens across the distribution infrastructure.

cluster_id: High-pressure vessel explosion (200-ton)
display_name: High-pressure vessel explosion (200-ton)

instances.name: High-pressure vessel explosion (200-ton)
instances.summary: A 200-ton high-pressure vessel failed catastrophically due to a half-inch crack initiated at a corrosion pit, combining thermal stress, low toughness, and hydrogen embrittlement in high-strength steel.
instances.key_quote: a half inch long that blew up a 200 ton vessel because everything all three things were just destroying it
instances.framework_lesson: Transient thermal stress analysis was not computationally feasible during design (early 1980s), leaving engineers unaware of stresses exceeding service calculations; modern FEM should have caught this.

cluster_id: French electrical generator rotor forging failure
display_name: French electrical generator rotor forging failure

instances.name: French electrical generator rotor forging failure
instances.summary: A large generator rotor forging in a French electrical utility failed from stress corrosion cracking caused by outdated water treatment (molybdates) and pitting in the same high-strength steel composition.
instances.key_quote: they had a failure in france of a great big generator rotor forging in a utility electrical generating plant that it had stress corrosion cracking
instances.framework_lesson: Water treatment specifications and corrosion-control practices become obsolete; systematic literature review and inspection protocols are needed to catch outdated standards before reuse.

cluster_id: Oil and gas transmission pipeline inspection and fracture assessment
display_name: Oil and gas transmission pipeline inspection and fracture assessment

instances.name: Oil and gas transmission pipeline inspection and fracture assessment
instances.summary: Companies operating 300,000 miles of U.S. gas and liquid transmission pipelines lacked historical records of steel quality and fracture properties; a company developed technology to test pipes in-service without interrupting operations, requiring excavation-site sampling during active transmission.
instances.key_quote: there's 300,000 miles of those pipes in just in the US for gas and liquid and what our technology allows is a lot of companies don't have historical records of the quality of the steel plus the steel is not tested for fracture properties
instances.framework_lesson: Existing regulations required life-safety oversight of pipelines but did not mandate that operators maintain fracture-property test data or toughness specifications for installed steel.

cluster_id: Battleship armor plate forging press weld repair
display_name: Battleship armor plate forging press weld repair

instances.name: Battleship armor plate forging press weld repair
instances.summary: An 18-inch-thick armor plate weld on a 60-year-old forging press cracked during repair welding and again after improper welding procedure, failing on first forge attempt due to residual stresses.
instances.key_quote: the first time they welded it and they didn't do the proper welding procedure it cracked before they even got it off the welding platform
instances.framework_lesson: Proper welding procedures, stress relief, and preheat are critical for thick sections; improper procedure led to cracking from residual stress even with defect-free welds.

cluster_id: Aluminum truck wheel press weld crack
display_name: Aluminum truck wheel press weld crack

instances.name: Aluminum truck wheel press weld crack
instances.summary: At a major aluminum truck wheel manufacturer, a newly welded press (8000-ton capacity) cracked on the first truck wheel forging attempt due to residual stresses from improper welding procedure.
instances.key_quote: the first one they hit got another crack because of residual stresses and improper welding procedures
instances.framework_lesson: Even successful defect-free welds can fail catastrophically if welding procedure and stress relief do not account for residual stress in thick structural components.

cluster_id: WWII submarine steel development
display_name: WWII submarine steel development

instances.name: WWII submarine steel development
instances.summary: The U.S. Navy developed HY-80 high-strength steel for submarines in the mid-1950s, replacing WWII-era ~50 ksi steel to achieve greater depth capability for the nuclear submarine USS Nautilus.
instances.key_quote: submarines in world war ii were basically kind of i don't know exactly what they were probably 50 ksi steel hy 80 was 80 ksi yield so you had a big improvement in depth capability
instances.framework_lesson: Higher strength alone was insufficient; concurrent work on ductile-brittle transition in welded ship steels was necessary to prevent catastrophic failures.

cluster_id: Pellini explosion bulge test development
display_name: Pellini explosion bulge test development

instances.name: Pellini explosion bulge test development
instances.summary: Pellini developed the explosion bulge test method to characterize ductile-brittle behavior in welded ship steels, revealing that steel becomes brittle near room temperature and correlating this to fracture energy.
instances.key_quote: pelini started out doing research and he developed things where you make sure you got nice ductile steel when it was welded
instances.framework_lesson: Pre-existing knowledge of ductile-brittle transition was not systematized or acted upon until Pellini's quantitative testing methodology made the phenomenon measurable and predictable.

cluster_id: General Electric GT-100 patent litigation
display_name: General Electric GT-100 patent litigation

instances.name: General Electric GT-100 patent litigation
instances.summary: General Electric developed the GT-100 nickel-based superalloy in the early 1980s without initially patenting it; competitors like Siemens and Pratt & Whitney designed it into their turbine systems; GE's patent on the heat treatment was unexpectedly approved in 1997-1998 after 15+ years, forcing competitors to pay royalties or substitute alloys.
instances.key_quote: all of a sudden general electric says oh well if you're using my alloy you owe me money okay royalties everybody was very unhappy
instances.framework_lesson: Patent office delays on heat-treatment intellectual property created competitive disadvantage and forced scrambling redesigns; purchasing components includes implicit licensing of patented processes.

cluster_id: Turbine blade coarsening and remanufacturing with HIP treatment
display_name: Turbine blade coarsening and remanufacturing with HIP treatment

instances.name: Turbine blade coarsening and remanufacturing with HIP treatment
instances.summary: Jet and land-based turbine blades using gamma-prime strengthened GT-100 alloy underwent microstructural coarsening after 10,000 hours of operation, losing high-temperature strength; a Greek student working with Chromaloid developed a hot isostatic press (HIP) heat treatment to restore the fine blocky gamma-phase structure without re-melting, allowing operators to reuse expensive patented components.
instances.key_quote: after 10000 hours of use in a jet engine these blocky carbides had a blocky gamma phase had become rounded and much larger and didn't have the same properties but we took these old turbine blades and gave them a hot isostatic press heat treatment
instances.framework_lesson: HIP remanufacturing enabled reuse of patent-protected components by restoring original microstructure without melting, circumventing royalty payments on replacement parts.

cluster_id: Unspecified nickel-based alloy cracking in inconel components
display_name: Unspecified nickel-based alloy cracking in inconel components

instances.name: Unspecified nickel-based alloy cracking in inconel components
instances.summary: Cracking problems were encountered in inconel (nickel-based) welded components during welding operations, requiring strict adherence to welding procedures, heat inputs, and joint preparation.
instances.key_quote: we had a problem with some cracks in some of the income that we had yeah and you got to follow the procedures
instances.framework_lesson: Nickel-based alloys require rigorous procedure control; cracking susceptibility demands validated joint design and thermal management.

cluster_id: Navy cupro-nickel pipe cracking and compression repair attempt
display_name: Navy cupro-nickel pipe cracking and compression repair attempt

instances.name: Navy cupro-nickel pipe cracking and compression repair attempt
instances.summary: US Navy vessels using cupro-nickel pipes experienced solidification cracking in welds; a proposed repair involved compressing the pipe diameter to introduce compressive residual stresses and arrest crack growth.
instances.key_quote: we had a problem with some cracks in some of the income that we had yeah and one of the solutions was going to be to you know propose to basically crush the pipe in order to close the cracks
instances.framework_lesson: Non-standard repair methods circumvent established welding codes that universally require crack removal or grinding; fracture mechanics evaluation is necessary before leaving cracks in service.

cluster_id: MIT steam plant pipe cracking during hot-weld repair
display_name: MIT steam plant pipe cracking during hot-weld repair

instances.name: MIT steam plant pipe cracking during hot-weld repair
instances.summary: A steam pipe on MIT campus near 100 Memorial Drive developed a weeping leak and cracked repeatedly during attempted hot welding due to hydrogen embrittlement from moisture in residual steam.
instances.key_quote: they're trying to hot weld it while it's still got steam in it and you got moisture coming out of the crack and guess what you get hydrogen
instances.framework_lesson: Field repair procedures must account for environmental hydrogen sources; temporary patched solutions can be acceptable when permanent shutdown poses greater institutional risk.

cluster_id: USS Sheffield fire (Falkland Islands War)
display_name: USS Sheffield fire (Falkland Islands War)

instances.name: USS Sheffield fire (Falkland Islands War)
instances.summary: The HMS Sheffield caught fire during the Falkland Islands War, prompting the U.S. Navy to reconsider aluminum superstructures on surface ships.
instances.key_quote: after the falklands war and the sheffield caught fire and the belknap caught fire um with the jf the first jfk carrier the navy then got out of aluminum

cluster_id: Naval cruiser 5456 aluminum corrosion and fatigue cracking
display_name: Naval cruiser 5456 aluminum corrosion and fatigue cracking

instances.name: Naval cruiser 5456 aluminum corrosion and fatigue cracking
instances.summary: A 30-year-old naval cruiser built with 5456 aluminum alloy experienced severe pitting corrosion and fatigue cracking in its superstructure due to alloy composition and residual stresses from thick welded sections.
instances.key_quote: the navy has had a lot of problems with the 5000 series alloys when they started using them in the 70s and 80s on the superstructures it took a few years for all the stuff to develop
instances.framework_lesson: Thicker aluminum structures develop severe residual stresses that initiate fatigue cracks at pitting sites; alloy selection must account for thickness-dependent stress state, not strength alone.

cluster_id: LCS (Littoral Combat Ship) aluminum alloy redesign
display_name: LCS (Littoral Combat Ship) aluminum alloy redesign

instances.name: LCS (Littoral Combat Ship) aluminum alloy redesign
instances.summary: The Navy switched from 5456 to 5083 aluminum alloy for LCS superstructures, sacrificing 10-20% strength to eliminate pitting and fatigue crack initiation problems observed in earlier vessels.
instances.key_quote: for the lcs's the navy said okay we don't want that extra 10 or 20 percent of strength we'll go with the 5083 which is readily available and we'll design with that
instances.framework_lesson: Design standards must balance alloy strength against environmental degradation mechanisms; specifying a stronger alloy without controlling for pitting-induced fatigue initiation can be counterproductive.

cluster_id: W-temper aluminum sale and incomplete heat treatment
display_name: W-temper aluminum sale and incomplete heat treatment

instances.name: W-temper aluminum sale and incomplete heat treatment
instances.summary: A vendor sold aluminum components in W temper (solution heat treated but not precipitation hardened) with the expectation that the customer would complete heat treatment, but the customer failed to do so, leaving the material in a weak condition.
instances.key_quote: someone was selling something in a w temper and they were supposed to heat treat it later but they didn't or something
instances.framework_lesson: Specifications and supplier-customer communication regarding post-delivery heat treatment must be explicit and verified; relying on downstream completion of critical heat-treatment steps creates risk.

cluster_id: Ohio automotive supplier emergency call from Honda
display_name: Ohio automotive supplier emergency call from Honda

instances.name: Ohio automotive supplier emergency call from Honda
instances.summary: A Honda manufacturing plant in Marysville, Ohio called an automotive parts supplier demanding immediate assistance with a defect, requiring a 2-hour emergency response.
instances.key_quote: they had a problem with the parts he was supplying and it turns out it's two hours away so they meant leave right now and come see us at one o'clock
instances.framework_lesson: Japanese manufacturers treated supplier problems as collaborative problem-solving opportunities rather than as grounds for litigation, contrasting sharply with American automakers' adversarial approach.

cluster_id: Rural washing machine failures with potato loads
display_name: Rural washing machine failures with potato loads

instances.name: Rural washing machine failures with potato loads
instances.summary: Post-WWII washing machines designed for clothes broke down in rural areas because farmers were using them to wash potatoes instead, causing overload failures the machines were not engineered to handle.
instances.key_quote: they found the problem was the farmers were washing potatoes rather than clothes and the potato the machines were not designed for potatoes
instances.framework_lesson: Listening to the voice of the customer—actually investigating field failures in their context—revealed that the machine's actual use case differed fundamentally from its assumed design use case.

cluster_id: Motorola control chart implementation variance
display_name: Motorola control chart implementation variance

instances.name: Motorola control chart implementation variance
instances.summary: Two Motorola facilities with identical Shewhart control chart policies showed vastly different outcomes: one facility's workers viewed charts as management busywork with no utility, while another facility's workers actively understood and used them for process improvement.
instances.key_quote: I go to another Motorola facility he said what's that and the guy would say oh and he'd start explaining it to me in detail I said is it useful he says oh yes I mean I can see what my body tells me how he's using it
instances.framework_lesson: Implementing statistical process control tools requires worker understanding and buy-in; top-down mandate without education renders charts ineffective.

cluster_id: Apollo 13 oxygen regeneration emergency
display_name: Apollo 13 oxygen regeneration emergency

instances.name: Apollo 13 oxygen regeneration emergency
instances.summary: NASA engineers improvised a carbon-dioxide scrubbing system using available materials aboard the lunar module to keep astronauts alive during the return to Earth.
instances.key_quote: the NASA engineers had to use duct tape and other things they had to figure out what was available on that lunar module or whatever the thing they were going to re-enter this and how they could make something that would regenerate the oxygen

cluster_id: British submarine shipyard piping interference problem
display_name: British submarine shipyard piping interference problem

instances.name: British submarine shipyard piping interference problem
instances.summary: Submarine construction experienced chronic delays and design-build conflicts where pipe routing drawings specified physically impossible installations; inventory theft created cascading supply-chain failures.
instances.key_quote: there were people would look at the drawing and they'd find well this is impossible to build what's on the drawing doesn't fit okay and they had various ways to measure things in the field
instances.framework_lesson: Pipe routing must be coordinated three-dimensionally and validated against actual shipyard capabilities; sequential part substitutions due to inventory depletion create compounding future defects.

cluster_id: Submarine construction schedule overrun
display_name: Submarine construction schedule overrun

instances.name: Submarine construction schedule overrun
instances.summary: A British submarine took ten years to build when it should have taken three years, primarily due to piping design-build conflicts and supply-chain inventory problems.
instances.key_quote: as a result it took them ten years to build a submarine when it should have only taken three okay that's a problem
instances.framework_lesson: Design-for-manufacturability and validated supply-chain processes are critical path items; unresolved build conflicts cause schedule expansion that exceeds design-stage pressure hull requirements.

cluster_id: Motorola circuit board component shortage
display_name: Motorola circuit board component shortage

instances.name: Motorola circuit board component shortage
instances.summary: Motorola faced chronic inventory shortages on circuit board assembly because spare-component stock ordering was a bottleneck constraint; defective parts couldn't be compensated with extra stock.
instances.key_quote: the biggest problem in making circuit boards that Motorola was finding the inventory to build it even though you had all kinds of people who were there trying to make sure they had all the stock ordered
instances.framework_lesson: Supply-chain inventory optimization must account for quality defect rates and lead-time variability; undersized safety stock becomes a manufacturing constraint.

cluster_id: Ford F-150 air conditioner assembly constraint
display_name: Ford F-150 air conditioner assembly constraint

instances.name: Ford F-150 air conditioner assembly constraint
instances.summary: Ford faced a capacity bottleneck on the air conditioner assembly line for the F-150 pickup truck in the mid-to-late 1990s; consultants advised introducing a constraint to increase throughput, but the real constraint was variable worker speed, not machine capacity.
instances.key_quote: they had spent a quarter million dollars on consultants who were experts in Theory of Constraints coming in looking at this thing and it turns out what they had told these consultants had told Ford was to introduce a constraint into the system to get more throughput through
instances.framework_lesson: Over-reliance on consultant-driven frameworks like Theory of Constraints without ground-level problem investigation leads to expensive, ineffective solutions; the actual constraint (human labor variability) was discovered only by asking the plant foreman directly.
materials_or_systems: manual hand assembly with some automation

cluster_id: Steel mill wet scrap incident
display_name: Steel mill wet scrap incident

instances.name: Steel mill wet scrap incident
instances.summary: A steel mill loaded wet scrap steel into a furnace during rain, introducing water that resulted in elevated hydrogen contamination in the finished steel.
instances.key_quote: water was just pouring out of it into the steel furnace okay so that's how you get three parts per million hydrogen in your steel
instances.framework_lesson: Manufacturing process control for hydrogen ingress was inadequate; scrap handling procedures did not account for moisture contamination from outdoor conditions.

cluster_id: Consulting engagement with Bruce Sylvester (metallurgical services)
display_name: Consulting engagement with Bruce Sylvester (metallurgical services)

instances.name: Consulting engagement with Bruce Sylvester (metallurgical services)
instances.summary: Eagar was hired as a consulting metallurgist to perform failure analysis work, completing 500 failure analyses per year and discovering recurring failure modes across different clients.
instances.key_quote: i was doing about 500 failure analyses a year and that's how you learn as you just do okay and you see the same thing you know after about 100 you start seeing some of the same things come in

cluster_id: Evanston Wyoming hydrogen sulfide gas field operations
display_name: Evanston Wyoming hydrogen sulfide gas field operations

instances.name: Evanston Wyoming hydrogen sulfide gas field operations
instances.summary: Gas drilling operations in Evanston, Wyoming on a gas dome containing up to 17% hydrogen sulfide required extraction and handling of the toxic gas; two competing companies (Chevron and Amoco) chose different infrastructure solutions for removing hydrogen sulfide.
instances.key_quote: they're on top of a gas dome in that area which has got up to 17 percent hydrogen sulfide in it and hydrogen sulfide it actually it's actually the reason it's toxic is because at first it has a terrible smell

cluster_id: Arctic hydrogen cracking in buried steel
display_name: Arctic hydrogen cracking in buried steel

instances.name: Arctic hydrogen cracking in buried steel
instances.summary: A welded steel structure buried in snow in the high Sierras experienced hydrogen cracking that remained dormant for nine months before causing damage, demonstrating the role of low temperature in delaying crack propagation.
instances.key_quote: i might tell you on monday a story of hydrogen crack where it got buried in the snow in the high sierras and it lasted for nine months in the steel okay before it did this damage

cluster_id: Lead-sealed water pipe failures
display_name: Lead-sealed water pipe failures

instances.name: Lead-sealed water pipe failures
instances.summary: Water pipes sealed with lead from the 1880s failed after approximately 90 years of service.
instances.key_quote: I've worked on pipes that failed water pipes that are lead sealed okay that failed you know 90 years later um these were kind of 1880s type of stuff

cluster_id: Wooden gas distribution pipes
display_name: Wooden gas distribution pipes

instances.name: Wooden gas distribution pipes
instances.summary: Original street gas lighting systems used gun-drilled wooden logs as pipes, which eventually rotted away.
instances.key_quote: the original street lights in order to pipe the gas down the street they would take a log and they would gun drill it and put a hole in it and it might be 6in diameter log and it might have a 2-in hole in it and you just hope that you know it wouldn't rot away before anyway

cluster_id: Blisk (bladed disc) development program
display_name: Blisk (bladed disc) development program

instances.name: Blisk (bladed disc) development program
instances.summary: U.S. Air Force spent tens of millions of dollars in the 1980s-1990s attempting to develop welded bladed discs for jet turbine engines using linear friction welding and other methods, but the technology never achieved sufficient quality and reliability for widespread commercial use.
instances.key_quote: the Air Force spent tens of millions of dollars trying to make bladed disc which are called blisk okay so this was back in the 80s and the '90s blisk technology
instances.framework_lesson: If one bad weld out of 100 creates scrap, the economic model fails unless Six Sigma quality welds can be achieved; linear friction welding never solved the quality consistency problem.

cluster_id: Kuwait depleted uranium contamination
display_name: Kuwait depleted uranium contamination

instances.name: Kuwait depleted uranium contamination
instances.summary: After the first Gulf War, Kuwait became contaminated with depleted uranium from munitions, prompting military research into tungsten alloy replacements.
instances.key_quote: after the first Gulf War and Kuwait got contaminated with depleted uranium and they weren't real happy

cluster_id: Titanium-clad steel gasoline refinery vessel production
display_name: Titanium-clad steel gasoline refinery vessel production

instances.name: Titanium-clad steel gasoline refinery vessel production
instances.summary: Large steel pressure vessels are clad with titanium sheets using explosive bonding at specialized facilities to create corrosion-resistant tanks for sulfuric acid processing in gasoline production.
instances.key_quote: they take a great big steel tank and they clad it with like a quarter in of tanum and they do it by explosive bonding

cluster_id: Intel chip wire bonding defect scrap problem
display_name: Intel chip wire bonding defect scrap problem

instances.name: Intel chip wire bonding defect scrap problem
instances.summary: Intel microprocessors require up to 400 wire bonds per chip; if even one bond fails during manufacturing, the entire $500 chip becomes scrap, creating high-cost consequences for poor bonding quality control.
instances.key_quote: you can make a $500 chip and has to have 400 good bonds and if it has 399 good bonds it's a scrap chip okay
instances.framework_lesson: Joining processes at the end of manufacturing create extreme cost penalties for defects, amplifying the importance of process reliability.

cluster_id: Turbine blade quality control theft risk
display_name: Turbine blade quality control theft risk

instances.name: Turbine blade quality control theft risk
instances.summary: Worn-out or defective turbine blades worth $4,000–$6,000 each create theft incentive if the defect marking is removed, risking engine failure if bad blades are installed in service.
instances.key_quote: when these things are worth four or five or $6,000 a piece you know there's a great incentive for people to steal bad ones and put them in engines which could cause the engine to blow up
instances.framework_lesson: High-value components require traceability and tamper-evident defect marking; economic incentives for fraud can override safety intent if marking systems are circumvented.

cluster_id: Aircraft engine hydrogen cracking failure
display_name: Aircraft engine hydrogen cracking failure

instances.name: Aircraft engine hydrogen cracking failure
instances.summary: A major aircraft engine component failed due to hydrogen cracking in the weld, costing $40 million and nearly forcing a three-year halt in new engine production.
instances.key_quote: only costs 40 million dollars okay so there's lots of stories of hydrogen cracking okay

cluster_id: LNG tanker shipyard plate selection and testing circumvention
display_name: LNG tanker shipyard plate selection and testing circumvention

instances.name: LNG tanker shipyard plate selection and testing circumvention
instances.summary: Shipyard workers identified high-toughness steel plates (30 foot-pounds) from incoming stock and cut them into test coupons, allowing weld degradation to occur while reporting inflated results to the Coast Guard.
instances.key_quote: they would go out and find that plate and they would cut it up into test plates and the weld might degrade something from 21 to 15 but the coast guard got a report that they were all above 20
instances.framework_lesson: Regulatory compliance testing of representative welds can be gamed through selective sampling of superior base material, undermining the intent of quality standards.

cluster_id: Sulfuric acid and sugar classroom demonstration incident
display_name: Sulfuric acid and sugar classroom demonstration incident

instances.name: Sulfuric acid and sugar classroom demonstration incident
instances.summary: Tom Eagar performed a sulfuric acid desication demonstration on sugar in a classroom (not a fume hood) that produced large volumes of carbon and sulfurous acid fumes, forcing early cancellation of class.
instances.key_quote: the classroom was a little had a little odor to it anyway and afterwards students set this up and they were doing it in the hood oh well okay so I next time I'll videotape

cluster_id: Carbon composite filter for precious metal catalyst recovery
display_name: Carbon composite filter for precious metal catalyst recovery

instances.name: Carbon composite filter for precious metal catalyst recovery
instances.summary: Coke Industries developed a carbon composite tube designed as a filter to recover precious metal catalysts (rhodium or iridium) from sulfuric acid baths in acetic acid production.
instances.key_quote: carbon has the best corrosion resistance of any material okay it's impervious to most metals and this was going to be a way to recover precious metal catalysts from baths of sulfuric acid

cluster_id: Kennecott Bingham Canyon copper mine operations
display_name: Kennecott Bingham Canyon copper mine operations

instances.name: Kennecott Bingham Canyon copper mine operations
instances.summary: The world's deepest open-pit copper mine near Salt Lake City mines ore at only 0.5% copper concentration, extracting one pound of copper from one ton of rock, making it exceptionally energy-intensive.
instances.key_quote: if you some sometimes depending on how the plane takes off out of the Salt Lake airport you'll look down you'll see this big hole in the ground goes down for over a mile deep as an open pit mine it's the Kennecott copper mine

cluster_id: Johnny Slokum aluminum-water hydrogen generation
display_name: Johnny Slokum aluminum-water hydrogen generation

instances.name: Johnny Slokum aluminum-water hydrogen generation
instances.summary: A graduate student developed a reproducible method to treat aluminum spheres with tin and gallium, allowing them to react with water and generate hydrogen gas efficiently for potential hydrogen economy applications.
instances.key_quote: Johnny found a way to do it reproducibly and quickly he brings it into my office and he drops it in and within 15 seconds the entire 5 MIM sphere of aluminum has consumed itself and generated all kinds of hydrogen

cluster_id: Bismuth-tin solder replacement causing steel scrap contamination
display_name: Bismuth-tin solder replacement causing steel scrap contamination

instances.name: Bismuth-tin solder replacement causing steel scrap contamination
instances.summary: When automotive and connector manufacturers attempted to replace lead-tin solders with bismuth-tin solders to eliminate lead toxicity, steel recycling operations faced contamination of scrap steel, rendering it worthless if bismuth concentration exceeded acceptable limits.
instances.key_quote: you start putting bismo tin solders in automobiles which is a major part of the recycling of steel scrap and you make all of the scrap worthless if you get 10 parts
instances.framework_lesson: Material substitution driven by toxicity concerns (lead removal) was not coordinated with downstream recycling and material recovery standards, creating an unintended circular-economy failure.

cluster_id: F-111 composite radar dome bird strike
display_name: F-111 composite radar dome bird strike

instances.name: F-111 composite radar dome bird strike
instances.summary: An F-111 aircraft struck a bird with its composite radar dome in the nose, and the brittle composite material fractured into fibers.
instances.key_quote: this is an F-111 and this is the composite radar Dome the nose of the aircraft to hit a bird and you know brittle composite just breaks up into a bunch of Little Fibers

cluster_id: Pratt & Whitney vs. British engine bird strike specification
display_name: Pratt & Whitney vs. British engine bird strike specification

instances.name: Pratt & Whitney vs. British engine bird strike specification
instances.summary: British engine manufacturers failed bird strike tests using frozen chickens while Pratt & Whitney passed; a British observer discovered the difference was that frozen chickens are significantly stronger than fresh ones at temperatures below 32°F.
instances.key_quote: the British were throwing in Frozen 2 and a half pound chickens okay and the point is as a function of temperature when you get below 32 degrees chickens get a lot stronger
instances.framework_lesson: Engine bird-strike specifications must account for the temperature-dependent properties of the test medium; frozen versus fresh chicken yielded fundamentally different material behavior.

cluster_id: Metallurgical consulting firm tornado-chicken vacuum chamber experiment
display_name: Metallurgical consulting firm tornado-chicken vacuum chamber experiment

instances.summary: A metallurgical testing firm, attempting to replicate a tornado effect that reportedly plucked live chickens, constructed a vacuum chamber and tested a live chicken; the animal was severely injured when internal organs were exposed due to pressure differential.
instances.key_quote: they got a live chicken and they put it in the vacuum chamber and they started pumping it down and they opened it up and this chicken kind of stumbles out and anyway he was his int Trails were coming out the other side

cluster_id: Artificial hip replacement failures - early iterations
display_name: Artificial hip replacement failures - early iterations

instances.name: Artificial hip replacement failures - early iterations

instances.key_quote: you don't get off the crutches for six months and the thing fails and you need another operation is you know that wasn't very good

cluster_id: Soft tissue engineering in biomedical materials
display_name: Soft tissue engineering in biomedical materials

instances.name: Soft tissue engineering in biomedical materials
instances.summary: Traditional biomedical material science focused on corrosion resistance in metallic implants; soft tissue engineering emerged as a new frontier requiring material scientists to expand beyond metal corrosion.
instances.key_quote: I don't just want someone who looks at corrosion of metals in the human body we've started that for years

cluster_id: US Steel Industry Crisis and Japanese Competition
display_name: US Steel Industry Crisis and Japanese Competition

instances.name: US Steel Industry Crisis and Japanese Competition
instances.summary: American steel companies in the 1950s-1960s developed advanced steelmaking technology but failed to commercialize it, allowing Japan to adopt and deploy superior technology by 1985, gaining competitive advantage.
instances.key_quote: they were doing was using the technology that has been developed at some of the American steel companies in the 1950s and the 1960s but never got commercialized because those companies didn't have the foresight
instances.framework_lesson: Poor management and lack of foresight in commercializing advanced materials technology can cede market dominance to foreign competitors who adopt and refine the technology.

display_name: U.S. Navy heavy-section titanium welding (1980s)

display_name: Army helicopter rotor casting weld repair (early 1980s)

cluster_id: Lost nuclear warhead recovery (1960s Atlantic)
display_name: Lost nuclear warhead recovery (1960s Atlantic)

cluster_id: USS Seacliff (GMAW welding failure on titanium submarine)

instances.key_quote: the US Navy tried in the nineteen 1980s they built the Seacliff which is a little bit bigger titanium a little mini sub they tried their gas metal arc welding technology they'd spent millions of dollars on they were building at Mare Island Naval Shipyard they didn't they wanted to develop their own the Navy wanted to develop their own technology so they tried and they finally gave up
instances.framework_lesson: GMAW proved inadequate for titanium submarine welding; process control and shielding requirements necessitated return to slower but more reliable GTAW (TIG) process.

cluster_id: John Wolfe stainless steel sensitization patent (1940s)

instances.framework_lesson: Sensitization—the precipitation of chromium carbides at grain boundaries during heating—was identified early but not adequately controlled in nuclear reactor applications until lower-carbon practices became standard.

cluster_id: Farm equipment engine fire (loose bolt)
display_name: Farm equipment engine fire (loose bolt)

instances.framework_lesson: Designers must account for vibration-induced fastener loosening during transportation of machines designed for field operation, or risk converting manufacturing defects into design defects in litigation.

cluster_id: 1985 Ford Aerostar rear axle fatigue failures
display_name: 1985 Ford Aerostar rear axle fatigue failures

instances.name: 1985 Ford Aerostar rear axle fatigue failures
instances.summary: Ford's 1985 Aerostar van used high-strength steel for the rear axle and designed it with fatigue strength proportional to tensile strength; welded brackets on the axle caused premature fatigue failures within the first six months, leading to a product recall.
instances.key_quote: the 1985 Ford came up with the aerostar van and they were using high school oily steel for the first time right here old carbon steel sheet and they designed the rear axle to have a petite strength that was better than was proportional to the tensile strength because that's what they're designers knew but that's true that would be true if they had welded it but the rear axle had some brackets on its wellman and they found out people through axles with this petite off
instances.framework_lesson: Designers cannot apply tensile strength–based fatigue rules to welded structures; welds reduce fatigue strength to that of lower-strength steel regardless of base metal strength, and welded joints introduce geometric and microstructural stress concentrations.

cluster_id: High-strength steel rear axle failure
display_name: High-strength steel rear axle failure

instances.key_quote: they found out people threw axles with this petite off okay the first six months this was not in the day for four okay banana product recalls up
instances.framework_lesson: Designers relied on tensile strength specifications appropriate for welded designs without accounting for fatigue and stress concentration in welded bracket attachments.

cluster_id: High-strength truck frame welding violations
display_name: High-strength truck frame welding violations

instances.key_quote: you're not allowed to weld on that it's a National Highway Transportation Safety Authority you're not allowed to well now you get some guys that old boy you know comes along he wants to add some little bracket to the bottom of this truck he puts a little well down there and you know 100,000 miles later which is not a lot on a truck okay and this frame falls apart
instances.framework_lesson: High-strength steels cannot be welded without loss of ductility; NHTSA prohibits welding on heavy-truck ladder frames, requiring bolted connections only.

cluster_id: Pennsylvania water tank truck explosion

cluster_id: Polyacetylene conductive polymers
display_name: Polyacetylene conductive polymers

instances.framework_lesson: New material claims can be misleading when the stated property (specific resistivity) differs from the actual engineering requirement (absolute resistivity); material selection requires correct identification of the true figure of merit.

cluster_id: Century-old carbon steel gas pipe fractures

instances.key_quote: years ago all the plumbing was joined together by lead tin solder all right but over time what happened was if there's environmental restraints on the lead things like that I don't know you've probably seen all over the news all the people up in arms over the lead in the school drinking water
instances.framework_lesson: Legacy material choices in building systems create long-term environmental and health risks that may not manifest or be regulated until decades after initial deployment.

cluster_id: Vape pen battery spontaneous ignition
display_name: Vape pen battery spontaneous ignition

cluster_id: Skyscraper glass curtain wall panel breakage
display_name: Skyscraper glass curtain wall panel breakage

instances.name: Skyscraper glass curtain wall panel breakage
instances.summary: Designers optimized insulated glass unit thickness and gap spacing to minimize weight and cost, but thermal expansion in narrow air gaps caused thermal stress and spontaneous glass panel breakage on large buildings.
instances.key_quote: they've made that gap layer smaller and smaller but what's happening you've seen in some buildings that when the Sun hits it you know in the morning you get your thermal expansion and it gets hot in those layers and you get thermal spent thermal stress breakage
instances.framework_lesson: Optimization for single properties (weight, cost, environmental credits) without considering coupled thermal and stress behavior in composite glazing systems led to catastrophic failures.

cluster_id: Crane rail forging burst failure
display_name: Crane rail forging burst failure

instances.name: Crane rail forging burst failure
instances.summary: A crane rail used for dredging operations on a barge fractured in service due to a forging burst defect that formed during manufacturing when refractory material fell into the molten steel, creating an unmelted cavity.
instances.key_quote: what this is called a forging burst and this happened during the manufacture of the rail so a lot of times you have hot steel molten steel you need a refractory when you're manufacturing it right so chances are maybe some of the refractory fell off
instances.framework_lesson: Manufacturing defects in structural rails should have been caught by ultrasonic inspection before service entry; absence of UT protocols allowed a life-critical defect into operation.

cluster_id: Train rail fatigue cracking
display_name: Train rail fatigue cracking

instances.name: Train rail fatigue cracking
instances.summary: Steel railroad rails in transit systems develop fatigue cracks over decades of service due to repeated cyclic loading from train trucks, requiring regular ultrasonic inspection via Sperry car systems to detect propagating cracks.
instances.key_quote: when I take the tea and I look you know I think of it from a maintenance perspective and I've worked on train derailments where you actually see the fractured rail afterwards
instances.framework_lesson: Long-service-life infrastructure requires continuous nondestructive inspection protocols to detect fatigue-crack growth that develops over decades of cyclic loading.

display_name: Container ship wire rope fracture

instances.name: Container ship wire rope fracture
instances.summary: A wire rope used for lifting cargo onto container ships fractured, requiring full-scale testing to determine whether the failure was due to material deficiency.

cluster_id: Miami waterfront building material selection dispute
display_name: Miami waterfront building material selection dispute

instances.name: Miami waterfront building material selection dispute
instances.summary: An engineering firm specified 316 martensitic stainless steel for a tension connection in a 6-story structure near Biscayne Bay in Miami, creating risk of stress-corrosion cracking in a marine salt-spray environment; the consultant warned them in writing and they eventually switched to duplex stainless steel after $1 million in testing.
instances.key_quote: they were using a swedish steel company and they had seven million dollars worth of metal here and the first response was we're 50 yards from biscayne bay and we're up six stories in the air so it's not a marine environment
instances.framework_lesson: Engineers failed to recognize that hurricane salt spray at elevation constitutes a marine corrosion environment requiring corrosion-resistant material selection and design avoidance of tensile stress in vulnerable alloys.

cluster_id: 308L weld in heat exchanger with caustic attack
display_name: 308L weld in heat exchanger with caustic attack

instances.name: 308L weld in heat exchanger with caustic attack
instances.summary: A heat exchanger with 308L weld metal and 316L tube sheet cracked when exposed to caustic sodium hydroxide solution, demonstrating stress corrosion cracking vulnerability in austenitic stainless steel welds.
instances.key_quote: 316l tube sheet in some heat exchanger and they had some caustic uh sodium hydroxide solution that just these things just crack
instances.framework_lesson: Standard 308 weld metal without low-carbon specification can precipitate chromium carbides at grain boundaries during welding, creating sensitized regions vulnerable to stress corrosion cracking in caustic environments.

cluster_id: Tom Eagar's hot dog cooker stress corrosion cracking
display_name: Tom Eagar's hot dog cooker stress corrosion cracking

instances.name: Tom Eagar's hot dog cooker stress corrosion cracking
instances.summary: Eagar's personal hot dog cooker experienced stress corrosion cracking in the base material, likely from chromium depletion at grain boundaries in stainless steel.
instances.key_quote: that's basically what happened to my stuff that just you know my hot dog cooker it was basically stress corrosion cracking although that was in the base material

cluster_id: Tom Eagar's home high-efficiency boiler chimney liner specification
display_name: Tom Eagar's home high-efficiency boiler chimney liner specification

instances.name: Tom Eagar's home high-efficiency boiler chimney liner specification
instances.summary: A heating contractor quoted Eagar an expensive superferritic stainless steel chimney liner (costing $2,000–3,000 in the 1970s) to resist cracking from moist chlorides in boiler exhaust, a cost he declined to pay.
instances.key_quote: they told me only if we line it with this special stainless steel and i think i did ask or i looked it up and found out what type of stainless steel which it was one of the superfoodics
instances.framework_lesson: Chloride-induced stress corrosion cracking in austenitic stainless steel requires specification of superferritic grades; standard austenitic grades fail in hours under moist chloride attack.
materials_or_systems: moist chlorides in gas/oil burner exhaust

cluster_id: Oil refinery pipe lot rejection
display_name: Oil refinery pipe lot rejection

instances.name: Oil refinery pipe lot rejection
instances.summary: An oil refinery discovered a defective pipe among 600 newly received seamless pipes and condemned all 599 remaining pipes, demanding full refund rather than accepting the statistical nature of defect detection.
instances.key_quote: out of these 600 pipes at this oil refinery they were building they found one pipe and they condemned all the rest all the 599 brothers and sisters and they said we want our money back okay which was not the appropriate way to handle that problem

cluster_id: Allegheny River interstate highway bridge crack
display_name: Allegheny River interstate highway bridge crack

instances.name: Allegheny River interstate highway bridge crack
instances.summary: A tugboat captain observed a growing crack in an interstate highway bridge over the Allegheny River in Pittsburgh that expanded from a small size to nine feet long, forcing closure of lanes until repair.
instances.key_quote: a tugboat captain who's going up and down the Allegheny River in Pittsburgh noticed that when he went down the river the crack in that interstate highway bridge was shorter than when he came back up it was getting longer
instances.framework_lesson: Redundant structure design allowed the bridge to remain standing despite a nine-foot crack, demonstrating the value of redundancy even when individual elements failed.

cluster_id: San Francisco earthquake and fires
display_name: San Francisco earthquake and fires

instances.name: San Francisco earthquake and fires
instances.summary: A major earthquake in San Francisco in 1906 destroyed the city and killed many people through subsequent fires, prompting the adoption of the first building codes for earthquake protection in the United States.
instances.key_quote: we had one in san francisco in 1912 or whatever and it wiped out San Francisco okay they had fires afterwards and that's when we started our building codes for earthquake protection
instances.framework_lesson: Catastrophic earthquake and fire damage led to the first earthquake-aware building codes; this established the standard for future seismic design.

cluster_id: 1906 San Francisco earthquake and fire
display_name: 1906 San Francisco earthquake and fire

instances.name: 1906 San Francisco earthquake and fire
instances.summary: Major earthquake in San Francisco around 1912 (speaker is approximate) caused widespread fires and destruction, prompting the development of building codes for earthquake protection.
instances.key_quote: they had fires afterwards and that's when we started our building codes for earthquake protection
instances.framework_lesson: The San Francisco earthquake demonstrated that building codes for earthquake resistance did not exist; the disaster prompted creation of the first such codes.

cluster_id: C-5A probabilistic fracture mechanics program
display_name: C-5A probabilistic fracture mechanics program

instances.name: C-5A probabilistic fracture mechanics program
instances.summary: Lockheed Martin (formerly Lockheed Corporation) in Georgia employed classified Air Force research in the early 1950s to develop probabilistic fracture mechanics methods for determining aircraft fleet reliability.
instances.key_quote: his father was brought from Norway about 1950 because he was one of the world's experts in the new field of probabilistic fracture mechanics
instances.framework_lesson: The Air Force recognized that deterministic fracture mechanics formulas were insufficient for fleet-wide aircraft safety; probabilistic approaches were needed to estimate the reliability of large numbers of vehicles.

cluster_id: Sailboat trailer weld failure on Route 24
display_name: Sailboat trailer weld failure on Route 24

instances.name: Sailboat trailer weld failure on Route 24
instances.summary: A farmer-built trailer carrying a 40-foot sailboat broke apart on a Massachusetts highway near Boston when a slugged weld failed, causing the sailboat to fall onto the roadway.
instances.key_quote: they're cruising along about how about 55 or 60 miles an hour with their trailer behind them and they hear a bang okay and of course they kind of slow down and put their brakes on and there's a sailboat look just like there's just passing them on the highway okay because the world broke
instances.framework_lesson: Lack of inspection and quality control on farm-built trailers using informal welding practices creates hidden defects that standards for commercial trailers now prohibit.

cluster_id: Tractor-trailer fatigue cracking on frame rails
display_name: Tractor-trailer fatigue cracking on frame rails

instances.name: Tractor-trailer fatigue cracking on frame rails
instances.summary: Heavy tractor-trailers experienced fatigue crack initiation and sudden catastrophic failure of welded frame rails carrying high loads, leading to highway accidents.
instances.key_quote: fatigue cracks start and they don't get noticed and all of a sudden the trailer is going down the highway and it breaks it into it's not a good day
instances.framework_lesson: Codes now prohibit welding on tractor-trailer frames in fatigue-critical zones because welded joints exhibit poor fatigue strength despite high static strength; bolted connections are mandated instead.

cluster_id: Jet engine removal bolt design failure
display_name: Jet engine removal bolt design failure

instances.name: Jet engine removal bolt design failure
instances.summary: An aircraft maintenance technician dropped a jet engine worth approximately five million dollars on the tarmac when an improperly designed bolt for the engine-removal dolly was over-torqued, causing the load path through the bolt to exceed its fatigue limit and fracture.
instances.key_quote: the proper torque for this is just enough to start a crack in this okay so you have to worry about your load path if the load path was only from here to here and there's no stress on this when you're doing the torque of the large-diameter what's the problem however if your load path ignores this little shoulder and you don't grab it and keep it restrained now your load path goes all the way through to the other nut and all of a sudden you over torque this thing
instances.framework_lesson: The design failed because engineers did not properly specify the load path and torque procedure; technicians could inadvertently misuse the tool by torquing the wrong diameter, bypassing the hardened bolt's intended load distribution.

cluster_id: Oil rig repair design-as-built mismatch
display_name: Oil rig repair design-as-built mismatch

instances.name: Oil rig repair design-as-built mismatch
instances.summary: During repairs on an oil rig platform, engineers used original drawings to design a correction, but discovered the structure had not been built according to those original drawings, causing million-dollar replacement work to fail to fit.
instances.key_quote: they get down in the bottom of the ocean on an oil rig or something and they find out oh they didn't build it that way just because we have the drawings that's not the way it was really built and everything they just spent a million dollars putting together doesn't fit
instances.framework_lesson: As-built drawings are essential documentation; relying on as-planned specifications rather than as-built reality can invalidate repair designs in the field.

cluster_id: General Motors sliding door van lemon-law case
display_name: General Motors sliding door van lemon-law case

instances.name: General Motors sliding door van lemon-law case
instances.summary: A van with a defective sliding side door that repeatedly failed and hung from sheet metal was eventually replaced under Massachusetts lemon-law after three repair attempts.

display_name: Steel mini mills rise (1970s–1990s)

cluster_id: Corning Willow Glass development
display_name: Corning Willow Glass development

instances.name: Corning Willow Glass development
instances.summary: Corning developed ultra-thin willow glass (100–200 microns) with scratch resistance and strength retention by using alkali-free borosilicate composition and laminated layer structure with compressive surface stresses to resist moisture degradation.
instances.key_quote: two years ago Corning came out with something called willow glass very thin glass sheet it's a hundred to two hundred microns thick you can buy it in two sizes
instances.framework_lesson: Alkali-free composition eliminates corrosion from humidity; laminated structure with surface compression extends glass strength retention over time by preventing critical flaw propagation.

cluster_id: German WWII ball bearing factory—critical mass of management
display_name: German WWII ball bearing factory—critical mass of management

instances.summary: A German underground ball-bearing factory during WWII experienced management dysfunction when Hitler sent multiple burned-out generals to oversee operations, requiring the colonel in charge to build successive buffer offices and eventually a conference room to isolate management from operational interference.

cluster_id: Weld failure in overloaded metal part
display_name: Weld failure in overloaded metal part

instances.name: Weld failure in overloaded metal part
instances.summary: A consulting case where a weld failed on a part, but investigation revealed the metal had deformed significantly before fracture, indicating the weld actually performed correctly and the part was simply overloaded beyond design.
instances.year_or_era: occurs at least once or twice per year (recurring)
instances.key_quote: if the metal bent and deform before the weld broke or not and many times I see the whole thing has been stretched well it's a metal stretched before the weld broke the well did its job
instances.framework_lesson: Failure investigation must trace the sequence of deformation and fracture; a weld fracture after metal deformation is a symptom of overload, not a weld defect.
instances.source_module: How to be a Successful Engineer

display_name: Space Shuttle Main Tank lithium-aluminum development

display_name: Boeing 787 aluminum-lithium components

cluster_id: Navtech marine hydraulic piston failure (America's Cup yacht)
display_name: Navtech marine hydraulic piston failure (America's Cup yacht)

instances.name: Navtech marine hydraulic piston failure (America's Cup yacht)
instances.summary: A 7075-T6 aluminum hydraulic piston cylinder operating at 7,500 psi in saltwater failed due to stress-corrosion cracking after ~12 years of service on a high-end racing yacht, creating thumbnail cracks ~1 mm deep that propagated through the wall.
instances.key_quote: the problem was it was stress corrosion cracking which shone and I always tend to do in salt water environments American stuff spin-off drive software requires
instances.framework_lesson: T6 heat treatment maximizes strength but is highly susceptible to stress-corrosion cracking in saltwater; engineers must trade peak strength for corrosion resistance using T73 overaging.

cluster_id: America's Cup design philosophy and lightweight failure margins
display_name: America's Cup design philosophy and lightweight failure margins

instances.name: America's Cup design philosophy and lightweight failure margins
instances.summary: High-performance America's Cup yacht design teams intentionally design components to fail frequently within 100–200 service hours to ensure minimal weight; failure is accepted as evidence the design is not overbuilt.
instances.key_quote: if something doesn't fail after a hundred hours or 200 hours you may be too heavy okay you're going for lightweight I mean this is a hundred-million-dollar rich man's no men's room venture
instances.framework_lesson: Extreme weight-optimization in racing vessels requires deliberate acceptance of frequent component failure and explicit management of fail-safe vs. fail-unsafe modes; design criteria must enforce leak-before-break rather than sudden fracture.

cluster_id: Pressure vessel failure case (consulting engagement)
display_name: Pressure vessel failure case (consulting engagement)

instances.name: Pressure vessel failure case (consulting engagement)
instances.summary: A pressure vessel failed in service due to brittle fracture from an embedded crack; consulting analysis determined critical flaw size and proposed material and design changes to achieve leak-before-break safety.
instances.key_quote: we proved the critical flaw size was one millimeter we looked at the sample and I can see it was 10 meter deep before get a brittle fracture
instances.framework_lesson: Initial design prioritized lightweight and maximum strength (higher yield strength material) but sacrificed fracture toughness; re-engineering required trading yield strength for toughness and wall thickness to achieve fail-safe leak-before-break behavior.
materials_or_systems: material substitution (59% to 37 ASI chemistry change)

cluster_id: Electron beam melter fatal electrocution
display_name: Electron beam melter fatal electrocution

instances.name: Electron beam melter fatal electrocution
instances.summary: A technician was electrocuted and killed on Christmas Eve 1962 when he reached to pull a sparking wire on an electron beam melter during rain, contacting 6000 volts through wet feet.

instances.framework_lesson: Equipment connectors and cable management were inadequate; poor design allowed accessible high-voltage hazards and moisture ingress in a rain-exposed outdoor setting.

cluster_id: Cannon connector arc tracking fault in electron beam melter
display_name: Cannon connector arc tracking fault in electron beam melter

cluster_id: 1948 seaplane exfoliation failure
display_name: 1948 seaplane exfoliation failure

cluster_id: Homemade aluminum water-jet fishing boat exfoliation failure
display_name: Homemade aluminum water-jet fishing boat exfoliation failure

instances.name: Homemade aluminum water-jet fishing boat exfoliation failure
instances.summary: A fisherman in Maine built a custom aluminum water-jet boat with high-velocity seawater flow that suffered rapid exfoliation corrosion within six months.
instances.key_quote: this guy up in vain decided to build his own fishing boat with a water jet pump and he had made sergeant make it out of aluminum and used the hope of all the way they had enough velocity ok coming to that water going past there as just seawater did he hit up with exfoliation pro-white comes to us within six months
instances.framework_lesson: High-velocity seawater flow past aluminum structures creates differential oxygen potentials that trigger exfoliation; material selection must account for flow conditions.

cluster_id: Dedham, Massachusetts 30-year-old brass plumbing valve failure
display_name: Dedham, Massachusetts 30-year-old brass plumbing valve failure

instances.name: Dedham, Massachusetts 30-year-old brass plumbing valve failure
instances.summary: A 30-year-old brass plumbing valve in Dedham, Massachusetts suffered dezincification, lost structural integrity, and caused a flood with approximately half a million dollars in damage.
instances.year_or_era: 1970s or 1980s (age 30 years at time of failure)
instances.key_quote: 30 year old plumbing valve down here in dedham massachusetts and it had been easy goodbye over 30 years and just lost must strengthen browbone caused the flood in the helm box a half a million dollars damage
instances.framework_lesson: Dezincification in brass alloys is a slow but inevitable failure mode in certain water conditions; long-term reliability requires alloy selection resistant to selective leaching.

cluster_id: Bell Helicopter rotor blade corrosion fatigue failure (Brazil)
display_name: Bell Helicopter rotor blade corrosion fatigue failure (Brazil)

instances.name: Bell Helicopter rotor blade corrosion fatigue failure (Brazil)
instances.summary: A Bell Helicopter rotor blade repaired by unauthorized Brazilian service center failed in flight within two weeks, killing several people; the failure was attributed to corrosion fatigue after unauthorized paint removal.
instances.key_quote: they took off the throes of renowned paint Dean in the blade they just kind of blended it's smooth figure got rid of any stress concentrations and then they didn't move very good job painting it again okay and it turns out they went to fly it and within a couple of weeks the plane flew off killed a few people
instances.framework_lesson: Removal of protective paint exposes critical components to corrosion fatigue; fracture mechanics analysis showed that corrosion grew pre-existing flaws faster than mechanical fatigue alone, and service bulletins must mandate protection of painted surfaces.

cluster_id: Mass General subway station rust removal
display_name: Mass General subway station rust removal

instances.name: Mass General subway station rust removal
instances.summary: A 1910-built subway station under renovation had half-inch-thick rust deposits on steel beams that were being stripped to white metal; Eagar advised against bare-metal removal because the corrosion product had become protective and bare steel would resume rapid corrosion.
instances.key_quote: they go in there and chip out all the rust down the white metal they call me up said Tom now what do we do then not down the white Melisande you better plug it up with something
instances.framework_lesson: Maintenance specifications that mandate removal of all corrosion to bare metal fail to account for the protective oxide layer that develops over time in stagnant environments; a better approach is to leave mature rust intact and seal it.

cluster_id: High-strength bridge bolts hydrogen embrittlement in Northeast
display_name: High-strength bridge bolts hydrogen embrittlement in Northeast

instances.name: High-strength bridge bolts hydrogen embrittlement in Northeast
instances.summary: High-strength (140 ksi) bolts installed in bridge applications in New Jersey or Connecticut without cathodic protection or coating failed within one or two years due to hydrogen embrittlement from corrosion.
instances.key_quote: they use 140 k SI bolts and different grades of moles and they put them in there and they don't do anything to give them cathodic protection or anything just put them in there and they might have sinks but the same stuff don't always cover as well like in within a year or two these things are breaking integrated or failure hydrogen aroma
instances.framework_lesson: Specifications that upgrade to higher-strength fasteners without mandating corrosion protection or hydrogen embrittlement testing can introduce failure modes that lower-strength fasteners would not exhibit.

cluster_id: Design alert for corrosion-fatigue cracking
display_name: Design alert for corrosion-fatigue cracking

instances.name: Design alert for corrosion-fatigue cracking
instances.summary: An engineering alert was issued for a component with apparent corrosion pitting; Eagar's fracture mechanics analysis showed the small original flaw could not have caused the observed crack growth without stress-assisted corrosion fatigue, not simple impact.
instances.key_quote: I said no you could never grow tea crack and corrosion seat there unless it was stress assistant I was frozen system it was stressful protein-protein
instances.framework_lesson: Inspection specifications that mandate detection of finer and finer flaws without addressing the root cause (corrosion-fatigue interaction from inadequate protective coating) cannot prevent failure.

cluster_id: Mexican airline hot corrosion engine failure
display_name: Mexican airline hot corrosion engine failure

instances.name: Mexican airline hot corrosion engine failure
instances.summary: A Mexican airline engine suffered severe hot corrosion damage because operators skipped the normal engine warm-up procedure and took off at full power, operating in the narrow temperature range where sulfur-oxide hot corrosion is most aggressive.
instances.key_quote: they were basically starting their engines in 1 full power take off without warming up their engines broccoli instead of takeoff power getting them in this range
instances.framework_lesson: Operating procedures and startup protocols exist to avoid narrow temperature windows where corrosion kinetics peak; bypassing warm-up for fuel economy places engines in maximum vulnerability range.

cluster_id: Ford plastic bumper painting failure
display_name: Ford plastic bumper painting failure

instances.name: Ford plastic bumper painting failure
instances.summary: Ford attempted to introduce a new low-surface-energy plastic bumper that could not be painted because paint would peel off; they had to develop a sandblasting process to roughen the surface before painting could succeed.
instances.key_quote: whenever they tried to paint it the paint would just peel off they eventually had to introduce a sand blasting operation to paint this to roughen the surface
instances.framework_lesson: Low surface energy materials require surface modification to enable adhesive bonding; this was solved through mechanical interlocking rather than relying on chemical adhesion alone.

cluster_id: Millennium cookware hydrogen cracking and Teflon coating
display_name: Millennium cookware hydrogen cracking and Teflon coating

instances.name: Millennium cookware hydrogen cracking and Teflon coating
instances.summary: Faberware Millennium cookware shipped to Japan developed cracks in the stainless steel interior that were not present when shipped from Brooklyn; investigation revealed hydrogen from the plasma spray process (95% argon, 5% helium, 5% hydrogen) used to create a porous surface for Teflon infiltration caused delayed hydrogen cracking in the martensitic regions.
instances.key_quote: we sent this to Japan and we found cracks when it got to Japan it didn't have cracks when we shipped it from Brooklyn what happened
instances.framework_lesson: Plasma spray gas composition containing hydrogen can cause delayed cracking in martensitic stainless steel, especially in regions of high deformation; surface treatment methods can introduce hydrogen embrittlement risks.

cluster_id: Adhesive-bonded automotive door panels failure (1980s–1990s)
display_name: Adhesive-bonded automotive door panels failure (1980s–1990s)

instances.name: Adhesive-bonded automotive door panels failure (1980s–1990s)
instances.summary: Automotive door panels adhesively bonded together failed after 3–5 years when moisture and road salt attacked the water-soluble adhesive, causing inner and outer panels to rattle and separate.
instances.key_quote: they worked just fine for about three or four or five years and after about five years the adhesives were attacked by the moisture you know Road salts or whatever rainwater getting splashing up in there and these door panels were just completely you know the inner and outer were just sort of crimp together
instances.framework_lesson: Water-soluble adhesives specified for cost and environmental compliance failed to provide adequate moisture resistance in automotive applications exposed to road salt.

cluster_id: Chrysler Dodge minivan adhesive bonding problems (early-to-mid 1980s)
display_name: Chrysler Dodge minivan adhesive bonding problems (early-to-mid 1980s)

instances.name: Chrysler Dodge minivan adhesive bonding problems (early-to-mid 1980s)

cluster_id: Boeing aircraft skin and wing adhesive bonding
display_name: Boeing aircraft skin and wing adhesive bonding

instances.name: Boeing aircraft skin and wing adhesive bonding
instances.summary: Boeing developed adhesive bonding technology for aircraft skins and wings but continued using rivets as backup for safety over 30-year service life due to long-term adhesive degradation.
instances.key_quote: they would usually use adhesive bonding but then they were putting rivets in and he says well why are you doing that and they said oh just to be sure because in 30 years these bonds are going to get weaker
instances.framework_lesson: Adhesive joint strength degrades over time through corrosion at the interface and material migration, requiring mechanical fasteners as a fail-safe despite advanced bonding technology.

cluster_id: Canadian Aviation Authority Teflon carburetor coating test
display_name: Canadian Aviation Authority Teflon carburetor coating test

instances.name: Canadian Aviation Authority Teflon carburetor coating test
instances.summary: The Canadian Aviation Authority tested Teflon coatings on carburetors to prevent ice buildup, but found the coating only prevented water droplet adhesion, not frost deposition.
instances.key_quote: the Canadian Aviation Authority and I have since gotten a cop of this report and looked at it but they ran tests to see if putting Teflon on the surface of the carburetor would prevent ice from building up
instances.framework_lesson: Surface energy solutions must distinguish between droplet and frost formation mechanisms; a single coating cannot solve both icing modes.

cluster_id: Boston Logan Airport deicing fluid transition

instances.key_quote: the EPA get very upset if you just let it get into the ocean uh but now I've noticed they've been deising just back from the gate which means they've switched to polypropylene glycol
instances.framework_lesson: Regulatory pressure and toxicology discovery (polyethylene glycol metabolizes to methanol causing blindness) forced airports to adopt more expensive but safer deicing formulations.

cluster_id: 1854 Broad Street cholera epidemic
display_name: 1854 Broad Street cholera epidemic

instances.name: 1854 Broad Street cholera epidemic

instances.key_quote: he plotted on a map of London and it turns out right here in the centre there's a pump and the most deaths, each one of these represents a little coffin
instances.framework_lesson: Data analysis and visualization must account for alternative explanations and outliers rather than selectively confirming an initial hypothesis.

cluster_id: Hall-Héroult cell anode effect and perfluorocarbon emissions
display_name: Hall-Héroult cell anode effect and perfluorocarbon emissions

instances.name: Hall-Héroult cell anode effect and perfluorocarbon emissions
instances.summary: In traditional aluminum smelting, CO2 bubbles form along the graphite anode creating high current density zones where fluoride ions react with the anode to produce perfluorocarbons (CF4 and C2F6) with extremely high global warming potential.
instances.key_quote: if you get CO2 bubbles forming along this you get very high current density at the anode and you form per fluorocarbon so cf4 has about 6,500 times the global warming potential of CO2

cluster_id: Zirconia tube durability breakthrough in molten salt cells
display_name: Zirconia tube durability breakthrough in molten salt cells

instances.name: Zirconia tube durability breakthrough in molten salt cells
instances.summary: Swiss aluminum patented a zirconia oxide ion conductor concept in the early 1970s for non-graphite aluminum smelting, but the technology languished for decades until researchers discovered that magnesium fluoride-calcium fluoride eutectic molten salt combinations minimally corrode zirconia tubes, enabling practical 5000+ hour operating lifetimes.
instances.key_quote: if your zirconia tube doesn't last very long then your tube costs basically outweigh the advantages so UD PA's real breakthrough was a combination of you know chemical Metallurgy thinking and some stubborn persistence that finally found molten salt combinations that don't destroy the zirconia

cluster_id: Stainless steel weld sensitization problem (1940s)
display_name: Stainless steel weld sensitization problem (1940s)

instances.name: Stainless steel weld sensitization problem (1940s)
instances.summary: Welding of stainless steels in the 1940s caused weld sensitization—precipitation of chromium carbides at grain boundaries depleted chromium in adjacent regions, causing intergranular corrosion when exposed to acids.
instances.key_quote: it created a real problem for trying to weld stainless steels in the 1940s it people developed these um other steels that had low carbon but they had double and triple the price until aod came along
instances.framework_lesson: The weld sensitization phenomenon was not understood until the 1940s; engineers initially addressed it by developing expensive low-carbon stainless steel variants until the AOD process made low-carbon steel economically practical.

cluster_id: Aluminum can recycling contamination problem
display_name: Aluminum can recycling contamination problem

instances.name: Aluminum can recycling contamination problem
instances.summary: Recycling aluminum beverage cans creates material contamination issues because different alloy series (3000, 5000, 6000) are mixed together, complicating reprocessing and creating unwanted alloying elements in the final product.
instances.key_quote: if you mix them all together and melt them all at once you got a problem because you don't want manganese in your 5000 series and you're recycling this is 40 of your recycling stream maybe more
instances.framework_lesson: Recycling streams lack sufficient sorting mechanisms to maintain alloy purity, creating downstream manufacturing constraints.

cluster_id: Little League baseball bat cardiac arrest case
display_name: Little League baseball bat cardiac arrest case

instances.key_quote: a kid was playing little league down in new jersey and it was a -12 bat that little league approved and the pitcher got hit in the chest and his heart stopped
instances.framework_lesson: Little League approved high-performance aluminum bats without restricting bat-ball stiffness matching, allowing dangerous impact velocities that caused severe injury.

cluster_id: GE lightning research on stainless steel containers for nuclear waste isolation

instances.framework_lesson: Expensive experimental validation was conducted at universities when simple heat-flow calculations could have predicted the outcome; engineering relies on testing even when theory provides clear answers.
