cluster_id: Massachusetts flexible gas piping regulatory approval threat
display_name: Massachusetts flexible gas piping regulatory approval threat

instances.name: Massachusetts flexible gas piping regulatory approval threat
instances.summary: A manufacturer seeking approval to install flexible gas piping in Massachusetts threatened to sue the State Plumbing Board for restraint of trade; the board used Eagar's technical letter recommending full encasement in iron pipe as leverage to force the company to accept restrictive installation requirements.
instances.key_quote: the attorneys for the people who made this that were trying to get approval had come to the State Plumbing Board and threatened to sue them for Restraint of trade if they didn't approve their product
instances.framework_lesson: Regulatory bodies can enforce safety via implicit threat of public disclosure of expert criticism, but this mechanism is ad-hoc and depends on individual relationships rather than systematic code review.

cluster_id: NFPA flexible gas piping product approval renewal deadline
display_name: NFPA flexible gas piping product approval renewal deadline

instances.name: NFPA flexible gas piping product approval renewal deadline
instances.summary: The NFPA approved flexible gas piping for 10 years with conditional renewal in 2014; by 2007–2008 sufficient safety questions had emerged that the committee issued a letter requiring manufacturers to prove product safety or lose re-approval.
instances.key_quote: they basically went back to the companies and said well we gave you a 10-year approval which expires in 2014. you have to prove to us by 2014 that your product is safe
instances.framework_lesson: Time-limited approvals with explicit re-certification requirements can force re-evaluation of products whose hazards emerge post-approval, but only if the standards body is willing to enforce the deadline.

cluster_id: Residential grounding conduit bonding failure
display_name: Residential grounding conduit bonding failure

instances.name: Residential grounding conduit bonding failure
instances.summary: New residential grounding products entered the market without proper codes or consideration of lightning strike physics, leading to a regulatory dispute over whether multiple-point bonding is required for safety.
instances.key_quote: a new product came to Market why it came to Market how didn't have codes no one thought about a particular failure mode they found out about the failure mode
instances.framework_lesson: Codes lagged behind product innovation; manufacturers lacked physics understanding (confusing resistance with impedance) and engineers were not consulted during product development, creating a regulatory vacuum.

cluster_id: Oil embargo supply chain disruption
display_name: Oil embargo supply chain disruption

instances.name: Oil embargo supply chain disruption
instances.summary: The 1972 Arab oil embargo cut off petroleum supplies to utility plants that had converted entirely to oil-fired boilers, preventing electricity generation until plants were retrofitted within five years to switch to coal or natural gas.
instances.key_quote: at the time of the oil embargo in 1972 all the utility plants were firing oil in their boilers they'd gotten rid of coal and stuff and when they said you can't have any oil they couldn't make electricity
instances.framework_lesson: Over-reliance on a single fuel source with geopolitical supply risk creates operational fragility; redundant fuel capability and design flexibility are essential infrastructure resilience measures.

cluster_id: General Electric/GE aerospace rhenium supply constraint
display_name: General Electric/GE aerospace rhenium supply constraint

instances.name: General Electric/GE aerospace rhenium supply constraint
instances.summary: GE faced severe disruptions to aircraft engine production when China restricted rhenium supply, a critical trace element required for turbine alloys; production timelines nearly doubled as a result.
instances.key_quote: gp got slammed really hard because they the trace elements that you use for the turbine they changed to rhemium and china had all the rhenium and cut supply and it caused their engine production they almost doubled right
instances.framework_lesson: Critical mineral dependencies embedded in high-performance materials can create single-point-of-failure vulnerabilities in manufacturing; strategic material sourcing and material substitution R&D are essential risk mitigation.

cluster_id: Belmont elementary school oil tank leak
display_name: Belmont elementary school oil tank leak

instances.key_quote: about 15 years ago they had an oil leak from one of the you know oil tanks at one elementary schools and some high school student looks at clay put pond and he sees an oil slick on the surface cost the town two million dollars for the environmental cleanup
instances.framework_lesson: Modern environmental regulations impose strict liability for contamination that would have been legally acceptable decades earlier, shifting costs retroactively to property owners.

cluster_id: American Electric Power Texas transmission line investment

cluster_id: Deep TIG (Deep Gas Tungsten Arc) development for thick-section horizontal welding
display_name: Deep TIG (Deep Gas Tungsten Arc) development for thick-section horizontal welding

cluster_id: Steel water tank corrosion failure
display_name: Steel water tank corrosion failure

instances.name: Steel water tank corrosion failure
instances.summary: Steel hot water tanks exported to maquiladoras in Juárez, Mexico developed corrosion holes through gaps in glass lining, leading to tank failures and floods despite magnesium anode protection.
instances.key_quote: within two or three years you can blow a hole right through that tank okay and that creates floods and i probably put one or two children through college that way
instances.framework_lesson: Magnesium anode protection fails if impurities like nickel precipitate become cathodic, requiring specifications with less than seven parts per million nickel to prevent preferential corrosion of the anode itself rather than the steel.
materials_or_systems: thermal expansion mismatch in glass coating

cluster_id: Magnesium automotive paint flaking failure
display_name: Magnesium automotive paint flaking failure

instances.name: Magnesium automotive paint flaking failure
instances.summary: Magnesium components in automobiles experience atmospheric corrosion under paint that causes paint flaking within four to five years, unlike aluminum which maintains corrosion resistance.
instances.key_quote: even just a little bit of atmospheric corrosion underneath that and all of a sudden the paint starts flaking off after four or five years okay not with aluminum
instances.framework_lesson: Magnesium's poor atmospheric corrosion resistance makes it unsuitable for exterior automotive body panels where paint protection is relied upon; aluminum is superior for this application.

cluster_id: Steel automotive rust and paint failure resolution via galvanization
display_name: Steel automotive rust and paint failure resolution via galvanization

instances.name: Steel automotive rust and paint failure resolution via galvanization
instances.summary: Steel rusted rapidly in automobiles when manufacturers extended vehicle design life targets from 3–4 years to 10 years in the 1970s–1980s, forcing adoption of galvanized steel as a solution.
instances.key_quote: 30 years ago is one of the biggest problems they had when they decided they didn't want to sell disposable cars anymore then the energy crisis of 73 hit and all of a sudden they wanted cars that lasted five years
instances.framework_lesson: Extending vehicle service life requirements exposed inadequacy of bare steel; the automotive industry had to adopt galvanization and develop new welding processes to meet corrosion durability targets.

cluster_id: Magnesium metallographic polishing corrosion problem
display_name: Magnesium metallographic polishing corrosion problem

instances.name: Magnesium metallographic polishing corrosion problem
instances.summary: Magnesium-carbon composites developed by the U.S. Navy corroded faster during water-based metallographic polishing than material could be removed, requiring oil-based polishing solutions instead.
instances.year_or_era: Not specified, described as historical application
instances.key_quote: when you tried to polish that metal metallographically if you're using a water based polishing solution it would corrode faster than you could polish okay so you had to use diamond oil-based polishing solutions
instances.framework_lesson: Magnesium's high reactivity with moisture means even laboratory sample preparation procedures must be adapted; water-based solutions are incompatible with magnesium metallography.

cluster_id: Post-WWII aluminum and magnesium industry cartel
display_name: Post-WWII aluminum and magnesium industry cartel

instances.name: Post-WWII aluminum and magnesium industry cartel
instances.summary: Research by doctoral student George Kenney proved that ALCOA and Dow Chemical agreed after WWII to divide markets—with Dow controlling magnesium and ALCOA controlling aluminum—despite both companies having technical capability to compete in both sectors.
instances.key_quote: george kenney who was joel clark's first doctoral student about 1976 basically looked at and he could prove by sales and prices that basically alcoa probably al can and dow chemical had all agreed after world war ii
instances.framework_lesson: Market-division cartels can persist in the U.S. during periods of weak antitrust enforcement; technical capability does not determine market participation when companies collusively agree to abstain.

display_name: Aluminum chloride kitchen reaction (personal anecdote)

cluster_id: 1986 church building galvanized steel handrail paint failure
display_name: 1986 church building galvanized steel handrail paint failure

instances.name: 1986 church building galvanized steel handrail paint failure
instances.summary: A newly constructed church building's galvanized steel handrails and light posts experienced massive paint flaking within one year because paint was applied immediately after hot-dip galvanizing, before zinc hydroxide protective layer formed.
instances.key_quote: if you hot dip galvanize your steel you have to paint it within a few hours before the zinc hydroxide is really formed because if you get zinc hydroxide under there the paint will not adhere to it
instances.framework_lesson: ASTM specifications govern the timing and surface preparation sequence for galvanized steel painting; painting zinc hydroxide-coated surfaces violates adhesion requirements and causes premature failure.

cluster_id: Church building galvanized handrails and light posts paint failure
display_name: Church building galvanized handrails and light posts paint failure

instances.name: Church building galvanized handrails and light posts paint failure
instances.summary: A brand new church building constructed around 1986 experienced widespread paint flaking on galvanized steel handrails and light posts within one year because paint was applied immediately after hot-dip galvanizing before zinc hydroxide formation was complete.
instances.key_quote: all the paint was flaking off and that's because someone galvanized the steel and then painted it right away
instances.framework_lesson: ASTM specifications require waiting several hours after hot-dip galvanizing before painting to allow zinc hydroxide formation, otherwise the paint will not adhere; ignoring this timing requirement causes premature failure.

cluster_id: Mach 5 hypersonic aircraft material limits

cluster_id: Louisiana blowout preventer failures

instances.framework_lesson: Critical safety components sold at hundreds of thousands of dollars were designed by junior engineers without oversight from experienced engineers, and lacked proper scientific validation before deployment.

cluster_id: Army brake shoes fraud and specification conflict

instances.framework_lesson: A conflict between the engineering drawing and the procurement contract allowed the supplier to argue they met contract requirements even though the parts failed specifications, demonstrating how specification ambiguity can shield misconduct.

cluster_id: Medical aneurysm clip MRI safety controversy
display_name: Medical aneurysm clip MRI safety controversy

instances.name: Medical aneurysm clip MRI safety controversy
instances.summary: Neurosurgeons debated whether stainless steel aneurysm clips used to stop brain hemorrhages would become ferromagnetic when placed in MRI machines, risking patient death from clip movement.
instances.key_quote: if you actually had a blood clot in the brain they come in they take a little a little steel uh or stainless steel or titanium clip and they basically stop the bleeding by just putting this little clip on the blood vessel so it's not hemorrhaging in the brain anymore the problem is you then want to go get an mri and if that has any ferromagnetic moment to it and you put it in a five tesla mri machine it just torques right off and you now got another hemorrhage in the brain
instances.framework_lesson: Medical professionals with incomplete materials knowledge misunderstood that austenitic stainless steel could transform to magnetic martensite under cold-working, creating false alarm about all iron-containing alloys.

cluster_id: Brain aneurysm clip ferromagnetism controversy
display_name: Brain aneurysm clip ferromagnetism controversy

instances.framework_lesson: Medical professionals with incomplete materials knowledge created overly restrictive specifications based on ferromagnetism without understanding Curie temperature and phase-dependent magnetic behavior.

cluster_id: Aluminum casting aircraft engine repair consulting case

instances.framework_lesson: Filler metal selection for aircraft castings requires careful trade-offs among strength, corrosion resistance, color matching, and regulatory approval; a 40-year process can come under scrutiny after an accident.

cluster_id: Qatar LNG tank explosion and Shell litigation

instances.name: Qatar LNG tank explosion and Shell litigation

instances.key_quote: They blew up the entire harbor in Qatar in the Persian Gulf when a tank of LNG uh exploded about the same time back about 30 years ago. Shell designed the steel tank for holding the LNG and they did it to kind of like 1940s specs only they built this in like the 1970s or 80s and it was a brittle fracture.
instances.framework_lesson: Steel tanks were designed to outdated 1940s API specifications decades later, with poor welds and low-toughness steel, enabling brittle fracture that standards had not evolved to prevent.

cluster_id: Singapore forged shaft with shrinkage cavity
display_name: Singapore forged shaft with shrinkage cavity

instances.name: Singapore forged shaft with shrinkage cavity

instances.key_quote: they didn't cut off enough of the top of the ingot because they really didn't have the ingot size capacity in this mill to make this product, but they wanted to get the business
instances.framework_lesson: Quality control failures occur when supervisors suppress defect reports under business pressure, and when customers abandon incoming inspection assuming supplier quality control is adequate.

cluster_id: Clink defects in heavy-section forged steel (1920s-1980s)
display_name: Clink defects in heavy-section forged steel (1920s-1980s)

instances.name: Clink defects in heavy-section forged steel (1920s-1980s)
instances.summary: Internal brittle fractures called 'clinks' form in large forged ingots when they are heated rapidly in furnaces while still below the ductile-brittle transition temperature, creating thermal stress that cracks around pre-existing voids.
instances.key_quote: they call it a clink because you can hear it's like a rifle shot clink when the crack the brittle crack forms. Okay. And so in the old Ford shops in the 1920s and 30s they called them clinks
instances.framework_lesson: Knowledge of clink prevention through staged heating (first heating to 800-1100°F, holding for hours, then full heating) was learned in the 1930s-40s but was forgotten or not transmitted to modern metallurgists by the 1980s.

cluster_id: Submarine shaft clink failure (1985)
display_name: Submarine shaft clink failure (1985)

instances.name: Submarine shaft clink failure (1985)
instances.summary: A large submarine shaft developed a brittle fracture (clink) due to improper heat treatment procedure, with a 2-foot diameter crack on a 33-inch diameter shaft, which then propagated as a fatigue crack.
instances.key_quote: they tried to heat the thing too fast, set up bigger stresses. They had a flaw here and whether they did a two-step heat treatment or not, they ended up getting a crack, a clink.
instances.framework_lesson: Specifications for multi-stage heat treatment were developed in the 1930s-40s to prevent thermal stress cinking, but by 1985 younger metallurgists had forgotten this ancient technology.

cluster_id: Wind turbine shaft clink failure (Dallas)
display_name: Wind turbine shaft clink failure (Dallas)

instances.key_quote: it was a brittle fracture followed by a fatigue fracture on the outside. That's a clink. This was probably 10 years ago that I saw my second clink.

cluster_id: MIT Plasma Fusion Center welding project

cluster_id: Pratt & Whitney niobium alloy development failure

cluster_id: Transformer steel evolution and amorphous metal competition
display_name: Transformer steel evolution and amorphous metal competition

instances.name: Transformer steel evolution and amorphous metal competition
instances.summary: In the 1940s-1950s, oriented silicon iron steals replaced carbon steel in motors, then amorphous metal foil (met glass) was invented with 10 times lower magnetic losses but 5 times higher cost; established steel companies improved their silicon iron designs to remain competitive rather than ceding the market.
instances.key_quote: they improved their silicon iron that they had not really improved on for about 40 years and so now the two of them are in competition again continual competition continual improvement
instances.framework_lesson: Market competition and threat of displacement drive material innovation more reliably than initial material superiority; entrenched suppliers will reinvest rather than abandon profitable business.

cluster_id: Big steel companies decline through market abandonment
display_name: Big steel companies decline through market abandonment

instances.name: Big steel companies decline through market abandonment
instances.summary: Large integrated steel companies prioritized protecting their profitable high-margin business segments and ceded lower-margin commodity steel segments to smaller competitors, who eventually grew to dominate and undermine the large companies' overall market position.
instances.key_quote: big steel companies they basically wanted to protect their more profitable parts of their business and they let the less profitable parts go and it turns out eventually those people doing the less profitable part ate their lunch
instances.framework_lesson: Strategic focus on defending high-margin segments at the expense of lower-margin business can inadvertently enable disruption from below; entrenched companies often fail to defend the full market.

cluster_id: Alcoa-Boeing aircraft wing spar business loss
display_name: Alcoa-Boeing aircraft wing spar business loss

instances.framework_lesson: Proprietary tighter specifications and trade secrets, while giving competitive advantage, can be lost if a competitor replicates or improves upon the metallurgical process.

cluster_id: Hurricane Sandy cast iron component failure

cluster_id: New England Aquarium sea water piping corrosion
display_name: New England Aquarium sea water piping corrosion

cluster_id: Mormon Temple heat exchanger erosion-corrosion failure
display_name: Mormon Temple heat exchanger erosion-corrosion failure

instances.name: Mormon Temple heat exchanger erosion-corrosion failure

cluster_id: Phoenix warehouse accelerated corrosion during construction

instances.framework_lesson: Construction sequencing and weather protection are critical to corrosion performance; the greenhouse effect from wet insulation under metal decking vastly exceeded standard design assumptions for corrosion rates in Arizona.

cluster_id: Tom Eagar's aluminum soldering demonstration failure
display_name: Tom Eagar's aluminum soldering demonstration failure

instances.name: Tom Eagar's aluminum soldering demonstration failure
instances.summary: An aluminum soldered assembly used for a classroom demonstration on slip planes deteriorated and fell apart within a year due to corrosion from residual tin chloride flux exposed to moisture and air in blind joints.
instances.year_or_era: early 2000s (described as 'a few years ago before you were born' during 2013 lecture)
instances.key_quote: I had some blind joints and I'd left some of the flux on the inside. Air got on the inside, attacked this, the solder flux that was the residue and just corroded the joint within a year.
instances.framework_lesson: Hygroscopic chloride-based fluxes (tin chloride, zinc chloride) used for aluminum soldering are so moisture-sensitive that flux residue left in inaccessible joints will corrode the solder bond within a year if exposed to ambient air.

cluster_id: Car battery terminal corrosion (road salt environment)
display_name: Car battery terminal corrosion (road salt environment)

instances.name: Car battery terminal corrosion (road salt environment)
instances.summary: Lead battery terminals in New England vehicles corrode and form white lead carbonate deposits when road salt chlorides interact with the lead oxide surface and atmospheric CO2 and moisture, regenerating hydrochloric acid in a continuous corrosion cycle.
instances.key_quote: chloride will react with the CO2 and moisture in the air to form a lead here. And it regenerates the hydrochloric acid. So this thing just runs around in a circle.
instances.framework_lesson: Chloride contamination in automotive environments initiates a self-perpetuating electrochemical cycle that regenerates the corrosive acid, necessitating protective measures like rubber boots and preventive jellies from auto parts suppliers.

cluster_id: Tin-bismuth solder steel recycling contamination
display_name: Tin-bismuth solder steel recycling contamination

instances.name: Tin-bismuth solder steel recycling contamination
instances.summary: Industry pursuit of tin-bismuth lead-free solders failed to recognize that bismuth cannot be removed from recycled steel like lead can, and would contaminate the billion-ton annual steel recycling stream from automobiles.
instances.key_quote: Because it would destroy the recyclability of a billion tons of steel each year. You cannot get bismuth out of steel. Bismuth goes to the grain boundaries of the steel.
instances.framework_lesson: Material substitution decisions made without systems-level analysis of downstream recycling and metallurgical consequences can render entire waste streams unrecyclable.

cluster_id: Halogen flux residue corrosion in printed circuit boards
display_name: Halogen flux residue corrosion in printed circuit boards

instances.name: Halogen flux residue corrosion in printed circuit boards
instances.summary: Failure to completely clean halogen-activated flux residue from solder joints on printed circuit boards caused chloride-induced corrosion that degraded thin solder joints within one year.
instances.key_quote: If you don't clean it off completely, very small amounts of chlorine just lead to corrosion in the air, and if you're talking about very small solder joints it doesn't take more than a year's worth of corrosion
instances.framework_lesson: Process control and cleanliness requirements for flux residues were underestimated in miniaturized electronics; thin solder joints lack margin against even small corrosion depths.

cluster_id: Lead-acid battery terminal corrosion from road salt
display_name: Lead-acid battery terminal corrosion from road salt

instances.name: Lead-acid battery terminal corrosion from road salt
instances.summary: Road salt chloride splash onto automobile lead-acid battery terminals initiates a corrosion cycle that regenerates hydrochloric acid and deposits white lead carbonate on positive terminals.
instances.key_quote: If you have any chlorides left over such as New England road salts that splash up onto your lead battery in your engine compartment, you will actually form this white lead carbonate growing at the anode.

cluster_id: Copper pipe pitting corrosion self-sealing
display_name: Copper pipe pitting corrosion self-sealing

instances.name: Copper pipe pitting corrosion self-sealing
instances.summary: Copper pipes in hot water systems developed holes through their full thickness due to pitting corrosion, which self-sealed when salts and organics from the water flowed through and plugged the holes.
instances.key_quote: we have uh projects where we see copper pipes that have holes that go all the way through the thickness uh by pitting corrosion and they seal themselves
instances.framework_lesson: Inspection standards for piping must account for the fact that through-thickness corrosion defects can self-seal via product deposition, preventing visual detection of leaks and violating the assumption that leaks will be discovered during service.

cluster_id: Honda ATV design optimization history
display_name: Honda ATV design optimization history

instances.name: Honda ATV design optimization history
instances.summary: Honda's early ATVs were intentionally overdesigned with excessive strength and longevity (e.g., exhaust systems lasting far longer than expected) because the company lacked historical failure data; they later optimized based on accumulated service experience.
instances.key_quote: when Honda started to do these little ATVs, they were clearly overdesigned. They were much stronger and much more longlasting than they probably wanted them to be as well, but they wanted to penetrate the market and they didn't have the history
instances.framework_lesson: New product categories cannot be efficiently designed using statistical methods without historical failure data; conservative overdesign is often the only safe approach when entering a market with no precedent.

cluster_id: Helicopter blade intellectual property dispute
display_name: Helicopter blade intellectual property dispute

instances.name: Helicopter blade intellectual property dispute
instances.summary: A consulting case involving reverse-engineered helicopter rotor blade designs where Eagar's team had to verify whether a new aluminum alloy design was equivalent to the original design across multiple failure modes.
instances.key_quote: Somebody was trying to copy somebody else and a question was their design to us was their design good enough or as good because there's a lot in reverse engineering in aerospace
instances.framework_lesson: Qualification of redesigned components in aerospace requires testing for stress-corrosion cracking, fatigue, and crack propagation; material property variability must be characterized with statistical confidence intervals rather than single data points.

cluster_id: Amtrak derailment near Philadelphia
display_name: Amtrak derailment near Philadelphia

instances.name: Amtrak derailment near Philadelphia
instances.summary: An Amtrak train derailed near Philadelphia while traveling too fast into a curve; initial investigations ruled out cell phone use and drug use as causes.
instances.key_quote: why did the train outside of Philadelphia a couple weeks ago derail? Too fast. It's going too fast. Uh, and it was a curve going too fast into a curve.

cluster_id: Teenage rock-dropping from overpasses
display_name: Teenage rock-dropping from overpasses

instances.name: Teenage rock-dropping from overpasses
instances.summary: Teenagers drop rocks from highway overpasses onto vehicles below, causing injuries and deaths; infrastructure responses include protective fencing.
instances.key_quote: Teenagers left to go to overpasses and drop rocks on top of speed and cars and I mean they kill people and all kinds of things.

cluster_id: Pipeline ruptures and oil spills
display_name: Pipeline ruptures and oil spills

instances.name: Pipeline ruptures and oil spills
instances.summary: Buried oil and gas pipelines from the 1940s-50s have experienced ruptures, contaminating water supplies and requiring proof of material properties for 45,000 miles of infrastructure.
instances.key_quote: they've had some ruptures and people don't like 50,000 gallons of crude oil in their backyard or in their lake where they drink the water from
instances.framework_lesson: Pipeline safety administration required material property verification decades after installation because original specifications and inspection methods were insufficient to prevent failures.

cluster_id: Naval vessel inspection resistance
display_name: Naval vessel inspection resistance

instances.name: Naval vessel inspection resistance
instances.summary: Electric Boat engineers initially refused to adopt a new metal inspection technique because they wanted to avoid additional testing requirements that would delay production.
instances.key_quote: they wanted to throw me out of the ship. The last thing they wanted was another test they had to meet.
instances.framework_lesson: Engineers designing new inspection methods must address the real cost of additional testing; organizational resistance to new tests can be as significant an obstacle as the technical challenge.

cluster_id: Nazi ball bearing factory management failure
display_name: Nazi ball bearing factory management failure

instances.name: Nazi ball bearing factory management failure
instances.summary: A WWII German ball bearing plant under Luftwaffe control experienced cascading management dysfunction when multiple general staffs were stationed on-site, resulting in excessive bureaucracy that required separate meeting facilities to contain interference with production.
instances.key_quote: this was the critical mass of management. These two general staffs could keep themselves busy and not interfere with production.

cluster_id: Oakland Bay Bridge steel tie rod hydrogen cracking
display_name: Oakland Bay Bridge steel tie rod hydrogen cracking

instances.name: Oakland Bay Bridge steel tie rod hydrogen cracking
instances.summary: Large steel tie rods on the Oakland Bay Bridge are corroding and cracking due to hydrogen embrittlement from corrosion; the cracks develop slowly over days and pose risk to a multi-billion-dollar structure.
instances.key_quote: It's hydrogen cracking because it doesn't show up right away. It takes a few days to show up. Okay. And it's due to corrosion.
instances.framework_lesson: Hydrogen cracking is not limited to welding; corrosion in service (seawater, atmospheric exposure) can introduce hydrogen into steel and cause delayed cracking in unstressed or pre-stressed components.

cluster_id: LNG tanker aluminum tank welds—Coast Guard Charpy toughness specification
display_name: LNG tanker aluminum tank welds—Coast Guard Charpy toughness specification

instances.name: LNG tanker aluminum tank welds—Coast Guard Charpy toughness specification
instances.summary: Liquefied natural gas tankers with large aluminum tanks required exhaustive Charpy toughness testing at multiple positions (1 mm, 3 mm, 5 mm, and base metal) across welds to meet a 20 ft-lbf minimum, driven by Coast Guard safety mandate.
instances.key_quote: the Coast Guard decided, well, we're going to be carrying liqufied natural gases on some of these ships, and we want to be extra safe. So, we'll require it at the 1 millm position, the 3 mm position, and the 5mm position, and the base metal.
instances.framework_lesson: Regulatory escalation (Navy 15 ft-lbf → Coast Guard 20 ft-lbf, multiple positions) was driven by hazard severity (cryogenic cargo); the specification created large manufacturing burdens but ensured brittle fracture prevention in critical infrastructure.
materials_or_systems: Charpy impact toughness (heat-affected zone, weld metal, fusion line)

cluster_id: Quincy LNG tanker aluminum tank skirts
display_name: Quincy LNG tanker aluminum tank skirts

instances.name: Quincy LNG tanker aluminum tank skirts
instances.summary: Coast Guard required enhanced Charpy impact testing (20 ft-lb at multiple positions through weld depth) for liquefied natural gas tanker construction at Quincy shipyard, revealing baseline steel variability and inspection practices.
instances.key_quote: they're building Quincy. That's what I had to do. Okay. Uh I had to measure each one of those locations along the weld.
instances.framework_lesson: Stricter regulatory toughness requirements exposed fundamental variability in base metal supply that standard single-position Charpy tests had masked, forcing either material requalification or testing manipulation.

cluster_id: Diving ship pipe failure
display_name: Diving ship pipe failure

instances.name: Diving ship pipe failure
instances.summary: A subsea diving support ship's pipe failed during pressure testing after a mandrel fatigue crack embedded steel debris that initially passed two tests but leaked on the third, costing $5 million to replace.
instances.key_quote: one of those mandrels that goes pushing through this piece of hot steel finally after multiple uses had developed a fatigue crack and spalled off the piece, they got rolled into the surface

cluster_id: Steel mill pipe composition lawsuit
display_name: Steel mill pipe composition lawsuit

instances.name: Steel mill pipe composition lawsuit
instances.summary: A pipe with decarburization and poor bond failed, and through statistical fingerprinting of residual trace elements (nickel, copper), Eagar proved with one-in-100-million confidence that it was the defendant steel company's product despite their claim otherwise.
instances.key_quote: I can prove I proved that was that same piece of steel produced by that steel company with a probability of one part in 100 million
instances.framework_lesson: Manufacturers cannot defend against defects by denying product identity when chemical composition analysis can statistically fingerprint steel to a specific producer.

cluster_id: Highway bridge weathering steel misidentification
display_name: Highway bridge weathering steel misidentification

instances.name: Highway bridge weathering steel misidentification
instances.summary: A highway bridge on the interstate built with weathering steel developed terrible toughness; Eagar's analysis revealed the steel was from US Steel (containing chromium) not Bethlehem Steel, resolving a blame dispute.
instances.key_quote: I analyzed it and I wrote back and said yes you're right it is junk it's got chromium in it go talk to US steel

cluster_id: Quenched-and-tempered bridge plate brittleness
display_name: Quenched-and-tempered bridge plate brittleness

instances.name: Quenched-and-tempered bridge plate brittleness
instances.summary: A batch of HY-100-class quenched-and-tempered bridge steel showed extremely poor Charpy toughness (5 foot-pounds) because the plate slipped through the mill without being tempered, resulting in glass-brittle material at 160 ksi strength.
instances.key_quote: through the mill without tempering it had 160 ksi strength and it was glass brittle because they hadn't bothered to temper it
instances.framework_lesson: Process control and destructive sampling verification failed; untreated material was shipped with falsified certification paperwork claiming heat-treatment compliance.

cluster_id: Submarine and rocket fastener heat-treatment recall
display_name: Submarine and rocket fastener heat-treatment recall

instances.name: Submarine and rocket fastener heat-treatment recall
instances.summary: Untreated fasteners certified as heat-treated were discovered in submarines and rocket systems; the manufacturer produced paperwork with company logo certifying compliance despite never performing the required tempering treatment.
instances.key_quote: they machined it manufactured it produced it and produced paperwork with their company logo on it saying it's qualified to these standards and there's never heat treated
instances.framework_lesson: Certification and paperwork compliance can mask actual process failures; destructive testing protocols must be enforced to prevent brittle fasteners from reaching critical aerospace and submarine applications.

cluster_id: US Navy particle beam weapons welding research
display_name: US Navy particle beam weapons welding research

instances.name: US Navy particle beam weapons welding research
instances.summary: The U.S. Navy invested approximately $250 million developing particle beam weapons in the mid-1980s through early 1990s for anti-missile defense, then sought to repurpose the technology for submarine welding after the Cold War ended.
instances.key_quote: the US Navy was trying to develop these particle beam weapons from the mid 80s through uh the early 90s and then peace broke out and they weren't quite as concerned about it, but they had spent a quarter billion dollars
instances.framework_lesson: New heat-source technologies are adopted without sufficient understanding of their fundamental physics or actual engineering requirements for the application.

cluster_id: U.S. Navy titanium submarine welding research (Alvin prototype program)
display_name: U.S. Navy titanium submarine welding research (Alvin prototype program)

instances.name: U.S. Navy titanium submarine welding research (Alvin prototype program)
instances.summary: The U.S. Navy conducted research in the late 1970s on welding thick titanium (1-inch) for submarine hulls, developing prototype welding processes at Oregon Graduate Center for potential deep-diving submarines, though operational titanium submarines were never built.
instances.key_quote: My first research project um in 1977 was from the US Navy to weld titanium for submarines. Here's one of the welds that we made back in 1977 or 78. This probably auto belongs in the Smithsonian. Now, we were welding 1-in thick titanium for submarines.

cluster_id: Unspecified major welding failures (corporate scale)
display_name: Unspecified major welding failures (corporate scale)

instances.name: Unspecified major welding failures (corporate scale)
instances.summary: Multiple instances where companies experienced major welding-related failures costing tens or hundreds of millions of dollars, prompting hasty internal assignments of engineers to 'fix welding problems' despite those engineers having no welding background.
instances.key_quote: their boss came to them. They just had a major failure. Cost the company tens or hundreds of millions of dollars and someone's decided we're going to study welding and we're going to fix these problems
instances.framework_lesson: Major engineering failures are often not solved by reassigning untrained personnel; the root causes are frequently already understood, but the newly assigned engineers lack the domain knowledge to recognize or address them.

cluster_id: Gas pipe corrosion and home explosions
display_name: Gas pipe corrosion and home explosions

instances.name: Gas pipe corrosion and home explosions
instances.summary: Steel gas pipes buried under streets corrode after 30–40 years, causing gas leaks into soil that have led to explosions in homes, prompting industry switch to plastic pipe.
instances.key_quote: They used to make gas pipe runs down the street out of metal, out of steel. But after 30, 40 years, it corrods and now you have gas leaking into the soil and you can blow up homes and things like that. Now they use plastic.
instances.framework_lesson: Long-service buried infrastructure requires materials resistant to sustained environmental corrosion; steel pipe codes did not adequately account for soil corrosion over multi-decade lifecycles.

cluster_id: Carbon nanotubes structural applications (overpromised research)
display_name: Carbon nanotubes structural applications (overpromised research)

instances.summary: Billions of dollars invested in carbon nanotube research over 25 years based on theoretical strength predictions of 3 million PSI, but actual measured strength data is absent and defects significantly reduce real-world performance.

instances.framework_lesson: Material scientists and physicists predicted defect-free performance without accounting for statistical mechanics guaranteeing defects at any temperature above absolute zero, leading to overhyped commercial claims.

instances.key_quote: they actually put in half a mile or something of as a trial and that was a number of years ago but I don't know they've ever done anything beyond that trial but all the technology had to go with that was horrendous

cluster_id: DC transmission line in Manhattan
display_name: DC transmission line in Manhattan

instances.name: DC transmission line in Manhattan

instances.key_quote: they've tried to put a transmission line in the center of New York City for a DC transmission line and they actually put in half a mile or something of as a trial and the technology had to go with that was horrendous

cluster_id: High-temperature superconductor critical current density problem
display_name: High-temperature superconductor critical current density problem

instances.name: High-temperature superconductor critical current density problem
instances.summary: Despite 25 years of development, high-temperature superconductors improved temperature and magnetic field tolerance but failed to improve critical current density, limiting practical applications.
instances.key_quote: they had improved one property temperature and they actually had along with that got in a high magnetic field but they hadn't improved critical current density and they still haven't today 25 years later
instances.framework_lesson: Overselling of new materials without fundamental understanding of underlying physics constraints (J cross B forces and Maxwell's equations) misled peer review and publication.

cluster_id: Space Shuttle reusable spaceplane hydrogen tank failure
display_name: Space Shuttle reusable spaceplane hydrogen tank failure

instances.name: Space Shuttle reusable spaceplane hydrogen tank failure
instances.summary: A composite hydrogen tank for an experimental reusable spaceplane burst during cryogenic testing at NASA Huntsville due to hydrogen ingress into material pores, causing a 1.3 billion dollar program cancellation.
instances.key_quote: hydrogen got inside the pores as it warmed up and built up pressure and it blew up and they canceled the 1.3 billion dollar program
instances.framework_lesson: Operating with a safety factor of 1.05 due to budget pressure and delamination defects left no margin for failure modes not captured in design analysis, such as cryogenic gas ingress into composite matrix.

cluster_id: Lockheed Skunk Works X-33 spaceplane composite tank (rapid prototyping redesign)
display_name: Lockheed Skunk Works X-33 spaceplane composite tank (rapid prototyping redesign)

instances.name: Lockheed Skunk Works X-33 spaceplane composite tank (rapid prototyping redesign)
instances.summary: During a 33-month rapid prototyping design phase, engineers switched from 3D woven composites to off-the-shelf sandwich construction, but used 3M adhesive beyond its 10-day shelf life window, resulting in only 10% adhesive strength instead of intended 80-90%, causing delamination and requiring design safety factor reduction to 1.05.
instances.key_quote: they didn't say was the strength was only 10% of the original adhesive strength after 10 days okay they were figuring it would have 80 90 percent of its strength not ten percent
instances.framework_lesson: Cost and schedule pressure caused engineers to misread adhesive datasheets and accept degraded material properties; manufacturing process windows were not adequately controlled or verified before full-scale tank fabrication.

cluster_id: 7500-ton forging press failure
display_name: 7500-ton forging press failure

instances.name: 7500-ton forging press failure
instances.summary: A large forging that came off a 7500-ton forging press fractured due to inadequate preheat, post-heat treatment, stress relief, and peening of welds.

instances.framework_lesson: Large forgings require comprehensive stress-relief and mechanical stress-relief procedures; neglect of these steps led to catastrophic failure due to residual stresses.

cluster_id: American Airlines composite rudder separation
display_name: American Airlines composite rudder separation

cluster_id: 50,000-ton forging press development (Air Force/Alcoa/Wyman Gordon)
display_name: 50,000-ton forging press development (Air Force/Alcoa/Wyman Gordon)

instances.name: 50,000-ton forging press development (Air Force/Alcoa/Wyman Gordon)
instances.summary: The U.S. Air Force partnered with Alcoa and Wyman Gordon in the 1950s to build 50,000-ton forging presses—among the largest in the free world—to produce aluminum forgings for fighter and bomber aircraft wings, avoiding welded joints that were considered unreliable.
instances.key_quote: no one trusts a weld in an aircraft the Air Force doesn't trust welding and they have these huge forging so well men need forgings the size of this room
instances.framework_lesson: Aircraft design philosophy rejected welded joints in critical structures due to weld unreliability; massive forging capacity was built as an alternative.

cluster_id: Wyman Gordon 50,000-ton forging press beam fatigue crack
display_name: Wyman Gordon 50,000-ton forging press beam fatigue crack

instances.name: Wyman Gordon 50,000-ton forging press beam fatigue crack
instances.summary: A massive welded I-beam in the basement of the Wyman Gordon forging press developed a 10-foot fatigue crack after 30 years of service, originating from weld residual stresses at the toe of a small attachment pad.
instances.key_quote: after about 20 min the mid-eighties so after 30 years of use they developed a fatigue crack that started at the toe of one of these and went straight through it 10 foot crack
instances.framework_lesson: 2-inch filler welds on small pads created yield-level residual stresses that, combined with stress concentration, caused fatigue failure decades later; the original 1950 welding procedure had been lost, requiring months to redevelop.
materials_or_systems: welded steel I-beam (10 feet high, 10-inch thick web)

cluster_id: Aircraft crankshaft stress concentration failure
display_name: Aircraft crankshaft stress concentration failure

instances.name: Aircraft crankshaft stress concentration failure

cluster_id: Pratt & Whitney / General Electric jet engine disc HIP vessel failure
display_name: Pratt & Whitney / General Electric jet engine disc HIP vessel failure

instances.name: Pratt & Whitney / General Electric jet engine disc HIP vessel failure

instances.framework_lesson: Design specifications assumed the vessel would achieve 'leak before break' based on nominal toughness, but the actual heat-treated microstructure had insufficient toughness to prevent brittle fracture.

cluster_id: Nuclear submarine seawater piping corrosion

cluster_id: Frozen copper water pipe failure
display_name: Frozen copper water pipe failure

instances.name: Frozen copper water pipe failure
instances.summary: A copper water pipe inside a house developed a hoop stress crack from freezing when left full of water during winter, despite having a safety factor of 8-10 against normal operating pressure.
instances.key_quote: this whole tube is empty in the winter time when it's closed but if you hook up something and you leave the valve open then you have water all the way through and you can have the over pressure
instances.framework_lesson: Safety factors designed for static pressure loads do not account for volumetric growth from phase change; plastic pipe testing standards omit freezing cycles, creating a gap between certification and real-world failure modes.

cluster_id: Tall condominium riser pipe failure

cluster_id: Large condominium complex piping failure

instances.summary: Vertical copper piping risers in a large condominium complex failed due to thermal expansion and overly tight clamping, causing uncontrolled displacement and bending loads at connections.
instances.key_quote: in a large condominium complex it happens but it's more in the vertical direction so people have risers they go all the way from the basement to the attic and if you clamp the pipe too hard top and bottom you can end up with uncontrolled displacement of the riser
instances.framework_lesson: Building codes specify thermal expansion must be accounted for, but in practice these displacement-induced bending loads are not included in allowable load calculations, creating a gap between code intent and real failure modes.

cluster_id: Spring steel pressure vessel design study
display_name: Spring steel pressure vessel design study

instances.name: Spring steel pressure vessel design study
instances.summary: An attempt to build a high-pressure vessel from spring steel to maximize pressure capacity per unit weight revealed that welded joints and end closures cannot achieve the same strength as the cylinder body.
instances.key_quote: so you see we take a cylinder like this let's make it complete but so we have this cylinder and we want to put pressure into it and say we want to get as much pressure as we can for the weight of that vessel that's a great idea you can go to very high yield strength steel and have a thin wall but then the question is what are you going to do with these ends
instances.framework_lesson: High-strength materials cannot be fully exploited in pressure vessels because the joining method (welding) introduces strength reductions that codes address with blanket safety factors rather than material-specific allowances.

cluster_id: Speedboat propeller shaft fatigue failure
display_name: Speedboat propeller shaft fatigue failure

instances.name: Speedboat propeller shaft fatigue failure
instances.summary: A sport speedboat's propeller shaft, half-submerged with rotating props, experienced fatigue cracking and eventually failed, causing the owner to lose the propeller and become stranded.
instances.key_quote: somebody that came to me about five years ago he had this fancy boat um it was pretty much not a competition it was a speed boat and the uh going from the back you had the water say this is the water level and the shaft was right about water level and you had these props
instances.framework_lesson: Using yield strain rather than fatigue strain in safety factor calculations can mask actual failure modes; a design certified for 400 hours may not account for bending fatigue in half-submerged rotating shafts.

cluster_id: Jet ski fuel cap hose adapter failure
display_name: Jet ski fuel cap hose adapter failure

instances.name: Jet ski fuel cap hose adapter failure
instances.summary: A molded nylon hose adapter connecting a jet ski fuel tank to the engine block experienced fatigue failure due to assembly misalignment and unquantified displacement loads between the hull and engine platform.
instances.key_quote: if somebody decides oh would like to save some money here it becomes difficult because you don't know what your factor of safety is that's that assembly situation
instances.framework_lesson: Assembly tolerance and displacement loads in multi-component systems cannot be reliably predicted by finite element analysis alone; design must rely on prototype testing and service history rather than first-principles calculation.

cluster_id: Propeller shaft fatigue failure (boat/sport unit)
display_name: Propeller shaft fatigue failure (boat/sport unit)

instances.name: Propeller shaft fatigue failure (boat/sport unit)
instances.summary: A nickel-based alloy propeller shaft designed for 400 hours of service failed after approximately 500 hours due to stress concentration from contact rubbing and fatigue crack propagation.
instances.key_quote: by design they only plan for the shaft to last 400 hours of service it was a you know very it was a nickel nickel-based alloy an in-canal alloy and that's how they decided that's all you get out of it
instances.framework_lesson: Safety factors tied to intended service life and acceptance criteria; a factor of safety below one or equal to one is acceptable when design explicitly limits operational life and maintenance intervals are enforced, shifting risk from initial design to operator discipline.

cluster_id: Cisco Systems PCB packaging and bonding
display_name: Cisco Systems PCB packaging and bonding

instances.name: Cisco Systems PCB packaging and bonding
instances.summary: Cisco Systems manufactured multi-layer composite circuit boards (13-22 layers thick) using metallized and tin-plated resin plastic with tight registration requirements for forming liquid-phase diffusion joints between layers.
instances.key_quote: you put a bunch of uh packages on each one of these slots and this is a composite that's probably about 13 layers thick

cluster_id: IBM System/360 mainframe helium cooling
display_name: IBM System/360 mainframe helium cooling

instances.name: IBM System/360 mainframe helium cooling

cluster_id: Norton asphalt reclamation carbide-tip braze joint
display_name: Norton asphalt reclamation carbide-tip braze joint

instances.name: Norton asphalt reclamation carbide-tip braze joint
instances.summary: A student developed an improved braze alloy for carbide tips on asphalt-reclamation road equipment by using titanium hydride powder composite with copper and tin to better match thermal expansion coefficients, extending tip life from one night to three nights of operation.
instances.key_quote: they lasted three times as long well that's pretty good i mean before they might have to re-tip once a night and lose hours

cluster_id: San Diego vacuum brazing furnace zinc contamination disaster
display_name: San Diego vacuum brazing furnace zinc contamination disaster

instances.name: San Diego vacuum brazing furnace zinc contamination disaster
instances.summary: A technician loaded the wrong braze alloy containing zinc into a $2 million vacuum brazing furnace, contaminating the entire system; subsequent failed attempts to vaporize the zinc by heating only redistributed it to cooler regions, ultimately destroying the furnace.
instances.key_quote: a guy had a two million dollar brazing furnace down in san diego and someone put the wrong braze alloy in there ran the brazing system contaminated the whole system with zinc

cluster_id: Molybdenum-copper cross-field amplifier radar waveguide braze joint
display_name: Molybdenum-copper cross-field amplifier radar waveguide braze joint

instances.name: Molybdenum-copper cross-field amplifier radar waveguide braze joint
instances.summary: Early gold-nickel braze joints for molybdenum-to-copper connections in radar cross-field amplifiers formed brittle nickel-molybdenum intermetallics that fractured after only 5 degrees of bending; switching to transient liquid phase bonding with low nickel content eliminated intermetallics and allowed 180-degree bending.
instances.key_quote: they originally had an 82 gold 18 nickel and they were forming nickel three molly in the inner metallic and you get like five degrees bend and the whole thing would snap
instances.framework_lesson: Intermetallic formation during brazing can embrittle joints; transient liquid phase processing eliminates brittle phases and dramatically improves ductility.

cluster_id: Naval destroyer heat exchanger brazed assembly
display_name: Naval destroyer heat exchanger brazed assembly

instances.name: Naval destroyer heat exchanger brazed assembly
instances.summary: A large stainless steel heat exchanger approximately room-sized was brazed as a single assembly with crossing internal tubes for use as an internal cooled recuperator on naval destroyers, enabling extended operational range by recovering waste heat from oil engines.
instances.key_quote: this was a heat exchanger about the size of this room it's all brazed together all at once this consists of stainless steel and there are little tubes and big holes

cluster_id: Naval ship recuperator heat exchanger failure
display_name: Naval ship recuperator heat exchanger failure

instances.name: Naval ship recuperator heat exchanger failure
instances.summary: A room-sized brazed stainless steel heat exchanger for naval destroyers failed catastrophically within two minutes of operation due to asymmetric gas flow and non-uniform heating causing thermal expansion stress that snapped six-inch-thick hardened steel tie bars.
instances.key_quote: i calculated it lasted for two minutes before it started leaking and it turns out um it's like a 100 million navy program about 15 20 years ago
instances.framework_lesson: A 2D finite element model of heat transfer failed to account for three-dimensional asymmetric flow patterns; wishful thinking about uniform symmetry is not good engineering.

cluster_id: Aerospace company gold-nickel braze powder inventory control failure
display_name: Aerospace company gold-nickel braze powder inventory control failure

instances.name: Aerospace company gold-nickel braze powder inventory control failure
instances.summary: An aerospace company in New England lost significant quantities of expensive gold-nickel braze powder (used in jet engine shrouds) because production supervisors could obtain 50-ounce jars without proper accountability controls, allowing theft or loss to scale with production volume.
instances.key_quote: in the mid 70s they were having they're building a lot of this particular military engine and the guy would go through that was in charge of the brazing area would go through about one jar of this 50-ounce stuff a week but then production scaled up and so he would get two jars a week
instances.framework_lesson: Supply-chain accountability systems must scale with production volume; signature-based controls on high-value materials (gold at 400/ounce) were insufficient to prevent loss.

cluster_id: Gold theft from military engine brazing operation
display_name: Gold theft from military engine brazing operation

instances.name: Gold theft from military engine brazing operation
instances.summary: A brazing technician at a military aerospace contractor stole approximately 1.6–2.5 million dollars' worth of gold over 16 months by taking extra jars of gold-nickel braze alloy powder without proper inventory controls.
instances.key_quote: production ramped up and he was using a couple of weeks then predictions started ramped down he thought ah what the heck i'll still get two jars one for the company and one for me okay no one caught him until about 16 months later
instances.framework_lesson: Bonded government contracts with cost-plus reimbursement and no material-tracking controls created zero accountability; the insurance company bore losses while the contractor passed costs on without incentive to prevent theft.

cluster_id: Aluminum radiator field brazing development
display_name: Aluminum radiator field brazing development

instances.name: Aluminum radiator field brazing development
instances.summary: Before the early 1980s, aluminum radiators could not be field-repaired because no practical brazing flux existed; Alcan's development of a fluoride-based flux enabled mechanics to repair aluminum radiators on site, transforming the automotive industry.
instances.key_quote: around 1980 or so alcan aluminum developed a brazing flux for aluminum that was not so fussy you had to do it in a plant and this was a fluoride based flux i mean don't tell him that he's you know strengthening his teeth by breathing this in

cluster_id: Cadmium oxide electrical switch ban and house fires
display_name: Cadmium oxide electrical switch ban and house fires

instances.name: Cadmium oxide electrical switch ban and house fires
instances.summary: After cadmium was outlawed in electrical switches, houses began burning down at high rates, forcing regulators to reconsider the ban within three years.
instances.key_quote: they decided they would rather have cadmium oxide in their electrical switches than have people's houses burn down because they were burning down houses left and right
instances.framework_lesson: Regulatory bans on toxic materials without adequate substitute qualification can create worse safety hazards than the original material posed.

cluster_id: Mercury thermometer lab spill protocol change
display_name: Mercury thermometer lab spill protocol change

instances.key_quote: when i was a student and you break a mercury thermometer in the lab you just go get some sulfur and sprinkle it on the floor to make mercury sulfide to tie it up you know or you just swept it into the corner and let it vaporize into the room for the next six months

cluster_id: Lead-tin solder in semiconductor packages toxicity acceptance
display_name: Lead-tin solder in semiconductor packages toxicity acceptance

instances.name: Lead-tin solder in semiconductor packages toxicity acceptance

instances.key_quote: because lead tin has such great soldering characteristics they still use it in that microscopic amount in those solder bumps they still use cadmium

cluster_id: NASA X-33 liquid hydrogen tank failure
display_name: NASA X-33 liquid hydrogen tank failure

instances.name: NASA X-33 liquid hydrogen tank failure
instances.summary: A $50 million prototype liquid hydrogen tank failed due to differential thermal expansion between graphite epoxy, Nomex, and adhesive joints, causing leakage as frost sublimated during warm-up after pressure testing.
instances.key_quote: they had a 50 million dollar piece of junk and they ended up eventually cancelling the 1.3 billion dollar program
instances.framework_lesson: Material selection codes did not account for coefficient of thermal expansion mismatch between composite constituents and adhesive joints in cryogenic applications.

cluster_id: Oil field blowout repair weld

cluster_id: Lawrence Livermore plutonium weld observation
display_name: Lawrence Livermore plutonium weld observation

instances.name: Lawrence Livermore plutonium weld observation
instances.summary: A researcher at Lawrence Livermore showed Eagar a cross-section of a welded plutonium sample with no visible heat-affected zone, which he initially questioned multiple times before learning the material's low thermal conductivity explained the phenomenon.
instances.key_quote: I said what type of material is this I don't see the heat affected zone they just kept on talking and finally I pinned him down and they said well it's plutonium
instances.framework_lesson: Heat-affected zone depth is governed by material thermal conductivity, not just heat source intensity; materials with low thermal conductivity behave differently from conventional metals.

cluster_id: General Electric adaptive weld control feedback timing failure
display_name: General Electric adaptive weld control feedback timing failure

instances.name: General Electric adaptive weld control feedback timing failure
instances.summary: GE's adaptive weld control algorithm measured feedback with a two-tenths second delay, making control impossible since the welding process completed in one-tenth second.
instances.key_quote: their control algorithm which had to control within a tenth of a second actually didn't even start measuring until about two tenths of a second so the weld was already over before it was adaptively controlling
instances.framework_lesson: Control system specifications must account for process duration and response time requirements; adaptive feedback designed without validating temporal constraints cannot function.

cluster_id: Navy 25 kilowatt laser welder at Naval Research Laboratory
display_name: Navy 25 kilowatt laser welder at Naval Research Laboratory

instances.name: Navy 25 kilowatt laser welder at Naval Research Laboratory
instances.summary: The U.S. Navy acquired one of the first high-power industrial lasers in the mid-1970s for welding applications, but economic and reliability challenges limited its practical adoption despite technological capability.
instances.key_quote: everybody wanted to use laser high-power lasers which were coming into their own in the early 70s for improved productivity but in fact if you don't have either a very high value-added part like an aerospace part or high volume that you can just keep feeding in there then it turns out not to be economical
instances.framework_lesson: Capital equipment investment decisions must be driven by production volume and part value-add, not by technological novelty alone; unreliable research machines cannot substitute for proven manufacturing methods.

cluster_id: Avco Everett laser welder demonstrations (early 1980s)
display_name: Avco Everett laser welder demonstrations (early 1980s)

display_name: Bimetallic bandsaw and hacksaw blade electron beam welding

cluster_id: Alcoa Davenport quench-and-temper failure
display_name: Alcoa Davenport quench-and-temper failure

instances.framework_lesson: Specifications for quench-and-temper of high-strength aluminum alloys were not verified in-process; manufacturing process control failed to catch localized regions that never received proper thermal treatment.

cluster_id: Bay Bridge California corrosion-induced hydrogen cracking

cluster_id: Destroyer rudder steel preheating logistics case
display_name: Destroyer rudder steel preheating logistics case

instances.name: Destroyer rudder steel preheating logistics case
instances.summary: When preheating thick steel on a destroyer rudder, the heat spreads into the surrounding area, creating an oven-like environment where welders risk burns while working in tight spaces.
instances.key_quote: we were welding on a destroyers rudder stool yep and when you have to preheat thick steel that heat goes somewhere and spreads so you have and if a welder has to get into a tight spot too well
instances.framework_lesson: Preheating specifications must account for practical working conditions and thermal stress on personnel in confined spaces during shipboard construction.

cluster_id: US Navy HSLA Steel Mill Investment
display_name: US Navy HSLA Steel Mill Investment

instances.name: US Navy HSLA Steel Mill Investment
instances.summary: The US Navy considered investing $100 million in a domestic steel mill under a Title III program to produce high-strength low-alloy steels that Japanese manufacturers had mastered, as American steel companies lacked the capability.
instances.key_quote: the US Navy was considering investing a hundred million dollars into a steel mill under up what they call a title three program because the Japanese had the best technology in the world for making hsla steals we didn't even have a steel company that could make it
instances.framework_lesson: American industrial policy recognized a critical materials technology gap relative to foreign competitors that required government intervention to close.

cluster_id: Naval Research Lab Laser Welding of HY-80
display_name: Naval Research Lab Laser Welding of HY-80

instances.name: Naval Research Lab Laser Welding of HY-80
instances.summary: Naval Research Lab discovered in the early 1980s that laser welding of HY-80 high-strength steel produced superior toughness and hydrogen embrittlement resistance because the laser vaporized inclusions, creating an ultra-clean microstructure.
instances.key_quote: the naval research lab found in the nineteen early 1980s they could weld hyee lasers and have better toughness and more resistance to hydrogen in the weld though because the laser actually vaporized away the inclusions in the steel
instances.framework_lesson: Process-driven microstructural cleanliness (via laser vaporization of inclusions) can overcome inherent hydrogen embrittlement susceptibility in ultra-high-strength steels.

cluster_id: Titanium submarine hull design
display_name: Titanium submarine hull design

instances.name: Titanium submarine hull design

cluster_id: Seawolf-class submarine keel beam bending
display_name: Seawolf-class submarine keel beam bending

instances.name: Seawolf-class submarine keel beam bending
instances.summary: A four-inch thick HY-80 steel keel beam bent during sea trials, prompting the Navy to switch to 4340 steel for higher strength despite welding challenges.
instances.key_quote: when they were doing the trots sea trials it was bending that wasn't a good thing okay so he said uh we want to use 4340 steel
instances.framework_lesson: High-strength materials require matching welding procedures and preheat strategies; material selection must account for welding process difficulty and cost.

cluster_id: US Navy Japan steel mill visit and HSLA technology transfer
display_name: US Navy Japan steel mill visit and HSLA technology transfer

instances.name: US Navy Japan steel mill visit and HSLA technology transfer
instances.summary: The US Navy sent Eagar to Japan in the mid-1980s to study high-strength low-alloy (HSLA) plate production using controlled water quenching in steam tunnels, which enabled finer grain structure and superior toughness without high alloy content.
instances.key_quote: the Navy sent me to Japan in the mid 80s because the Japanese had these high-strength low alloy steel facilities where basically the hot plate after coming off the Rolling Mill would go into this tunnel it was just a big spray booth of water and you would quench it or you cool it at a very controlled way
instances.framework_lesson: Government-industry partnerships (Title III programs) can accelerate domestic adoption of foreign manufacturing innovations to maintain naval superiority in materials and weldability.

cluster_id: Lukens Steel HSLA plate line government funding
display_name: Lukens Steel HSLA plate line government funding

instances.name: Lukens Steel HSLA plate line government funding
instances.summary: Lukens Steel received approximately $100 million in Title III government funding in the 1990s to build a high-strength low-alloy plate production line matching Japanese capabilities, enabling the US Navy to produce weldable high-strength steel plate domestically.
instances.key_quote: turns out Luke and Steele got 100 million dollars or whatever of title three money and they put in a high strength alloy plate line that was in the 90s and that's where the hsla Steels that you use come from
instances.framework_lesson: Government funding of domestic steel processing infrastructure can close capability gaps with foreign competitors and improve naval material specifications.

cluster_id: 1920s steel bridge repair consulting (MTA/MGH station)
display_name: 1920s steel bridge repair consulting (MTA/MGH station)

instances.name: 1920s steel bridge repair consulting (MTA/MGH station)
instances.summary: A civil engineering company rebuilding a 1920s structure (MTA or MGH station) needed a welding procedure for steel of unknown ASTM specification; Eagar used chemical composition analysis and the Stout-Dodie steel tables to match the unknown steel to modern standards and determine appropriate preheat and welding parameters.
instances.key_quote: they're rebuilding something and it was built in the 1920s and they say Tom we got a piece of Steel we got a weld to it and when we're doing this repair what's the welding procedure okay well I go to Stout and I try to find one of these 200 Steels that has we don't have ASTM specs back in the 1920s for this particular steel
instances.framework_lesson: When ASTM specifications did not exist for historical steel grades, chemical composition testing and matching to modern steel property tables allows engineers to develop safe welding procedures for retrofit and repair.

cluster_id: Fire sprinkler system plastic pipe failures
display_name: Fire sprinkler system plastic pipe failures

instances.name: Fire sprinkler system plastic pipe failures
instances.summary: Buildings across the United States installed plastic sprinkler pipes to save money, but the biocide coating from old steel pipes degraded the plastic in stagnant water, causing leaks and secondary damage despite the systems functioning as designed.

instances.framework_lesson: Material substitution for cost savings ignored secondary effects from incompatibility with legacy infrastructure and water chemistry in stagnant systems.

cluster_id: Military vs. civilian gas turbine engine divergence
display_name: Military vs. civilian gas turbine engine divergence

instances.name: Military vs. civilian gas turbine engine divergence

cluster_id: Naval fighter flameout recovery over Atlantic
display_name: Naval fighter flameout recovery over Atlantic
