cluster_id: Turbine blade manufacturing precision limits
display_name: Turbine blade manufacturing precision limits

instances.name: Turbine blade manufacturing precision limits
instances.summary: Modern turbine blade casting requires such precision that any deviation of 2–3 thousandths of an inch in the ceramic core position (along twisted geometry) causes the blade to fail at operating temperature (1300°C), necessitating CAT scanning of every blade for quality control.
instances.key_quote: if that core is off by two or three thousandths of an inch from its perfect position and it's perfect position it's not just a senator axis it's the center axis in a twist right you're off by two or three thousandths over a couple of inches on something that's going to heat up to 1,300 degrees centigrade and melt it
instances.framework_lesson: Extreme material performance requirements demand manufacturing tolerance control so tight that 100% inspection (CAT scanning) becomes economically necessary, not optional.

cluster_id: Polyurethane insulation fires in homes
display_name: Polyurethane insulation fires in homes

instances.name: Polyurethane insulation fires in homes
instances.summary: Polyurethane foam insulation caused house fires when sprayed thicker than recommended due to its exothermic reaction and high insulation value trapping heat, igniting the foam.
instances.key_quote: the codes say you're not allowed to put it down in layers more than two inches thick at a time and so if people go in there spraying it in homes they would spray it four inches six inches thick and it would basically ignite
instances.framework_lesson: Building codes were updated to limit polyurethane spray thickness to prevent exothermic self-ignition from occurring in residential applications.

cluster_id: Japanese steel industry dominance 1970s
display_name: Japanese steel industry dominance 1970s

instances.name: Japanese steel industry dominance 1970s
instances.summary: Japanese steelmakers outcompeted American mills in the 1970s by producing better quality steel at lower cost.
instances.key_quote: built them and they were beating our pants off in the 1970s with better quality steel at lower cost

cluster_id: High-carbon forged parts failure from fast cooling
display_name: High-carbon forged parts failure from fast cooling

instances.name: High-carbon forged parts failure from fast cooling
instances.summary: If high-carbon forged parts cool too rapidly after the basic oxygen furnace process, martensite forms and cracks develop, which General Electric would not tolerate.
instances.key_quote: if you have higher carbon stuff and it cools down too fast you'll get martensite in my crack okay so in a very high quality Ford shop

cluster_id: Air Force blisk (bladed disc) welding development
display_name: Air Force blisk (bladed disc) welding development

instances.name: Air Force blisk (bladed disc) welding development
instances.summary: The Air Force spent billions of dollars attempting to develop weldable turbine blade-disc assemblies (blisks) to eliminate heavy attachment flanges and enable significant weight savings in military aircraft engines.
instances.key_quote: the air force has spent billions of dollars trying to make blisk a bladed disc with turbine blades sticking off around it

cluster_id: Aluminum ship fabrication (naval)
display_name: Aluminum ship fabrication (naval)

instances.name: Aluminum ship fabrication (naval)
instances.summary: Large aluminum naval ships experienced unanticipated fabrication fatigue cracking and fatigue-related failures due to lack of design and construction experience with aluminum welded structures compared to steel.
instances.key_quote: the problem with the larger boats with making aluminum is cracking fabrication fatigue cracking they don't have the experience when they first started building steel ships
instances.framework_lesson: Design methodology for new materials (aluminum vs. steel) differs fundamentally; fatigue crack initiation and propagation behavior requires different welding and design practices not initially understood.

cluster_id: Audi all-aluminum automobiles
display_name: Audi all-aluminum automobiles

instances.name: Audi all-aluminum automobiles
instances.summary: High-volume production aluminum automobiles developed by Audi encountered numerous unexpected failures and problems despite successful low-volume hand-built aluminum cars built for J.P. Morgan in the 1930s.
instances.key_quote: when they went to high volume production they started running into lots of problems that they never realized they would have
instances.framework_lesson: Low-volume hand-crafted designs do not scale to high-volume production; new manufacturing and design learning curves are required when transitioning materials to mass production.

cluster_id: Japanese steel soda cans
display_name: Japanese steel soda cans

instances.name: Japanese steel soda cans
instances.summary: Japan used domestic steel soda cans instead of aluminum in the 1980s-1990s due to government policy protecting the steel industry, but steel corrodes more easily than aluminum.
instances.key_quote: they actually have steel soda cans back 25 years 30 years ago when i lived in japan because the japanese government there's an externality didn't want to support the aluminum industry
instances.framework_lesson: Government industrial policy can override material selection for superior engineering performance, leading to inferior corrosion resistance.

cluster_id: Gas piping in Boston streets
display_name: Gas piping in Boston streets

instances.name: Gas piping in Boston streets
instances.summary: Early gas distribution systems in Boston streets were constructed from wood, a material choice that later proved problematic.
instances.key_quote: anybody know with first gas piping in the streets of boston what material they used wood yup i'll tell you that story tomorrow

cluster_id: Amtrak Accela trolley wire quality investigation
display_name: Amtrak Accela trolley wire quality investigation

instances.name: Amtrak Accela trolley wire quality investigation
instances.summary: MIT conducted tensile testing and quality assessment of overhead catenary wire used on Amtrak Accela trains operating at 125-150 mph between Boston and New Haven, Connecticut, due to concerns about wire quality and the reliability of the electrical contact system.
instances.key_quote: There was a big problem or big question about the quality of the wire and we had to make tensil bars and in fact um here are some well actually let me put the cross-section of the wire up there.

cluster_id: Percy Bridgeman high-pressure materials research
display_name: Percy Bridgeman high-pressure materials research

instances.name: Percy Bridgeman high-pressure materials research
instances.summary: Percy Bridgeman invented the tetrahedral anvil press in the early 20th century to study material properties at extremely high pressures (gigapascals), opening an entirely new scientific field and earning a Nobel Prize.
instances.key_quote: professor Bridgeman took diamond dyes and he would put different materials in there and he would do uh studies at extremely high pressure and by extremely high pressure we mean gigapascals thousands of atmospheres

cluster_id: Beer keg failure consulting case
display_name: Beer keg failure consulting case

instances.name: Beer keg failure consulting case
instances.summary: A failure investigation of a beer keg at the second-largest brewery in the United States, which led to Eagar's first major consulting job in 1976-1977.
instances.key_quote: my first big consulting job in 1976 or 77 was a failure of a beer keg in the second largest brewery in the United States

cluster_id: Lou Gerstner IBM building access incident
display_name: Lou Gerstner IBM building access incident

instances.name: Lou Gerstner IBM building access incident
instances.summary: New IBM CEO Lou Gerstner could not enter the building on his first day because he lacked an ID badge and could not obtain one without already being inside, exposing a broken security system.
instances.key_quote: he couldn't get in the building he didn't have an ID sort of a catch-22 couldn't get an ID until he got in the building but he didn't have an idea

cluster_id: IBM PowerPoint executive meetings under Gerstner
display_name: IBM PowerPoint executive meetings under Gerstner

instances.name: IBM PowerPoint executive meetings under Gerstner
instances.summary: IBM executives presented business information via PowerPoint; Gerstner shut off the projector after 15-20 minutes and demanded direct discussion, causing widespread internal reaction within five minutes.
instances.key_quote: after about 15 or 20 minutes he went up he turned off the overhead projector he says let's just talk about your business well emails went around I B M within five minutes

cluster_id: Cement truck case
display_name: Cement truck case

instances.name: Cement truck case
instances.summary: A regulatory case illustrating conflict between federal and state government authority over codes and standards.
instances.key_quote: we've talked in the cement truck case about how the federal government always trumps the state governments and it gets complex about who whether the federal government or the state government is going to have responsibility
instances.framework_lesson: Federal preemption doctrine determines which level of government has authority when state and federal codes conflict.

cluster_id: Watertown Arsenal Charpy bar standards
display_name: Watertown Arsenal Charpy bar standards

instances.name: Watertown Arsenal Charpy bar standards
instances.summary: The U.S. Army's Watertown Arsenal historically calibrated Charpy impact test bars until the Mansfield Amendment of 1972 redirected military research funding, after which NIST assumed responsibility.
instances.key_quote: those were calibrated over here at watertown arsenal which is now watertown mall okay but it used to be an army arsenal and they did that for years because the army was interested in steel and the impact properties of steel
instances.framework_lesson: When federal defense budgets shift priorities, civilian industry standards can be orphaned unless civilian agencies like NIST step in to maintain critical reference materials.

cluster_id: Iranian pipeline construction
display_name: Iranian pipeline construction

instances.name: Iranian pipeline construction
instances.summary: Historically, American welders from Tulsa, Oklahoma were contracted to build oil pipelines in Iran, working six-month assignments, until geopolitical relations deteriorated and this practice ceased.
instances.key_quote: it used to be that they'd be american welders from tulsa oklahoma who would spend six months in iran putting together an oil pipeline in iran that's no longer true because we don't have great relations with the iranians

cluster_id: Defect found in uninspected weld during 10% sampling inspection
display_name: Defect found in uninspected weld during 10% sampling inspection

instances.name: Defect found in uninspected weld during 10% sampling inspection
instances.summary: A contractor performed 10% weld inspection as specified in the contract, but a defect was discovered in an uninspected weld, triggering a dispute over whether 100% inspection must now be performed at whose cost.
instances.key_quote: probably happens someone gives me a call twice a year on this type of problem one of the wells that wasn't inspected they found a defect
instances.framework_lesson: The code requires 100% visual inspection if defects are found after partial sampling, creating contractual disputes when owners demand full inspection retroactively; this gray area generates litigation.

cluster_id: Ladder standards specification outdated by regulation
display_name: Ladder standards specification outdated by regulation

instances.name: Ladder standards specification outdated by regulation
instances.summary: Government and OSHA regulations specified ladder construction standards frozen at 1966 editions and never updated, causing 2010-era ladders to be built to nearly 50-year-old design specifications.
instances.key_quote: a lot of times when the state government will or osha will specify that all ladders shall be built to such and such standard and they'll put dash 1966 and they never go back to update it
instances.framework_lesson: Codes and standards locked into law without update mechanisms become obsolete; regulations should reference 'current edition' or specify the edition in effect on a particular date, not fixed historical editions.

cluster_id: U.S. Army Underground Storage Tank Corrosion Study
display_name: U.S. Army Underground Storage Tank Corrosion Study

instances.name: U.S. Army Underground Storage Tank Corrosion Study
instances.summary: Study of 15,000 Army underground storage tanks showed rapid corrosion failures in aggressive soils, with some tanks leaking within 6 years despite eighth-inch walls.
instances.key_quote: the quickest leaks were in six years and typically some of these tanks have about an eighth inch thick wall

cluster_id: West Virginia Chemical Tank Spill / Charleston Water Crisis
display_name: West Virginia Chemical Tank Spill / Charleston Water Crisis

instances.name: West Virginia Chemical Tank Spill / Charleston Water Crisis
instances.summary: An old riveted chemical storage tank leaked into Charleston, West Virginia's water supply, contaminating the city water system and causing a major public health incident.
instances.key_quote: the tank started leaking and they got over a protective barrier into the City of Charleston Water Supply okay well those old tanks were riveted tanks okay they were from the 1940s someone might have thought that maybe they should have been inspected
instances.framework_lesson: Old riveted tanks from the 1940s were not being inspected despite known corrosion risks; lack of inspection protocols allowed failure to reach catastrophic consequences.

cluster_id: Nuclear Reactor Microbiologically Induced Corrosion
display_name: Nuclear Reactor Microbiologically Induced Corrosion

instances.name: Nuclear Reactor Microbiologically Induced Corrosion
instances.summary: Sulfur-reducing bacteria colonies were discovered surviving in high-radiation zones of a nuclear reactor core, causing microbiologically induced corrosion damage.
instances.key_quote: they had some Mick corrosion and a nuclear reactor and these bugs were surviving forty thousand rad okay so those who use literally a human would last for about three minutes
instances.framework_lesson: Microbiologically induced corrosion was not recognized in corrosion literature until nuclear reactor failures revealed sulfur-reducing bacteria could survive extreme radiation and cause localized attack.

cluster_id: HMS Copper-Sheathed Naval Vessels
display_name: HMS Copper-Sheathed Naval Vessels

instances.name: HMS Copper-Sheathed Naval Vessels
instances.summary: British Navy ships sheathed with copper in 1836 (including Humphrey Davy's design) used copper hulls to prevent barnacle fouling and extend service life.
instances.key_quote: in 1836 a Humphrey Davey made a copper hold ship for the British Navy to get rid of all the Barnacles that would grow on there

cluster_id: Florida high-rise copper-plated stainless steel water piping failure
display_name: Florida high-rise copper-plated stainless steel water piping failure

instances.name: Florida high-rise copper-plated stainless steel water piping failure
instances.summary: A 40-story oceanfront condominium in Florida experienced rapid perforation of copper-plated stainless steel water pipes within 18 months due to galvanic corrosion through plating holidays, creating an unintended sprinkler system from pipe leaks.
instances.key_quote: they put this in a big condo 40-story condo down in Florida turns out right on the ocean in Florida turns out they have salt in the air they have hurricanes come by and within about a year and a half they had a sprinkler system
instances.framework_lesson: Plating a noble metal (copper) onto a non-noble substrate (stainless steel) without understanding galvanic couples and the role of microscopic plating defects (holidays) causes accelerated corrosion at defect sites.

cluster_id: Gold-plated hydrocephalus shunt valve consulting case
display_name: Gold-plated hydrocephalus shunt valve consulting case

instances.name: Gold-plated hydrocephalus shunt valve consulting case
instances.summary: A medical device manufacturer proposed gold-coating hydrocephalus shunt valves based on a physician's recommendation; a consulting metallurgist demonstrated the failure risk by immersing a sample valve in saline solution over a weekend, producing visible black corrosion that halted the project.
instances.key_quote: Bob just reaches over to his briefcase he pulls out this jar of saline solution which is all black and murky because it's been corroding all weekend okay he says I put one of the shunts in Saline Solution on Friday he said everyone just sort of got sick and walked out of the room
instances.framework_lesson: Medical device design decisions made by physicians without metallurgical expertise led to specification of a noble coating on a non-noble substrate, which accelerates corrosion through galvanic attack—a critical oversight that engineering consultation should have prevented earlier.

cluster_id: Pacemaker battery case corrosion
display_name: Pacemaker battery case corrosion

instances.name: Pacemaker battery case corrosion
instances.summary: Stainless steel battery cases in pacemakers from three major manufacturers developed intergranular pitting attacks, likely caused by low-pH deionized water (pH 3) used in post-manufacturing cleaning.
instances.key_quote: they were having corrosion problems they were having little pits and you can see here I copied this one you can see the intergranular attack this is the pit
instances.framework_lesson: Deionized water assumed to be inert in cleaning specifications can degrade in storage and develop acidic pH, causing unexpected corrosion of stainless implant materials if not monitored.

cluster_id: Admiralty brass heat exchanger plugging failure
display_name: Admiralty brass heat exchanger plugging failure

instances.name: Admiralty brass heat exchanger plugging failure
instances.summary: A utility heat exchanger made of admiralty brass experienced rapid pitting failure within approximately one year due to insufficient filtration allowing sediment films to accumulate on tube surfaces.
instances.key_quote: if you didn't have filters to keep the solids out of your heat exchanger tubes and you get a little layer of a film of that stuff you just pit right through that admiralty brass in about a year
instances.framework_lesson: Protective oxide films on copper alloys require active maintenance and filtration to prevent velocity-enhanced erosion and pitting; passive design assumptions are insufficient.

cluster_id: 118-foot pressure vessel stress relief
display_name: 118-foot pressure vessel stress relief

instances.name: 118-foot pressure vessel stress relief
instances.summary: A large welded pressure vessel approximately 118 feet long, 3-4 inches thick steel wall, designed for high-pressure gas service at an oil refinery, required on-site stress relief because it was too large to fit in a standard furnace.
instances.key_quote: Cooper heat will come to your site for a fee and they will build a furnace around your part and so it could be a hunt and the 118-foot long when they actually that's a little kind of railroad tracks
instances.framework_lesson: Extremely large pressure vessels exceed practical furnace capacity and require mobile or localized stress-relief solutions to manage residual welding stresses.

cluster_id: Granite Mountain water pipeline failure (California)
display_name: Granite Mountain water pipeline failure (California)

instances.name: Granite Mountain water pipeline failure (California)
instances.summary: A large welded steel water pipeline section ruptured catastrophically at 188 psi after improper field welding in winter conditions, causing hydrogen cracking and releasing millions of gallons of water down a canyon.
instances.key_quote: they were welding this thing with snow on top of it an inch and a quarter away I don't think they got very good pre and 20 hours later there's some workmen there and he hears a crack he sees water streaming out of this thing
instances.framework_lesson: Pressure vessel codes and specifications failed to account for the practical reality of winter field welding without adequate supervision, preheating, or post-weld heat treatment on critical infrastructure.

cluster_id: American Bridges slander case
display_name: American Bridges slander case

instances.name: American Bridges slander case
instances.summary: A thousand-person consulting firm made premature public accusations against American Bridges three days after a bridge failure, leading to a lawsuit where American Bridges won $17 million in punitive damages for slander instead of paying $100 million in failure liability.
instances.key_quote: they not only didn't have to pay a hundred million dollars they receive seventeen million dollars in punitive damages for slander okay because the number just hired by PG&E to investigate it was on site basically made a press announcement
instances.framework_lesson: Rushing to public attribution of failure before completing investigation creates legal liability for the investigating firm.

cluster_id: Steinmetz General Electric generator failure
display_name: Steinmetz General Electric generator failure

instances.name: Steinmetz General Electric generator failure
instances.summary: A newly installed General Electric generator malfunctioned; Steinmetz diagnosed the problem as a faulty copper winding connection in the steel case through expert analysis and specified a precise repair.
instances.key_quote: he draws a little box and puts an X in it he says cut the steel case open here cut this connection in the copper windings of the generator and you know sealed back up and that will fix it and they did and it worked
instances.framework_lesson: Expert knowledge of where the failure root cause lies has enormous value; the consultant's fee reflects diagnostic insight, not time spent.

cluster_id: 1985 automotive galvanized steel spot-weld electrode failure
display_name: 1985 automotive galvanized steel spot-weld electrode failure

instances.name: 1985 automotive galvanized steel spot-weld electrode failure
instances.summary: Ford and Chrysler encountered premature copper electrode wear when spot-welding galvanized steel in the mid-1980s; zinc from the coating alloyed with copper electrodes, creating high-resistance interfaces and limiting weld count to 100–200 before tip failure.
instances.key_quote: they were getting 100 or 200 Wells before the tips were worn out and that's why I said you know you got to put two three thousand spot Wells an average automobile because you need two thousand good ones
instances.framework_lesson: Ford's specification of 2,000 welds per electrode set was driven by production-line economics (5,000–10,000 dollars per minute downtime) rather than metallurgical limits, masking the underlying zinc–copper interaction problem.

cluster_id: Chrysler refrigerated spot-weld electrode cooling proposal (mid-1980s)
display_name: Chrysler refrigerated spot-weld electrode cooling proposal (mid-1980s)

instances.name: Chrysler refrigerated spot-weld electrode cooling proposal (mid-1980s)
instances.summary: A vendor proposed a 50-million-dollar refrigeration system to chill water flowing through copper spot-weld electrodes at Chrysler; Eagar's Fourier-number calculation proved cooling during the 1/3-second weld pulse was ineffective, but condensation on unpainted steel would damage assembly quality.
instances.key_quote: I did 4a's law right there in front of them they didn't have a clue I put an equation in front of them and their eyes glazed over and but nonetheless I prove that
instances.framework_lesson: Engineering managers rejected analytical proof in favor of experimental validation; intuition about heat removal masked the transient Fourier-number constraint and overlooked practical failure modes (condensation corrosion).

cluster_id: MIT ice-water chiller experimental validation (1980s)
display_name: MIT ice-water chiller experimental validation (1980s)

instances.name: MIT ice-water chiller experimental validation (1980s)
instances.summary: Eagar's group performed a 55,000-dollar research contract for Chrysler using a 55-gallon trash can filled with ice and water as a makeshift chiller to experimentally validate that chilled-water electrode cooling provided no benefit during steady operation.
instances.key_quote: we actually did the test we did some experiments we did the calculations and I went and convinced pricer Chrysler for a mere fifty five thousand dollars I convinced them not to spend 50 million dollars on water chillers
instances.framework_lesson: Experimental proof—even with improvised apparatus—was required to convince industrial partners that theoretical analysis was correct, demonstrating the barrier between academic rigor and industrial decision-making.

cluster_id: Automotive spot-weld electrode wear improvement (1980–1990)
display_name: Automotive spot-weld electrode wear improvement (1980–1990)

instances.name: Automotive spot-weld electrode wear improvement (1980–1990)
instances.summary: Over a decade of research funded by General Motors, Ford, and lead-zinc consortia, electrode life improved from ~200 welds in 1980 to ~2,000 welds by 1990 through galvanizing-process refinement, thinner zinc gauge, and rigorous tip-alignment maintenance.
instances.key_quote: by 1990 they were getting about 2 000 spots but one of the things they did they changed some of the steel sheet surface the galvanizing and they went to thinner gauge of zinc they one of the more important things is they decided to do maintenance on their tips
instances.framework_lesson: Manufacturing defects caused by misalignment and equipment wear—not material properties alone—drove early failures; systematic maintenance discipline and process control were as critical as metallurgical optimization.

cluster_id: General Motors spot welding maintenance study
display_name: General Motors spot welding maintenance study

instances.name: General Motors spot welding maintenance study
instances.summary: General Motors Research conducted a study of plant problems in spot welding and found that 35 of 39 root cause issues were maintenance-related, leading the research manager to cancel all spot welding research funding.
instances.key_quote: out of 39 root cause problems they found that something like 35 of them were all maintenance issues in the plant and the top ten were all maintenance issues
instances.framework_lesson: Research funding priorities misaligned with actual production problems; maintenance and equipment care in manufacturing plants were the critical bottleneck, not new welding science.

cluster_id: Ford steel supplier qualification discrepancy
display_name: Ford steel supplier qualification discrepancy

instances.name: Ford steel supplier qualification discrepancy
instances.summary: Ford required steel suppliers to pass 2000-weld qualification tests using 45-degree electrode geometry, but plants used 15-degree geometry in production, creating a mismatch between specification and practice.
instances.key_quote: what were they using in the plant a 15 degree angle what were they using to run the test it was actually part of the Ford spec 45 degrees
instances.framework_lesson: Specifications can fail when the test method differs from production practice; validation conditions must match real-world manufacturing to ensure quality.

cluster_id: Ford EB maintenance crisis
display_name: Ford EB maintenance crisis

instances.name: Ford EB maintenance crisis
instances.summary: A plant manager at Ford who operated out-of-vacuum electron beam welding machines experienced such severe maintenance headaches that he expressed homicidal rage at the mere mention of the technology.
instances.key_quote: if you just mention how to vacuum Electron Beam he would just pull out a knife wanting to kill anyone in an area
instances.framework_lesson: Out-of-vacuum EB systems with continuous vacuum-system leakage create unsustainable maintenance burdens despite superior welding speed, making them operationally impractical despite theoretical advantages.

cluster_id: MIT fraternity house tutor death
display_name: MIT fraternity house tutor death

instances.name: MIT fraternity house tutor death
instances.summary: A house tutor at MIT drank himself to death in a fraternity refrigerator in October of the early 2000s, prompting the administration to reinstitute the house tutor program.
instances.key_quote: until this guy in the early 2000s drank himself to death in the fridge in October and they decided oh we should have a house tutor program

cluster_id: Amorphous metals development
display_name: Amorphous metals development

instances.name: Amorphous metals development
instances.summary: In the 1970s-1980s, researchers including Nick Rant pursued splat-cooling of metals to create amorphous metallic structures without grain boundaries for highly efficient transformer cores, but could not economically manufacture cores of usable substance.
instances.key_quote: the problem is he couldn't make amorphous metal transformers you couldn't make cores or anything of any substance from a splat cooled metal all you had was a metal splat
instances.framework_lesson: New materials require not only scientific feasibility but also economically viable manufacturing processes to transition from intellectual curiosity to commercial application.

cluster_id: Amorphous metal ribbon commercialization
display_name: Amorphous metal ribbon commercialization

instances.name: Amorphous metal ribbon commercialization
instances.summary: A materials company developed amorphous metal ribbon for transformer cores with ten times lower hysteresis losses than silicon-iron, but faced a decade-long, $100 million development cycle and ultimately marginal economic competitiveness against incumbent steel.
instances.key_quote: ten years and 100 million dollars later people figured out how to do that the problem is that steel is used for transformer applications is cheap
instances.framework_lesson: New material technologies must satisfy strict economic thresholds to displace entrenched commodity materials, even with superior technical properties.

cluster_id: Allegheny Ludlum intellectual property loss
display_name: Allegheny Ludlum intellectual property loss

instances.name: Allegheny Ludlum intellectual property loss
instances.summary: Allegheny Ludlum sent amorphous metal samples to University of Pennsylvania professor Chad Graham for magnetic property measurement without a non-disclosure agreement, resulting in the university claiming intellectual property rights to the low-loss magnetic properties and causing a hundred-million-dollar loss to the company.
instances.key_quote: they hadn't signed a nondisclosure agreement with Jack Gramm and so it turns out the University of Pennsylvania and Chad Graham of the intellectual property rights to the low magnetic properties lost properties of the amorphous metal
instances.framework_lesson: Failure to execute IP protection agreements before sharing proprietary technical data with external experts can result in loss of intellectual property rights and competitive advantage.

cluster_id: Silicon-iron competitive response
display_name: Silicon-iron competitive response

instances.name: Silicon-iron competitive response
instances.summary: When amorphous metals threatened to displace silicon-iron in transformers with ten times lower losses, incumbent steel producers Allegheny Ludlum and Arco invested in laboratory improvements to their product, closing the performance gap and preventing market displacement.
instances.key_quote: Allegheny Ludlum and Arco and a few other steel companies that made the soap in honour and that was a billion-dollar business they decided they didn't want to lose it so they went back to the laboratory
instances.framework_lesson: Entrenched competitors with billion-dollar revenue streams will innovate defensively when threatened by superior technologies, preventing assumed market disruption.

cluster_id: Turbine blade cooling technology development
display_name: Turbine blade cooling technology development

instances.name: Turbine blade cooling technology development
instances.summary: High-temperature turbine blades operating at 3000°F (600°F above material melting point of 2400°F) are cooled internally by compressor air to enable modern jet engines; MIT professor Shank and colleague Nick Grant developed the materials technology at Pratt & Whitney in the 1960s-70s.
instances.key_quote: he was the guy who actually in the nineteen sixties and seventies development materials technology for these hours so he actually can always you can often go back to MIT it was an MIT and Nick rad that doesn't first spectrally was a guy at Caltech

cluster_id: Student carbon fiber automotive shell project
display_name: Student carbon fiber automotive shell project

instances.name: Student carbon fiber automotive shell project
instances.summary: MIT student designed and built a small carbon fiber-bodied vehicle that achieved a $2 per pound weight savings while reducing vehicle cost approximately $40,000 through cascade effects including smaller battery requirements for electric/hybrid powertrains.
instances.key_quote: that was one of the students in this class his paper which is a carbon fiber shell all now that vehicle cost there's about a $40,000 meaning okay the size of the vehicle it's a small heart but it turns out the way they were able to do that plus carbon fibers are kind of expensive this day said then he still got this $2 a pound savings

cluster_id: Pratt & Whitney 1975 laser disk additive manufacturing experiment
display_name: Pratt & Whitney 1975 laser disk additive manufacturing experiment

instances.name: Pratt & Whitney 1975 laser disk additive manufacturing experiment
instances.summary: Pratt & Whitney attempted to make a six-inch disk using a 75 kilowatt laser in an early additive manufacturing experiment around 1975.
instances.key_quote: the first time they ever tried to make a disk by additive manufacturing was 1975 Pratt Whitney had the 75 kilowatt laser
